  communication_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the language in which the consent was presented and captured. Ensures consent was obtained in the customer\'s preferred language.']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'Category of consent or preference being managed. Defines the specific processing purpose or communication channel covered by this consent event.']
  consent_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational scope to which this consent applies. Defines the boundaries of consent applicability across different jurisdictions or business units.']
  consent_text varchar [note: 'The exact consent language or terms that were displayed to the customer at the time of consent. Provides evidence of what the customer agreed to for regulatory audits.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent policy or terms and conditions that were presented to the customer at the time of this event. Enables tracking of policy changes over time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this consent event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  customer_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer at the time of the consent event. Captured for verification and communication purposes in consent lifecycle management.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that data will be retained under this consent. Used to enforce data minimization principles and automated data deletion policies.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (mobile, tablet, desktop) from which the consent event was initiated. Used for cross-device consent tracking and fraud prevention.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the consent event occurred, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). This is the authoritative timestamp for regulatory audit trails.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of consent event that occurred. Categorizes the nature of the customer\'s action for regulatory reporting and lifecycle tracking.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this consent is set to expire and require renewal. Used for time-bound consent management and automated re-consent workflows.']
  geolocation_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location from which the consent event originated. Used for jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting.']
  geolocation_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region from which the consent event originated. Provides additional geographic context for jurisdiction-specific privacy laws (e.g., CCPA for California).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Internet Protocol address of the device from which the consent event was initiated. Used for fraud detection and regulatory evidence of consent origin.']
  is_minor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer was identified as a minor (under age of consent) at the time of this event. Critical for GDPR Article 8 and COPPA compliance.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under which personal data processing is justified for this consent event. Required for GDPR Article 6 compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The consent status after this event was applied. Represents the current state resulting from the customer\'s action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments associated with this consent event. May include customer service representative notes for call center-originated events or system-generated messages.']
  opt_out_sale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has exercised their right to opt out of the sale of personal information under CCPA. Must be honored across all business processes.']
  parental_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was required for this event due to the customer being a minor. Used for COPPA and GDPR Article 8 compliance tracking.']
  parental_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was successfully verified for this event. Critical for processing consent events for minors under COPPA and GDPR.']
  prior_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The consent status before this event occurred. Captures the previous state to enable audit trail reconstruction and change tracking.']
  processing_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consent event was fully processed and applied across all relevant systems. Used to measure compliance with GDPR\'s one-month response requirement.']
  processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific purpose for which personal data will be processed under this consent. Must be explicit and granular per GDPR requirements.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the consent event. Tracks whether the event has been successfully applied to downstream systems and customer profiles.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the user session during which the consent event occurred. Enables correlation with other user actions in the same session for behavioral analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system that originated this consent event record. Identifies the operational system of record for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  third_party_sharing_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to sharing their personal data with third parties. Critical for CCPA \'Do Not Sell\' compliance and GDPR Article 6 lawful basis.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user agent string at the time of consent. Provides technical context about the device and software used for the consent action.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the customer before processing the consent event. Critical for data deletion and portability requests to prevent fraud.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of identity verification for this consent event. Indicates whether the customer\'s identity was successfully confirmed before processing the request.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of every consent change event for a customer, required for GDPR Article 7 and CCPA compliance. Stores event type (consent-given, consent-withdrawn, consent-updated, data-deletion-requested, data-portability-requested), consent category, prior value, new value, event timestamp, collection method (web form, mobile app, call center, email link), IP address at time of consent, user agent string, legal basis (legitimate interest, contract, consent), and processing purpose. This is the regulatory evidence trail for consent lifecycle.'
}

Table customer.cltv_score {
  cltv_score_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CLTV scoring record. Primary key for this operational scoring entity that drives real-time personalization and retention workflows.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this CLTV score is calculated. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.']
  aov_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per order placed by this customer. Key input to CLTV calculation and used to identify high-ticket customers for upsell opportunities.']
  churn_probability_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predicted likelihood that this customer will stop purchasing within the next 90 days. Expressed as a probability between 0 and 1. Scores above 0.7 trigger retention campaigns.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical segmentation of customer value. High-value customers receive premium service and retention offers; at-risk customers trigger win-back campaigns; dormant customers enter re-engagement workflows.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the CLTV prediction, expressed as a probability between 0 and 1. Higher values indicate more reliable predictions based on sufficient historical data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CLTV score record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this record. Ensures correct interpretation of CLTV amounts in multi-currency environments.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification based on CLTV tier, RFM scores, and behavioral patterns. Drives differentiated marketing strategies and service levels.']
  days_since_first_order varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the customer placed their first order. Represents customer tenure and is used to calculate customer lifetime metrics.']
  days_since_last_order varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the customer placed their most recent order. Critical input to recency scoring and churn prediction models.']
  frequency_score varchar [note: 'RFM frequency component: score from 1 to 5 representing how often the customer purchases. 5 = most frequent, 1 = least frequent. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.']
  historical_cltv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cumulative revenue generated by this customer to date. Represents realized value from all completed orders and transactions.']
  model_features_used varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of feature names used by the CLTV model for this customer. Enables model explainability and debugging of score anomalies.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the CLTV prediction model used to calculate this score. Enables model performance tracking and A/B testing of scoring algorithms.']
  monetary_score varchar [note: 'RFM monetary component: score from 1 to 5 representing how much the customer spends. 5 = highest spender, 1 = lowest spender. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.']
  next_recalculation_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next CLTV score refresh. Ensures scores remain current and reflect recent customer behavior. Typically set to 30, 60, or 90 days from calculation date.']
  predicted_cltv_12m decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue expected from this customer over the next 12 months. Used for short-term marketing budget allocation and campaign targeting.']
  predicted_cltv_36m decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue expected from this customer over the next 36 months. Used for long-term strategic planning and customer segment investment decisions.']
  preferred_category varchar [note: 'Product category with the highest purchase frequency or spend for this customer. Used for personalized product recommendations and targeted marketing campaigns.']
  purchase_frequency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Normalized score representing how often this customer makes purchases compared to the customer base average. Higher scores indicate more frequent buyers.']
  recency_score varchar [note: 'RFM recency component: score from 1 to 5 representing how recently the customer made a purchase. 5 = most recent, 1 = least recent. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.']
  rfm_composite_score varchar [note: 'Three-digit RFM score combining recency, frequency, and monetary scores. Example: 555 represents the best customers (recent, frequent, high-value). Used for customer segmentation and targeting.']
  score_calculation_date date [note: 'Date when this CLTV score was calculated. Enables tracking of score changes over time and ensures users understand the freshness of the prediction.']
  score_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this CLTV score record. Active scores are used in real-time decisioning; expired scores trigger recalculation workflows.']
  total_orders_count varchar [note: 'Total number of orders placed by this customer to date. Used as a key input to frequency scoring and customer lifecycle stage determination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CLTV score record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for change data capture and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) scoring record capturing the calculated and predicted value of a customer relationship. Stores CLTV model version, historical CLTV (actual spend to date), predicted CLTV (12-month and 36-month horizon), CLTV tier (high-value, mid-value, low-value, at-risk), AOV contribution, purchase frequency score, churn probability score, recency score, frequency score, monetary score (RFM composite), score calculation date, and next recalculation date. This is an operational scoring record, not an analytics aggregate — it drives real-time personalization and retention workflows.'
}

Table customer.nps_response {
  nps_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NPS survey response record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who submitted this NPS response.']
  follow_up_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent or account manager who handled the follow-up action for this response.']
  survey_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign or survey program that this response belongs to. Used for cohort analysis and A/B testing.']
  triggering_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Links NPS feedback to the purchased product, enabling product‑level sentiment analysis in the NPS reporting dashboard.']
  anonymized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this response has been anonymized (customer identifier removed) for privacy compliance or public reporting.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Customer classification based on NPS score: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6).']
  consent_given_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer provided explicit consent for their feedback to be used for marketing, testimonials, or case studies.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the customer\'s location at time of response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NPS response record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment classification at the time of survey (e.g., VIP, regular, new, at-risk). Used for segment-level NPS analysis.']
  customer_tenure_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the customer has been active with the company at the time of survey response. Used to correlate NPS with customer lifecycle stage.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer to complete the survey: desktop, mobile, tablet, or unknown.']
  follow_up_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken in response to customer feedback: none (no action needed), contacted (customer reached out to), resolved (issue addressed), escalated (sent to management), pending (awaiting action).']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Date when follow-up action was completed or customer was contacted regarding their feedback.']
  follow_up_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting the follow-up action taken, customer conversation summary, and resolution details. Business-confidential operational data.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this response requires follow-up action from customer service or management (typically true for detractors with critical feedback).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code indicating the language in which the survey was presented and response was submitted.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Customer rating on 0-10 scale indicating likelihood to recommend the company to others. 0 = not at all likely, 10 = extremely likely.']
  previous_nps_score varchar [note: 'The customer\'s most recent prior NPS score before this response. Used to track individual customer sentiment trends and score changes.']
  response_date date [note: 'Date when the customer submitted their NPS response.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Status of the survey response: completed (full response received), partial (started but not finished), bounced (delivery failed), opted_out (customer declined), expired (survey window closed).']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time elapsed in hours between survey send and customer response submission. Used to measure survey engagement speed.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the customer submitted their NPS response.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'AI-derived sentiment analysis score of the verbatim comment text, ranging from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive). Generated by natural language processing engine.']
  sentiment_topics varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of topics or themes extracted from verbatim comment via natural language processing (e.g., delivery_speed, product_quality, customer_service, pricing).']
  survey_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the NPS survey was delivered and completed: email, in-app notification, SMS, web intercept, IVR (Interactive Voice Response), or post-purchase embedded.']
  survey_send_date date [note: 'Date when the NPS survey invitation was sent to the customer.']
  survey_send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the NPS survey invitation was sent to the customer.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of NPS survey administered: transactional NPS (triggered by specific event), relational NPS (periodic relationship health check), onboarding, post-purchase, post-support, or annual.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used. Tracks survey design changes over time for longitudinal analysis.']
  triggering_event_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific business event that triggered this survey (e.g., order number, return RMA number, support case ID). Format varies by event type.']
  triggering_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of business event that triggered this transactional NPS survey: order fulfillment, return processing, support case closure, delivery completion, account creation, or subscription renewal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NPS response record was last modified (e.g., follow-up action added, sentiment score updated).']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text customer feedback explaining the reason for their NPS score. May contain sensitive customer opinions and business-confidential feedback.']

  Note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response record capturing individual customer feedback events. Stores survey type (transactional NPS, relational NPS), NPS score (0-10), promoter/passive/detractor classification, verbatim comment text, survey channel (email, in-app, SMS, post-purchase), triggering event reference (order, return, support case), survey send date, response date, follow-up action taken, follow-up agent reference, and response status. SSOT for customer satisfaction measurement and NPS program management.'
}

Table customer.wishlist {
  wishlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wishlist. Primary key.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns this wishlist. Links to the customer master record in the Customer Data Platform.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was archived by the customer or system. Null if never archived. Supports lifecycle reporting and data retention policies.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'Number of items from this wishlist that have been purchased by the owner or gift contributors. Measures wishlist effectiveness and purchase intent conversion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was first created by the customer. Immutable. Used for lifecycle analytics and customer engagement measurement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this wishlist (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency e-commerce operations.']
  deleted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was soft-deleted. Null if active. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure and data retention compliance. Hard deletion occurs after retention period.']
  event_date date [note: 'Target date for gift registries (wedding date, baby due date, birthday). Used to trigger expiration logic and prioritize gift-giving urgency. Null for non-event wishlists.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the wishlist automatically transitions to expired status. Typically set for gift registries (e.g., 90 days post-event). Null for permanent wishlists.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the customer\'s default wishlist. When a customer adds an item without specifying a wishlist, it goes to the default. Only one wishlist per customer can be default.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active line items currently in the wishlist. Excludes removed or purchased items. Updated in real-time as items are added or removed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist metadata (name, type, visibility, preferences) was last updated. Excludes line item changes. Used for audit and sync purposes.']
  last_viewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer last opened or viewed this wishlist. Used for engagement scoring and re-engagement campaign targeting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form internal notes or metadata about the wishlist. May include customer service annotations, special handling instructions, or system-generated insights. Not visible to customer.']
  notification_back_in_stock_enabled boolean [note: 'Customer preference flag indicating whether to send notifications when out-of-stock items in this wishlist become available again. Drives conversion from latent demand.']
  notification_event_reminder_enabled boolean [note: 'Customer preference flag for gift registries indicating whether to send reminders as the event date approaches. Helps drive gift purchases from registry contributors.']
  notification_price_drop_enabled boolean [note: 'Customer preference flag indicating whether to send notifications when any item in this wishlist experiences a price reduction. Supports re-engagement campaigns.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Customer-assigned priority ranking for this wishlist relative to other wishlists owned by the same customer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for personalization and recommendation engines.']
  privacy_consent_marketing boolean [note: 'Customer consent flag indicating permission to use wishlist data for personalized marketing campaigns (price drop alerts, recommendations, promotions). Required under GDPR and CCPA.']
  privacy_consent_sharing boolean [note: 'Customer consent flag indicating explicit permission to share wishlist data with third parties (e.g., gift registry partners, social media). Required under GDPR and CCPA.']
  registry_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'For gift registries, the percentage of items that have been purchased by contributors. Calculated as (purchased_items / total_items) * 100. Null for non-registry wishlists.']
  share_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the wishlist sharing URL has been generated or distributed. Tracks viral/social engagement for gift registries.']
  sharing_token varchar [note: 'Secure token embedded in sharing URL for access control. Prevents unauthorized access to shared wishlists. Regenerated if sharing is disabled and re-enabled.']
  sharing_url varchar [note: 'Unique shareable URL for gift registries and shared wishlists. Enables customers to distribute wishlist access to friends and family without requiring account login.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the wishlist was originally created (web browser, mobile app, API integration, customer service agent). Used for channel attribution and UX optimization.']
  source_device_type varchar [note: 'Device type used when the wishlist was created. Supports device-specific personalization and responsive design optimization.']
  total_current_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all item prices at current market rates. Recalculated nightly or on-demand. Enables price drop alerts and conversion optimization. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified.']
  total_value_at_save decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all item prices at the time they were added to the wishlist. Provides baseline for price change tracking and demand forecasting. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified.']
  view_count_by_others varchar [note: 'Number of times the wishlist has been viewed by users other than the owner (gift contributors, shared link recipients). Measures gift registry engagement.']
  visibility varchar [note: 'Access control setting for the wishlist. Private: only owner can view; Public: discoverable by others; Shared Link: accessible via unique URL.']
  wishlist_description varchar [note: 'Optional customer-provided description or notes about the wishlist purpose, occasion, or preferences. Supports gift registry messaging and personalization.']
  wishlist_name varchar [note: 'Customer-defined name for the wishlist (e.g., \'Birthday Gifts\', \'Home Office Setup\', \'Wedding Registry\'). Enables customers to organize multiple wishlists by purpose.']
  wishlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wishlist. Active: in use; Archived: preserved but inactive; Expired: past event date for registries; Deleted: soft-deleted pending purge.']
  wishlist_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wishlist purpose. Personal wishlists are private shopping lists; gift registries are shared for events (weddings, baby showers); shared wishlists allow collaborative curation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personal|gift_registry|shared|wedding_registry|baby_registry|holiday|wishlist — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Customer wishlist representing products explicitly saved for future consideration. Stores wishlist header: name, type (personal, gift-registry, shared), visibility setting, creation date, sharing URL, expiry date for gift registries. Owns individual line items: each item records SKU reference, product name at save time, price at save time, current price, price change indicator, availability status, priority rank, customer notes, date added, date removed, and notification preferences for price drops and back-in-stock alerts. Supports personalization, re-engagement campaigns, and gift registry workflows. Distinct from cart (active purchase intent) — wishlist represents longer-term interest signals and demand forecasting inputs.'
}

Table customer.merge_event {
  merge_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each merge event record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'System user (e.g., data steward or automated process) that initiated the merge.']
  created_by_user_agent_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or service that initially created the merge event record.']
  primary_merge_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer profile that remains active after the merge.']
  reversed_by_merge_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on merge_event (reversed_by_merge_event_id)']
  conflict_resolution_log varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured log describing how attribute conflicts were resolved during the merge.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the merge event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  is_test_event boolean [note: 'True if the merge event was generated in a test environment; otherwise False.']
  merge_method varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the duplicate profiles: golden‑record algorithm or manual selection.']
  merge_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the merge was performed: automatic match, agent‑initiated, or customer‑requested.']
  merge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the merge event.']
  merge_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the merge operation was executed.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the merge event record.']
  rollback_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a merge is rolled back or undone.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that emitted the merge event (e.g., Customer Data Platform, Order Management System).']

  Note: 'Tracks customer identity merge and deduplication events when the Customer Data Platform identifies duplicate profiles. Stores surviving profile reference, merged (retired) profile reference, merge reason (CDP auto-match, agent-initiated, customer-requested), merge method (golden record, manual selection), merge timestamp, merged-by reference, merge status (pending, completed, rolled-back), rollback reason, and data conflict resolution log. Critical for maintaining SSOT integrity in the profile entity and supporting audit trails when customer records are consolidated. Enables downstream systems to redirect references from retired profiles to surviving profiles.'
}

Table customer.wishlist_item {
  wishlist_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the wishlist_item association']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the catalog item']
  wishlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the wishlist']
  added_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item was added to the wishlist']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes the customer adds for the item']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Customer-assigned priority for this item within the wishlist']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Desired quantity of the catalog item in the wishlist']

  Note: 'This association product represents the relationship between a customer wishlist and a catalog item. It captures when an item was added to a wishlist, the desired quantity, priority ranking, and any user notes. Each record links one wishlist to one catalog item and stores data that belongs only to the association.. Existence Justification: Customers actively create and manage wishlists, adding multiple catalog items to each wishlist. Each catalog item can appear in many different customers\' wishlists. The platform records attributes such as when an item was added, desired quantity, priority, and user notes, making the association a managed business entity.'
}

Table customer.organization {
  organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for organization']
  buyer_org_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on organization (buyer_org_id)']
  parent_organization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent organization in a corporate hierarchy, if applicable.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the organization’s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the organization’s mailing address (optional).']
  annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Reported annual revenue of the organization in US dollars.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the organization’s address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification used for segmentation and reporting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture with GDPR, CCPA, and other regulatory frameworks.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the organization’s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the organization record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for financial reporting.']
  data_protection_officer varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for data protection compliance.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Alternative name used for marketing or sales purposes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the organization’s business purpose.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the organization became active in the system.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the organization’s record is considered inactive or superseded (nullable).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for business correspondence.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems to reference the organization.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (e.g., NAICS) describing the organization’s primary activity.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Higher‑level sector grouping (e.g., Retail, Logistics, Technology).']
  is_subsidiary boolean [note: 'True if the organization is a subsidiary of another entity.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the organization (e.g., B2C, B2B, C2C).']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the organization as registered.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total number of employees employed by the organization.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the legal form of the organization.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for the organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the organization’s address.']
  primary_contact_detail varchar [note: 'Contact information (e.g., email address or phone number) used for the primary contact method.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for official communications with the organization.']
  privacy_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the organization provided consent for data processing.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned based on compliance and financial factors.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the organization’s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the organization within the ecosystem.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the organization is exempt from tax under applicable regulations.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Business name under which the organization operates publicly.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the organization record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for organization. Referenced by buyer_org_id.'
}

Table customer.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  parent_party_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on party (parent_party_id)']
  account_creation_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s account was initially created.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the primary address.']
  cltv_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated lifetime value score for the party.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has given consent for marketing communications.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when consent was recorded.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the party data.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the party (if a person).']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for deactivating the party record, if applicable.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communication.']
  external_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used for the party in external partner systems.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score indicating likelihood of fraudulent activity.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the party (if applicable).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the party record is currently active.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the party\'s most recent login to the platform.']
  loyalty_program_member boolean [note: 'Indicates if the party is enrolled in the loyalty program.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty tier of the party.']
  marketing_opt_out boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the party has opted out of marketing communications.']
  name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the party.']
  national_id varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text internal notes about the party.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest Net Promoter Score for the party.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party indicating its role in the business ecosystem.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the primary address.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for contacting the party.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code preferred by the party.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  profile_picture_url varchar [note: 'URL to the party\'s profile picture.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number for organizational parties.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system of the party record.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the primary address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party.']
  tax_id varchar [note: 'Tax registration number for the party.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table product.catalog_item {
  catalog_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the catalog item. Primary key for the product catalog master record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.brand']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Preferred carrier for this catalog item; required for carrier compliance checks and shipping cost estimation reports.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.product_category']
  marketing_content_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: REQUIRED: Product detail pages pull rich HTML content from CMS; linking catalog items to content items drives PDP rendering.']
  primary_seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Default Seller Assignment for catalog items, needed for pricing, fulfillment routing and sales reporting; obvious to marketplace operators.']
  asin varchar [note: '10-character alphanumeric unique identifier used for product catalog management in Amazon-style marketplaces.']
  color varchar [note: 'Primary color or color variant of the product, used for product differentiation, filtering, and variant management.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or assembled, required for customs, tariffs, and trade compliance.']
  cpsc_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the product meets CPSC safety standards and regulations, critical for product safety compliance and recall management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this catalog item record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  ean varchar [note: '13-digit international product identifier used globally for retail scanning and supply chain tracking.']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'The date when the product will be or was discontinued and removed from active catalog, used for inventory planning and clearance strategies.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Product height dimension in centimeters, used for packaging, shipping, and warehouse storage optimization.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International standardized system of names and numbers for classifying traded products, used for customs clearance, tariff calculation, and international trade compliance.']
  is_age_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product has age restrictions for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, mature content), requiring age verification at checkout.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, shipping restrictions, and compliance documentation per CPSC and DOT regulations.']
  is_returnable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is eligible for return under the standard Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) policy.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is subject to sales tax, VAT, or other consumption taxes in applicable jurisdictions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this catalog item record was last updated, used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date when the product was first made available for sale on the platform, used for new product performance tracking and lifecycle analytics.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Product length dimension in centimeters, used for packaging, shipping, and warehouse storage optimization.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the product in its lifecycle. Active products are available for sale, discontinued products are phased out, draft products are under development, archived products are historical records.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the company that manufactures the product, used for supplier management and compliance tracking.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The unique part number assigned by the manufacturer, used for warranty claims, technical support, and supplier coordination.']
  material_composition varchar [note: 'Description of the materials used in the product construction (e.g., 100% Cotton, Stainless Steel), used for product specifications and compliance labeling.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'The minimum age in years required to purchase this product, enforced during checkout for age-restricted items.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'The specific model identifier assigned by the manufacturer, used for product differentiation and technical specifications lookup.']
  pim_source_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this product in the source PIM system, used for data lineage and system integration reconciliation.']
  pim_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the Product Information Management system that is the authoritative source for this catalog item master data.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The primary display name of the product shown on Product Detail Pages (PDP) and Product Listing Pages (PLP). Used for customer-facing search and discovery.']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular subcategory classification within the product taxonomy, enabling precise product discovery and merchandising strategies.']
  product_title varchar [note: 'Extended product title optimized for Search Engine Optimization (SEO) and Search Engine Marketing (SEM), including key attributes and brand information.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the product category (e.g., Electronics, Apparel, Home & Garden), used for catalog organization and merchandising.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from delivery date within which the customer can initiate a return under the RMA policy.']
  size varchar [note: 'Size designation of the product (e.g., Small, Medium, Large, or numeric sizes), used for apparel, footwear, and size-variant products.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier for the product used across inventory, order management, and fulfillment systems. The authoritative SKU master identifier.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to determine applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST rates for the product in different jurisdictions.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit barcode identifier used for retail scanning and inventory tracking in North America.']
  warranty_duration_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the manufacturer or seller warranty in months, used for warranty claim validation and customer service.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty coverage provided with the product (manufacturer warranty, seller warranty, extended warranty, or no warranty).']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Product weight in kilograms, used for shipping cost calculation, carrier selection, and warehouse capacity planning.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Product width dimension in centimeters, used for packaging, shipping, and warehouse storage optimization.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a sellable product in the e-commerce catalog. Serves as the authoritative SKU master record, capturing product identity, UPC/EAN/ASIN identifiers, brand, manufacturer, model number, product type, lifecycle status (active, discontinued, draft, archived), launch date, end-of-life date, and PIM system source reference. This is the SSOT for all product identity data across the platform.'
}

Table product.sku {
  sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SKU record. Primary key.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Specific carrier service assigned to SKU for fulfillment planning and SLA performance monitoring.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent catalog item. Each SKU maps to exactly one catalog product.']
  inventory_general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Needed for inventory valuation accounting; each SKU posts inventory movements to its specific GL account for COGS and stock balance.']
  owner_seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Seller SKU Ownership links each SKU to its owning seller for inventory, compliance and fulfillment decisions; standard in multi‑seller marketplaces.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier or vendor for this SKU.']
  age_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU has age verification requirements (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, adult content).']
  asin varchar [note: '10-character alphanumeric unique identifier used for product identification in Amazon-style marketplaces.']
  bopis_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is eligible for buy online, pick up in store fulfillment.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where this SKU was manufactured or produced.']
  cpsc_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU meets CPSC product safety standards and testing requirements.']
  cpsc_tracking_label varchar [note: 'CPSC-required tracking label containing manufacturer, production date, batch, and cohort information for children\'s products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SKU record was first created in the system.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU was discontinued and removed from active catalog. Null for active SKUs.']
  dtc_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is eligible for direct-to-consumer shipping from distribution centers.']
  ean varchar [note: '13-digit European Article Number for international product identification and barcode scanning.']
  fba_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is eligible for FBA-style fulfillment services (stored in marketplace fulfillment centers).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling and shipping restrictions.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for packaging and shipping optimization.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU was first made available for sale.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for packaging and shipping optimization.']
  lot_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is tracked by lot or batch number for quality control and traceability.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s unique part number for this SKU, used for supplier ordering and warranty claims.']
  minimum_age_years varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this SKU. Null if no age restriction applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SKU record was last modified.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required when purchasing this SKU from the supplier.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging required for this SKU (e.g., standard, fragile, oversized, gift wrap, bulk, refrigerated).']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is perishable and requires temperature-controlled storage and expedited fulfillment.']
  serialized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether each unit of this SKU is tracked by unique serial number for warranty, recall, or anti-counterfeiting purposes.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days this SKU remains sellable from receipt date. Null for non-perishable items.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying this specific sellable variant. The atomic unit tracked by inventory and order systems.']
  sku_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU (active, inactive, discontinued, pending approval, blocked, seasonal).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Sellable unit of measure for this SKU (e.g., each, case, pack, box). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pack|box|pallet|dozen|pair|set — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for North American retail scanning and inventory tracking.']
  variant_color varchar [note: 'Color dimension of the variant (e.g., Blue, Red, Black, Navy).']
  variant_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the variant (e.g., Cotton, Polyester, Leather, Stainless Steel).']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing this specific variant (e.g., \'Blue Cotton T-Shirt - Size M\').']
  variant_size varchar [note: 'Size dimension of the variant (e.g., S, M, L, XL, 32x34, 10.5).']
  variant_style varchar [note: 'Style or design dimension of the variant (e.g., Crew Neck, V-Neck, Slim Fit, Classic).']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the SKU in kilograms, used for shipping cost calculation and carrier selection.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for packaging and shipping optimization.']

  Note: 'Stock Keeping Unit record representing a specific, orderable variant of a catalog item. Captures the unique SKU code, variant dimensions (size, color, material, style), sellable unit of measure, weight, dimensions (length, width, height), packaging type, hazmat flag, age restriction flag, country of origin, and fulfillment eligibility flags (FBA-eligible, BOPIS-eligible, DTC-eligible). Each SKU maps to exactly one catalog item and is the atomic unit tracked by inventory and order systems.'
}

Table product.category {
  category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product category node in the hierarchical taxonomy. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budgeting and expense allocation per product category uses a cost center; linking enables category‑level cost tracking in financial reports.']
  parent_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent category node in the hierarchy. Null for root-level categories. Enables tree traversal and multi-level category structures.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory Mapping Report requires each product category to reference the primary regulation governing that category, enabling category-level compliance audits.']
  average_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average gross margin percentage for products in this category. Used for pricing strategy, promotional planning, and financial forecasting. Business-confidential metric.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric business identifier for the category. Used for system integration, API (Application Programming Interface) calls, and internal catalog management.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the category displayed on PLP (Product Listing Page) headers. Provides context and improves SEO (Search Engine Optimization) content richness.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product category displayed on Product Listing Pages (PLP) and navigation menus. Used for customer-facing catalog browsing.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default commission rate percentage applied to marketplace seller transactions for products in this category. Used for GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) and seller disbursement calculations.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created the category record. Used for audit trail and governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  default_shipping_class varchar [note: 'Default shipping class for products in this category. Used for carrier selection, rate calculation, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) determination in TMS (Transportation Management System).']
  depth_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the category node in the hierarchy tree. Root categories are level 0 or 1. Used for tree traversal algorithms and display logic.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the sort order of categories within the same parent level. Lower values appear first in navigation menus and PLP (Product Listing Page).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the category is scheduled to be deactivated or archived. Null for indefinite categories. Used for seasonal category management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the category becomes active and visible to customers. Used for scheduled category launches and merchandising campaigns.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the source Product Information Management (PIM) system or ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system. Used for data integration and reconciliation.']
  full_path varchar [note: 'Complete hierarchical path from root to current node, typically slash-delimited (e.g., Electronics/Smartphones/Android Phones). Used for breadcrumb navigation and SEO (Search Engine Optimization).']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Hazardous material classification for products in this category. Used for shipping restrictions, carrier selection in TMS (Transportation Management System), and warehouse handling in WMS (Warehouse Management System).']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL to the category icon or glyph stored in CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used for compact navigation menus and mobile interfaces.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the category thumbnail or banner image stored in CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used for visual navigation tiles and PLP (Product Listing Page) headers.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the category is currently active and visible to customers. Inactive categories are hidden from navigation and search but retained for historical data.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category is promoted as a featured category on the homepage or main navigation. Used for merchandising campaigns.']
  is_leaf boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category is a terminal node (leaf) with no child categories. Leaf categories typically contain SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) assignments.']
  is_navigable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category appears in customer-facing navigation menus. Hidden categories may exist for internal classification only.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category is included in site search indexing and autocomplete suggestions. Used to control search faceting.']
  meta_description varchar [note: 'HTML meta description tag content for the category page. Displayed in search engine results snippets to improve CTR (Click-Through Rate).']
  meta_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords associated with the category for search engine indexing and internal search relevance tuning.']
  meta_title varchar [note: 'HTML meta title tag content for the category page. Optimized for search engine ranking and displayed in browser tabs and search results.']
  product_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) products currently assigned to this category. Used for merchandising analytics and empty category detection.']
  requires_age_verification boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether products in this category require age verification at checkout (e.g., alcohol, tobacco). Used for compliance with FTC (Federal Trade Commission) and state regulations.']
  requires_cpsc_compliance boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether products in this category must comply with CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) safety standards. Used for product onboarding validation.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Default number of days customers have to return products in this category. Used for RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) policy enforcement and customer service.']
  seo_slug varchar [note: 'URL-friendly lowercase hyphenated string used in category page URLs for SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and clean URL structure (e.g., electronics-smartphones).']
  taxonomy_standard varchar [note: 'Industry classification standard used for this category (e.g., GS1 Global Product Classification, UNSPSC, eCl@ss). Supports multi-taxonomy mapping for B2B (Business to Business) and marketplace integration.']
  updated_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified the category record. Used for audit trail and change management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the category record was last modified. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation, and ETL (Extract Transform Load) incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical taxonomy node defining the classification structure for the product catalog. Stores category name, category code, parent category reference (for tree traversal), depth level, full path string, display order, SEO slug, meta title, meta description, is_leaf flag, and active status. Supports multi-level category trees (e.g., Electronics > Smartphones > Android Phones) used for PLP navigation, search faceting, and catalog governance.'
}

Table product.attribute_definition {
  attribute_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the attribute definition within the Product Information Management (PIM) system. Primary key.']
  allowed_values_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or JSON list of allowed values for enum and multi-select attributes. Defines the controlled vocabulary for categorical attributes.']
  applicable_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product category codes or taxonomy nodes to which this attribute applies. Defines the scope of attribute applicability across the catalog.']
  attribute_code varchar [note: 'Machine-readable unique code for the attribute, used as the technical identifier in API calls and data integrations. Typically snake_case format.']
  attribute_group varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the attribute for PIM governance (e.g., Core, Marketing, Technical, Compliance, Digital Asset). Used for attribute set management.']
  attribute_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the attribute displayed in the PIM system and administrative interfaces.']
  attribute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the attribute definition. Active attributes are available for product enrichment; deprecated attributes are phased out; draft attributes are under review; archived attributes are retained for historical data.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is required for regulatory compliance (e.g., Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) metadata, General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) consent tracking).']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory standard or governing body that mandates this attribute (e.g., CPSC, GDPR, ISO 27001, PCI DSS). Null for non-compliance attributes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this attribute definition record was first created in the PIM system.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'The data type that defines the structure and validation rules for attribute values. Determines how the attribute is stored, validated, and rendered on Product Detail Page (PDP) and Product Listing Page (PLP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: string|numeric|boolean|enum|multi_select|date|rich_text — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  default_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Default value automatically populated for this attribute when a new product is created in the PIM system. Null if no default is defined.']
  display_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of attributes for organized presentation on PDP and PLP (e.g., Technical Specifications, Dimensions, Compliance). Used to structure attribute rendering.']
  display_label varchar [note: 'Customer-facing label for the attribute displayed on PDP and PLP. May differ from the internal attribute name for better user experience.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for attribute presentation within its display group. Lower numbers appear first.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this attribute definition is deprecated or retired. Null for currently active attributes with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this attribute definition becomes active and available for use in product catalog enrichment.']
  external_attribute_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this attribute in external systems or third-party integrations (e.g., vendor PIM, industry standard attribute codes). Used for cross-system mapping.']
  facet_display_type varchar [note: 'UI component type used to render this attribute as a filter facet on PLP (e.g., checkbox for multi-select, slider for numeric ranges, swatch for colors).']
  help_text varchar [note: 'Instructional text or tooltip displayed to catalog managers during product enrichment to guide accurate data entry.']
  is_comparable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is displayed in side-by-side product comparison views to help customers evaluate alternatives.']
  is_filterable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute can be used as a facet filter on Product Listing Page (PLP) to narrow search results.']
  is_localizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute supports multi-language or region-specific values for international catalog management.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute must be populated for a product to be considered complete and publishable. Drives catalog enrichment completeness scoring.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is indexed by the Search and Recommendation Engine for product discovery and relevance ranking.']
  is_system_attribute boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is a core system attribute managed by the PIM platform (e.g., SKU, UPC, ASIN) versus a custom business attribute.']
  is_unique boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attribute values must be unique across all products (e.g., UPC, EAN, ASIN identifiers). Enforces uniqueness constraints during catalog enrichment.']
  is_visible_on_pdp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is displayed on the Product Detail Page (PDP) for customer viewing.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier of the PIM administrator who last modified this attribute definition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this attribute definition record was last updated in the PIM system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this attribute definition for internal PIM governance and documentation purposes.']
  search_weight varchar [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this attribute for search relevance ranking. Higher weights increase the attributes influence on search result ordering.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system or data source from which this attribute definition originated (e.g., PIM, ERP, external vendor catalog). Used for data lineage tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for numeric attributes (e.g., kg, cm, liters, watts). Null for non-numeric attributes.']
  validation_max_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowed value for numeric attributes. Used to enforce data quality rules during catalog enrichment.']
  validation_min_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum allowed value for numeric attributes. Used to enforce data quality rules during catalog enrichment.']
  validation_regex_pattern varchar [note: 'Regular expression pattern used to validate string attribute values. Enforces format rules such as SKU patterns, UPC format, or custom identifiers.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the attribute definition, incremented with each modification. Supports change tracking and audit trail for PIM governance.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier of the PIM administrator who created this attribute definition.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a product attribute within the PIM system, forming the schema for dynamic product data. Captures attribute name, attribute code, data type (string, numeric, boolean, enum, multi-select, date, rich_text), unit of measure, validation rules (min/max values, regex pattern, allowed values list), is_required flag, is_searchable flag, is_filterable flag, is_comparable flag, display group, display order, display label, help text, and applicable product category scope. Drives dynamic attribute rendering on PDP and PLP pages, powers faceted search configuration, and governs catalog enrichment completeness scoring.'
}

Table product.attribute_value {
  attribute_value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product attribute value record. Primary key.']
  attribute_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attribute definition that describes the type and metadata of this attribute.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Attribute-level compliance tracking links hazardous attribute values to the governing compliance obligation for safety reporting.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU or catalog item to which this attribute value is assigned.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute value meets regulatory compliance requirements such as Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) standards, GDPR data accuracy, or industry-specific regulations.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or documentation related to compliance status, including references to certifications, test results, or regulatory submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this attribute value record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0-100) representing the completeness and accuracy of the attribute value. Used to prioritize enrichment efforts and ensure high-quality Product Detail Page (PDP) content.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'The sequence order in which this attribute value should be displayed on the Product Detail Page (PDP) relative to other attributes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this attribute value is no longer effective. Supports time-based attribute changes such as seasonal specifications or regulatory updates.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this attribute value becomes effective and should be displayed on Product Detail Page (PDP) and used in search and filtering.']
  enrichment_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the attribute value in the enrichment workflow. Tracks whether the value has been validated, enriched, or requires further action.']
  enrichment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the attribute value was last enriched or validated in the PIM system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute value is currently active and should be used in product display, search, and analytics.']
  is_comparable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute value should be displayed in product comparison tools to help customers evaluate similar products.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute value is indexed for search and faceted navigation on Product Listing Page (PLP) and search results.']
  is_variant_defining boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute value defines a product variant (e.g., size, color) that creates a distinct SKU within a product family.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this attribute value record was last updated or modified.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'The locale or language code for localized attribute values, following ISO 639-1 language and ISO 3166-1 country format (e.g., en_US, fr_FR, de_DE). Supports multi-language Product Detail Page (PDP) and Product Listing Page (PLP) content.']
  raw_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The raw string representation of the attribute value as captured from the source system or enrichment process.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this attribute value originated, such as PIM, ERP, or third-party data provider.']
  typed_value_boolean boolean [note: 'The attribute value when the attribute type is boolean. Used for yes/no or true/false product characteristics.']
  typed_value_date date [note: 'The attribute value when the attribute type is date. Used for time-based product attributes such as release date or expiration date.']
  typed_value_numeric decimal(18,4) [note: 'The attribute value when the attribute type is numeric. Enables range filtering and comparison operations.']
  typed_value_string varchar [note: 'The attribute value when the attribute type is string or text. Used for faceted search and filtering.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for numeric attribute values, such as kg, cm, liters, watts, etc. Enables consistent product comparison.']

  Note: 'Stores the actual attribute values assigned to a specific SKU or catalog item as managed in the PIM system. Captures the SKU or catalog item reference, attribute definition reference, raw value (string), typed value (numeric, boolean, date as applicable), unit of measure, locale/language code for localized values, source system, enrichment status, and last enrichment timestamp. Enables faceted search, product comparison, and PDP attribute display.'
}

Table product.product_digital_asset {
  product_digital_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product digital asset record. Primary key for this entity.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: REQUIRED: Central Digital Asset Management links SKU assets to DAM for consistent image/video reuse across channels.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU this digital asset belongs to. Links asset to product master data.']
  accessibility_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the asset meets WCAG accessibility standards (alt text, captions, transcripts). Required for regulatory compliance.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the digital asset media type. Determines rendering and display logic on PDP and PLP pages.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Specific content classification within the asset type. Distinguishes between hero images, lifestyle images, product titles, SEO metadata, and other content elements for PDP/PLP presentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hero_image|lifestyle_image|thumbnail|product_video|size_chart|instruction_manual|warranty_document|product_title|short_description|long_description|bullet_points|seo_meta_title|seo_meta_description|seo_canonical_url|seo_keywords|structured_data_markup — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the digital asset record was first created in the system. Audit trail for content lifecycle tracking.']
  enrichment_completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score (0-100) measuring content enrichment quality and completeness. Calculated based on presence of required attributes, SEO metadata, and multi-locale coverage.']
  external_asset_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the source system or external DAM platform. Enables cross-system reconciliation and integration.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the digital asset is currently active and available for use. Supports soft-delete and content lifecycle management.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Language and region code for multi-locale content support (e.g., en_US, fr_FR, de_DE). Enables international catalog syndication.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the digital asset record was last modified. Tracks content update activity for governance and audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that provided the digital asset (e.g., PIM, DAM, vendor portal). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  tags_metadata varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of descriptive tags for asset categorization and search. Supports content discovery and filtering in DAM systems.']
  usage_rights_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing usage restrictions, attribution requirements, or special licensing terms. Supports legal compliance and rights management.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the digital asset. Increments with each update to support version control and rollback capabilities.']

  Note: 'Unified content and media asset entity serving as the single source of truth for ALL product presentation content on PDP and PLP pages. Manages both digital media (hero images, lifestyle images, videos, 360-degree spins, AR/3D models, size charts, PDFs) and all textual marketing content (product titles, short descriptions, long descriptions with HTML/rich text, bullet points, SEO meta titles, SEO meta descriptions, SEO canonical URLs, SEO keywords, structured data markup/JSON-LD). Captures asset type, content type, file/CDN URL, resolution, alt text, locale/language code, content status (draft, approved, published, archived), sort order, is_primary flag, rights/license metadata, content author, publishing timestamps, and enrichment completeness score. Supports multi-locale content for international catalog syndication. Governed by PIM/DAM systems for enrichment quality and brand consistency. This is the SSOT for all product content — no other entity in this domain stores product titles, descriptions, or SEO metadata.'
}

Table product.bundle {
  bundle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product bundle. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand or manufacturer of the bundle. Used for brand-specific merchandising, reporting, and vendor management.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product taxonomy category this bundle belongs to. Used for navigation, search filtering, and merchandising.']
  revenue_general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Bundle sales are posted to a specific revenue GL account; linking ensures correct revenue recognition for bundled offerings.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketplace seller offering this bundle. Supports multi-seller marketplace models and Fulfillment By Amazon (FBA)-style operations.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bundle was approved for publication by merchandising or compliance teams. Supports governance workflows and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) audit requirements.']
  bundle_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the bundle, used in catalog systems, order management, and fulfillment. Typically alphanumeric SKU-style code.']
  bundle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing description of the bundle, including value proposition, contents summary, and usage scenarios. Displayed on PDP for customer decision-making.']
  bundle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the bundle displayed on Product Detail Pages (PDP) and Product Listing Pages (PLP).']
  bundle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bundle in the catalog. Controls visibility and sellability across channels.']
  bundle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bundle structure: fixed bundle (pre-defined items), configurable bundle (customer selects components), gift set (curated collection), kit (assembly required), promotional bundle (limited-time offer), subscription box (recurring delivery).']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory certifications and compliance standards the bundle meets (e.g., Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), CE, RoHS, FDA). Required for cross-border trade and marketplace listing approval.']
  component_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) included in the bundle. Used for fulfillment complexity scoring and customer messaging.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the bundle is manufactured or assembled. Required for customs, tariffs, and World Trade Organization (WTO) compliance.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system account that created the bundle record. Supports audit trails and General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) data lineage requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bundle record was first created in the Product Information Management (PIM) system. Used for catalog audit trails and data lineage.']
  cross_sell_bundle_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of bundle IDs recommended for cross-sell on PDP. Supports recommendation engine and Average Order Value (AOV) optimization.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bundle price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discontinued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bundle was marked as discontinued. Used for product lifecycle reporting and inventory liquidation planning.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the bundle compared to sum-of-parts or list price. Used for promotional messaging and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) calculations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the bundle is no longer available for sale. Nullable for evergreen bundles. Used for limited-time offers and seasonal discontinuation.']
