  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total promotional discounts applied to the order.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection engine.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes about the order, visible to staff only.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at order submission.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if any line items are on backorder at order creation.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order requires expedited handling.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is a gift purchase.']
  is_split_shipment boolean [note: 'True if the order will be fulfilled in multiple shipments.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  order_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the customer or support staff.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable order identifier used in customer communications and external systems.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the order was submitted by the customer.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used for fulfillment sequencing.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the order request.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Logical classification of the order (e.g., standard purchase, gift, subscription).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to settle the order.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier returned by the payment gateway.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payment.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Code of any promotion applied to the order.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual fulfillment time in minutes measured for the order.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed fulfillment time in minutes for the SLA tier.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service‑level agreement tier promised for the order.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the order.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is exempt from tax.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the order before taxes, discounts, or fees.']
  total_volume_cm3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items in the order, expressed in cubic centimeters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the order, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order header.']

  Note: 'The authoritative master record for a confirmed customer order within the OMS. Captures the full order header including order number, channel (B2C, B2B, marketplace, BOPIS, DTC), order placement timestamp, customer reference, billing address, total GMV, AOV contribution, tax amount, discount amount, currency, order status, SLA tier, and payment reference. This is the SSOT for order-level identity and lifecycle management across all channels.'
}

Table order.order_line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each order line item.']
  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order_order_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier assignment per order line is required for carrier performance reporting and SLA compliance; e‑commerce ops track carrier_id for each line.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Link enables compliance, merchandising and sales reporting by tying each order line to its master catalog item record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Track line‑level cost allocation to cost centers for detailed expense reporting.']
  dynamic_price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.dynamic_price_rule. Business justification: DYNAMIC PRICING LOG: When a dynamic pricing rule adjusts a line, storing the rule ID supports performance reporting and rule‑impact analysis.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: REQUIRED: Directly map each order line to a GL account for precise accounting postings.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent order header.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.indexed_document. Business justification: Enables linking purchased line items to the catalog document used in search, supporting search relevance and conversion attribution analysis.']
  line_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Product‑level regulations (hazardous, restricted items) must be validated per order line; linking ensures regulatory checks and reporting.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: LOT TRACEABILITY: Regulatory and quality processes need to link sold items to their originating lot for recall, expiry, and compliance reporting.']
  markdown_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown_schedule. Business justification: DISCOUNT AUDIT: A markdown schedule may be applied to a line; linking enables tracking of schedule source for discount compliance.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace (B2C, B2B, C2C) where the order originated.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: LINE PRICE SOURCE: Order line price is calculated from a concrete price‑list item; FK allows audit of which item supplied the unit price.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Attribute each order line to a profit center for granular margin analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Drop‑ship fulfillment creates a PO per marketplace order line; linking enables PO tracking, cost allocation, and delivery ETA reporting.']
  result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.result. Business justification: Tracks which search result led to the purchase, essential for click‑to‑purchase conversion metrics and A/B test evaluation.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller offering the product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Order line needs a stable reference to the SKU entity for shipping, tax calculation and returns; replaces the denormalized sku string.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: PICK PLAN: Mapping each order line to a specific stock position is required for the warehouse picking system to generate accurate pick lists and optimize routing.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or fulfillment node assigned to this line.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the line was actually delivered to the customer.']
  asin varchar [note: 'Marketplace‑specific product identifier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Package dimensions formatted as LxWxH in centimeters.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fulfillment process for this line.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the line is fulfilled.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product is classified as hazardous.']
  is_backordered boolean [note: 'True if the line is currently on backorder.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'True if the line is marked as a gift for the recipient.']
  is_returnable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product can be returned under policy.']
  line_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute discount applied to this line.']
  line_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to this line.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order line.']
  line_total_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount before tax and after discounts (quantity × unit_price – discount).']
  line_total_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax (gross total + tax_amount).']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the product.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date promised to the customer for delivery of this line.']
  quantity_fulfilled varchar [note: 'Number of units actually shipped to the customer.']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of units requested by the customer.']
  restricted_item_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product is subject to sales restrictions.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after delivery during which a return is allowed.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Selected shipping service for the line.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary tax charged for this line.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is exempt from tax.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the tax authority for this line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the shipment.']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per unit before discounts and taxes.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Global barcode identifier for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the line item in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level line items within a confirmed order. Captures SKU/ASIN/UPC, product name, quantity ordered/fulfilled, unit selling price, line-level discount, line total, seller ID for marketplace orders, fulfillment node assignment, line status, promised delivery date, and line-level tax details (jurisdiction, rate, amount, exemption status). Supports GMV decomposition, line-level fulfillment tracking, returns attribution, and tax compliance reporting.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each status transition record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the actor (user, system, seller, etc.) that performed the transition.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this status transition belongs.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that triggered the status change.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context for the status change.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transition was automatically generated by the system (true) or manually entered (false).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the status changed (e.g., payment_failed, inventory_allocated).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record (e.g., correction of comment).']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time between the previous status and this transition, in seconds.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the actual duration exceeded the SLA target, otherwise false.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Configured service‑level‑agreement target time for this status transition, expressed in seconds.']
  status_from varchar [note: 'The order status before the transition occurred.']
  status_to varchar [note: 'The order status after the transition occurred.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status change was recorded.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all order lifecycle state transitions for a sales order. Records each status change event (e.g., PLACED → CONFIRMED → ALLOCATED → SHIPPED → DELIVERED → CANCELLED), the timestamp of transition, the actor or system that triggered the change, and any associated reason codes. Enables SLA breach detection, order lifecycle analytics, and OMS orchestration tracing.'
}

Table order.order_address {
  order_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the order address record.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Provides audit link from the order shipping address to the customer\'s saved address record, required for address change compliance and delivery performance analysis.']
  privacy_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_notice. Business justification: Privacy notices govern personal address data collection; linking address records to the applicable notice supports GDPR/CCPA compliance reporting.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose for the order (shipping, billing, or pickup).']
  carrier_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred shipping speed or carrier service level.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the recipient for delivery.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the recipient.']
  external_address_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by an external address verification or logistics provider.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Indicates the confidence level of the geocoding result.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'True if the address is a residential location; false otherwise.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the address (e.g., Home, Office).']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  order_address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the recipient.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person receiving the shipment.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the address.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the address expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of address validation (valid, invalid, or partially valid).']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated.']

  Note: 'Shipping and billing address records associated with a sales order. Stores address type (shipping/billing/pickup), recipient name, address lines, city, state/province, postal code, country, geolocation coordinates, address validation status, and residential vs. commercial flag. Supports last-mile delivery routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and fraud screening.'
}

Table order.order_payment {
  order_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the order payment allocation record.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this payment allocation belongs.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconcile each payment to its accounting journal entry for audit and financial reporting.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (seller) receiving the payment.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Payment processing must adhere to PCI DSS obligations; linking each payment to its compliance obligation enables security audit and reporting.']
  primary_payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction from the payment gateway.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Required for marketplace split‑payment reconciliation; each payment line must be tied to the seller receiving funds, a standard financial report in e‑commerce.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount authorized for this payment allocation.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for this payment allocation.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was authorized.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was captured.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the payment.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if settlement currency differs from transaction currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fee charged by the payment processor.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of fraud detection checks for this payment.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the payment gateway.']
  is_gift_card boolean [note: 'True if part of the payment was made using a gift card.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'Indicates if this record represents a refund transaction.']
  is_split_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates if the order payment is part of a split payment across multiple methods.']
  is_store_credit boolean [note: 'True if part of the payment was made using store credit.']
  line_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of payment units allocated (typically 1).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of this payment allocation line within the payment transaction.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after taxes and fees.']
  note varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the payment.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Optional free-text description of the payment allocation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment allocation.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial reconciliation for this payment allocation.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference or token from the payment gateway for this payment transaction.']
  refunded_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount refunded for this payment allocation, if any.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code used for settlement, may differ from transaction currency.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment amount, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']

  Note: 'Order-level payment allocation records linking a sales order to one or more payment transactions. Captures payment method type, payment reference from the payment gateway, amount allocated to this order, currency, payment status (authorized, captured, failed, refunded), split-payment flags, and gift card or store credit redemption amounts. This is the order domains local reference to payment events — it does NOT own gateway integration, PCI-scoped card data, or settlement processing (those belong to the payment domain). Enables order-level payment reconciliation, refund eligibility checks, and revenue recognition without crossing domain boundaries.'
}

Table order.order_promotion {
  order_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order promotion record.']
  applied_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific order line to which the promotion was applied, when applicable.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated the promotion, if applicable.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this promotion is applied.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program associated with the promotion, if applicable.']
  merchandising_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.merchandising_rule. Business justification: Allows analysis of promotion effectiveness tied to specific merchandising rules, required for promotion performance reporting.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller that funded or owns the promotion.']
  applied_by varchar [note: 'Mechanism that applied the promotion: system, customer service agent, or automated rule.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the promotion was applied to the order.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events related to the promotion record.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied (currency defined by discount_currency).']
  discount_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which discount_amount is expressed.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value of the discount when promotion_type is percentage.']
  eligibility_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation when eligibility_status is ineligible or pending.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Result of promotion eligibility validation for this order.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires and can no longer be applied.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is currently active in the system.']
  is_combination_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if this promotion may be combined with other promotions beyond stacking_rule logic.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the promotion excludes all other promotions when applied.']
  is_refunded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion discount was later reversed or refunded.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the promotion within the order\'s promotion list.']
  max_uses_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of times a single customer may use this promotion.']
  max_uses_per_order varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this promotion can be applied within a single order.']
  net_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount amount after tax adjustments, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or internal comments.']
  order_promotion_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the promotion\'s purpose and mechanics.']
  promotion_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the promotion for reporting.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the promotion rule.']
  promotion_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the promotion – marketing campaign, loyalty program, seller‑funded, or system generated.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotion: percentage discount, fixed amount, buy‑one‑get‑one, or free shipping.']
  quantity_applied varchar [note: 'Number of discount units applied (e.g., number of items covered by a BOGO).']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded for the promotion, if any.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the promotion refund was processed.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or policy governing the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI DSS).']
  stacking_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that defines whether this promotion can be combined with other promotions.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes valid for use.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount amount is exempt from sales tax.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the promotion record.']

  Note: 'Records of promotions, discount codes, and coupons applied at the order or line level. Captures promotion code, promotion type (percentage discount, fixed amount, BOGO, free shipping), discount amount applied, promotion source (marketing campaign, loyalty program, seller-funded), eligibility validation status, and stacking rules applied. Supports GMV-net-of-discount reporting and ROAS attribution.'
}

Table order.tax {
  tax_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each tax line applied to an order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this tax line belongs.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Tax can be applied at the line level; adding order_line_id FK enables precise tax calculations per line and removes ambiguity of using only header_id.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Tax compliance requires attributing tax amounts to the responsible seller for jurisdictional reporting and settlement.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated monetary tax amount (taxable_amount * tax_rate).']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the tax engine performed the calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tax record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  engine_reference varchar [note: 'Opaque identifier returned by the tax calculation engine for audit and traceability.']
  exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tax was exempted for this order line (true = exempt, false = taxable).']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction (state, province, country) that governs the tax calculation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the tax line within the order for ordering purposes.']
  nexus_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where tax nexus exists.']
  nexus_state varchar [note: 'State or province code within the nexus country that creates tax liability.']
  rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 for 7.5%).']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the tax line.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax applied: sales tax, VAT, GST, customs duty, or other.']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the order line(s) that is subject to this tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax record.']

  Note: 'Tax calculation and jurisdiction records applied to a sales order. Captures tax jurisdiction code, tax type (sales tax, VAT, GST, customs duty), taxable amount, tax rate applied, tax amount, tax exemption status, nexus state/country, and the tax engine calculation reference. Supports SOX-compliant tax reporting, GDPR-aligned data residency, and cross-border e-commerce compliance.'
}

Table order.order_sla {
  order_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each SLA commitment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier responsible for shipment.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this SLA applies.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the order was actually delivered to the customer.']
  actual_handling_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed time taken to handle the order before shipping, in hours.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date the order was actually shipped.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if any SLA metric was missed, otherwise False.']
  breach_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized cause for SLA breach when breach_flag is true.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Scope of the SLA (e.g., handling only, shipping only, delivery only, or full end‑to‑end).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA commitment was created in the OMS.']
  delivery_date_delta_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days the actual delivery date deviates from the promised delivery date.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA commitment becomes effective (typically order date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA commitment expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  handling_time_delta_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between actual and promised handling time (positive = overrun).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit used for time‑based SLA metrics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the SLA.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the order is expected to be delivered to the customer.']
  promised_handling_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum time allotted for order handling before shipping, expressed in hours.']
  promised_ship_date date [note: 'Date by which the order is expected to be shipped according to the SLA.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA record was first persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SLA record.']
  ship_date_delta_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of days the actual ship date deviates from the promised ship date.']
  sla_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable SLA code assigned by the Order Management System.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA record.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of service level commitment (e.g., standard, expedited).']

  Note: 'SLA commitment records governing order fulfillment and delivery timelines. Defines the promised handling time, promised ship date, promised delivery date, SLA tier (standard, expedited, same-day, next-day), SLA breach flag, breach reason, and actual vs. promised delta in hours. Supports OMS SLA orchestration, carrier SLA enforcement, and customer NPS correlation with delivery performance.'
}

Table order.cancellation {
  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cancellation event.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated or is affected by the cancellation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this cancellation applies.']
  actor varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the cancellation request.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation request was approved.']
  cancellation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable cancellation reference number used in communications and reporting.']
  cancellation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cancellation request.']
  cancellation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the domain of the cancellation (e.g., order‑level, line‑item, shipment, payment).']
  cancelled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the cancelled items before any refunds.']
  cancelled_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of items cancelled (relevant for partial cancellations).']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation process reached its final state (refunded or closed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation record was first created in the system.']
  is_partial boolean [note: 'True if the cancellation is partial; false if it is a full order cancellation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or internal notes about the cancellation.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the cancellation occurred.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the cancellation reason.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer as a result of the cancellation.']
  refund_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the refund amount.']
  refund_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation qualifies for a refund according to policy.']
  refund_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund was actually processed and posted.']
  rejected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation request was rejected.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why a cancellation request was denied.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation was initially requested.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation applies to the entire order or only specific line items.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the cancellation request was received.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cancellation record.']

  Note: 'Records of order or order-line cancellation events initiated by customers, sellers, or the OMS. Captures cancellation request timestamp, reason code (customer-initiated, out-of-stock, fraud hold, seller cancellation, payment failure), status workflow (requested → approved/rejected → completed), refund eligibility, cancellation actor, and partial vs. full cancellation scope. Supports cancellation rate KPI tracking, GMV leakage analysis, and refund workflow triggering.'
}

Table order.delivery_confirmation {
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery_confirmation']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Dispute resolution and compliance need the delivery proof image stored as a digital asset linked to the delivery confirmation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this delivery confirmation belongs.']
  actual_delivery_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Measured time from order dispatch to successful delivery, in seconds.']
  carrier_scan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code from the carrier\'s scan event confirming hand‑off.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery confirmation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  delivery_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of delivery attempts made for this order.']
  delivery_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a delivery attempt failed or was exceptional (e.g., address_not_found, recipient_unavailable).']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'Physical type of the delivery location where the package was left.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the delivery after this confirmation event.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the delivery was completed and confirmed.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handheld device or scanner used to record the confirmation.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery point.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery point.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by the driver or carrier about the delivery.']
  otp_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTP provided by the recipient was successfully verified.']
  proof_of_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture proof of delivery (e.g., signature, photo, one‑time‑password).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the package, captured for signature or verification.']
  recipient_signature_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored image of the recipient\'s signature, if captured.']
  scheduled_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for delivery as per the order SLA.']
  sla_delivery_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target delivery time expressed in seconds.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the delivery confirmation (e.g., WMS, TMS, OMS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this delivery confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Final delivery confirmation records for fulfilled orders. Captures actual delivery timestamp, delivery location type (door, locker, neighbor, pickup point), proof-of-delivery reference (signature, photo, OTP), carrier scan event reference, delivery attempt number, and delivery exception codes (failed attempt, returned to sender). This is the SSOT for confirmed delivery events within the order domain, distinct from logistics shipment tracking.'
}

Table order.fulfillment_allocation {
  fulfillment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fulfillment_allocation']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who manually overrode or confirmed the allocation.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'FK to fulfillment.center.center_id — Order allocation to warehouse tracing. Without this FK, the OMS cannot determine which fulfillment center was assigned to process an order line — breaking allocation-to-dispatch traceability.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Carrier hand‑off and regulatory compliance require associating the generated shipping label PDF asset with each allocation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this allocation belongs.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific order line being allocated.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Allocation of inventory to an order line must reference the goods receipt that supplied the stock for accurate inventory and financial reconciliation.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, 3PL center, or seller location assigned to fulfill the line.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units allocated to the fulfillment node.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to select the fulfillment node.']
  allocation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the allocation algorithm or rule set applied.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the allocation decision (automated system, manual entry, API call, batch process).']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the allocation decision.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation decision was made.']
  carrier_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred carrier or service level for the shipment.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of fulfilling the allocated quantity before taxes and shipping.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Length x Width x Height expressed in centimeters.']
  estimated_ship_date date [note: 'Projected date the allocated items will be shipped.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Category of fulfillment node handling the allocation.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Specific instructions for packing or handling the item.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if the allocated quantity is on backorder.']
  is_drop_ship boolean [note: 'True if the fulfillment is performed directly by the supplier.']
  is_eligible_for_fast_ship boolean [note: 'True if the line qualifies for accelerated shipping options.']
  is_eligible_for_free_shipping boolean [note: 'True if the line meets criteria for free shipping.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the allocation is marked for expedited processing.']
  is_fragile boolean [note: 'True if the item requires special handling due to fragility.']
  is_gift_wrap boolean [note: 'True if the allocation includes gift‑wrap service.']
  is_oversized boolean [note: 'True if the item dimensions exceed standard handling limits.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status change for the allocation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the line within the order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments from the allocation engine or operator.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Marks the allocation as high priority for expedited handling.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  shipping_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated shipping charge for the allocated quantity.']
  shipping_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic shipping zone used for rate calculation.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time from allocation to shipment.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if actual SLA exceeds the target.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target time for allocation fulfillment.']
  split_shipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order line is split across multiple nodes.']
  tax_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated tax amount applicable to the allocation.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of cost, shipping, and tax estimates for the allocation.']
  volume_cubic_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the allocated quantity.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the allocated quantity.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of order lines to specific fulfillment nodes (warehouses, 3PL centers, FBA-style nodes, seller locations, dark stores). Captures the fulfillment node ID, allocation timestamp, allocation method (nearest node, lowest cost, seller-fulfilled, zone-skipping), allocated quantity, allocation status (pending, confirmed, picked, cancelled), split-shipment flag, and estimated ship date. This is the order domains handoff point to the fulfillment domain — it owns the allocation decision, not the pick-pack-ship execution.'
}

Table order.b2b_order {
  b2b_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the B2B order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the order.']
  buyer_org_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate buyer organization placing the order.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract governing pricing and terms for this order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: b2b_order is an extended order record for B2B purchases; linking to the core order_header provides a single source of truth for order-level attributes and eliminates a silo.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the order was actually delivered to the buyer.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for the order.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order received final approval.']
  billing_address varchar [note: 'Full billing address associated with the order.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code for the billing address.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Logistics carrier responsible for shipping.']
  credit_check_passed boolean [note: 'True if the buyer passed the creditworthiness assessment.']
  credit_limit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the credit limit check for the buyer organization.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Chosen delivery service level for the order.']
  edi_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the EDI transaction.']
  edi_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated EDI transaction, if applicable.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date based on carrier SLA.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the automated fraud screening.']
  order_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order was submitted.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the order is a new purchase, contract renewal, or return.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the buyer (e.g., NET30, NET60).']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Buyer‑provided purchase order reference number.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier or discount level applicable under the contract.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the order record.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  shipping_address varchar [note: 'Full shipping address for order delivery.']
  shipping_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code for the shipping destination.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order failed to meet its SLA.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service‑level agreement tier promised to the buyer.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the order is tax‑exempt under buyer’s tax status.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount value applied to the order.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after discounts and taxes.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']

  Note: 'Extended order record for B2B (business-to-business) purchase orders placed by corporate buyers. Captures PO number, buyer organization reference, contract pricing tier, payment terms (NET30, NET60), credit limit check status, bulk quantity, EDI transaction reference, approved buyer contact, and B2B-specific SLA tier. Extends the core order_header for enterprise procurement workflows distinct from B2C consumer orders.'
}

Table order.subscription_order {
  subscription_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription order record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: subscription_order represents recurring orders; linking to order_header centralizes order-level data and removes duplication, connecting the previously siloed table.']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subscription plan associated with this order.']
  arr_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Revenue recognized on an annual basis from this subscription.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews after each cycle.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence interval for billing the subscription.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the customer or system for cancelling the subscription.']
  delivery_preference varchar [note: 'Customer‑selected delivery option for recurring shipments.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the subscription.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription terminates or is scheduled to end (null for open‑ended).']
  is_trial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is currently in a trial period.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful payment for this subscription.']
  mrr_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Revenue recognized on a monthly basis from this subscription.']
  next_order_date date [note: 'Date on which the next recurring order will be generated.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments or special instructions.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Code of any promotion applied to the subscription.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription order record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription order record.']
  recurring_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard recurring charge for each billing cycle.']
  renewal_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of completed renewal cycles for the subscription.']
  skip_count varchar [note: 'Number of billing cycles the customer has chosen to skip.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription becomes effective.']
  subscription_term_months varchar [note: 'Total length of the subscription contract in months.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period expires and regular billing begins.']

  Note: 'Records for recurring subscription-based orders (subscribe-and-save, auto-replenishment, membership-based orders). Captures subscription plan reference, billing frequency (weekly, monthly, quarterly), next order generation date, subscription status (active, paused, cancelled), discount rate applied, skip count, and customer-configured delivery preferences. Distinct from one-time order_header due to its recurring lifecycle and MRR/ARR contribution.'
}

Table order.allocation {
  allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the Allocation association']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order line being allocated']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the purchase order line providing inventory']
  allocated_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of inventory allocated from the PO line to the order line']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for allocation (e.g., automatic, manual)']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the allocation (e.g., pending, completed, cancelled)']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation was recorded']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of inventory from purchase order line items to order line items. Each record links one order_order_line to one po_line_item and captures the quantity allocated, the time of allocation, the status of the allocation, and the method used.. Existence Justification: In the e‑commerce fulfillment process, a single order line can be satisfied by multiple purchase order line items (split shipments), and a single PO line can supply multiple order lines (multiple customers or orders). The allocation of quantities, timestamps, and status is recorded in an allocation table that is actively created, updated, and deleted by operations staff.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the order_line association']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order header']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketplace listing']
  discount_amount decimal [note: 'Discount applied to this line item']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of units of the listing ordered in this line']
  tax_amount decimal [note: 'Tax applied to this line item']
  total_gross decimal [note: 'Gross total (quantity * unit price) before discounts and taxes']
  total_net decimal [note: 'Net total after applying discounts and taxes']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Price per unit of the listing at the time of the order']

  Note: 'This association product represents the transactional line items linking an order_header to a marketplace_listing. It captures the quantity, unit price, discounts, taxes, and total amounts for each listing within an order.. Existence Justification: An order can contain multiple marketplace listings, and a single marketplace listing can be sold in many different orders over time. The line item captures the quantity, price, discounts, and taxes for each listing within an order, which are attributes that belong to the relationship itself, not to either the order or the listing alone.'
}

Table order.session {
  session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for session']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer associated with the session.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the session, if any.']
  browser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser (e.g., Chrome, Safari).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the session resulted in a conversion event (e.g., purchase).']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Category of conversion that occurred during the session.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from IP geolocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for the session (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Class of device used for the session.']
  exit_page_url varchar [note: 'Last page URL visited before the session ended.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the session originated.']
  is_bounce boolean [note: 'True if the session consisted of a single page view.']
  is_new_visitor boolean [note: 'True if this is the first recorded session for the user.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'First page URL visited in the session.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language selected or inferred for the session.']
  os_name varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., Windows, iOS).']
  page_view_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct pages viewed during the session.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the user to the site.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sub‑national region or state code derived from IP geolocation.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total length of the session in seconds, calculated as end minus start.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session ended or was terminated.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session began.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the session.']
  traffic_source varchar [note: 'Marketing channel that brought the user to the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the session record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser and operating system details reported by the client.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table order.subscription_plan {
  subscription_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for subscription_plan']
  upgraded_from_subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on subscription_plan (upgraded_from_subscription_plan_id)']
  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan automatically renews at the end of each billing cycle.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which customers are billed for the plan.']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how and when a subscriber may cancel the plan.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing description of the plan, including benefits and features.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes legally binding and available for subscription.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the plan is retired or expires; null for open‑ended plans.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which customers may subscribe to the plan.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plan in an external billing or CRM system.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan is the default offering for new customers.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Flag to highlight the plan in marketing channels.']
  max_items varchar [note: 'Maximum number of items (e.g., orders, downloads) a subscriber may consume per billing cycle.']
  max_users varchar [note: 'Maximum number of distinct user accounts allowed under the plan.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the subscription plan displayed to customers.']
  overage_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary charge applied per unit when usage exceeds the defined limit.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the plan for segmentation and pricing strategy.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier code for the subscription plan, used in marketing and billing systems.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan based on billing cadence or usage model.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the plan definition, incremented on each change.']
  price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount charged per billing cycle.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the plan price.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Optional code that provides a discount or special terms for the plan.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription plan record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription plan record.']
  renewal_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which the subscription is renewed when auto‑renew is enabled.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for customers to contact support regarding this plan.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the plan when not tax‑exempt.']
  trial_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a customer can use the plan for free before regular billing starts.']
  trial_price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged during the trial period, if any.']
  usage_limit varchar [note: 'Overall usage limit (e.g., API calls, storage) associated with the plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for subscription_plan. Referenced by subscription_plan_id.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the stock position record representing a SKU at a specific warehouse node or virtual location. Primary key of this entity.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Needed to associate a primary product image with each stock position for warehouse UI and pick‑face displays, a standard e‑commerce operational requirement.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Required for inventory dashboards to link each stock position to its product detail page, enabling real‑time UI and reporting of SKU information.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables PO‑level stock traceability for recall, cost allocation and supplier performance dashboards.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketplace seller who owns this inventory. Null for first-party (1P) inventory owned by the platform operator.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU whose inventory position is being tracked. Links to the product catalog master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Provides supplier‑wise inventory health reporting and compliance checks, a core e‑commerce operational need.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or virtual warehouse node (fulfillment center, dark store, 3PL facility, virtual location) where this stock position is held.']
  atp_qty varchar [note: 'Net units available for new order commitments, calculated as on_hand_qty minus reserved_qty minus quarantined_qty. The ATP quantity is the real-time sellable inventory figure exposed to the storefront and OMS for order promising.']
  bin_location_code varchar [note: 'WMS bin, slot, or storage location code within the warehouse node where this SKU is primarily slotted (e.g., A-01-03-B). Used for pick path optimization, slotting analysis, and physical inventory audits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the system, typically when the SKU was first received at this warehouse node or when the node was onboarded. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  cycle_count_variance_qty varchar [note: 'Difference between the system-recorded on-hand quantity and the physically counted quantity at the last cycle count (counted minus system). Positive = overage, negative = shrinkage. Used for inventory accuracy KPI reporting and shrinkage investigation.']
  damaged_qty varchar [note: 'Units at the node that have been identified as damaged and are unsellable in their current condition. May be eligible for liquidation, refurbishment, or disposal. Tracked for shrinkage reporting and insurance claims.']
  days_of_supply decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of days the current ATP quantity will last at the current 30-day DSR before reaching zero stock. Calculated as atp_qty divided by dsr_30d. Key operational metric for replenishment urgency triage and OOS risk scoring.']
  dsr_30d decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average units sold per day for this SKU at this node over the trailing 30-day window. Medium-term velocity metric used for safety stock calculation, reorder point setting, and monthly replenishment planning.']
  dsr_7d decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average units sold per day for this SKU at this node over the trailing 7-day window. Short-term velocity metric used for immediate replenishment decisions and flash sale impact assessment. Recalculated nightly from order fulfillment data.']
  dsr_90d decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average units sold per day for this SKU at this node over the trailing 90-day window. Long-term velocity baseline used for seasonal trend analysis, annual replenishment planning, and velocity tier classification.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the current lot/batch at this node. Applicable for perishable, pharmaceutical, or regulated goods. Drives FEFO pick sequencing and OOS-before-expiry alerts. Null for non-perishable SKUs.']
  in_transit_qty varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU currently in transit to this warehouse node from a supplier, another node, or a returns processing center. Included in future ATP projections but not in current on-hand. Tracked via Transportation Management System (TMS) shipment records.']
  inventory_ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classifies who owns the inventory: first_party (platform-owned), third_party_fba (Fulfillment By Amazon-style seller inventory held at platform warehouse), consignment, dropship (never physically held), or vendor_managed inventory (VMI). Drives cost accounting and liability treatment.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this stock position record. Active = normal operations; quarantined = held pending quality inspection; suspended = temporarily blocked from sale; archived = historical record no longer in active use.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is classified as a hazardous material requiring special storage, handling, or shipping restrictions at this node. Drives WMS storage zone assignment, carrier eligibility filtering in TMS, and regulatory compliance checks.']
  is_oos boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is currently out of stock (OOS) at this node, meaning ATP quantity is zero or below safety stock threshold. Drives storefront availability suppression, OOS alerts, and replenishment escalation. Key KPI for inventory health reporting.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual units of this SKU are tracked by unique serial number at this node. When true, each unit has a distinct serial number record in the WMS. Relevant for high-value electronics, regulated goods, and warranty management.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count performed for this SKU at this warehouse node. Cycle counting is the primary mechanism for validating perpetual inventory accuracy. Frequency is driven by velocity tier (A-items counted more frequently).']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent inventory movement transaction (receipt, pick, transfer, adjustment) for this SKU at this node. Used to identify dormant stock, trigger dead-stock reviews, and validate real-time inventory visibility.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inbound goods receipt (GR) posted for this SKU at this warehouse node. Used to assess inventory freshness, identify slow-moving stock, and validate replenishment cycle adherence.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the storage location: physical_warehouse (owned/leased FC), virtual_location (logical inventory bucket), dropship_node (supplier ships direct), store_backroom (BOPIS-enabled store), or 3pl_facility (Third-Party Logistics partner site).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or internal lot/batch identifier for the current stock at this node. Enables traceability for product recalls, quality investigations, and FEFO rotation. Null when lot_tracking_method is \'none\'.']
  lot_tracking_method varchar [note: 'Inventory costing and lot rotation method applied to this SKU at this node: FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out for perishables), or none (no lot tracking). Governs pick sequencing in WMS and cost-of-goods-sold (COGS) calculation in ERP.']
  max_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Upper bound on inventory units to hold at this node for this SKU, constrained by bin capacity, working capital policy, or perishability. Replenishment orders are sized to not exceed this level. Used in min-max replenishment planning.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered per replenishment purchase order for this SKU at this node, as stipulated by the supplier contract. Constrains replenishment order sizing in the ERP procurement module.']
  on_hand_qty varchar [note: 'Total physical units of the SKU currently present at the warehouse node, regardless of reservation or allocation status. Includes allocated, quarantined, and damaged units. Source of truth from WMS cycle count or perpetual inventory.']
  oos_since_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SKU first became out of stock at this node in the current OOS episode. Used to calculate OOS duration for SLA compliance, lost sales estimation, and seller performance monitoring. Null when is_oos is false.']
  peak_dsr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Highest single-day sales rate observed for this SKU at this node within the trailing 90-day window. Used to size safety stock for peak demand scenarios (e.g., flash sales, holiday events) and to stress-test replenishment plans.']
  quarantined_qty varchar [note: 'Units physically present at the node but held in quarantine pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, or recall investigation. Not available for sale or fulfillment. Tracked separately for compliance and quality management reporting.']
  reorder_point_qty varchar [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment purchase order (PO) should be triggered for this SKU at this node. Calculated as safety_stock_qty plus demand during replenishment lead time. Drives automated replenishment in the ERP/WMS.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from purchase order (PO) placement to receipt of goods at this warehouse node for this SKU. Used in reorder point and safety stock calculations. Sourced from supplier SLA agreements.']
  reserved_qty varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU that have been soft-allocated to open orders or reservations but not yet physically picked. Reduces available-to-promise (ATP) quantity. Managed by the Order Management System (OMS) allocation engine.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum buffer stock level maintained at the node to protect against demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. When ATP quantity falls below safety stock, a replenishment alert is triggered. Calculated from DSR metrics and lead time data.']
  seasonal_velocity_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Multiplicative seasonal adjustment factor applied to the baseline DSR to account for predictable seasonal demand patterns (e.g., 1.5 = 50% above baseline in peak season, 0.7 = 30% below in off-season). Used in demand forecasting and safety stock uplift calculations.']
  stock_condition varchar [note: 'Physical or logical state of the stock at this node: on_hand (available in bin), allocated (reserved for an order), quarantined (held for QC), damaged (unsellable), in_transit (moving between nodes). Drives WMS pick eligibility and ATP calculations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure in which inventory quantities for this SKU are tracked at this node (e.g., EA = each, CASE, PALLET, KG, LB, L, M). Must align with the UOM defined in the product catalog and supplier purchase orders. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CASE|PALLET|KG|LB|L|M — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this stock position record, including quantity adjustments, status changes, velocity recalculations, or cycle count postings. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  velocity_recalculated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent recalculation of DSR metrics and velocity tier for this stock position record. Indicates data freshness for downstream consumers (replenishment engine, slotting optimizer, search ranking). Recalculated nightly or on-demand.']
  velocity_tier varchar [note: 'ABC/D velocity tier assigned to this SKU at this node based on Best Seller Rank (BSR) and sales volume: A = fast-moving (top ~20% by volume), B = medium-moving, C = slow-moving, D = dead stock / near-zero velocity. Drives slotting decisions, replenishment frequency, and pick path optimization in WMS.']

  Note: 'Core master record and SSOT representing the current stock position and velocity profile for a SKU at a specific warehouse node or virtual location. Tracks on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, available-to-promise (ATP) quantity, in-transit quantity, and physical/logical inventory states (on-hand, allocated, quarantined, damaged). Includes comprehensive velocity metrics: rolling daily sales rate (DSR) at 7/30/90-day windows, velocity tier classification (A/B/C/D based on BSR and sales volume), average units sold per day, peak DSR, seasonal velocity index, and last velocity recalculation timestamp. Velocity data drives slotting decisions, replenishment frequency, and safety stock calculations. No separate velocity entity exists — stock_position is the SSOT for both inventory quantities and SKU velocity at the node level. Integrates with WMS for real-time inventory visibility across all fulfillment nodes.'
}

Table inventory.warehouse_location {
  warehouse_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a physical or virtual storage location within a warehouse node. Primary key for this master record.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent warehouse node that contains this storage location. Used to scope location records to a specific fulfillment center or distribution node.']
  accessibility_type varchar [note: 'Equipment or method required to access this storage location. Determines which picking and putaway resources the WMS can assign to tasks involving this location. Automated locations are served by Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems (AS/RS).']
  aisle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the aisle within the warehouse zone where this location is physically situated. Used by WMS for pick-path optimization and travel-time minimization in fulfillment operations.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode value printed on the physical label affixed to this storage location. Scanned by warehouse operatives using handheld devices or fixed scanners during pick, putaway, and cycle count operations to confirm location identity.']
  bin_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the individual bin or slot within the shelf. The most granular physical unit of the warehouse location hierarchy. Directly maps to a scannable barcode label affixed to the physical bin in the warehouse.']
  capacity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which the capacity of this storage location is expressed. Aligns with the WMS capacity management configuration for the warehouse node (e.g., \'pallets\' for bulk storage, \'units\' for pick-face bins, \'kg\' for weight-constrained locations).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Target number of days between cycle counts for this location. Drives the WMS cycle count scheduling engine. High-velocity or high-value locations typically have shorter intervals (e.g., 7 days) while low-velocity locations may be counted quarterly (e.g., 90 days).']
  dedicated_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code of the product permanently assigned to this location under a fixed slotting strategy. Null for floating or multi-SKU locations. Used by WMS to enforce single-SKU integrity and prevent mixed-SKU putaway errors.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this storage location record became operationally active and available for inventory allocation in the WMS. Supports bi-temporal data management and warehouse expansion tracking.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this storage location record ceases to be operationally active. Null for currently active locations. Used to manage decommissioned or seasonally inactive locations without hard-deleting master records.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'Hazardous materials classification code assigned to this location, indicating the category of dangerous goods that may be stored here (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Null for non-hazmat locations. Governs WMS slotting restrictions and regulatory compliance for storage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1_explosives|class_2_gases|class_3_flammable|class_4_flammable_solids|class_5_oxidizers|class_6_toxic|class_7_radioactive|class_8_corrosives|class_9_misc|none — promote to reference product]']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimetres. Used for vertical clearance validation during slotting and for determining forklift or reach-truck accessibility requirements.']
  is_bulk_storage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as a bulk or reserve storage position. Bulk locations hold overstock inventory that replenishes pick face locations. Used in WMS replenishment trigger logic.']
  is_hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location has been certified and approved for storing hazardous materials. Drives WMS slotting rules to prevent non-approved locations from receiving hazmat inventory.']
  is_pick_face boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face — a forward-pick position from which pickers directly fulfil customer orders. Pick face locations are replenished from bulk storage and are optimized for pick speed and ergonomics.']
  is_quarantine boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as a quarantine hold area. Inventory placed in quarantine locations is blocked from pick and shipment pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, or Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a virtual (system-only) storage location with no physical counterpart in the warehouse. Virtual locations are used for in-transit inventory, damaged goods holding, or system adjustment buckets in the WMS.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location. Used to schedule future cycle counts, assess inventory accuracy compliance, and prioritize locations overdue for counting per the warehouse cycle count program.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimetres. Used in WMS slotting optimization to match product dimensions to available location space and to calculate volumetric utilization.']
  level_number varchar [note: 'Numeric vertical level of the storage location within the rack structure (e.g., 1 = ground level, 2 = second level). Used for ergonomic slotting rules, forklift accessibility constraints, and pick-path optimization in the WMS.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this storage location within the warehouse. Used by WMS for pick-pack-ship operations, barcode scanning, and slotting optimization. Typically formatted as ZONE-AISLE-RACK-SHELF-BIN (e.g., A-01-03-02-05).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the storage location, used in WMS user interfaces, slotting reports, and operational dashboards (e.g., \'Aisle 3 Rack B Shelf 2 Bin 5\').']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the storage location. Controls whether the WMS can allocate inventory to or pick from this location. \'blocked\' indicates a temporary hold (e.g., quality inspection); \'under_maintenance\' indicates physical inaccessibility.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the storage location within the warehouse. Determines how the WMS uses this location in fulfillment workflows: pick_face (active picking slot), bulk_storage (reserve/overflow stock), receiving_dock (inbound goods receipt), returns_area (Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing), virtual (system-only allocation node), staging (pre-shipment consolidation area).']
  lot_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory stored at this location must be tracked at the lot or batch level. Required for locations holding perishables, pharmaceuticals, or regulated goods where First In First Out (FIFO) / First Expired First Out (FEFO) lot traceability is mandated.']
  max_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of this location expressed in the unit defined by capacity_uom. Used by WMS to enforce inventory allocation limits and prevent overstocking. Critical for slotting optimization and replenishment planning.']
  max_fill_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum inventory quantity (in capacity_uom) that should be stocked at this location. Used by the WMS to cap replenishment quantities and prevent overfilling. Typically set equal to or less than max_capacity to allow for operational buffer.']
