  packing_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the packing process, such as damaged item, missing item, substitution made, or oversized package. Null if no exceptions occurred.']
  packing_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any packing exceptions or issues, providing additional context beyond the exception code. Null if no exceptions occurred.']
  packing_materials_used varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of packing materials used in the shipment, such as bubble wrap, air pillows, packing peanuts, paper fill, or dunnage. Used for cost tracking and sustainability reporting.']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the packing slip document. Printed on the physical packing slip and used for customer service reference.']
  packing_slip_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packing slip record. Tracks the progression from draft through completion and any subsequent actions like reprinting or voiding.']
  qc_scan_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality control scan or verification performed during or after packing. Indicates whether the packed contents match the order requirements.']
  qc_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality control scan or verification was completed. Null if QC was not performed.']
  return_label_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a prepaid return shipping label was included in the shipment for customer convenience.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Shipping service level or speed selected for this shipment. Examples include standard ground, two-day, next-day, same-day, or economy.']
  shipping_label_printed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipping label has been printed for this packing slip. True once the label is generated and printed at the pack station.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a delivery signature is required for this shipment. True for high-value or restricted items.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing any special handling instructions for the packing operation, such as fragile item handling, temperature control, or hazmat requirements.']
  total_item_quantity varchar [note: 'Total count of individual items (units) packed in this carton across all SKUs. Sum of quantities for all line items in the packing slip.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct SKUs packed in this carton. Represents the variety of products in the shipment.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the shipment. Used by customers and customer service to track package delivery status.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the packed carton in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']

  Note: 'Consolidated operational record and SSOT for the full packing lifecycle of a fulfillment order — from pack station assignment and carton selection through QC verification and packing slip document generation. Stores pack station ID, packer associate, carton/box type, dimensional and actual weight, packing materials, QC scan results, packed SKUs with quantities, special handling instructions, gift message and branded insert flags, pack start/end timestamps, and generation timestamp. Serves as both the operational pack execution record (replacing standalone pack task) and the customer-facing documentation reference for shipment contents and returns processing.'
}

Table fulfillment.shipping_label {
  shipping_label_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the shipping label record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Shipping label generation needs carrier reference for tracking, customs, and cost allocation; FK ensures consistency with carrier master.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Required for label generation: each shipping label references the digital asset template stored in content for version control and compliance.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outbound shipment associated with this label.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with this shipping label.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Label generation uses pricing rules from a price list; needed for the Shipping Label Pricing audit.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Any surcharge, discount, or fee applied to the base carrier rate.']
  carrier_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base rate quoted by the carrier before adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipping label record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_flag boolean [note: 'True if a customs declaration is required for the shipment.']
  customs_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value for customs purposes.']
  destination_address_snapshot varchar [note: 'Serialized snapshot of the destination address at label generation time.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Package dimensions formatted as LxWxH in centimeters.']
  generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipping label was generated.']
  insurance_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the insurance coverage for the shipment.']
  insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is insured.']
  label_format varchar [note: 'File format of the generated shipping label.']
  label_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the shipping label, used for tracking and reference.']
  manual_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a user manually overrode the automated carrier selection.']
  origin_address_snapshot varchar [note: 'Serialized snapshot of the origin address at label generation time.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical packaging type used for the shipment.']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used for carrier rate calculation (weight, dimensional weight, or zone).']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the carrier rate.']
  selection_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to select the carrier for this label.']
  selection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier selection (automated or manual) occurred.']
  shipping_label_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipping label.']
  tms_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this label to the TMS transaction or batch.']
  total_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final cost of the shipping label after adjustments, in the specified currency.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number assigned by the carrier for shipment visibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipping label record.']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'True if the label has been voided or cancelled.']
  void_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the label was voided, if applicable.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Weight of the package in kilograms used for rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Master record and SSOT for each shipping label generated for an outbound shipment, incorporating the full carrier selection, rate-shopping, and label generation lifecycle. Stores carrier SCAC code, tracking number, label format (ZPL, PDF), service level (ground, 2-day, overnight, same-day), origin and destination address snapshots, label generation timestamp, carrier rate quoted, rate basis (weight, dimensional weight, zone), carrier selection method (cheapest, fastest, preferred, TMS-optimized), manual override flag, selection timestamp, label void status, and TMS reference identifier. Unified record for carrier assignment decision and physical label artifact; integrates with TMS for carrier selection and rate shopping.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment {
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating outbound shipping expenses to the appropriate cost center for accurate cost accounting and financial reporting.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who will receive the shipment.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Order‑to‑Shipment association needed for shipping status dashboard and carrier billing reconciliation.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Required for shipment‑payment reconciliation report linking each shipment to the payment transaction that funded it.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Shipping cost calculations reference a price list; required for the Shipment Cost Allocation process.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Outbound shipments for drop‑ship orders must be associated with the originating purchase order to align carrier billing and delivery performance metrics.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory compliance reporting for each shipment (customs, hazardous material) requires linking shipment to the applicable regulation.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse from which the shipment originated.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the shipment was actually delivered.']
  carrier_tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web URL to the carrier\'s tracking page for this shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  declared_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and liability purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  delivery_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of delivery attempts made for this shipment.']
  delivery_status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail about the delivery outcome (e.g., left with neighbor, held at depot).']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shipment destination address.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City component of the destination address.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the destination.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the destination address.']
  destination_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the destination address.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected date when the shipment is expected to be delivered.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Classification of freight for pricing and handling (e.g., Class 70).']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms of payment for freight charges.']
  fulfillment_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is insured.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual packages in the shipment.']
  proof_of_delivery_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the proof-of-delivery document or image.']
  return_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for a returned shipment, if applicable.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreement for delivery speed.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment was dispatched from the fulfillment center.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the shipment for tracking and reference.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is a normal outbound, a return, or an internal transfer.']
  shipping_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the shipping cost.']
  shipping_cost_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total shipping cost before taxes and adjustments.']
  shipping_cost_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net shipping cost after tax adjustments.']
  shipping_cost_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component of the shipping cost.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transport the shipment.']
  shipping_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the shipment.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the shipment package in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a physical outbound shipment dispatched from a fulfillment center to a customer or intermediate hub. Tracks shipment status (manifested, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, returned), carrier, tracking number, ship date, estimated delivery date, actual delivery date, shipment weight, dimensions, declared value, and proof-of-delivery reference. Bridges fulfillment execution and last-mile logistics; integrates with TMS for carrier tracking events.'
}

Table fulfillment.shipment_event {
  shipment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment tracking event record. Primary key for the shipment event log.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or carrier employee who performed the scan or recorded the event. Used for internal audit and quality tracking. Null for automated or carrier-reported events.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier (e.g., FedEx, UPS, USPS, DHL) that reported this tracking event. Links to carrier master data.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, fulfillment center, or carrier facility where the event occurred. Links to facility master data for internal events.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment container that this event belongs to. Links this tracking event to the overall shipment record.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order associated with this shipment event. Enables order-level tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Event‑level incidents (e.g., customs holds) must be linked to the governing compliance obligation for reporting.']
  carrier_event_code varchar [note: 'The carrier-specific status code or event identifier from the carrier\'s tracking system. Raw code as provided by carrier API or EDI message.']
  carrier_event_description varchar [note: 'The human-readable description of the tracking event as provided by the carrier. Raw scan message or status text from carrier system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this shipment event record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field for data governance and compliance.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'The specific location where the package was left upon delivery. Applicable only to delivered events. Provides proof-of-delivery detail for customer service and claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_door|back_door|side_door|mailroom|reception|neighbor|parcel_locker|carrier_facility — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The carrier\'s current estimated delivery date for the shipment as of this event. Updated dynamically as the shipment progresses. Used for customer communication and SLA tracking.']
  event_location_city varchar [note: 'The city where the tracking event was recorded by the carrier. May be null for certain event types that do not have precise location data.']
  event_location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the tracking event occurred (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Supports international shipment tracking.']
  event_location_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the location where the tracking event was recorded. Enables geographic analysis of shipment flow.']
  event_location_state varchar [note: 'The state or province where the tracking event occurred. Uses standard postal abbreviations (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  event_source varchar [note: 'The system or channel through which the tracking event was received: carrier_api (direct carrier integration), tms_webhook (Transportation Management System callback), edi_214 (Electronic Data Interchange shipment status), manual_entry (operator input), wms_scan (Warehouse Management System scan).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the tracking event occurred in the carrier\'s system. This is the real-world event time, not the system ingestion time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The category of tracking event representing the shipment milestone: picked_up (carrier collected package), in_transit (package moving through network), out_for_delivery (on vehicle for final delivery), delivered (successfully received), exception (delay or issue), attempted_delivery (delivery tried but failed).']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the exception condition if is_exception is True. Describes the nature of the delay, damage, or delivery issue. Null for non-exception events.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this event record was ingested into the data platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event actually occurred). Used for data lineage and latency monitoring.']
  is_customer_visible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event should be displayed to the customer in tracking interfaces. False for internal-only events (warehouse scans, carrier facility transfers).']
  is_exception boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event represents an exception or problem condition (delay, damage, lost package, address issue, weather delay, etc.). True if exception, False for normal milestone events.']
  is_sla_breach boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event represents or contributes to an SLA breach. True if the event timestamp exceeds the SLA target, False otherwise.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees. Enables precise geospatial analysis and mapping of shipment routes.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees. Used with latitude for route optimization and delivery zone analysis.']
  raw_payload varchar [note: 'The complete raw JSON or XML payload received from the carrier API, TMS webhook, or EDI message. Preserved for audit, troubleshooting, and reprocessing. May contain additional carrier-specific metadata.']
  signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the delivery. Captured for delivered events requiring proof of delivery. Null for events that do not involve signature capture.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The promised delivery date and time per the service level agreement. Used to detect SLA breaches by comparing against actual delivery timestamp.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or package temperature in degrees Celsius recorded at the time of the event. Critical for cold-chain shipments (pharmaceuticals, perishables). Null for non-temperature-controlled shipments.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Used for customer-facing tracking and carrier API queries.']
  weight_actual_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual measured weight of the shipment in kilograms as recorded during this event. May be captured at pickup, in-transit weighing, or delivery. Used for freight cost reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing carrier-reported and internal tracking milestones for a shipment throughout its transit lifecycle. Each record represents a discrete tracking event: event type (picked up, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, attempted delivery), event timestamp, event location (city, state, country), carrier event code, raw carrier scan message, and data source (carrier API, TMS webhook, EDI 214). Enables SLA breach detection and customer-facing tracking updates.'
}

Table fulfillment.proof_of_delivery {
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proof of delivery record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party who received the shipment.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment that this proof of delivery belongs to.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Proof‑of‑Delivery must be tied to originating order for compliance audit and customer dispute resolution.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier code of the carrier that performed the final delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof of delivery record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Street address where the delivery was made.']
  delivery_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'Code provided by the carrier confirming successful delivery.']
  delivery_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the delivery confirmation event.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode of delivery used for the order.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery was confirmed (actual event time).']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exception occurred during delivery (e.g., failed attempt, damage).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any delivery exception details.']
  gps_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Reported accuracy of the GPS coordinate in meters.']
  is_bopis boolean [note: 'True if the delivery was part of a Buy‑Online‑Pick‑Up‑In‑Store fulfillment.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery location.']
  otp_verified boolean [note: 'True if a one‑time password was successfully verified at delivery.']
  photo_captured boolean [note: 'True if a photo of the delivered package was captured.']
  pod_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the proof‑of‑delivery data format.']
  proof_asset_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital asset (image, signature file, etc.) that serves as proof.']
  proof_of_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery proof record.']
  proof_type varchar [note: 'Method used to capture delivery proof (e.g., signature, photo, GPS coordinate, one‑time password).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the delivery recipient.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the delivery recipient.']
  signature_captured boolean [note: 'True if a handwritten signature was captured as proof.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken to complete the delivery.']
  sla_compliance varchar [note: 'Result of SLA evaluation for this delivery.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target service‑level agreement time in minutes for delivery completion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the proof of delivery record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing confirmed delivery evidence for a shipment. Stores confirmation type (signature, photo, GPS, OTP), recipient name, delivery timestamp, location coordinates, photo/signature asset references, carrier confirmation code, and exception notes. Critical for dispute resolution, chargeback defense, RMA initiation, and last-mile SLA compliance.'
}

Table fulfillment.receiving_record {
  receiving_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the receiving record.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'System‑generated identifier for the inbound shipment batch.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.goods_receipt.goods_receipt_id — Dock receipt to stock putaway traceability. Links the physical receiving event at the dock to the inventory system\'s goods receipt for stock position updates — essential for inbound supply chain visib']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Receiving records must reference the purchase order they fulfill to support three‑way match, invoice validation, and supplier performance tracking.']
  receiving_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed the receipt.']
  receiving_putaway_associate_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who performed the putaway operation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Supports the \'Receiving and Put‑away\' workflow, linking each inbound receipt to the SKU for inventory reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier who shipped the goods.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ASN sent by the supplier for this inbound shipment.']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Condition of the received items (e.g., new, damaged, quarantine).']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with the received goods (pre‑tax, pre‑discount).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receiving record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  discrepancy_flag varchar [note: 'Flag indicating any quantity or condition discrepancy detected during receiving.']
  dock_door varchar [note: 'Code of the dock door where the shipment was received.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External purchase order identifier associated with the inbound shipment.']
  putaway_bin varchar [note: 'Location bin/slot where items were placed after receiving.']
  putaway_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the putaway task was completed.']
  putaway_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the putaway location (directed, random, or fixed).']
  putaway_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the putaway task.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment was physically received at the dock.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receipt (e.g., purchase order, transfer, return).']
  received_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKU types received in this transaction.']
  receiving_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receiving record.']
  total_quantity_received varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of all units received in this transaction.']
  total_volume_cubic_m decimal(12,3) [note: 'Combined volume of all items received, measured in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Combined weight of all items received, measured in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the receiving record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record and SSOT for the complete inbound receiving-through-putaway process at a fulfillment center. Tracks PO reference, ASN reference, received SKUs and quantities, condition codes (new, damaged, quarantine), receiving associate ID, dock door, receipt timestamp, discrepancy flags (over/short/damaged), assigned putaway bin/slot location, putaway method (directed, random, fixed), putaway associate ID, putaway task status, and putaway completion timestamp. Unified inbound flow record from dock receipt through inventory slotting, eliminating the need for separate putaway task tracking.'
}

Table fulfillment.bin_location {
  bin_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each physical storage location.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Bin locations belong to a specific fulfillment center; adding center_id FK enables hierarchy and eliminates silo.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment center or warehouse that owns this bin.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the aisle containing the bin.']
  bay varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the bay within the aisle.']
  bin_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or tier where the bin is located.']
  bin_location_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the bin (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bin location record was first created.']
  current_units varchar [note: 'Current count of units stored in the bin.']
  current_volume_cubic_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current total volume of inventory in the bin.']
  current_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current total weight of inventory in the bin.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bin became available for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the bin is scheduled to be decommissioned or repurposed (nullable).']
  is_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bin is accessible to standard picking equipment (e.g., wheelchair‑accessible).']
  is_hazmat_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous materials are permitted in this location.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the bin resides in a temperature‑controlled environment.']
  last_inventory_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory verification for this bin.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the bin within the fulfillment center.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special characteristics or notes about the bin.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the storage location, often used in UI displays and pick‑lists.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the storage element (e.g., bin, shelf, pallet, rack).']
  max_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual units the bin can hold.']
  max_volume_cubic_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum volume in cubic meters that the bin can accommodate.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms that the bin can safely store.']
  position varchar [note: 'Specific position index within the level (e.g., slot number).']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature classification of the zone, required for perishable or temperature‑sensitive inventory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bin location record.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone where the bin resides, driving pick path and handling rules.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical storage location (bin, slot, shelf, pallet position) within a fulfillment center. Captures location code, zone classification (pick, bulk, staging, returns, hazmat, cold chain), aisle/bay/level/position coordinates, location type, capacity constraints (units, weight, volume), temperature zone, hazmat flag, and active status. SSOT for warehouse slotting and zone-based pick path optimization.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_sla {
  fulfillment_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA record.']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment center or warehouse to which the SLA applies.']
  breach_threshold_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tolerance window in days for delivery‑time breaches.']
  breach_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tolerance window in hours beyond the target after which the SLA is considered breached.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Carrier service tier (e.g., ground, 2‑day, overnight) covered by the SLA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment (e.g., consumer, prime) for which the SLA is defined.']
  delivery_time_target_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed days from shipment dispatch to customer receipt.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method of order fulfillment to which the SLA applies.']
  fulfillment_sla_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the SLA purpose and scope.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, exceptions, or special handling instructions.']
  pack_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from pick completion to packing completion.']
  pick_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from order receipt to item pick completion.']
  same_day_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest order‑placement time (HH:MM) for same‑day fulfillment eligibility.']
  ship_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from packing completion to shipment dispatch.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business code for the SLA, used in operational reporting and carrier selection.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the SLA offering (e.g., "Prime Express SLA").']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA (e.g., standard, premium, custom).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SLA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for change‑management and audit tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining SLA commitments by fulfillment method, carrier service level, customer segment, and fulfillment center. Captures promised pick/pack/ship SLA windows (hours), delivery window (days), same-day cutoff time, breach thresholds, and effective date range. Drives SLA compliance monitoring, fulfillment_exception breach detection, and carrier selection logic.'
}

Table fulfillment.third_party_fulfillment {
  third_party_fulfillment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the third‑party fulfillment partner record.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Third‑party partners are required to meet specific compliance obligations; linking enables monitoring and reporting.']
  billing_model varchar [note: 'Billing arrangement with the partner.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of compliance certifications held by the partner (e.g., ISO 27001, PCI DSS).']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective.']
  contracted_sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier contracted with the partner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partner record was created.']
  data_privacy_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a data privacy agreement is in place.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email for escalation contact.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the contact for issue escalation.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for escalation contact.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the billing fees.']
  fee_per_shipment decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost charged per shipment processed by the partner.']
  geographic_coverage_zones varchar [note: 'Regions or zones the partner serves.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum insurance coverage amount provided by the partner.']
  insurance_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the insurance coverage amount.']
  integration_type varchar [note: 'Method used to integrate with the partner\'s system.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks about the partner.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the partner onboarding process.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the 3PL partner.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner (e.g., logistics provider, carrier).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the partner.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall performance score (0‑100) based on scorecard metrics.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main operational contact at the partner.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the primary contact.']
  sla_target_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for order fulfillment as per SLA.']
  supported_fulfillment_methods varchar [note: 'Fulfillment methods the partner can perform.']
  third_party_fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner relationship.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner record.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) partner engagement for fulfillment outsourcing. Captures 3PL partner name, integration type (EDI, API, portal), supported fulfillment methods, geographic coverage zones, contracted SLA tiers, onboarding status, performance scorecard metrics, billing model, and operational contact details. SSOT for 3PL partner configuration within the fulfillment domain; distinct from seller domain marketplace partner records.'
}

Table fulfillment.bopis_order {
  bopis_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the BOPIS order record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Needed to attribute BOPIS sales to specific campaigns for performance dashboards; marketing budgets are allocated based on in‑store pickup conversions.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: BOPIS orders are picked up at a specific fulfillment center; linking provides clear center association.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Links BOPIS order to the in‑store pickup signage asset, enabling consistent branding and localized asset management.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the BOPIS order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: BOPIS orders are a subset of standard orders; linking to order header allows inventory allocation and sales reporting.']
  pickup_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical store where the customer will collect the order.']
  associate_handoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store associate handed the order to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOPIS order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the order.']
  customer_checkin_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer checked in at the store to collect the order.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp after which an unclaimed BOPIS order is automatically cancelled.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the BOPIS order after the pickup window closes.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated fraud screening for the order.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Message entered by the purchaser to be included with the gift.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the order before taxes, discounts, or fees.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pickup was performed without physical contact.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the customer selected an expedited handling option.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'True if the order was marked as a gift for another recipient.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by staff for internal reference.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External order identifier used in customer communications and tracking.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOPIS order was initially placed by the customer.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the BOPIS order was created.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BOPIS order.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the order.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payment.']
  pickup_confirmation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the customer at pickup.']
  pickup_location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code representing the specific pickup area or locker within the store.']
  pickup_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the customer‑selected time window for order pickup.']
  pickup_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the customer‑selected time window for order pickup.']
  ready_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was marked ready for customer pickup.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method selected for delivering the order when not picked up in store.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time (in minutes) taken to make the order ready for pickup.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Service level agreement target time (in minutes) for order readiness.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any additional handling or customer‑provided instructions for the order.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOPIS order record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing the Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) fulfillment workflow. Captures BOPIS order reference, designated pickup store/location, customer-selected pickup window, order ready notification timestamp, customer check-in timestamp, associate-assisted handoff timestamp, pickup confirmation method (QR code, ID check, PIN), expiry timestamp for unclaimed orders, and final disposition (picked up, cancelled, converted to ship). Distinct from standard fulfillment orders due to its store-based pickup workflow.'
}

Table fulfillment.return_receipt {
  return_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return receipt record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who logged the receipt.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Return receipts are generated for returns of a fulfillment order; linking enables traceability back to the original order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original order linked to this return.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Return receipts are linked to the original purchase order to reconcile refunds, restocking fees, and supplier credit.']
  refund_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the refund authorization workflow.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Enables the \'Return Processing\' system to attribute refunds and restocking decisions to the specific SKU returned.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the return was received.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the carrier used for the return shipment.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the returned items\' condition for disposition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the refund.']
  disposition_route varchar [note: 'Routing decision for the returned items after inspection.']
  gross_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total refund amount before deductions.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the returned items were inspected.']
  is_priority_return boolean [note: 'True if the return is marked for expedited processing.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Specific location or bin code within the warehouse.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Refund amount after tax and any fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the associate during receipt.']
  original_order_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable order number of the original purchase.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the physical return was received at the fulfillment center.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the refund process.']
  restocking_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned items can be restocked.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the item was returned.']
  return_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the return receipt.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the return.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'External RMA reference number associated with the return.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual number of hours taken to process the return.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours to complete return processing per SLA.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the refund.']
  total_items varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKU identifiers in the return.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all returned items.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number for the inbound return shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the return receipt record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical receipt and inspection of a customer return at a fulfillment center or returns processing hub. Records RMA reference, returned SKUs and quantities, condition assessment per item (resellable, refurbish, liquidate, destroy), return reason codes, restocking eligibility flags, receiving associate ID, inspection timestamp, and disposition routing (back to stock, quarantine, vendor return, disposal). Feeds inventory reintegration and refund authorization workflows.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_exception {
  fulfillment_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each fulfillment exception record.']
  compliance_exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_exception. Business justification: Operational exceptions often trigger a formal compliance exception record for audit and risk tracking.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the exception.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment linked to the exception.']
  handling_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: Exceptions are assigned to a service team for resolution; storing the handling team ID supports workload balancing, SLA monitoring, and root‑cause reporting.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Fulfillment exceptions are analyzed per order to calculate SLA breach impact; linking enables Order Exception Summary report.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Needed for the \'Exception Management\' report to identify which SKU caused the fulfillment exception and drive corrective actions.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific location (e.g., dock, zone) related to the exception.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the exception originated.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the activity actually completed.']
  breach_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of SLA breach measured in minutes.']
  breach_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of SLA breach measured in seconds.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the carrier involved in the exception, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was first created in the system.']
  customer_impact boolean [note: 'True if the exception directly affected the customer experience.']
  escalated_to varchar [note: 'Role or individual to whom the exception was escalated.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception was escalated to a higher authority.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to classify the exception for reporting.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception record.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the exception was detected in the fulfillment process.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the exception (e.g., short pick, damaged item, mispick, label error, carrier refusal, address undeliverable, lost in transit, dock delay, SLA breach types). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: short_pick|damaged_item|mispick|label_error|carrier_refusal|address_undeliverable|lost_in_transit|dock_delay|pick_sla_breach|pack_sla_breach|ship_sla_breach|delivery_sla_breach — promote to reference product]']
  expected_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the activity that was breached (used for SLA calculations).']
  fulfillment_exception_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details of the exception event.']
  impact_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to quantify the business impact of the exception.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception is deemed critical for business continuity.']
  is_repeat boolean [note: 'Indicates if this exception is a repeat occurrence for the same order or shipment.']
  is_sla_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception resulted in an SLA breach.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the handling team.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the exception.']
  repeat_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the same exception has recurred.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to remediate or mitigate the exception.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception was resolved.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Entity accountable for the exception (e.g., fulfillment center, carrier, 3PL, seller, system).']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the underlying cause of the exception.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of the exception on fulfillment operations.']
  sla_actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA‑bound activity actually finished.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the SLA promised completion time.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Specific SLA category the exception relates to.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the exception record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing all exceptions and disruptions during the fulfillment lifecycle, including SLA breaches. Records exception type (short pick, damaged item, mispick, label error, carrier refusal, address undeliverable, lost in transit, dock delay, pick_sla_breach, pack_sla_breach, ship_sla_breach, delivery_sla_breach), severity level, affected fulfillment order or shipment, exception timestamp, expected vs actual completion timestamps (for SLA breaches), breach duration, root cause category, responsible party (FC, carrier, 3PL), resolution action, and resolution timestamp. Enables unified exception management, SLA compliance monitoring, and root cause analysis for operational improvement.'
}

Table fulfillment.store {
  store_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for store']
  pickup_store_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on store (pickup_store_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the store.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders the store can process per day.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the store is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date when the store ceased operations (null if still open).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the store location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store record was first created.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for store communications.']
  has_parking boolean [note: 'True if the store provides customer parking.']
  is_24h_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store operates 24 hours a day.']
  is_accessible boolean [note: 'True if the store meets accessibility standards (e.g., ADA).']
  last_inventory_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory audit for the store.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the store (decimal degrees).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the store (decimal degrees).']

  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the store manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the store manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the store.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the store.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the store began operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Daily operating window expressed as HH:MM-HH:MM.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the store.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the store address.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total floor area of the store in square feet.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the store location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the store.']
  store_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the store in operational systems.']
  store_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of stores for operational management.']
  store_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region grouping for reporting (e.g., North America, EMEA).']
  store_sla_minutes varchar [note: 'Target fulfillment service‑level agreement time in minutes for orders processed at this store.']
  store_type varchar [note: 'Category of the store indicating its fulfillment role.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the store (e.g., America/New_York).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the store record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for store. Referenced by pickup_store_id.'
}

Table fulfillment.carton {
  carton_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carton']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse location where the carton is stored.']
  parent_carton_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on carton (parent_carton_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase order that was used to acquire the carton.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier that provided the carton.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle where the carton is located.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric barcode printed on the carton for scanning.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the production run of the carton.']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'External reference code or barcode printed on the carton.']
  carton_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the physical characteristics or special requirements of the carton.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance or safety certifications applicable to the carton.']
  cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the carton.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of initial record creation in the data lake.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the carton was removed from inventory and disposed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the carton is considered expired or unsuitable.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Textual instructions for safe handling of the carton.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the hazardous material inside the carton.']
  height_cm varchar [note: 'Height of the carton in centimeters.']
  humidity_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True if the carton requires humidity‑controlled storage.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the latest inspection of the carton.']
  is_fragile_flag boolean [note: 'True when the carton holds fragile goods.']
  is_hazardous_flag boolean [note: 'True when the carton contains hazardous items.']
  last_inspected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent physical inspection.']
  length_cm varchar [note: 'Length of the carton in centimeters.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number for traceability of the carton.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the carton.']
  material varchar [note: 'Material from which the carton is constructed (e.g., cardboard, plastic).']
  max_load_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible load weight for the carton.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the carton used in reports and UI.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the carton was manufactured.']
  recycle_status varchar [note: 'Current recycling disposition of the carton.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier attached to the carton.']
  shelf varchar [note: 'Shelf on which the carton rests.']
  slot varchar [note: 'Slot position on the shelf for the carton.']
  status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the carton is in service, damaged, lost, etc.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True if the carton must be stored in a temperature‑controlled environment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the carton record.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Internal cubic meter capacity of the carton.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the carton’s warranty expires.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the empty carton in kilograms.']
  width_cm varchar [note: 'Width of the carton in centimeters.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Zone designation within the warehouse.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for carton. Referenced by carton_id.'
}

  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate.']

  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for packing SLA.']

  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the station based on automation level.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pack station.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current ambient temperature at the station.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of capacity currently utilized.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pack_station. Referenced by pack_station_id.'
}
