  wave_type varchar [note: 'Category of the wave indicating its operational priority or purpose.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pick_wave. Referenced by pick_wave_id.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier record.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: UI displays carrier logo on tracking pages; each carrier must reference a single digital asset containing its logo.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for Carrier Compliance Program enrollment; carriers must be linked to a compliance program to track certification and audit status.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the carrier\'s primary business address (optional).']
  capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity the carrier can transport per shipment.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the transportation carrier.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier (e.g., active, inactive, suspended, pending onboarding).']
  carrier_tier varchar [note: 'Internal tier used to prioritize carriers based on performance, volume, and strategic importance.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary service category the carrier provides (e.g., Full‑Truck‑Load, Less‑Truck‑Load, parcel, last‑mile, cross‑border).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  compliance_ctpat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid C‑TPAT certification.']
  compliance_iata boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is certified by IATA for air freight operations.']
  compliance_wto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier complies with WTO trade regulations for cross‑border shipments.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the carrier.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier contract expires or is scheduled for renewal (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the active contract with the carrier.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier contract becomes effective.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the carrier is headquartered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for carrier contract financials.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'Seven‑digit identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Transportation to the carrier.']
  edi_integration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Electronic Data Interchange integration with the carrier.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the carrier\'s liability insurance coverage.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier\'s insurance coverage expires.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier record was last reviewed for compliance or data quality.']
  max_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in pounds the carrier can handle for a single shipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the carrier.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the carrier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  rating_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Aggregated performance rating (0‑5) based on on‑time delivery, damage rate, and service quality.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the carrier in industry systems.']
  service_capabilities varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the carrier\'s capabilities (e.g., temperature‑controlled, hazardous‑material, LTL consolidation).']
  sla_delivery_time_actual varchar [note: 'Actual delivery time in minutes recorded for the carrier.']
  sla_delivery_time_target varchar [note: 'Target delivery time in minutes defined in the carrier SLA.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the carrier.']

  Note: 'Master record for all transportation carriers (FTL, LTL, parcel, last-mile, cross-border) contracted by Ecommerce. Captures carrier identity (SCAC, DOT number), service capabilities, EDI integration status, compliance certifications (C-TPAT, IATA, WTO), carrier tier classification, performance SLA thresholds, and active contract references. SSOT for carrier identity across TMS and logistics operations.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_service {
  carrier_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each carrier service offering.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier that provides this service.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service fees are allocated to cost centers to reflect true cost of carrier services per business unit.']
  base_rate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base price for the service before any surcharges or discounts, in US dollars.']
  carrier_service_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the service, including any special handling notes.']
  carrier_service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service offering.']
  cost_per_kg_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variable cost component calculated per kilogram of shipment weight.']
  cost_per_mile_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variable cost component calculated per mile of distance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier service record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for pricing.']
  customs_document_required boolean [note: 'True if shipments using this service require customs paperwork.']
  destination_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or region where shipments are delivered for this service.']
  dimension_limit_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum linear dimension (e.g., longest side) allowed for the service, in centimeters.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the service offering becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the service offering is retired or expires (null if indefinite).']
  fuel_surcharge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to the base rate to account for fuel price volatility.']
  is_express boolean [note: 'True if the service is classified as express (priority handling).']
  level_code varchar [note: 'Carrier‑specific code representing the service level tier.']
  max_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum height of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  max_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum length of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume allowed for the service, expressed in cubic meters.']
  max_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum width of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or special conditions for the service.']
  origin_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or region where shipments originate for this service.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the service based on geographic scope.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Carrier‑assigned alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the service.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service (e.g., "Express Overnight").']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the service offering (e.g., standard ground, express, LTL, FTL).']
  sla_transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement guaranteed transit time in days.']
  surcharge_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is subject to surcharges (e.g., fuel, residential).']
  tracking_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real‑time shipment tracking is available for this service.']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of calendar days from pickup to delivery for this service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the carrier service record.']
  weight_limit_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable shipment weight for the service, expressed in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific service offerings provided by each carrier — e.g., standard ground, express, overnight, same-day, LTL freight, FTL, cross-border. Captures service code, transit time SLA, weight/dimension limits, supported origin-destination lanes, surcharge eligibility, and carrier-assigned service level identifiers. Enables carrier selection logic in TMS route optimization.'
}

Table logistics.logistics_shipment {
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the shipment record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Needed for Marketing ROI attribution linking each shipment to the campaign that generated the order, enabling shipment‑level performance reporting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link logistics_shipment to carrier for carrier ownership; carrier_name string becomes redundant.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for internal accounting: allocate each shipment\'s cost to a cost center for expense reporting and profitability analysis.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Required for shipment fulfillment reports linking each shipment to the customer\'s shipping address used for origin, enabling address‑level delivery performance analysis.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment in the Transportation Management System.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Linking shipments to the originating order is essential for order‑to‑shipment tracking reports and customer service dashboards.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Inbound Shipment Receipt Matching report ties each shipment to the Goods Receipt that records quantity received in the warehouse.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Link logistics_shipment to route for route planning reference.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the shipment was delivered to the recipient.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date the shipment actually left the origin.']
  carrier_service_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific service level offered by the carrier (e.g., UPS Ground, FedEx Express).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was initially created in the TMS.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs paperwork.']
  customs_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value for customs duty calculation.']
  customs_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the customs value.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and insurance purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery was confirmed by the carrier.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full delivery address of the customer.']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery destination.']
  dimension_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  dimension_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  dimension_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery to the destination.']
  estimated_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for the shipment to leave the origin.']
  insurance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the insurance coverage.']
  insurance_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the insurance amount.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is insured.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous goods.']
  logistics_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment.']
  origin_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the shipment origin (warehouse or fulfillment center).']
  origin_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the origin location.']
  proof_of_delivery_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic proof of delivery document.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first captured.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  route_optimization_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the route optimization run used for this shipment.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Delivery speed commitment for the shipment.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the shipment by the transportation management system.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on load size.']
  shipping_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base shipping charge before taxes and fees.']
  shipping_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the shipping cost.']
  shipping_cost_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the shipping charge.']
  shipping_cost_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total shipping charge including taxes and fees.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required on delivery.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a physical shipment dispatched from a fulfillment node to a destination. Captures shipment reference number, origin/destination addresses, carrier assignment, service level, tracking number, weight, dimensions, declared value, shipment status (derived from tracking_event stream), estimated and actual ship/delivery dates, FTL/LTL indicator, and TMS shipment ID. Central logistics record linking orders to physical movement. SSOT for shipment identity and carrier assignment. Status is derived from tracking_event — this entity owns the shipment header, not the event history.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_package {
  shipment_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment package record.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary product contained in the package.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment to which this package belongs.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the package was actually delivered.']
  barcode_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the barcode image used on the package label.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the shipping carrier (e.g., UPS, FedEx).']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shipping carrier.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier selected for the shipment (e.g., standard, express, overnight).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the package record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the declared value.']
  customs_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to electronic customs documentation associated with the package.']
  declared_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and insurance purposes, expressed in US dollars.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery was confirmed by the carrier or recipient.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for package delivery based on carrier service level.']
  height_cm varchar [note: 'External height of the package in centimeters.']
  insurance_amount_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of insurance coverage for the package, in US dollars.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the package is insured for loss or damage.']
  is_return boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package is part of a return shipment.']
  label_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the shipping label was created.']
  label_url varchar [note: 'Link to the generated shipping label PDF or image.']
  length_cm varchar [note: 'External length of the package in centimeters.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the package.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product shipped in this package.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the package.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the package within the shipment.']
  shipment_package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the package.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a recipient signature is required for delivery.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking identifier for the package.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web URL where the package tracking status can be viewed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the package record.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight of the package in kilograms.']
  width_cm varchar [note: 'External width of the package in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Individual physical packages within a shipment. A single shipment may contain multiple packages (cartons, pallets, parcels, envelopes). Captures package sequence number, dimensions (L×W×H), weight, package type, tracking barcode, carrier label format and file reference URL, label generation timestamp, seal number, hazmat flag, and package-level status. Subsumes shipping label data — label attributes are properties of the package, not a separate entity. Enables parcel-level tracking, carrier billing reconciliation, and manifest generation.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_item {
  shipment_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a shipment.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment record to which this line belongs.']
  shipment_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical package/container that holds this line item.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transporting the shipment.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating where the product was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment item record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code of the declared value (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  declared_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for a single unit of the item for customs purposes.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory export classification (e.g., EAR99, ITAR) for the item.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Classification code indicating hazardous material type, if applicable.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the item in centimeters.']
  hs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'International customs classification code for the product.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'True if the item is classified as hazardous material.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the product for customs and carrier manifests.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the item in centimeters.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line item within the shipment for ordering.']
  line_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for the line (quantity × declared unit value).']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the product included in the shipment line.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU shipped in this line.']
  restricted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the item is subject to export restrictions.']
  shipment_item_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the shipment line item.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Standardized product identifier used across the ecommerce platform.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number assigned by the carrier for this shipment line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment item record.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated volume of the line item in cubic meters (derived from dimensions).']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the line item in kilograms, used for freight calculations.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the item in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail of SKUs included within a shipment package. Captures SKU reference, quantity shipped, unit of measure, item description, HS tariff code for customs, country of origin, declared unit value, and hazmat classification. Supports customs documentation, carrier manifests, and shipment-to-order reconciliation.'
}

Table logistics.tracking_event {
  tracking_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique surrogate key for each shipment tracking event.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link tracking_event to carrier for carrier reference; carrier_code becomes redundant.']
  carrier_facility_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier\'s hub or facility where the event occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tracking event record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  delivery_attempt_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the delivery attempt.']
  delivery_attempt_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the delivery attempt for this shipment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier generated the tracking event.']
  event_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of shipment event.']
  event_type_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the event type.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why an exception event occurred.']
  exception_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation of the exception cause.']
  exception_resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the exception (e.g., address corrected, customs cleared).']
  exception_resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was resolved.']
  exception_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Entity accountable for resolving the exception.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  proof_of_delivery_photo_url varchar [note: 'Link to the delivery confirmation photo.']
  proof_of_delivery_recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the shipment.']
  proof_of_delivery_signature_url varchar [note: 'Link to the captured signature image for the delivery.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when proof of delivery was captured.']
  raw_event_description varchar [note: 'Original free‑text payload received from the carrier.']
  reattempt_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next delivery attempt after a failure.']
  scan_location varchar [note: 'Physical address or location description where the scan was performed.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment met the SLA delivery time (true) or missed it (false).']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Committed delivery time defined by the SLA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the tracking event record.']

  Note: 'Unified event stream recording all shipment lifecycle events from carrier integrations (EDI 214, carrier APIs). Event types include: pickup scan, in-transit scan, out-for-delivery, delivery attempt (with attempt sequence, outcome, and reattempt scheduling), exception (address correction, customs hold, damage, carrier delay — with exception reason code, responsible party, resolution action, and resolution timestamp), and proof-of-delivery confirmation (recipient name, signature capture, delivery photo URL, GPS coordinates). Captures event timestamp, event type code, scan location, carrier facility code, GPS coordinates, and raw carrier event description. SSOT for ALL shipment status derivation, last-mile visibility, exception management, delivery confirmation, and SLA compliance monitoring. No other entity in this domain records shipment events or delivery outcomes.'
}

Table logistics.route {
  route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the transportation route.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Tracking UI shows a visual map of the route; each route needs a reference to one map‑image digital asset.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Route operating costs are charged to a cost center to enable route‑level cost analysis.']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link route to primary carrier; primary_carrier_code becomes redundant.']
  average_cost_per_mile decimal(10,4) [note: 'Historical average cost incurred per mile for operating the route (currency unit omitted for brevity).']
  average_transit_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average number of days the route has taken to complete.']
  corridor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transportation corridor (e.g., last‑mile, linehaul, cross‑dock, domestic, international, cross‑border).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the route record was first created.']
  cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the route traverses an international border.']
  customs_document_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs documentation is mandatory for the route.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'HS or tariff code applicable to shipments on this route.']
  destination_hub_code varchar [note: 'Code of the destination hub, facility, or region where the route ends.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the destination hub.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the route is retired or no longer usable (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the route becomes active for planning and execution.']
  estimated_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned travel distance for the route in kilometers.']
  estimated_transit_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of calendar days required to complete the route.']
  lane_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of expected shipment volume on the lane (e.g., low, medium, high, very high).']
  last_mile_type varchar [note: 'Mode of final delivery to the consumer (e.g., door‑to‑door, locker, pickup point).']
  max_load_weight_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible weight for a single shipment on the route, expressed in metric tons.']
  max_volume_cubic_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume that can be transported on the route.']
  optimization_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the algorithm used to generate or optimize the route.']
  origin_hub_code varchar [note: 'Code of the origin hub, facility, or region where the route starts.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the origin hub.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the route\'s governance and performance.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the route based on factors such as security, weather, and regulatory exposure.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the route in logistics and carrier contracts.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the route, including notable characteristics or constraints.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the route.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route.']
  stop_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered list of stop identifiers for multi‑stop routes, stored as a delimited string.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0‑100) reflecting the environmental impact of the route.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the route record.']
  vehicle_type_requirement varchar [note: 'Required vehicle type for the route (e.g., truck, van, bike, drone, container).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the route record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of all origin-to-destination transportation corridors — including last-mile delivery routes with stop sequences, linehaul lanes, inter-facility transfer lanes, and cross-border corridors. Captures route/lane code, origin hub/facility/region, destination zone/facility/region, stop sequence (for multi-stop routes), estimated distance and transit duration, corridor type (last-mile, linehaul, cross-dock, domestic, international, cross-border), lane volume tier, primary and backup carrier assignment, vehicle type requirement, average transit days, active status, and route optimization algorithm version. SSOT for ALL transportation lane and route definitions supporting TMS route optimization, capacity planning, carrier assignment, rate management, and DSR planning. Subsumes both point-to-point lanes and multi-stop delivery routes.'
}

Table logistics.route_stop {
  route_stop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the route stop record.']
  customs_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for customs documentation associated with cross‑border stops.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver handling the stop.']
  pod_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the POD document.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent delivery route.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle serving the stop.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle actually arrived at the stop.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle left the stop.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier responsible for the stop.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the stop\'s delivery address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the stop location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the route stop record was created.']
  customs_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for the stop.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the stop\'s delivery address.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the stop\'s delivery address, if applicable.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exception occurred at the stop.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the exception, if any.']
  fuel_consumption_liters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Fuel consumed by the vehicle up to this stop.']
  is_last_stop boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop is the final stop on the route.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the stop location.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the stop location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the driver or operations staff.']
  odometer_reading_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at arrival at the stop.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the stop\'s delivery address.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when proof of delivery was captured.']
  scheduled_arrival_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end time of the arrival window for the stop.']
  scheduled_arrival_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start time of the arrival window for the stop.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned departure time from the stop.']
  signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer signature was captured at the stop.']
  signature_image_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored image of the captured signature.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration measured against the SLA target.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Service level agreement target duration for the stop.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the stop\'s delivery address.']
  stop_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the stop within the route.']
  stop_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stop.']
  stop_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stop purpose (e.g., pickup, delivery).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the stop for temperature‑sensitive shipments.']
  time_on_site_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes the vehicle spent at the stop location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the route stop record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the load measured at the stop.']

  Note: 'Individual stop records within a planned delivery route. Captures stop sequence number, delivery address, scheduled arrival window, actual arrival time, stop type (pickup, delivery, cross-dock), stop status, time-on-site duration, and exception flag. Enables granular last-mile route execution tracking and SLA compliance monitoring.'
}

Table logistics.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the freight order record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier party responsible for transporting the freight.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight orders generate expenses that need to be booked against the appropriate cost center for budgeting.']
  destination_facility_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the freight is to be delivered.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Freight orders are subject to specific compliance obligations (e.g., hazardous material handling); linking enables obligation tracking and reporting.']
  primary_freight_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the freight is picked up.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Freight Order tracking of each Purchase Order; PO fulfillment teams need to trace logistics execution back to the originating PO.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the shipper (originating party) for the freight.']
  agreed_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary rate agreed with the carrier for the shipment, before any adjustments.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Document reference number for the shipment\'s bill of lading.']
  carrier_acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier accepted the freight tender.']
  carrier_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier\'s rate (spot, contract, or negotiated).']
  carrier_rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason supplied by the carrier if the tender was rejected.']
  carrier_response_deadline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Latest timestamp by which the carrier must accept or reject the tender.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the goods being transported.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values on the freight order.']
  delivery_appointment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the carrier to deliver the freight.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer or container used for the shipment.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Standard freight classification (e.g., NMFC) used for pricing and routing.']
  freight_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight order.']
  freight_order_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the order is a tender, contract, or spot shipment.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is Full Truckload (FTL) or Less‑Than‑Truckload (LTL).']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode for the freight.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the freight order, used in carrier communications and audit.']
  pickup_appointment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the carrier to pick up the freight.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is marked as high priority.']
  shipping_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm governing responsibility and cost allocation for the shipment.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions for carrier regarding loading, temperature control, or safety.']
  tender_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the freight order was tendered to the carrier.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final charge amount billed to the shipper after rate, surcharges, and discounts.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the shipment in kilograms.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier for real‑time visibility.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier printed on the trailer used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the freight order record.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume values.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for weight values.']

  Note: 'Represents a planned FTL or LTL freight movement order tendered to a carrier via EDI 204 Load Tender. Captures freight order number, shipment references, origin/destination facilities, tender date, carrier acceptance status, agreed rate, load type (FTL/LTL), commodity description, total weight/volume, pickup and delivery appointments, and carrier response deadline. Bridges TMS load planning with carrier execution and supports freight audit.'
}

Table logistics.customs_declaration {
  customs_declaration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the customs declaration record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customs expenses are allocated to the cost center responsible for the import operation.']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customs broker handling the declaration.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Customs duties and taxes are posted to GL accounts; linking declarations to general_ledger enables accurate tax reporting.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Customs Clearance process must be linked to the Goods Receipt to verify that cleared items match received inventory.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Customs declarations must reference the governing regulation to ensure correct duty calculation and legal compliance.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing change history for compliance.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date when customs clearance was completed.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter currency code for declared value.']
  customs_declaration_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the declaration record.']
  declaration_date date [note: 'Date when the customs declaration was submitted.']
  declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs declaration number assigned by authorities.']
  declared_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of goods as declared for customs.']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'Code of the port where goods are imported.']
  duties_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customs duties assessed on the shipment.']
  ead_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if an electronic advance declaration was submitted.']
  exporting_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter code of the country exporting the goods.']
  goods_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the declared goods.']
  hs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Internationally standardized code for classifying goods.']
  importing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter code of the country importing the goods.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing the shipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is on hold for inspection.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the record.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Count of individual packages in the shipment.']
  origin_port_code varchar [note: 'Code of the port where goods are exported.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Standard type of packaging used.']
  party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., exporter or importer) associated with the declaration.']
  pre_clearance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if pre-clearance procedures were performed.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes on compliance issues or special requirements.']
  shipment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking the declaration to the shipment.']
  taxes_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total taxes (e.g., VAT, GST) assessed on the shipment.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of duties, taxes, and any additional fees.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'WTO-compliant customs documentation for cross-border shipments. Captures declaration number, shipment reference, exporting/importing country codes, HS tariff codes, declared goods value, currency, duties and taxes assessed, customs broker reference, clearance status, inspection hold flag, and regulatory compliance notes. Required for international e-commerce shipments under WTO and EU Electronic Commerce Directive.'
}

Table logistics.transport_cost {
  transport_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each transport cost record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier providing the transportation service.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment or freight order to which this cost line belongs.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Transport costs contribute to profit‑center profitability; linking enables P&L attribution per profit center.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation route used.']
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: Finance reconciliation of transport cost requires a direct FK to the Supplier Invoice that records the freight charge.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current billing state of the cost line.']
  cost_allocation_center varchar [note: 'Internal cost center or accounting code to which the cost is allocated.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the cost component.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost component (e.g., base freight, fuel surcharge).']
  cost_date date [note: 'Date on which the cost was incurred or recorded.']
  cost_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the cost data (e.g., system generated, manually entered, partner feed).']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cost is an actual incurred amount or an estimated forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transport cost record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs clearance documentation.']
  customs_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Duty charged by customs for cross‑border shipments.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery was confirmed by the recipient.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the shipment leg in kilometers.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when an estimated cost becomes effective for planning.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when cost currency differs from reporting currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate was sourced.']
  is_late_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery was later than the promised date.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of cost lines within the same shipment.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary mode used for the shipment (e.g., air, sea, road).']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code of the original cost before conversion.']
  payment_term varchar [note: 'Payment terms associated with the carrier invoice.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (e.g., pallets, containers) associated with this cost line.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier agreed with the carrier.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the cost.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction or code used for the cost.']
  transport_cost_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the cost line or any special notes.']
  transport_cost_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transport cost record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual cost and contracted/estimated amount.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage representation of the cost variance.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Cubic meter volume of the shipment.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the shipment used for cost calculations.']

  Note: 'Records actual and estimated transportation costs per shipment or freight order. Captures base freight charge, fuel surcharge, residential delivery surcharge, dimensional weight adjustment, accessorial charges, carrier invoice number, billing status, cost allocation center, currency, and variance against contracted rate. Feeds finance domain for freight cost accruals and carrier invoice reconciliation.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_rate_card {
  carrier_rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the carrier rate card record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier organization that owns this rate card.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rate‑card charges are allocated to cost centers for chargeback and profitability reporting.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller‑specific rate cards are used in the \'Rate Card Assignment\' workflow for volume‑based pricing and discount calculations.']
  accessorial_charges_included varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether standard accessorial fees are bundled into the base rate.']
  accessorial_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'Reference to the schedule or code list that defines additional fees (e.g., lift‑gate, residential delivery).']
  base_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fundamental freight charge per kilogram before surcharges, expressed in the specified currency.']
  carrier_rate_card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate card (e.g., active, inactive, expired, pending approval).']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks applicable to the rate card (e.g., WTO, ISO 27001, PCI DSS).']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (master agreement, spot rate, or contract rate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for all monetary values in the rate card.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rate card becomes legally binding and usable for pricing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rate card expires; null indicates an open‑ended agreement.']
  fuel_surcharge_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the base rate to account for fuel price volatility.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Term governing responsibility and cost allocation (e.g., EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAT, DAP, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  is_accessorial_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accessorial rates from the schedule are applicable for this lane.']
  is_fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fuel surcharge percentage should be applied to shipments under this rate.']
  lane_destination varchar [note: 'Standardized code (e.g., IATA, UN/LOCODE) representing the shipment destination point for the lane.']
  lane_origin varchar [note: 'Standardized code (e.g., IATA, UN/LOCODE) representing the shipment origin point for the lane.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the origin‑destination lane is domestic or crosses international borders.']
  negotiation_reference varchar [note: 'Internal reference or document ID linking the rate card to its contract negotiation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks, exceptions, or special conditions related to the rate card.']
  rate_card_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the rate card such as lane‑based, zone‑based, or region‑based.']
  rate_card_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the rate card by the carrier or logistics contract team.']
  rate_card_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the rate card (e.g., Transportation Management System, ERP).']
  service_level_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the service commitment (e.g., standard, express, overnight) for this rate.']
  service_level_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the service level associated with the rate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rate card record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for the rate card to track revisions over time.']
  weight_tier_max_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the weight range (in kilograms) for which the base rate applies.']
  weight_tier_min_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the weight range (in kilograms) for which the base rate applies.']

  Note: 'Contracted rate structures negotiated with carriers for specific lanes, service levels, and weight breaks. Captures rate card version, effective/expiry dates, origin-destination lane, service level code, weight tier, base rate, fuel surcharge percentage, accessorial rate schedule, currency, and negotiation reference. SSOT for freight cost estimation and carrier selection optimization in TMS.'
}

Table logistics.delivery_zone {
  delivery_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each delivery zone record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link delivery_zone to its preferred carrier; carrier_preferred string becomes redundant.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Zone‑level delivery costs are charged to cost centers for regional budgeting and performance tracking.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Allows the \'Default Delivery Zone\' setting for each seller, used in shipping cost and SLA calculations.']
  carrier_zone_mapping varchar [note: 'Mapping string that links carrier‑specific zone identifiers to this internal zone.']
  cost_per_shipment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard base cost charged for a shipment to this zone before surcharges.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the country covered by the zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the zone.']
  delivery_sla_days varchar [note: 'Committed number of calendar days for delivery within this zone.']
  delivery_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM) when delivery can be performed in this zone.']
  delivery_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM) when delivery can be performed in this zone.']
  delivery_zone_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the zone, including any special handling notes.']
  delivery_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the zone record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the zone definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the zone definition expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is the default fallback for unmapped addresses.']
  is_express_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether express (same‑day/next‑day) service is offered in this zone.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total shipment volume allowed for a single package in this zone.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total shipment weight allowed for a single package in this zone.']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Range of postal codes (e.g., 10000‑19999) included in the zone.']
  region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region name within the country that the zone covers.']
  requires_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required for deliveries in this zone.']
  surcharge_remote_area boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a remote‑area surcharge applies to shipments in this zone.']
  surcharge_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a residential‑delivery surcharge applies to this zone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the delivery zone, used in carrier rate tables and routing logic.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the delivery zone.']
  zone_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification used for pricing and service level differentiation.']

  Note: 'Geographic delivery zone definitions used for carrier assignment, rate calculation, and SLA determination. Captures zone code, zone name, country/region/state coverage, postal code ranges, carrier-specific zone mapping, delivery day SLA, surcharge applicability (remote area, residential), and zone tier classification. Reference entity supporting route optimization and last-mile planning.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_exception {
  shipment_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the shipment exception record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (carrier, customer, seller, or warehouse) responsible for the exception.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Exception handling incurs costs that must be charged to the responsible cost center for variance analysis.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment to which this exception relates.']
  help_content_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: When an exception occurs, the system presents a relevant help article; link exception to the appropriate content item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated loss amount.']
  estimated_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of loss or cost impact caused by the exception.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the exception type, used for reporting and analytics.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details about the exception.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the exception was first detected in the shipment lifecycle.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the shipment exception describing the root cause.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the exception is deemed critical to business operations.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the geographic location (e.g., warehouse, hub, city) where the exception occurred.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the exception for handling urgency.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the exception.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the resolution action was completed.']
  responsible_party_type varchar [note: 'Entity type responsible for addressing the exception.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of the underlying cause of the exception.']
  shipment_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception handling process.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception caused a breach of the service‑level agreement.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number associated with the exception.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Operational records of shipment exceptions requiring intervention — e.g., address correction, delivery failure, damaged goods, customs hold, carrier delay. Captures exception type, exception timestamp, shipment reference, exception description, responsible party, resolution action taken, resolution timestamp, and exception status. Drives exception management workflows and SLA breach tracking.'
}

Table logistics.return_shipment {
  return_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the return shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link return_shipment to carrier; carrier_name and carrier_code become redundant.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or facility where the returned items are received.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing costs are allocated to a cost center for accurate return expense reporting.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the forward shipment that is being returned.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RMA record authorizing this return.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return shipment was actually received.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance validation for the return (e.g., hazardous material checks).']
  condition_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item has been inspected for condition.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return shipment involves international transit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the refund.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs documentation when the return crosses borders.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected date when the return shipment will arrive at the destination facility.']
  height_cm varchar [note: 'Height dimension of the returned package.']
  insurance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount covered by insurance for the return.']
  insurance_claim_flag boolean [note: 'True if an insurance claim is filed for the returned item.']
  is_expedited_return boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return is being processed on an expedited basis.']
  label_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return shipping label was created.']
  length_cm varchar [note: 'Length dimension of the returned package.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by staff or the customer regarding the return.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return shipment record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the return shipment record.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer for this return.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'How the refund is issued to the customer.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the refund.']
  return_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return process was initiated by the customer or system.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer returns the item.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the item is being returned.']
  return_shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the return shipment for tracking and reference.']
  return_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return shipment.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number assigned by the carrier for the return shipment.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the returned package measured in kilograms.']
  width_cm varchar [note: 'Width dimension of the returned package.']

  Note: 'Manages reverse logistics shipments initiated via RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization). Captures return shipment number, original shipment reference, RMA reference, return reason code, carrier assignment, return label generation timestamp, return tracking number, return destination facility, condition assessment flag, and return shipment status. SSOT for reverse logistics movement distinct from forward shipment.'
}

Table logistics.transport_lane {
  transport_lane_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the transportation lane.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lane operating costs are charged to the cost center that owns the lane for cost‑center level analysis.']
  destination_facility_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the lane terminates.']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Carrier assigned as the primary service provider for this lane.']
  primary_transport_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the lane originates.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Lane profitability is measured against profit centers; linking enables lane‑level P&L reporting.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the lane.']
  average_load_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average percentage of capacity utilized on the lane based on historical shipments.']
  average_transit_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of calendar days shipments take to travel the lane.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier offered by the primary carrier for this lane.']
  compliance_ctpat varchar [note: 'Customs‑Trade Partnership Against Terrorism compliance indicator for the lane.']
  compliance_wto varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lane meets World Trade Organization cross‑border regulations.']
  cost_per_mile_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost charged per mile for transportation on this lane, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lane record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for lane cost calculations.']
  customs_documentation_required boolean [note: 'True when shipments on the lane must include customs paperwork.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state code of the destination facility.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the lane definition expires or is retired; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the lane definition becomes effective.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the lane involves international border crossing.']
  lane_category varchar [note: 'Primary freight classification for the lane.']
  lane_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely identify the lane in external and internal systems.']
  lane_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the transportation lane.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lane based on geography and customs requirements.']
  lane_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Categorized tier of shipment volume historically observed on the lane.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last audit or compliance check performed on the lane.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the lane definition was last reviewed for relevance or changes.']
  max_volume_cubic_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume permitted for a shipment on the lane.']
  max_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total weight allowed for a single shipment on the lane.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state code of the origin facility.']
  route_optimization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is considered in automated route optimization algorithms.']
  sla_delivery_time_actual_days varchar [note: 'Most recent measured delivery time in days for shipments on this lane.']
  sla_delivery_time_target_days varchar [note: 'Target number of days promised to customers for delivery via this lane.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lane record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of origin-to-destination transportation lanes used for capacity planning, carrier assignment, and rate management. Captures lane code, origin facility/region, destination facility/region, lane type (domestic, international, cross-border), primary carrier, backup carrier, average transit days, lane volume tier, and active status. Foundational reference for TMS routing logic.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_contract {
  carrier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each carrier contract record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier entity associated with this contract.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carrier contracts are budgeted to specific cost centers; this FK supports cost‑center level budgeting and variance tracking.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Contract expenses must be posted to a GL account; linking contracts to general_ledger enables accurate financial statements.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per seller; the \'Carrier Contract Management\' process requires linking each contract to the seller it serves.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the latest amendment was executed.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Summary of changes introduced by the latest amendment.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the most recent amendment to the contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry.']
  carrier_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  compliance_ctpat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier complies with the Customs‑Trade Partnership Against Terrorism program.']
  compliance_iata boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with International Air Transport Association standards where applicable.']
  compliance_wto boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with World Trade Organization regulations for cross‑border shipments.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Marks the contract as confidential for internal handling.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract applies to domestic or international shipments.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the signed contract.']
  contract_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract manager.']
  contract_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person managing day‑to‑day contract activities.']
  contract_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the contract manager.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number assigned by the carrier or Ecommerce.']
  contract_owner varchar [note: 'Internal stakeholder responsible for the contract\'s overall performance.']
  contract_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region covered by the contract.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally signed by both parties.']
  contract_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract status last changed.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or narrative explaining the most recent status change.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract: master, spot, or dedicated.']
  contracted_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volume of freight (e.g., tons) the carrier commits to transport under the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts in the contract.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract grants exclusive rights to the carrier.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Marks the carrier as a preferred partner for routing decisions.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last audit or compliance review of the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special conditions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition (e.g., Net30) agreed with the carrier.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for SLA breaches or contract violations.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  rate_card_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the carrier\'s rate card that defines pricing structures.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months for each renewal period when auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  sla_delivery_time_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual delivery time recorded for the contract period.']
  sla_delivery_time_target_hours varchar [note: 'Targeted delivery time in hours as defined in the SLA.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Required notice period in days before contract termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contracted volume.']

  Note: 'Master records of commercial contracts with transportation carriers. Captures contract number, carrier reference, contract type (master, spot, dedicated), effective and expiry dates, contracted volume commitments, rate card references, SLA terms, penalty clauses, auto-renewal flag, and contract status. Distinct from carrier_rate_card which holds rate structures — this entity holds the legal and commercial agreement metadata.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_tag_assignment {
  carrier_tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the carrier_tag_assignment association']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to carrier']
  tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tag']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the tag was assigned to the carrier']
  confidence_score decimal [note: 'Confidence level of the tag assignment from ML or rule‑based process']

  Note: 'This association captures the assignment of tags to carriers. Each record links one carrier to one tag and stores the timestamp of the assignment and the confidence score from the tagging process.. Existence Justification: Carriers are classified with multiple tags (e.g., express, eco‑friendly) to support routing and reporting decisions. Each tag can be applied to many carriers, and the assignment is actively managed with timestamps and confidence scores. The relationship itself is a managed entity that business users query and update.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_cost_center_contract {
  carrier_cost_center_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the carrier_cost_center_contract association']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to carrier']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cost center']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier‑cost_center contract']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO currency code for contract pricing']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is scheduled for renewal']
  rate_card_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate card governing pricing for the carrier']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contract between a logistics carrier and a finance cost center. It captures the contractual terms, effective dates, rate references and currency that are specific to each carrier‑cost_center pairing.. Existence Justification: Carriers negotiate and sign contracts with one or more cost centers, and each cost center can have contracts with multiple carriers. The contract itself carries attributes such as contract number, effective dates, rate information and currency, which are not intrinsic to either the carrier or the cost center. The business actively creates, updates, and retires these contracts as a distinct operational entity.'
}

Table logistics.customs_broker {
  customs_broker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customs_broker']
  parent_customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on customs_broker (parent_customs_broker_id)']

  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the broker\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the broker\'s mailing address (optional).']
  broker_type varchar [note: 'Category of services the broker provides.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the broker\'s address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the customs broker.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the customs broker.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the broker\'s location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the broker record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the broker became an active partner.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the broker relationship ends or is scheduled to end (null if ongoing).']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broker is a preferred partner for the organization.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the customs brokerage organization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or remarks about the broker.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the broker\'s address.']
  rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average performance rating of the broker (scale 0.00‑5.00).']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or license number issued to the broker.']
  service_coverage_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the broker provides customs services.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the broker\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the broker.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the broker record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the customs broker.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for customs_broker. Referenced by customs_broker_id.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle {

  capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum payload weight the vehicle is rated to carry.']

  current_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net book value of the vehicle after accumulated depreciation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the vehicle over its useful life.']
