  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Most recent longitude coordinate reported by the GPS device.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the current insurance policy expires.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vehicle\'s insurance coverage.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle most recently completed a scheduled service.']
  license_plate varchar [note: 'State‑issued license plate identifier displayed on the vehicle.']
  maintenance_last_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle most recently underwent maintenance.']
  maintenance_next_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance event.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of scheduled maintenance activities.']
  make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer of the vehicle (e.g., Ford, Toyota).']
  mileage_since_service_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Distance traveled since the most recent service.']
  model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the vehicle (e.g., F‑150, Corolla).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or designation of the vehicle used in operations and reporting.']
  next_service_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next routine service.']
  odometer_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled by the vehicle in kilometres.']
  purchase_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial cost paid to acquire the vehicle.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle\'s registration registration expires.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the transport authority.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the vehicle.']
  telematics_last_sync timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful data sync from the telematics system.']
  telematics_status varchar [note: 'Current connectivity state of the vehicle\'s telematics system.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of vehicle based on size and purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vehicle record.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique 17‑character identifier for the vehicle chassis.']
  year varchar [note: 'Calendar year the vehicle was manufactured.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for vehicle. Referenced by vehicle_id.'
}

Table pricing.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price list record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Accounting of price‑list changes requires posting to a GL account for revenue recognition; finance team uses GL mapping to track price‑list impact on financial statements.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory compliance report requires each price list to be linked to the governing regulation, ensuring pricing adheres to jurisdictional rules.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Segment‑based pricing; marketing analytics join price list to customer segment to evaluate segment‑specific discount performance.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Warehouse-specific price list assignment used in regional pricing strategy to apply correct prices per fulfillment node.']
  base_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Core price value before any adjustments, expressed in the list currency.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) where the price list is applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the prices are expressed.']
  discount_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if additional discounts may be stacked on top of this price list.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price list expires; null for open‑ended lists.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the price list becomes active for transactions.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this list is the default for its scope when no other list matches.']
  max_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ceiling price for the list; used to enforce pricing caps.']
  max_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity for which the price is valid; null for no upper bound.']
  min_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor price for the list; used to enforce pricing floors.']
  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required for the price to apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  price_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the prices in this list.']
  price_elasticity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Historical elasticity metric used for dynamic pricing decisions.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the price list in external systems and contracts.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context, purpose, and any special rules for the price list.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price list (e.g., "US Retail MSRP").']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price list.']
  price_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for price values in this list.']
  price_rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding behavior applied to calculated prices.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., manually entered, system‑generated, partner feed).']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Logical tier or band within the price list (e.g., "Gold", "Silver").']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Category of the price list indicating its purpose (e.g., base list, discount list, promotional list).']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'True if the price list is part of a promotional campaign.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the price list applies.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are already included in the listed prices.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a named price list (e.g., MSRP, wholesale, member, regional) with effective date ranges, currency, applicable customer segments, and channel scope. Serves as the authoritative base price catalog from which all pricing rules derive. Owned by the Pricing domain as the SSOT for base price structures.'
}

Table pricing.price_list_item {
  price_list_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each price list item record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the last audit action.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent price list to which this item belongs.']
  product_variant_sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the specific product variant when SKU alone is insufficient.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Item‑level pricing must be validated against specific regulations; linking enables audit of compliance per SKU.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables seller‑specific SKU price overrides; pricing engine looks up seller_profile_id to apply custom base prices per seller.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price List Item must reference a specific SKU to apply pricing; required for price calculation, catalog sync, and pricing audit reports.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Location-based price list items require warehouse node to apply inventory‑aware pricing per fulfillment center.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who performed the last audit action (e.g., pricing_manager).']
  base_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard unit price before any tiered, volume, or promotional adjustments.']
  bundle_component_skus varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKUs that constitute the bundle.']
  bundle_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the total of bundled SKUs when price_type = \'bundle\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list item record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this price becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this price expires; null for open‑ended pricing.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price is part of a promotional campaign.']
  last_price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price modification.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for this price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining why the price was changed.']
  price_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the pricing rule or context.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(5,3) [note: 'Calculated elasticity metric used for dynamic pricing decisions.']
  price_list_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list item.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price value (e.g., manually entered, generated by pricing algorithm, or imported from external feed).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing structure applied to this item.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'End date of the promotional pricing period.']
  promotional_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the promotional pricing period.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'True if the base_price already includes applicable taxes.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage; used when tax_inclusive = false.']
  tier_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price applied when the ordered quantity meets the tier_quantity_threshold.']
  tier_quantity_threshold varchar [note: 'Quantity breakpoint for tiered pricing; applicable when price_type = \'tiered\' or \'volume\'.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the price (e.g., each, kg, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list item.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control and change tracking.']
  volume_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied for volume purchases; used when price_type = \'volume\'.']

  Note: 'Line-level entry within a price list associating a specific SKU or product variant to its base unit price, minimum order quantity (MOQ), unit of measure, and pricing structure type (flat, tiered, volume-based, bundle). For tiered/volume pricing, captures quantity thresholds and per-tier unit prices. For bundle pricing, captures constituent SKUs and bundle discount. Serves as the authoritative base price per SKU per price list supporting all pricing structure variants.'
}

Table pricing.dynamic_price_rule {
  dynamic_price_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the dynamic pricing rule.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules may be scoped to a specific carrier (e.g., discounts for a carrier); the FK supports rule evaluation per carrier.']
  content_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated A/B test experiment, if any.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace channel (e.g., third‑party marketplace) the rule applies to.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: dynamic_price_rule is defined for a specific price list; linking provides parent context']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller to which the rule is scoped (if seller‑specific).']
  activation_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule expires or is deactivated.']
  activation_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule becomes effective.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the rule adjusts price by a percentage, a fixed amount, sets a floor, or sets a ceiling.']
  adjustment_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the adjustment value, matching the adjustment_type (percentage or currency).']
  adjustment_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value used with the adjustment_type (e.g., 5.0 for 5% or 2.50 for $2.50).']
  applicable_sku_pattern varchar [note: 'Wildcard or regex pattern that matches SKUs to which the rule applies.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from auditors or reviewers about the rule.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the brand whose products the rule may affect.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product category targeted by the rule.']
  ceiling_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum price that the rule will not allow the SKU to exceed.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule complies with MAP and other regulatory pricing constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for price adjustments.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the rule has been applied to transactions.']
  floor_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum price that the rule will not allow the SKU to drop below.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is currently enabled for execution.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'Marks the rule as part of an A/B pricing experiment.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies across all marketplaces and sellers.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information or comments about the rule.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pre‑computed elasticity metric used by the rule engine to gauge demand sensitivity.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules apply; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the rule\'s purpose.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing rule for identification.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for the rule\'s lifecycle.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is calculated in real‑time (dynamic), pre‑computed (static), or a combination (hybrid).']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines the applicability level of the rule (entire platform, specific seller, product category, brand, or marketplace).']
  test_group varchar [note: 'Indicates the A/B test group assignment for the rule.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Serialized condition expression (e.g., JSON) that determines when the rule fires, based on demand, inventory, competitor price, etc.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the rule for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines algorithmic or rules-based dynamic pricing logic applied to SKUs, categories, or seller listings across platform-owned and marketplace channels. Includes trigger conditions (demand signals, inventory levels, competitor price thresholds, MAP violations, seller compliance triggers), adjustment type (percentage, fixed, floor/ceiling), rule scope (platform-wide, seller-specific, category-level, brand-level), priority rank, activation schedule, and enforcement actions. For marketplace-scoped rules: MAP enforcement thresholds, maximum price caps, commission-adjusted floor prices, seller-specific pricing agreements, and brand protection policies. Powers real-time price optimization, surge/markdown automation, MAP enforcement, marketplace pricing integrity, and seller pricing governance.'
}

Table pricing.markdown_schedule {
  markdown_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the markdown schedule.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Markdown schedules are often executed as part of a marketing campaign; linking enables campaign‑level markdown effectiveness analysis.']
  content_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the A/B test that validates the markdown effectiveness.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: markdown_schedule applies to a price list; linking ties schedule to its governing list']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Sellers create markdown schedules for their products; linking to seller_profile ties schedule ownership for audit and reporting.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Warehouse-level markdown schedule clears overstock by applying discounts specific to each fulfillment node.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the markdown schedule.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule received formal approval.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel(s) where the markdown is applied.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Regulatory or policy remarks associated with the markdown (e.g., price‑floor constraints).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the markdown schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the markdown amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Targeted customer segment for the markdown.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the markdown ceases to be applicable (nullable for open‑ended schedules).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the markdown becomes applicable.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule should block other overlapping markdowns for the same SKU.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is a test (non‑production) run.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the schedule.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the markdown schedule.']
  markdown_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule.']
  markdown_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the markdown is a percentage reduction or an absolute monetary amount.']
  markdown_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The reduction amount: either a percentage (e.g., 0.15 for 15%) or a fixed currency amount, depending on markdown_type.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region for which the markdown is valid.']
  max_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum purchase quantity for which the markdown is valid (optional).']
  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity required for the markdown to apply.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Execution priority when multiple schedules overlap; lower numbers run first.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category or department of the product (e.g., electronics, apparel).']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product to which the markdown applies.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the markdown (e.g., end‑of‑season, clearance, overstock).']
  run_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the schedule has been applied to orders.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Specific sales channel where the markdown is offered.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible code used to reference the schedule in external systems.']
  schedule_description varchar [note: 'Extended free‑text description of the markdown schedule.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the markdown schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the schedule for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the schedule.']

  Note: 'Planned markdown calendar for SKUs or product categories, specifying markdown percentage or absolute reduction, start and end dates, markdown reason (end-of-season, clearance, overstock), and approval status. Supports seasonal clearance cycles and inventory liquidation workflows.'
}

Table pricing.promotional_campaign {
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the promotional campaign.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Campaign expenses and discount liabilities must be recorded in a specific GL account for expense reporting and audit compliance.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Integrated promotion management ties a pricing promotional campaign to the marketing campaign that launches it, needed for unified performance dashboards.']
  oos_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.oos_event. Business justification: Out‑of‑stock event driven promotional campaign recovers lost sales by targeting affected SKUs.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Campaign budgeting, performance dashboards, and compliance reports need to know which seller launched the campaign.']
  a_b_test_variant varchar [note: 'Variant assignment for A/B test campaigns.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary budget allocated for the campaign.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the budget currency.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique code for the campaign used in reporting and external communications.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign based on its business purpose.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the campaign is delivered.']
  compliance_gdpr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign processes personal data subject to GDPR.']
  compliance_pci_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign involves payment card data subject to PCI DSS.']
  compliance_sox_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign impacts financial reporting subject to SOX.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code customers can enter to redeem the promotion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanic used to calculate the discount.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount (percentage or fixed amount).']
  discount_value_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the discount value – either percent or currency.']
  eligibility_rule_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the rules that determine customer eligibility.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign expires or is deactivated.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the campaign.']
  gmv_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target GMV the campaign aims to generate.']
  gmv_target_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the GMV target.']
  is_a_b_test boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign is part of an A/B testing experiment.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign is a test (non‑production) run.']
  max_uses varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the campaign can be applied across all customers.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the campaign (e.g., marketing, pricing).']
  product_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product categories the campaign applies to. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electronics|fashion|home|beauty|sports|toys|automotive|grocery|health|books|music|software|other — promote to reference product]']
  promotional_campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign objectives, mechanics and any special notes.']
  promotional_campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotional campaign.']
  promotional_campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  redemption_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer may redeem the campaign.']
  redemption_total_limit varchar [note: 'Overall cap on the number of redemptions across all customers.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code representing the region when geographic_scope is regional or local.']
  sku_inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Rule expression defining which SKUs are eligible for the promotion.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign becomes active for customers.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Segment of customers targeted by the campaign.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the campaign record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Running total of how many times the campaign has been used.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a pricing-driven promotional campaign (e.g., flash sale, seasonal event, loyalty reward event), capturing campaign name, type, budget cap, GMV target, start/end timestamps, eligibility rules, and owning business unit. Acts as the parent entity for all promotional mechanics and discount structures within a campaign.'
}

Table pricing.promotion_rule {
  promotion_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion rule.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: promotion_rule belongs to a promotional campaign; linking enables campaign-level rule management']
  applies_to_brand varchar [note: 'Brand identifier(s) for which the rule is valid.']
  applies_to_bundle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule applies to product bundles rather than individual SKUs.']
  applies_to_category varchar [note: 'Category code(s) to which the rule is limited.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel(s) where the promotion is available.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that customers must enter to trigger the rule, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion rule record was first created.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Targeted customer segment for the rule.']
  discount_unit varchar [note: 'Indicates whether discount_value is a percentage or a monetary amount.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount; interpretation depends on discount_unit (e.g., 10.00 for $10 off or 15.0 for 15% off).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the promotion rule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the promotion rule expires (null for open‑ended).']
  eligible_sku_criteria varchar [note: 'Expression or list defining which SKUs qualify for the promotion (e.g., SKU prefixes, category codes).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'If true, the rule cannot be combined with any other promotion.']
  max_redemptions_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can use the rule.']
  max_redemptions_total varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the rule can be redeemed across all customers.']
  minimum_cart_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal required for the rule to apply.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple stackable rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  promotion_rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code or SKU‑like identifier that uniquely identifies the rule in external systems.']
  promotion_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the promotion rule and its business intent.']
  promotion_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotion rule used for display and reporting.']
  promotion_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current count of how many times the rule has been redeemed.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanic (e.g., Buy‑One‑Get‑One, percentage off, fixed amount off, free shipping, bundle deal).']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule can be combined with other promotions.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific discount mechanics within a promotional campaign, including rule type (BOGO, percentage off, fixed amount off, free shipping, bundle deal), qualifying conditions (minimum cart value, eligible SKUs, customer segment, channel), discount value, stacking priority, and maximum redemption limits.'
}

Table pricing.coupon {
  coupon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each coupon record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated or is associated with the coupon.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Coupon Landing Page: links a coupon to a content page that explains terms and redemption instructions, required for customer communication and audit.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric identifier used by customers to redeem the discount.']
  coupon_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the coupon purpose and terms.']
  coupon_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the coupon.']
  coupon_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coupon instrument (e.g., public coupon, targeted voucher, batch‑issued code, gift card).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the coupon record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for monetary fields.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary value subtracted from the order total when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value applied to the order total when discount_type is percentage.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism of the discount applied by the coupon.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the coupon code was delivered to the customer.']
  eligible_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product category identifiers for which the coupon is valid.']
  eligible_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment (e.g., new_user, high_value, churn_risk) that qualifies for the coupon.']
  eligible_product_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list or pattern of product identifiers (SKU, ASIN, etc.) that the coupon can be applied to.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the coupon can no longer be redeemed.']
  is_one_time_use boolean [note: 'True if the coupon may be redeemed only once across all customers.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coupon can be combined with other promotions.']
  issuance_channel varchar [note: 'System or process that generated the coupon (e.g., campaign engine, manual upload).']
  max_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper cap on the monetary value that can be deducted by the coupon.']
  min_order_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal required for the coupon to be applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current count of how many times the coupon has been redeemed.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the coupon becomes valid for redemption.']
  total_redemption_cap varchar [note: 'Overall maximum number of redemptions allowed across all customers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the coupon record.']
  usage_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer may redeem this coupon.']

  Note: 'Master record for all discount code instruments including public promotional coupons, customer-specific vouchers, batch-issued codes, and gift card discount codes. Captures code identifier, code type (public coupon, targeted voucher, batch-issued, gift card), discount type (percentage, fixed amount, free shipping), discount value, minimum order threshold, maximum discount cap, eligible product/category scope, customer segment eligibility, distribution method, usage limit per customer, total redemption cap, expiry date, issuance channel, and linked campaign. For voucher-type codes: tracks individual issuance records with customer assignment, face value, issuance date, redemption status, and linked loyalty program. Serves as the SSOT for all discount code lifecycle management across all code types.'
}

Table pricing.coupon_redemption {
  coupon_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each coupon redemption event.']
  coupon_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the coupon definition.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who redeemed the coupon.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the coupon redemption.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign linked to the coupon.']
  applied_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU that the coupon discount covered.']
  applied_to_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product to which the coupon discount was applied.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the coupon was redeemed (e.g., web, mobile app, in‑store, call center).']
  coupon_redemption_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the redemption attempt: success, failure, or partial.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied by the coupon.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the redemption was flagged for potential fraud.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information or comments about the redemption.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'The coupon or voucher code that was redeemed.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the coupon was redeemed.']
  redemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the coupon can be used: single‑use, multi‑use, or auto‑apply.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that generated the redemption record (e.g., OMS, PIM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the redemption record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each coupon or voucher redemption event, including the redeemed code, order reference, customer reference, discount amount applied, redemption timestamp, channel, and redemption outcome (success, failure, partial). Provides the audit trail for all discount code usage and supports fraud detection and budget tracking.'
}

Table pricing.price_override {
  price_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the price override record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who approved the price override.']
  audit_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent audit action on the record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the override is scoped, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Price overrides affect revenue; finance requires a GL posting to reflect adjusted sales amounts and maintain audit trail of overrides.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the price override.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific order line affected by the override.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: price_override overrides a specific price list item; linking eliminates redundant sku column']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price override took effect.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow for the price override.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the override was approved by the designated approver.']
  audit_note varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text note added during audit actions.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the override complies with pricing governance policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the override record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the overridden price becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the overridden price expires; null if indefinite.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price override is currently active (true) or has been superseded/revoked (false).']
  original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The price of the SKU before the override was applied.']
  overridden_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The new price after the override was applied.']
  override_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price override was applied manually, by an automated system, or via a pricing rule.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute difference between overridden price and original price.']
  price_change_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change represented by the price override.']
  price_override_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price override.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the price override was applied.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the override reason.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity of the override – whether it applies to the whole order, a single line, a specific customer, or globally.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the override record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the override record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a manual or system-initiated price override applied to a specific order line or SKU, capturing the original price, overridden price, override reason code, approving user, approval timestamp, and override scope (order-level, line-level, customer-specific). Supports exception pricing workflows and audit compliance.'
}

Table pricing.price_history {
  price_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price change event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Internal user identifier who manually approved or overrode the price change.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B testing experiment that caused the price adjustment, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Historical price changes are linked to GL for audit of revenue impact and compliance with financial reporting standards.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion or coupon that triggered the price change, if applicable.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price list (e.g., base, promotional, regional) that the price belongs to.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price History records must be tied to a SKU to support price audit, margin analysis, and regulatory reporting.']
  snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.snapshot. Business justification: Price change audit report links price history to inventory snapshot for stock level context at time of change.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation or business justification for the price modification.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the price values.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment when the new price became effective in the marketplace.']
  initiated_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or service (e.g., pricing engine, rule engine) that generated the change.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price change was manually overridden (true) or system‑driven (false).']
  price_after decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price value after the change was applied.']
  price_before decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price value before the change was applied.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between new and previous price (price_after - price_before).']
  price_change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change mechanism that caused the price update.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record (e.g., correction of metadata).']

  Note: 'Immutable append-only log of all price changes for each SKU across all price lists, capturing previous price, new price, effective timestamp, change source (dynamic rule, manual override, markdown, promotion, experiment adoption), triggering entity reference, and the user or system that initiated the change. Supports price elasticity computation, regulatory compliance (price display laws, consumer protection), competitive analysis, rollback capability, and historical price auditing for dispute resolution.'
}

Table pricing.competitor_price {
  competitor_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each competitor price observation record.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Internal product identifier (SKU) that the competitor price is matched to.']
  competitor_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the competitor providing the price.']
  competitor_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used by the competitor (e.g., SKU, ASIN) for the product whose price is observed.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: competitor_price is compared against a price list; linking associates it with the relevant list']
  competitor_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price observation record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the observation record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the observed price.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data: web scraping, third‑party feed, or manual entry.']
  match_confidence decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence percentage (0‑100) that the competitor product correctly matches our internal product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the price observation.']
  observed_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price value captured from the competitor at the observation time.']
  observed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the competitor price was captured.']
  price_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the observed price differs from the previous capture for the same product and competitor.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the observed price: list price, sale price, or discounted price.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the competitor price was observed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the observation record.']

  Note: 'Stores competitive price intelligence data captured via web scraping, third-party data feeds, or manual entry. Records competitor name, competitor SKU or product identifier, observed price, currency, observed timestamp, data source, and match confidence score. Feeds dynamic pricing rules and competitive positioning decisions.'
}

Table pricing.price_experiment {
  price_experiment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing experiment record.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: price_experiment runs against a specific price list; linking ties experiment to its list']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External reference code used by business teams to identify the experiment.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the experiment (e.g., pricing_experiment).']
  control_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Baseline price used for the control group in the experiment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the experiment record was first created.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of customers (e.g., new, high‑value) allocated to the experiment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the experiment becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the experiment ends or is retired.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the pricing experiment for reporting and analysis.']
  experiment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the experiment design: A/B test or multivariate test.']
  final_recommendation varchar [note: 'Business decision after experiment conclusion.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'Statement of the expected impact of the pricing change (e.g., increase conversion rate).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the experiment.']
  success_metric_aov varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to evaluate experiment success for average order value.']
  success_metric_cvr varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to evaluate experiment success for conversion.']
  success_metric_gmv varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to evaluate experiment success for GMV.']
  target_category varchar [note: 'Product category (or hierarchy node) that the experiment applies to.']
  target_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers included in the experiment.']
  traffic_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total traffic assigned to each variant (control and variants).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the experiment record.']
  variant_1_confidence_interval varchar [note: 'Confidence interval for the primary metric of variant 1 (e.g., 95% CI).']
  variant_1_gmv_lift decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage lift in GMV for variant 1 relative to control.']
  variant_1_observed_aov decimal(12,2) [note: 'Observed average order value for variant 1.']
  variant_1_observed_cvr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Observed conversion rate for variant 1 during the experiment.']
  variant_1_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price offered to the first experimental variant.']
  variant_1_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of unique customers exposed to variant 1.']
  variant_1_stat_sig_level decimal(4,3) [note: 'Statistical significance level (p‑value) for variant 1 results.']
  variant_2_confidence_interval varchar [note: 'Confidence interval for the primary metric of variant 2.']
  variant_2_gmv_lift decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage lift in GMV for variant 2 relative to control.']
  variant_2_observed_aov decimal(12,2) [note: 'Observed average order value for variant 2.']
  variant_2_observed_cvr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Observed conversion rate for variant 2 during the experiment.']
  variant_2_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price offered to the second experimental variant.']
  variant_2_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of unique customers exposed to variant 2.']
  variant_2_stat_sig_level decimal(4,3) [note: 'Statistical significance level (p‑value) for variant 2 results.']

  Note: 'Master record for an A/B or multivariate pricing experiment, capturing the full experiment lifecycle from design through conclusion and outcome analysis. Includes experiment name, hypothesis, control price, variant prices, target SKUs or categories, customer segment allocation, traffic split percentage, start/end dates, success metrics (CVR, AOV, GMV), and experiment status. Stores variant-level outcome data: observed conversion rate, average order value, GMV lift, statistical significance level, sample size, confidence interval, and final recommendation (adopt, reject, extend) per variant. Serves as the SSOT for pricing experimentation configuration, execution, and results.'
}

Table pricing.price_approval {
  price_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price approval record.']
  approver_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the employee or role authorized to approve or reject the request.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: price_approval tracks approval of changes to a price list; linking provides direct reference']
  affected_entity_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific product, category, seller, or order impacted by the price change.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of business object whose price is being changed.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the price change request for tracking and audit.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the request was approved.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded chronological log of actions, comments, and status changes for the approval record.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of the pricing modification being requested.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form notes added by requester, approver, or reviewers.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates any regulatory or internal compliance considerations attached to the price change.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the price values.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved price becomes active in the catalog or system.']
  escalation_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the request has been escalated to higher authority.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent escalation event.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Optional date when the approved price reverts or expires.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business reason behind the price modification.']
  original_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Current price before the proposed change, expressed in the applicable currency.']
  price_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price approval request.']
  price_difference decimal(12,4) [note: 'Calculated delta between proposed and original price (positive for increase, negative for decrease).']
  proposed_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'New price being requested, expressed in the applicable currency.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the approval record.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text field capturing why the request was denied.']
  rejection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the request was rejected.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the price change was submitted for approval.']
  sla_deadline timestamp [note: 'Target date‑time by which the approval decision must be completed to meet internal SLA.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval was completed within the defined SLA window.']

  Note: 'Workflow record tracking the approval lifecycle for price changes, new dynamic pricing rules, markdowns, or promotional discounts that exceed defined monetary or percentage thresholds. Captures the requested change type, affected entity reference, requester, designated approver(s), approval status (pending, approved, rejected, escalated), approval/rejection timestamp, rejection reason, escalation history, and SLA compliance. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance, internal controls, and segregation of duties requirements.'
}

Table pricing.price_zone {
  price_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing zone.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Price zones are defined per marketing channel to support channel‑specific pricing; the link is required for channel pricing compliance reports.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pricing zones are budgeted to specific cost centers; linking enables cost allocation reports and variance analysis per geographic/segment zone.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Pricing zones are derived from delivery zones; the FK lets the Pricing engine fetch zone attributes (e.g., distance, surcharge) for accurate shipping cost calculations.']
  parent_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_zone (parent_price_zone_id)']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the base price list associated with this zone.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Geographic price zones are subject to local regulations; FK supports zone‑specific compliance monitoring.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Price zone mapping to warehouse node enables region‑specific pricing based on fulfillment location.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) to which the zone applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for pricing within this zone.']
  customer_segment_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria defining which customer segments belong to this zone (e.g., loyalty tier, RFM segment).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing zone becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing zone expires or is superseded.']
  geographic_boundary varchar [note: 'Geospatial definition of the zone boundaries in GeoJSON format.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is the default fallback zone.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'Indicates if the zone is part of an experimental pricing strategy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes about the zone.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Score representing price sensitivity for the zone.']
  price_zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing zone purpose and scope.']
  price_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing zone.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating priority order when multiple zones match; lower number higher priority.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary region of the zone.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the zone record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the pricing zone.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the pricing zone.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the zone record.']

  Note: 'Geographic or customer-segment-based pricing zone definitions that determine which price list applies to a given buyer. Captures zone code, zone name, geographic boundaries, customer segment criteria, and effective date ranges.'
}

Table pricing.price_zone_assignment {
  price_zone_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the price_zone_assignment association']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to price_zone']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller_profile']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the seller\'s assignment to the price zone']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the seller\'s assignment to the price zone']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of a seller_profile to a price_zone. It captures the effective period during which a seller is eligible for the pricing rules of a specific zone.. Existence Justification: A seller can be linked to multiple price zones based on geography, customer segment, and effective dates, and each price zone can include many sellers. The assignment of a seller to a zone is managed as a distinct record with start and end dates, reflecting the business process of pricing eligibility.'
}

Table pricing.pricing_price_list_assignment {
  pricing_price_list_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the price_list_assignment association']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fulfillment center']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the price list']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price list expires for the center (null for open‑ended)']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price list becomes active for the center']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this price list is the default for the center']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority order when multiple price lists apply to the same center']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of a price list to a fulfillment center. It captures the effective period, priority, and default flag for each center‑price list pairing, enabling the business to manage which pricing rules apply where and when.. Existence Justification: A fulfillment center can be associated with multiple price lists (e.g., regional, promotional, channel‑specific) and a single price list can be applied to many centers across the network. The business actively manages these assignments, tracking effective dates, priority, and default status for each center‑price list pairing.'
}

Table pricing.pricing_agreement {
  pricing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the PricingAgreement association']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the internal price list']
  supplier_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier price list']
  discount_percentage decimal [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the supplier list price']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Agreed supplier lead time in days for this price list']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the unit price is gross list price or net after discounts']
  price_list_version varchar [note: 'Version of the supplier price list applicable to this agreement']

  Note: 'This association represents the contractual mapping between an internal price list and a supplier price list. It captures attributes that are specific to the relationship, such as the price basis, discount percentage, lead time, and versioning information.. Existence Justification: An internal price list aggregates multiple supplier price lists, and a given supplier price list can be used in multiple internal price lists. The mapping is actively managed by pricing and procurement teams and carries its own attributes such as discount, lead time, and price basis, making the relationship a distinct business entity.'
}

Table pricing.competitor {
  competitor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competitor']
  parent_competitor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor (parent_competitor_id)']
  average_price_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average listed price of the competitor\'s products expressed in US dollars.']
  competitor_rank varchar [note: 'Relative ranking of the competitor within the market.']
  competitor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitor based on its business model.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the competitor\'s pricing practices.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for contacting the competitor.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting the competitor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competitor record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for the competitor\'s pricing data.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score representing the overall quality and reliability of the competitor data.']
  is_price_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competitor\'s pricing is considered highly sensitive for competitive analysis.']
  last_price_update_date date [note: 'Date when the competitor\'s price information was last refreshed.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the competitor primarily operates.']
  market_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated market share of the competitor expressed as a percentage.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal or commonly used name of the competitor organization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the competitor.']
  price_update_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the competitor updates its pricing.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Typical pricing approach used by the competitor.']
  primary_product_category varchar [note: 'Main product category the competitor focuses on.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the competitor data.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competitor record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competitor record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the competitor.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for competitor. Referenced by competitor_id.'
}

Table pricing.competitor_product {
  competitor_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competitor_product']
  competitor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the competitor offering this product.']
  superseded_competitor_product_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor_product (superseded_competitor_product_id)']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current stock availability of the competitor product.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand name associated with the product.']
  bundle_components varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of component product codes when the product is a bundle.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory or safety certifications held by the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competitor product record was first created in the data lake.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the product features and specifications.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions (L×W×H) in centimeters, stored as a formatted string.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the list price, if any.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the primary product image.']
  is_bundle boolean [note: 'True if the product is sold as part of a bundle offering.']
  last_price_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price information was last refreshed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the product record.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region where the competitor product is sold.']
  price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current list price of the competitor product in the listed currency.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or free‑text explaining why the price changed.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the listed price became effective.']
  price_end_date date [note: 'Date when the listed price is scheduled to expire or be superseded.']
  price_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated margin percentage relative to internal cost (stored for reference, not a derived KPI).']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the price data was obtained.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the product as defined by the competitor.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'External catalog or SKU code used by the competitor to identify the product.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the competitor\'s product.']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular sub‑category within the main product category.']
  product_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the product detail page on the competitor site.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is currently part of a promotional campaign.']
  rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average customer rating on a 5‑point scale as reported by the competitor.']
  return_policy varchar [note: 'Textual description of the competitor\'s return policy for the product.']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer reviews submitted for the product.']
  shipping_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Weight of the product used for shipping calculations, expressed in kilograms.']
  source_marketplace varchar [note: 'Name of the marketplace or platform where the competitor lists the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of the manufacturer warranty in months.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for competitor_product. Referenced by competitor_product_id.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign (primary key).']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: REQUIRED: Campaigns are created per brand; brand‑level ROI reports need a direct link to product.brand.brand_id.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Tracks which carrier is featured in a shipping‑promotion campaign, supporting cost analysis and carrier‑specific promotional reporting.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Category‑targeted campaigns need to reference product_category.product_category_id for category performance dashboards.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Campaign uses a textual channel; replace with FK to channel for referential integrity and remove redundant column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Campaign Cost Allocation Report linking each campaign to the cost center that bears its expenses.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Supports GL posting of campaign‑related expenses for statutory accounting and audit trails.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Campaigns are executed to satisfy a primary compliance obligation (e.g., GDPR data‑processing) and need that reference.']
  platform_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external advertising platform (e.g., Google Ads ID).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables Profitability Dashboard by attributing campaign revenue and costs to the appropriate profit center.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: PROMO MATERIAL PROCUREMENT: links a campaign to the PO that purchases printed or physical assets needed for the campaign execution.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: SUPPLIER-SPONSORED CAMPAIGN: tracks which supplier funds each marketing campaign for compliance, ROI attribution, and budget approval.']
  target_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Campaign targets an audience segment; replace string with FK to audience_segment and remove redundant column.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Regional campaign fulfillment planning report requires linking each campaign to the warehouse node that will serve its target geography.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Creative format used in the campaign.']
  ad_group_hierarchy varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded representation of nested ad group structure for paid media.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Model used to attribute conversions to campaign interactions.']
  bidding_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithmic approach used to bid for ad placements.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Brand under which the campaign is executed.']
  budget_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of discounts, fees, or other adjustments applied to the gross budget.']
  budget_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget before any adjustments.']
  budget_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final usable budget after adjustments.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign purpose, messaging, and creative theme.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of campaign based on delivery mechanism.']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clicks that resulted in a conversion.']
  cost_per_acquisition decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each conversion.']
  cost_per_click decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each click.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign record was created.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary creative asset associated with the campaign.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for the budget.']
  daily_budget_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum spend allowed per day.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date or expiration of the campaign.']
  expected_roi decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected ROI for the campaign based on budget and performance forecasts.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is a test or production run.']
  lifetime_budget_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total spend allowed for the entire campaign lifecycle.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal the campaign is intended to achieve.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Product line or category the campaign promotes.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the campaign.']
  target_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) targeted by the campaign (e.g., country codes).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter indicating the marketing medium.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter for tracking traffic origin.']

  Note: 'Master record for all marketing campaigns across owned (email, push, SMS), paid (SEM, display, social, shopping, video), and earned (referral, affiliate, organic social) channels. Captures campaign identity, type, channel, objective (acquisition, retention, upsell, brand awareness), budget allocation, flight dates, target audience segment, campaign owner, status lifecycle, UTM parameters, bidding strategy, daily/lifetime budget caps, target geography, platform-specific IDs, ad group hierarchy (as nested structure for paid media), ad format, and associated brand or product line. SSOT for all campaign definitions — performance, attribution, budget, and creative records reference this master. For paid media campaigns, includes ad group-level detail (keyword themes, audience targeting, bid amounts, group status) as child records within the campaign structure.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_performance {
  campaign_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each campaign performance record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign performance records belong to a campaign; add FK and remove duplicated campaign descriptive fields.']
  aov decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average monetary value of orders generated from the campaign.']
  audience_size varchar [note: 'Number of unique contacts targeted by the campaign.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch job that executed the campaign.']
  bounce_count varchar [note: 'Number of messages that bounced back undelivered.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated monetary budget for the campaign.']
  cac decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average cost to acquire a new customer through this campaign.']
  campaign_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective the campaign aims to achieve (e.g., drive traffic, increase sales, boost engagement).']
  campaign_performance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign execution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the campaign was delivered to the audience.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Number of click actions generated by the campaign.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Number of desired conversion events (e.g., purchases, sign‑ups) attributed to the campaign.']
  cpc decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each click (spend_amount ÷ clicks).']
  cpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per one thousand impressions (spend_amount ÷ (impressions/1000)).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance record was first created in the data lake.']
  ctr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of clicks to impressions (clicks ÷ impressions).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  cvr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of conversions to clicks (conversions ÷ clicks).']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Overall delivery outcome for the campaign batch.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount or rebate applied to the campaign spend.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the campaign content was displayed.']
  marketing_owner_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal marketing owner or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Revenue attributed to the campaign after discounts and adjustments.']
  platform_job_reference varchar [note: 'Reference ID from the marketing platform for the execution job.']
  roas decimal(10,4) [note: 'Revenue generated per unit of spend (net_revenue ÷ spend_amount).']
  rpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Revenue generated per one thousand impressions (net_revenue ÷ (impressions/1000)).']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time for the campaign execution.']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time for the campaign execution.']
  send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign was sent or activated.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary spend on the campaign (gross).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this performance record.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing each campaign deployment event and its resulting performance metrics. Combines execution metadata (send date/time, channel, audience size, delivery status, bounce count, batch ID, platform job reference) with engagement metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, CVR, spend, revenue attributed, ROAS, CPC, CPM, RPM, CAC, AOV). One record per campaign activation per reporting period. Supports real-time campaign monitoring, ROI analysis, and budget optimization. Subsumes former campaign_execution concept.'
}

Table marketing.audience_segment {
  audience_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audience segment record.']
  audience_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or market scope of the segment.']
  audience_segment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the segment, unique within the marketing domain.']
  audience_segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the segment purpose, business rationale, and target audience.']
  audience_segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the segment used for reporting and UI display.']
  audience_segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  audience_segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment based on its creation logic (e.g., demographic, behavioral, predictive).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channel(s) for which the segment is intended.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created.']
  creation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment was created manually by a marketer or generated automatically by a model.']
  definition_logic varchar [note: 'Serialized logic (e.g., JSON, SQL, rule engine syntax) that defines inclusion criteria for the segment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the segment becomes active for targeting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the segment is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Human‑readable summary of rules that exclude customers from the segment.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Human‑readable summary of rules that include customers in the segment.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'True if the segment membership updates continuously; false if static snapshot.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the segment is used for A/B testing or experimentation.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent membership refresh operation.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of customers included in the segment.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the segment owner.']
  owner_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing user or team that owns the segment.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business objective of the segment (e.g., acquisition, retention, upsell, testing).']
