  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the segment membership is recomputed.']
  region varchar [note: 'Region code (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3) where the segment is applicable.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the segment definition is retained before archival.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or data source used to build the segment.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of free‑form tags for ad‑hoc categorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any segment attribute.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the segment definition.']

  Note: 'Master record for reusable customer audience segments including membership composition. Captures segment definition (name, type, logic, refresh frequency, owner, status) and per-member association records (customer ID, membership dates, inclusion reason, membership status). Enables historical tracking of segment composition changes, CLTV-based cohort analysis, and real-time personalization decisioning. SSOT for marketing audience definitions and their evaluated membership.'
}

Table marketing.creative_asset {
  creative_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for creative_asset']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that uses this creative asset.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Each creative asset must be linked to the regulation it complies with (spam, privacy) for compliance verification.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the asset.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset received approval.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the creative asset such as email_template, ad_text, ad_image, ad_video, ad_carousel, push, sms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email_template|ad_text|ad_image|ad_video|ad_carousel|push|sms — promote to reference product]']
  body_content varchar [note: 'Main body copy or description of the creative asset.']
  compliance_can_spam boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset complies with CAN‑SPAM regulations.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset complies with GDPR data‑privacy requirements.']
  content_block_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of content block identifiers used within the asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was created.']
  creative_asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the creative asset.']
  creative_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the asset.']
  destination_url varchar [note: 'Target URL where the user is directed after clicking the creative.']
  display_url varchar [note: 'URL shown to users in the creative (e.g., in email footer).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the asset in seconds (applicable to video or audio).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the asset should no longer be used.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., DAM, CMS) to reference the asset.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format or rendering type of the asset.']
  headline varchar [note: 'Primary headline text for ad creatives.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (e.g., en_US, fr_FR) indicating the language of the asset.']
  media_url varchar [note: 'URL to the image, video, or other media file associated with the asset.']
  personalization_tokens varchar [note: 'Tokens/placeholders for dynamic personalization (e.g., {{first_name}}).']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the target marketing platform (e.g., SFMC, Google Ads).']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Subject line used when the asset is an email template.']
  template_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the creative template.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all marketing creative assets across email templates, ad creatives (text, image, video, carousel, responsive), push notification content, and SMS templates. Captures asset name, format type (email_template, ad_text, ad_image, ad_video, ad_carousel, push, sms), subject line/headline, body content/description, display URL, destination URL, media references (image/video URLs), content block references, personalization tokens, language/locale, compliance flags (CAN-SPAM, GDPR), approval status, platform-specific IDs, template version, and active status. SSOT for all creative content deployed in campaign execution across owned and paid channels. Supports creative performance comparison and A/B test variant tracking.'
}

Table marketing.email_event {
  email_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the email interaction event.']
  campaign_execution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign execution that generated the email.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the email message within the marketing platform.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who received the email.']
  email_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the email template used to generate the message.']
  bounce_category varchar [note: 'Classification of bounce when event_type is bounced.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used to view the email.']
  email_client varchar [note: 'Email client or application used to open the email.']
  email_subject varchar [note: 'Subject line of the email message.']
  event_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the event (e.g., success for delivered/opened, failure for bounce).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the email event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of email interaction event (e.g., sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spam_complaint — promote to reference product]']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device that generated the event.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the email event was generated from a test send.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO 639‑1 code representing the language of the email.']
  link_url varchar [note: 'Destination URL that was clicked in the email (populated for click events).']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code inferred from the IP address.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was ingested into the lakehouse.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the event record (e.g., salesforce_marketing_cloud).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Full user‑agent string reported by the email client/device.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual email interaction events at the recipient level. Tracks event type (sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed, spam complaint), event timestamp, recipient customer ID, campaign execution ID, email message ID, link URL clicked, device type, email client, IP address, and bounce category. Sourced from Salesforce Marketing Cloud tracking extracts. Enables deliverability monitoring and engagement analysis.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_ab_test {
  marketing_ab_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the marketing A/B test record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: A/B test is executed within a campaign; add FK to link test to its campaign.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.experiment. Business justification: Supports cross‑channel AB testing where marketing campaigns are synchronized with search experiments to evaluate combined impact on conversions.']
  confidence_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (percentage) required to declare a winner.']
  control_conversions varchar [note: 'Number of conversions recorded for the control variant.']
  control_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the control variant was shown.']
  control_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue attributed to the control variant.']
  control_variant_name varchar [note: 'Identifier for the control group variant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment was concluded or scheduled to end.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'Business hypothesis or question the experiment is designed to validate.']
  marketing_ab_test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test.']
  metric_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target value for the primary success metric expressed as a percentage or absolute value.']
  primary_success_metric varchar [note: 'Key performance indicator used to evaluate the test (e.g., conversion_rate, revenue_per_visitor).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment became active.']
  test_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the test (e.g., ABT_2024_Q1).']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the experiment used for reporting and UI display.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the experiment: A/B test, multivariate test (MVT), or bandit algorithm.']
  total_conversions varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of conversion events recorded across all variants.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of times any variant was shown to users.']
  total_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to the test across all variants.']
  traffic_split varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited string describing how visitor traffic is allocated across variants (e.g., {"control":50,"variant_a":25,"variant_b":25}).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test record.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct variants (including control) defined for the test.']
  variant_details varchar [note: 'JSON string containing an array of objects with per‑variant metrics (name, allocation_pct, impressions, conversions, revenue, lift_vs_control).']
  winning_variant varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the variant declared as the winner after evaluation.']

  Note: 'Master record for A/B and multivariate tests including variant definitions. Captures test name, hypothesis, test type (A/B, MVT, bandit), start/end dates, traffic split, primary success metric, confidence threshold, status, winning variant, and per-variant detail (variant name, type, allocation percentage, impressions, conversions, revenue, statistical lift vs control). SSOT for all marketing experimentation records and their variant-level results.'
}

Table marketing.attribution_touchpoint {
  attribution_touchpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing touchpoint record.']
  ad_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ad group within the campaign.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the touchpoint.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Attribution touchpoint records a channel; replace string with FK to channel.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with this touchpoint.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the web or app session during which the touchpoint occurred.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model applied to this touchpoint for revenue allocation.']
  attribution_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric score representing the weight of this touchpoint in the chosen attribution model.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser used (e.g., Chrome, Safari).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the conversion event linked to this touchpoint, if any.']
  cost_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Marketing cost associated with this touchpoint.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the customer\'s location at touchpoint time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the touchpoint record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer (web, mobile, tablet).']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the interaction recorded (click, view, conversion, impression).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device generating the touchpoint.']
  is_attributed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the touchpoint contributed to a conversion.']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the touchpoint ultimately led to a conversion.']
  keyword varchar [note: 'Search keyword or term that triggered the ad (if applicable).']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the landing page associated with the touchpoint.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows).']
  page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page where the interaction occurred.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the customer to the touchpoint.']
  revenue_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount attributed to this touchpoint based on the selected model.']
  screen_resolution varchar [note: 'Screen resolution of the device (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the user session ended.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the user session began.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the touchpoint record (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Google Ads).']
  touchpoint_position varchar [note: 'Position of the touchpoint within the customer\'s conversion journey.']
  touchpoint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the marketing touchpoint was recorded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the touchpoint record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser user‑agent string captured for the touchpoint.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter used to differentiate ads or links.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter indicating the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, email).']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter indicating the origin of the traffic.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM term parameter for paid search keywords.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each marketing touchpoint in a customer\'s pre-conversion journey. Tracks customer ID, session ID, touchpoint timestamp, channel (paid search, organic, email, display, affiliate, social, direct), campaign ID, ad group, keyword, UTM source/medium/campaign/content/term, device type, and touchpoint position (first, middle, last). Feeds multi-touch attribution models (linear, time-decay, data-driven) for ROAS and CAC calculation.'
}

Table marketing.keyword {
  keyword_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each keyword record.']
  ad_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.ad_group']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the paid marketing campaign linked to this keyword.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Keyword is associated with a marketing channel; replace string with FK to channel.']
  actual_clicks varchar [note: 'Number of clicks generated by the keyword.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the ad or organic result was displayed for the keyword.']
  actual_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary spend incurred for the keyword in paid campaigns.']
  bid_strategy varchar [note: 'Automated or manual bidding approach applied to the keyword.']
  competition_level varchar [note: 'Relative difficulty to rank or bid for the keyword in paid search.']
  content_page_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SEO content page optimized for the keyword.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of clicks that resulted in a desired conversion (e.g., purchase).']
  conversion_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value attributed to conversions generated by the keyword.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑3 country code indicating the geographic market for the keyword.']
  cpc_bid decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum bid amount set for the keyword in paid campaigns (currency unit).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the keyword record was first created in the system.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Primary device category on which the keyword performance is measured.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the keyword became active in the marketing program.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the keyword is scheduled to be retired or become inactive (nullable).']
  estimated_cpc decimal(10,4) [note: 'Projected average CPC for the keyword based on auction dynamics.']
  estimated_ctr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Projected CTR based on historical data and algorithmic forecasts.']
  keyword_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the keyword purpose.']
  keyword_code varchar [note: 'System-generated alphanumeric code for internal tracking of the keyword.']
  keyword_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the keyword within the marketing strategy.']
  keyword_text varchar [note: 'The literal search term or phrase used in SEO and SEM campaigns.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code representing the language of the keyword.']
  last_bid_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the CPC bid value was most recently adjusted.']
  last_rank_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the organic rank was last refreshed or measured.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Search engine match rule defining how the keyword is interpreted in paid campaigns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the keyword.']
  organic_rank varchar [note: 'Current position of the target URL in organic search results for the keyword.']
  quality_score varchar [note: 'Search engine quality rating (0‑10) reflecting relevance and performance.']
  search_volume varchar [note: 'Average monthly number of searches for the keyword (estimated).']
  seo_difficulty_score varchar [note: 'Internal metric (0‑100) estimating effort required to rank organically.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the keyword data (e.g., search engine query, shopping feed, display network).']
  target_url varchar [note: 'Landing page URL that the keyword is intended to drive traffic to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the keyword record.']

  Note: 'Master record for SEO and SEM keywords managed across organic search optimization and paid search campaigns. Captures keyword text, match type (broad, phrase, exact, negative), search volume, competition level, CPC bid, quality score, SEO difficulty score, current organic rank, target URL, keyword status, associated campaign (for paid), and target content page (for SEO). SSOT for keyword strategy across SEM and SEO functions. Supports bid optimization, keyword gap analysis, and organic ranking tracking.'
}

Table marketing.seo_page {
  seo_page_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SEO page record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: SEO page is often used as a landing page for a campaign; add FK to campaign.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.indexed_document. Business justification: Required for SEO performance reporting linking each SEO page to its indexed document to monitor rankings and traffic.']
  average_position decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean ranking position of the page across all keyword queries.']
  canonical_url varchar [note: 'Canonical link element URL that indicates the preferred version of the page.']
  content_length_bytes varchar [note: 'Total size of the page HTML content in bytes.']
  country_target varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code of the primary market for the page.']
  crawl_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the page in the search engine crawl process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SEO page record was first created in the data lake.']
  ctr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of organic clicks to impressions expressed as a percentage.']
  domain_authority decimal(5,2) [note: 'Authority score of the page\'s domain as measured by third‑party SEO tools.']
  duplicate_content_flag boolean [note: 'True when the page has substantial duplicate content detected.']
  external_page_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external SEO tools or CMS for this page.']
  hreflang_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of hreflang annotations present on the page.']
  index_status varchar [note: 'Whether the page is indexed, excluded, or blocked from search results.']
  is_redirect boolean [note: 'True if the page URL redirects to another URL.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the page content.']
  last_crawled_date date [note: 'Date when the page was most recently crawled by the search engine.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the page within SEO management.']
  meta_description varchar [note: 'Meta description tag content that appears in search result snippets.']
  missing_meta_flag boolean [note: 'True if required SEO meta tags (title, description) are missing.']
  mobile_friendly boolean [note: 'Indicates if the page passes mobile usability criteria.']
  organic_clicks varchar [note: 'Number of clicks the page received from organic search results.']
  organic_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the page appeared in organic search results.']
  page_classification varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the page\'s primary purpose.']
  page_load_time_ms varchar [note: 'Measured page load time in milliseconds, impacting SEO performance.']
  page_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the page (e.g., Product Detail Page, Product Listing Page, blog, landing page, category).']
  redirect_target_url varchar [note: 'Destination URL of the redirect, if applicable.']
  robots_meta varchar [note: 'Value of the robots meta tag controlling indexing and crawling.']
  seo_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite SEO health score derived from multiple signals.']
  sitemap_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the page URL is listed in the XML sitemap.']
  structured_data_present boolean [note: 'True if the page contains valid schema.org structured data.']
  target_keyword_cluster varchar [note: 'Primary keyword group the page is optimized for.']
  title varchar [note: 'HTML title tag content of the page, used by search engines as the headline.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SEO page record.']
  url varchar [note: 'Full web address of the page being tracked for SEO performance.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Number of words in the page\'s visible text.']

  Note: 'Master record for pages tracked under SEO performance management. Captures page URL, page title, meta description, canonical URL, page type (PDP, PLP, blog, landing page, category), target keyword cluster, current organic impressions, clicks, average position, CTR, crawl status, index status, last crawled date, and SEO health flags (missing meta, duplicate content, slow load). Supports SEO optimization workflows and organic traffic growth tracking.'
}

Table marketing.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the lead record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Lead acquisition is tied to a campaign; replace string with FK to campaign and remove redundant column.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Required for Lead‑to‑Customer conversion tracking report; linking a lead to the resulting customer profile is standard in e‑commerce lead management.']
  lead_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales rep responsible for the lead.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Lead capture forms on product pages require linking lead to the specific listing for ROI and conversion funnel analysis.']
  owner_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who owns the lead.']
  source_query_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.query_log. Business justification: Needed for lead attribution report that ties each generated lead to the originating search query for ROI analysis.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel through which the lead was acquired.']
  address varchar [note: 'Full mailing address provided by the lead (if supplied).']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization the lead represents (for B2B leads).']
  content_downloaded varchar [note: 'Identifiers of marketing assets (e.g., whitepapers) the lead has downloaded.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the lead has been converted to a customer, otherwise false.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lead was converted to a customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lead record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for the estimated deal value.']
  demo_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead has requested a product demonstration.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for lead communication.']
  estimated_deal_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the opportunity if the lead converts.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Prospective individual\'s given name.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed at the time of lead capture.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the lead within the company.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent interaction (email open, form submit, etc.) with the lead.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Prospective individual\'s family name.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the lead (e.g., website form, trade show, referral).']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead.']
  lead_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lead is a business (B2B) or consumer (B2C).']
  number_of_visits varchar [note: 'Count of distinct website visits recorded for the lead.']
  origin varchar [note: 'High‑level source classification for the lead.']
  pages_visited varchar [note: 'Total number of unique pages viewed by the lead.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the lead.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating indicating lead quality.']
  score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing lead quality based on predictive model.']
  score_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the scoring algorithm used.']
  score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lead score was last calculated.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Marketing funnel stage the lead is currently in.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text reason explaining the current lead status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the lead record.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter captured at lead capture.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter captured at lead capture.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter captured at lead capture.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter captured at lead capture.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM term parameter captured at lead capture.']

  Note: 'Master record for prospective customers captured through marketing acquisition channels before conversion. Tracks lead source, acquisition channel, acquisition campaign, lead status lifecycle (new, contacted, qualified, converted, disqualified), lead score, contact information, company details (B2B), estimated deal value, CRM lead ID, and engagement activity history (emails opened, forms submitted, pages visited, demos requested, content downloaded, calls logged). Activity records feed lead scoring models and sales handoff workflows. SSOT for top-of-funnel acquisition tracking and CAC calculation.'
}

Table marketing.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the referral record.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Order identifier generated when the referred prospect completes a purchase, linking the referral to revenue.']
  referral_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the existing customer who made the referral.']
  referral_referee_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prospect who was referred (may be null until conversion).']
  source_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated the referral (if applicable).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the referral was shared (e.g., email, social media, direct link, SMS, in‑app).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the referral code is no longer valid.']
  incentive_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the incentive provided to the referee.']
  incentive_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the referee incentive amount.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive offered to the referred prospect (e.g., discount, credit, gift card, cash, loyalty points).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments or special conditions related to the referral.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to track and redeem the referral.']
  referral_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral was created or sent to the prospect.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral.']
  reward_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the reward given to the referrer.']
  reward_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the referrer reward amount.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive granted to the referrer (e.g., discount, store credit, gift card, cash, loyalty points).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the referral record.']

  Note: 'Master record for customer referral program instances where an existing customer refers a new prospect. Captures referrer customer ID, referred prospect identifier, referral code, referral channel (email, social share, link), referral date, referral status (pending, converted, expired, rewarded), conversion order ID, referrer reward type and amount, and referee incentive type and amount. Supports referral program ROI measurement and viral acquisition tracking.'
}

Table marketing.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consent record.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Consent record includes channel information; replace string with FK to channel for consistency.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who gave or withdrew consent.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulation‑specific consent records are required for GDPR/CCPA reporting and audit trails.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the consent capture, indicating how the consent was obtained.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the consent: whether it is granted or withdrawn.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the consent event occurred.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of marketing communication or data usage the consent applies to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the consent becomes effective, if different from the event timestamp.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consent expires, if applicable.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the consent was recorded.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent is currently active (true) or not (false).']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing personal data under GDPR.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes related to the consent event.']
  opt_in_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain consent, indicating whether single or double opt-in was applied.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy or terms in effect at the time of consent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the customer for withdrawing consent.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent was withdrawn, if status is withdrawn.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing customer marketing consent and communication preference changes as required by GDPR, CCPA, CAN-SPAM, and TCPA regulations. Each record represents a single consent event (grant or withdrawal) for a specific consent type. Tracks customer ID, consent type (email marketing, SMS marketing, push notifications, personalization, cross-site tracking, data sharing with partners), consent status (granted, withdrawn), event timestamp, consent source (checkout opt-in, preference center, double opt-in confirmation, import, API), policy version at time of consent, IP address, and legal basis (legitimate interest, explicit consent). SSOT for marketing communication compliance — feeds suppression list generation and audit trail for regulatory inquiries.'
}

Table marketing.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing channel.']
  parent_channel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on channel (parent_channel_id)']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Ownership category of the channel (owned, partner, third‑party).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined unique code for the channel.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel purpose and characteristics.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing channel.']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the channel (e.g., email, SMS, push notification, social media, display advertising, affiliate).']
  compliance_ccpa boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel complies with CCPA requirements.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel complies with GDPR requirements.']
  cost_model varchar [note: 'Pricing model applied to the channel (cost per mille, cost per click, cost per acquisition, fixed fee, subscription).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the channel record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data associated with the channel is retained.']
  default_bid_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Default bid amount used for bidding‑based cost models.']
  default_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard budget allocated for the channel per campaign or period.']
  default_language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code for default content in the channel.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the channel becomes active for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the channel is retired or no longer usable (nullable).']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect to the platform.']
  is_test_channel boolean [note: 'True if the channel is used for testing or sandbox purposes.']
  max_send_rate_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of messages that can be sent through the channel per minute.']
  opt_in_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit customer opt‑in is required to use the channel.']
  owner_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party (team or individual) that owns the channel.']
  performance_benchmark_ctr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target click‑through rate (percentage) used as a benchmark.']
  performance_benchmark_cvr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target conversion rate (percentage) used as a benchmark.']
  performance_benchmark_impressions varchar [note: 'Target number of impressions used as a performance benchmark for the channel.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Underlying platform or service provider used to deliver the channel (e.g., Mailchimp, Twilio, Facebook Ads).']
  privacy_policy_url varchar [note: 'Link to the privacy policy governing data collected via the channel.']
  send_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:mm) when the channel must stop sending messages.']
  send_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:mm) when the channel is allowed to start sending messages.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary audience segment targeted by the channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining marketing channels and their configurations (email, SMS, push notification, social media, display advertising, affiliate). Captures channel type, platform integration details, cost model, and performance benchmarks.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_budget {
  campaign_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign budget record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign to which this budget is allocated.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Budget Allocation Workflow linking each campaign budget record to the cost center responsible for spending.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Allows tracking of budgeted amounts against profit centers for profitability planning and variance analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: BUDGET RECONCILIATION: ties budget records to the PO that fulfills the allocated spend, enabling financial audit of campaign costs.']
  reallocated_from_campaign_budget_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_budget (reallocated_from_campaign_budget_id)']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount allocated to the campaign for the defined period.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate budget funds across channels or tactics.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the budget.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget was approved for execution.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the budget allocation.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget cadence or scope (e.g., daily, lifetime).']
  campaign_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget record.']
  cost_per_acquisition_actual decimal(12,4) [note: 'Realized cost per acquisition calculated from spend and conversions.']
  cost_per_acquisition_target decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned maximum cost per new customer acquisition for this budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in this record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective and can be spent.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the budget expires; null for open‑ended budgets.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget is for testing purposes only.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or justification.']
  owner_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing owner responsible for this budget.']
  remaining_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated amount minus spent amount; represents funds still available.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for changes to the budget record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the budget record.']
  spent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount actually spent against the allocated budget.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking marketing campaign-level budget allocations, spend tracking, and ROI calculations. Captures allocated amount, spent amount, remaining budget, cost-per-acquisition targets, and budget period.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_sku_allocation {
  campaign_sku_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the campaign_sku_allocation association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the campaign']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU']
  allocation_amount decimal [note: 'Monetary amount allocated to the SKU for this campaign']
  discount_percentage decimal [note: 'Discount percentage applied to the SKU within the campaign']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU allocation ends for the campaign']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU allocation becomes active for the campaign']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of a marketing campaign to a specific SKU, capturing the budget amount, discount percentage, and the active period for that allocation. Each record links one SKU to one campaign.. Existence Justification: In the ecommerce business, a marketing campaign can promote many different SKUs, and a single SKU can be featured in multiple campaigns (e.g., seasonal sales, channel‑specific promotions). The relationship is actively managed by marketing teams who allocate budget and discounts per SKU‑campaign pairing, and they track start/end dates for each allocation.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_kpi_assignment {
  campaign_kpi_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the campaign_kpi_assignment association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the campaign']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the KPI definition']
  target_period varchar [note: 'Reporting period for which the target applies']
  target_threshold_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the KPI target is an upper bound, lower bound, or a range']
  target_value decimal [note: 'Numeric target for the KPI within the campaign']

  Note: 'Associates a marketing campaign with a KPI definition and stores the target value, threshold type, and period for that specific campaign‑KPI pair. Each record represents a business‑managed assignment of performance metrics to campaigns.. Existence Justification: Marketing campaigns are linked to multiple KPI definitions, each defining performance targets for the campaign. Conversely, a single KPI definition can be applied to many campaigns. The business actively creates and maintains these links, storing target values and threshold settings for each campaign‑KPI pair.'
}

Table marketing.regulatory_compliance {
  regulatory_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the regulatory_compliance association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the campaign']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulation']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit confirming compliance for this campaign‑regulation pair']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the campaign for this regulation (e.g., compliant, non‑compliant, pending)']

  Note: 'This association captures the compliance relationship between a marketing campaign and a regulation. Each row records the compliance status and audit date for a specific campaign‑regulation pair.. Existence Justification: A marketing campaign must adhere to multiple regulations, and each regulation governs many campaigns. The business tracks the compliance status and audit date for every campaign‑regulation pair, creating a distinct record for each association.'
}

Table marketing.ad_group {

  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery condition of the ad group as reported by the platform.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group stops serving (nullable for ongoing).']

  frequency_cap_window varchar [note: 'Number of days over which the frequency cap applies.']
  is_experiment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad group is part of an A/B test or experiment.']

  optimization_goal varchar [note: 'Primary performance metric the ad group is optimized for.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']

  start_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group becomes eligible to serve.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad group.']
  targeting_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules defining audience, geography, device, and other targeting parameters.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of ad group based on the ad format or channel.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ad group record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the ad group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ad_group. Referenced by ad_group_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for campaign_execution. Referenced by campaign_execution_id.'

Table content.content_digital_asset {
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the digital asset.']
  asset_general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Required for asset accounting: digital media assets are expensed/depreciated against a GL account, enabling financial reporting of media costs.']
  rights_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.rights_license. Business justification: Digital assets have a rights_license string; replace with FK to rights_license table for proper licensing management.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Asset ownership report – each digital asset must be linked to the seller who uploaded it for royalty, compliance, and dispute tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Asset acquisition process requires linking each digital asset to its supplying vendor for cost, compliance, and rights tracking.']
  a_b_test_name varchar [note: 'Name of the A/B test associated with this asset.']
  a_b_test_variant varchar [note: 'Variant identifier for A/B testing of the asset.']
  alt_text varchar [note: 'Alternative text for accessibility and SEO.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'Width to height ratio (e.g., 16:9).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the digital asset.']
  cache_ttl_seconds varchar [note: 'Time‑to‑live for CDN caching in seconds.']
  cdn_url varchar [note: 'Public CDN URL where the asset is served.']
  checksum_md5 varchar [note: 'MD5 hash for file integrity verification.']
  color_profile varchar [note: 'Color space information (e.g., sRGB, AdobeRGB).']
  content_digital_asset_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the asset for editorial use.']
  content_digital_asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset used in catalogs and UI.']
  content_digital_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the digital asset record was first created in the system.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of video or audio asset in seconds.']
  edge_region varchar [note: 'Geographic CDN edge region serving the asset.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the asset may no longer be used.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the uploaded asset.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes.']
  height_px varchar [note: 'Pixel height of the asset (applicable to images and video).']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this asset is the primary representation for its product.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the asset content.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (e.g., en_US) for localized assets.']
  mime_type varchar [note: 'Internet media type (e.g., image/jpeg, video/mp4).']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Combined width x height string (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  seo_description varchar [note: 'Search‑engine‑optimized description for the asset.']
  seo_title varchar [note: 'Search‑engine‑optimized title for the asset.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the asset (e.g., PIM, CMS).']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'Internal storage location or bucket path for the asset.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of business tags for categorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the digital asset record.']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the asset was initially uploaded.']
  version_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the version was approved for publishing.']
  version_cdn_url varchar [note: 'CDN URL for this specific version of the asset.']
  version_change_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the change applied in this version.']
  version_editor varchar [note: 'User or system that edited this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the asset.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this version.']
  width_px varchar [note: 'Pixel width of the asset (applicable to images and video).']

  Note: 'Master record for all digital content assets in the DAM system including product images, videos, banners, icons, and rich media files. Tracks asset metadata (file format, resolution, file size, MIME type, color profile), CDN distribution details (CDN URL, cache TTL, edge region), storage path, asset status, rights/licensing reference, expiry date, and upload source. Maintains full version history as embedded detail records capturing version number, change type (crop, recolor, reformat, retranslate), version status (draft, approved, archived), editor identity, approval timestamp, and CDN-published URL per version. Enables rollback, audit trail, and A/B content variant management. SSOT for all binary content objects, their metadata, and their complete revision lifecycle.'
}

Table content.asset_version {
  asset_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each asset version record.']
  content_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test experiment that utilizes this version.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital asset to which this version belongs.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who created or modified the version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the version was approved for publishing.']
  asset_version_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the purpose or changes of this version.']
  asset_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the asset version.']
  cdn_url varchar [note: 'Public URL where the versioned asset is hosted on the CDN.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of modification applied in this version.']
  checksum_md5 varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the asset file for integrity verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the version record was initially created.']
  editor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the editor responsible for the version.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the version should no longer be served.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the asset file.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes.']
  is_a_b_test_variant boolean [note: 'True if this version is used as a variant in an A/B test.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version is the currently active primary version for the asset.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO code indicating the language of the asset content.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Pixel dimensions of the asset (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the asset version (e.g., PIM, DAM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the version record.']
  version_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the version (e.g., v1.0, v2‑beta).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer representing the version order of the asset.']

  Note: 'Tracks the full version history of each digital asset, capturing every revision, re-upload, or transformation applied to a digital asset over its lifecycle. Stores version number, change type (crop, recolor, reformat, retranslate), version status (draft, approved, archived), editor identity, approval timestamp, and CDN-published URL per version. Enables rollback, audit, and A/B content variant management.'
}

Table content.item {
  item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the content item.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the content version.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Required for personalization engine to map each content item to a defined marketing audience segment for targeted delivery.']
  author_seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Content authoring audit – seller‑generated product pages need the seller_profile ID to attribute content creation for quality and legal compliance.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Allows campaign performance dashboards to attribute impressions, clicks, and conversions back to the exact content items used in each campaign.']
  content_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability of individual content items is evaluated by assigning them to profit centers, supporting margin reporting and strategic decisions.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the author who created the content.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Required for Content Compliance Review Report: each content item must be linked to the specific compliance obligation it satisfies (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).']
  original_content_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original content item for which this record is a localized or variant copy.']
  production_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Content production costs are charged to a cost center for budgeting and variance analysis, a standard practice in e‑commerce content operations.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Target audience segment for which the content is intended.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Licensing contracts for content items must reference the governing vendor contract for legal compliance and renewal management.']
  ab_test_variant_name varchar [note: 'Name or label of the A/B test variant.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content was approved for publication.']
  compliance_legal_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the content has passed legal compliance review.']
  content_body varchar [note: 'Full text or HTML body of the content item.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'Broad business category the content belongs to.']
  content_format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the content body.']
  content_length_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the content body in bytes.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑5) assigned by editors or users.']
  content_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the content (e.g., created internally or supplied by a vendor).']
  content_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of taxonomy tags applied to the content.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content purpose and format.']
  copyright_notice varchar [note: 'Legal copyright statement attached to the content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content item was first created.']
  diff_summary varchar [note: 'Human‑readable summary of changes from the previous version.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content should be retired or archived (nullable).']
  is_ab_test_variant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version is part of an A/B test.']
  is_localized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the content has been localized for a specific market.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the content item.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the content.']
  legal_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the legal compliance review process.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (e.g., en_US) for regional variations.']
  publication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content was made publicly available.']
  publishing_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the content is delivered to end users.']
  readability_score double [note: 'Automated readability metric (e.g., Flesch‑Kincaid).']
  seo_keyword_density double [note: 'Percentage of target SEO keywords relative to total words.']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of target SEO keywords.']
  seo_meta_description varchar [note: 'Meta description tag used for search engine optimization.']
  seo_meta_title varchar [note: 'Meta title tag used for search engine optimization.']
  slug varchar [note: 'URL‑friendly identifier derived from the title, used in web addresses.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the content item.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the content item.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the content item.']
  version_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this version was created.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Total number of words in the content body.']

  Note: 'Master record for structured editorial content units including product descriptions, feature bullet points, PDP long-form copy, PLP category descriptions, landing page body text, and editorial articles. Tracks content type, locale, authoring status, word count, readability score, SEO keyword density, publication status, and owning editorial team. Maintains immutable version snapshots as embedded detail records capturing version number, full text body, diff summary from prior version, approval state, approver identity, and publish timestamp. Supports content rollback, editorial workflow approvals, regulatory audit trails, and A/B test variant tracking. SSOT for all text-based content objects and their complete revision lifecycle.'
}

Table content.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the immutable content version record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific A/B test variant linked to this version.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved or rejected the version.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent content item to which this version belongs.']
  approval_state varchar [note: 'Result of the editorial approval process for this version.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the content_body to verify integrity.']
  content_body varchar [note: 'Full textual or markup body of the content version, stored as a string.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category the content supports.']
  content_length varchar [note: 'Number of characters in the content_body, useful for editorial limits.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content format or purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the version record was initially created.']
  diff_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of changes from the previous version, supporting audit and rollback.']
  is_ab_test_variant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version is part of an A/B test experiment.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of keywords associated with the content for search relevance.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO 639‑1 language identifier for the content version.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last edited the version.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale string combining language and region (e.g., en_US).']
  publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the version was made publicly available.']
  seo_description varchar [note: 'Meta description shown in search results to improve click‑through.']
  seo_title varchar [note: 'Title used by search engines for indexing and display in SERPs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the content version.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the content version, used in editorial listings and SEO.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the version record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number indicating the order of this snapshot within the content item\'s history.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the content version.']

  Note: 'Immutable version snapshot of a content item capturing the full text body, structured fields, and metadata at a specific point in time. Supports content rollback, editorial workflow approvals, and regulatory audit trails. Stores version number, content body, diff summary from prior version, approval state, approver identity, and publish timestamp. Enables A/B test variant tracking at the content body level.'
}

Table content.seo_metadata {
  seo_metadata_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SEO metadata record.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: SEO metadata is scoped to a specific content item; linking provides direct access and eliminates silo.']
  canonical_url varchar [note: 'Canonical URL for the page, may differ from page_url if redirects exist.']
  content_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the page content used to detect changes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code indicating regional targeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SEO metadata record was created.']
  hreflang_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of hreflang locale tags for language targeting.']
  is_canonical boolean [note: 'Indicates if this URL is the canonical version.']
  keyword_target varchar [note: 'Primary SEO keyword targeted for the page.']
  last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SEO metadata.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Two-letter language code for the page content.']
  meta_description varchar [note: 'SEO meta description summarizing page content.']
  open_graph_description varchar [note: 'Description used in Open Graph metadata.']
  open_graph_image_url varchar [note: 'URL of the image used in Open Graph metadata.']
  open_graph_title varchar [note: 'Title used in Open Graph metadata for social sharing.']
  open_graph_type varchar [note: 'Object type for Open Graph metadata. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: website|article|product|video|profile|music|book — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  page_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the page for SEO targeting.']
  page_url varchar [note: 'The canonical URL of the page for SEO purposes.']
  robots_directive varchar [note: 'Robots meta tag directive controlling indexing and crawling.']
  seo_metadata_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SEO metadata record.']
  seo_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated SEO quality score (0-100) for the page.']
  sitemap_priority decimal(3,2) [note: 'Priority value for sitemap XML indicating page importance (0.0 to 1.0).']
  structured_data_json varchar [note: 'JSON-LD markup providing structured data for search engines.']
  title varchar [note: 'SEO meta title displayed in search results.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for the SEO metadata record, incremented on changes.']

  Note: 'Stores SEO-specific metadata for each content-bearing page or entity, including meta title, meta description, canonical URL, Open Graph tags, structured data markup (JSON-LD schema.org), hreflang locale tags, robots directive, sitemap priority, and keyword targeting. Linked to product PDPs, PLPs, category pages, and editorial landing pages. Supports SEO and SEM optimization workflows and search engine indexing compliance.'
}

Table content.localization {
  localization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the localization record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the translator or translation service used.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original digital asset before localization.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source content item being localized.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Localization must verify content complies with regional regulations (e.g., GDPR for EU). Supports Localization Compliance Check workflow.']
  translation_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Outsourced translation services are provided by suppliers; linking records supplier for each localized asset for cost and SLA tracking.']
  asset_alt_text varchar [note: 'Localized alternative text for images or media assets.']
  asset_url varchar [note: 'URL to the localized digital asset (image, video, etc.).']
  audience_segment varchar [note: 'Target audience segment for which the localized content is intended.']
  character_count varchar [note: 'Number of characters in the localized text.']
  compliance_gdpr_flag boolean [note: 'True if the localized content complies with GDPR requirements for the region.']
  compliance_legal_notice varchar [note: 'Localized legal disclaimer or notice required for the locale.']
  content_body varchar [note: 'Full localized text or markup for the content item.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Category of the content element that is localized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the localization record was created.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Optional date/time when the localized content should be retired.']
  is_default_locale boolean [note: 'True if this locale is the default for the content item.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review action.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Standard BCP-47 locale identifier (e.g., en-US, fr-FR).']
  localization_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the localization record.']
  meta_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of localized SEO keywords.']
  publish_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the localization is ready for publishing.']
  publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the localized content was made live.']
  quality_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from reviewers about translation quality.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO region code associated with the locale (e.g., US, FR).']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current review state of the localized content.']
  rtl_flag boolean [note: 'True if the locale requires right‑to‑left rendering.']
  seo_description varchar [note: 'Localized meta description used for SEO.']
  seo_title varchar [note: 'Localized SEO title for search engine listings.']
  source_language varchar [note: 'Language code of the original content before translation.']
  target_language varchar [note: 'Language code of the localized content.']
  translation_memory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the translation memory entry used for this localization.']
  translation_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality rating of the translation on a 0‑100 scale.']
  translation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the translation: human, machine, or hybrid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the localization.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the localized content.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Number of words in the localized text.']

  Note: 'Manages locale-specific translations and adaptations of content items and digital asset metadata for international storefronts. Tracks locale code (BCP-47), translated content body, translation source (human, machine translation, hybrid), translation quality score, review status, translator identity, character count, and publish readiness per locale. Supports GDPR-compliant regional content delivery, multi-language PDP/PLP rendering, and right-to-left (RTL) layout flagging for applicable locales.'
}

Table content.tag {
  tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each tag record.']
  parent_tag_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent tag in the hierarchy, if any.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag was approved for production use.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'How the tag was applied to content: manually, via machine‑learning, or rule‑based.']
  audience varchar [note: 'Intended audience for content using this tag.']
  color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for UI representation of the tag.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence (0‑100) from automated ML or rule‑based assignment processes.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification of the tag for internal policy compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag record was first created in the system.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Integer used to order tags in UI lists.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tag becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tag is retired; null if indefinite.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tag in an external taxonomy system.']
  governance_status varchar [note: 'Current governance state of the tag within the taxonomy lifecycle.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the tag within the taxonomy tree (root = 0).']
  icon varchar [note: 'Identifier of the icon asset used for the tag.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL of the visual icon or image representing the tag.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO language code of the tag text.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent review.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp the tag was applied to any content.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (e.g., US, GB, CA) for region‑specific tags.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating the tag\'s relevance to search ranking.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance review.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the latest tag governance review.']
  seo_metadata varchar [note: 'SEO‑related metadata (meta title, meta description) associated with the tag.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the tag (e.g., PIM, CMS, external taxonomy).']
  synonyms varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of alternative terms for the tag.']
  tag_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of tags (e.g., Fashion, Electronics).']
  tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code or short identifier for the tag, often used in integrations.']
  tag_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tag\'s meaning and intended usage.']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the tag used in editorial and UI contexts.']
  tag_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tag within the taxonomy.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tag purpose, e.g., topic, keyword, product attribute, campaign, or seasonal.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the tag.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tag record.']
  url varchar [note: 'Canonical URL for the tag\'s landing page.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of content items or assets currently assigned to this tag.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the tag definition, incremented on changes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who originally created the tag.']

  Note: 'Controlled vocabulary taxonomy entity representing editorial, SEO, and classification tags applied to content items and digital assets. Stores tag name, tag type (topic, keyword, product attribute, campaign, seasonal), tag hierarchy level, parent tag reference, usage frequency, and governance status (approved, deprecated). Owns the many-to-many tag-to-content assignment records as embedded details including assigned content/asset reference, assignment method (manual, auto-ML, rule-based), confidence score, assigning user/system, assignment date, and active status. SSOT for content taxonomy and all tagging relationships.'
}

Table content.tag_assignment {
  tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each tag assignment record.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital asset (image, video, etc.) associated with the tag assignment.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the content item (e.g., product description, page copy) to which the tag is applied.']
  tag_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the taxonomy tag applied to the content or asset.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tag assignment.']
  assigned_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or service that performed the tag assignment.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the tag: manual, auto‑ML, or rule‑based.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tag was assigned.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑100) returned by the ML model when the tag is auto‑assigned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag assignment record was first created in the system.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tag assignment has been logically removed (true) or is active (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the tag assignment.']
  source_application varchar [note: 'Name of the application or service that originated the tag assignment event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tag assignment record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking content items and digital assets to their assigned content tags. Carries its own business data including assignment date, assigning user or system, assignment method (manual, auto-ML, rule-based), confidence score for ML-assigned tags, and active status. Enables many-to-many tagging relationships with full audit trail.'
}

Table content.publication {
  publication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the publication event record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that performed the publish action.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Needed for publishing reports that attribute each publication event to the specific marketing channel (email, social, etc.) used.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital asset (image, video, file) associated with the publication.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the content item (e.g., product description, article) that is being published.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the publication_id of the event being rolled back to, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the publication record was first created in the system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the publication event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of action recorded by the event (e.g., publish, unpublish, rollback).']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the published content should automatically become inactive or be removed.']
  is_rollback boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event represents a rollback to a previous version.']
  is_test_variant boolean [note: 'True if the publication is part of an A/B test or experimental variant.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (language‑country) for which the content version is targeted, e.g., en-US, fr-FR.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the publication event.']
  scheduled_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date‑time for future publication when the event is scheduled.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the publication record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the content publication for audit and rollback purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each publish or unpublish event for a content item or digital asset to a specific storefront channel or locale. Stores publication target (web, mobile app, email, marketplace), publish timestamp, scheduled publish date, expiry date, publisher identity, publication status, and rollback reference. Enables content scheduling, time-limited promotions, and multi-channel content orchestration.'
}

Table content.ab_test_variant {
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the A/B test content variant.']
  content_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent A/B test to which this variant belongs.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital asset (image, video, copy) used by the variant.']
  ab_test_variant_code varchar [note: 'System‑generated alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the variant within a test.']
  ab_test_variant_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test variant used in reporting and UI.']
  ab_test_variant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the variant.']
  ab_test_variant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the variant: control, treatment, or multivariate.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the variant is served.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the variant record was first created.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the variant is retired from the experiment.']
