  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the bank branch is domiciled (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives currency controls, regulatory reporting jurisdiction, and cross-border payment rules.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the financial institution holding this account (e.g., JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.). Used for treasury reporting, bank relationship management, and counterparty risk assessment.']
  book_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Internal GL book balance for this bank account as recorded in SAP S/4HANA general ledger, in the account\'s base currency. Compared against current_balance during bank reconciliation to identify timing differences and unrecorded items.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle management, historical reporting, and SOX audit trail.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP company code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA for financial consolidation, GL posting, and regulatory reporting. Aligns with the owning legal entity for SOX compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account master record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record-keeping requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account\'s primary operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the base currency for all statement balances, transaction amounts, and FX conversion in treasury reporting.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recent confirmed closing balance of the bank account as reported on the latest electronic bank statement, in the account\'s base currency. Used for daily cash position reporting and liquidity forecasting.']
  dual_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank account requires dual authorization (two-person rule) for payment initiation per treasury policy and SOX segregation of duties controls. True: dual approval mandatory. False: single authorization permitted.']
  house_bank_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA house bank identifier that groups bank accounts under a single banking relationship. Used for payment program configuration, bank statement import, and electronic bank reconciliation in SAP FI-BL.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number (IBAN) assigned to this account per ISO 13616 standard. Used for cross-border payment instructions, SEPA transactions, and international treasury operations. Nullable for non-IBAN jurisdictions.']
  interest_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate (as a decimal percentage, e.g., 0.0425 = 4.25%) applicable to this bank account for credit balances or overdraft facilities. Used in treasury yield analysis and cost-of-funds reporting.']
  is_pooling_header boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account serves as the header/master account in a cash concentration or notional pooling structure. True: this is the concentration account that receives sweeps from subsidiary accounts. False: standard account.']
  last_reconciled_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent successful automated bank reconciliation was completed for this account in SAP S/4HANA. Used for cash position accuracy monitoring and SOX reconciliation controls.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently received and processed electronic bank statement (EBS) for this account. Used to monitor statement receipt timeliness and identify gaps in automated bank reconciliation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this bank account master record. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and SOX audit trail.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank account was officially opened and activated by the financial institution. Used for account tenure analysis, regulatory reporting, and audit trail.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft facility amount authorized by the bank for this account, in the account\'s base currency. Used in daily cash position reporting and liquidity forecasting to determine available credit headroom.']
  payment_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount in the account\'s base currency that can be authorized for a single payment transaction from this account without escalated approval. Enforces treasury payment controls and SOX segregation of duties.']
  payment_method_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SAP payment method codes authorized for use with this bank account (e.g., \'T,U,C\' for wire transfer, ACH, check). Controls which payment programs can use this account in SAP automatic payment runs.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current reconciliation status of the bank account as of the last statement processing cycle. Reconciled: all items matched. Unreconciled: outstanding items exist. In_progress: reconciliation run is active. Exception: unresolvable discrepancies requiring manual review.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number (9-digit) identifying the US financial institution for domestic ACH and wire transfers. Used in payment processing and settlement runs. Applicable for US-domiciled accounts only.']
  signatory_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names of individuals authorized to sign on this bank account per the bank mandate. Used for treasury governance, audit, and SOX segregation of duties controls.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system from which this bank account master record was sourced. SAP_S4HANA: extracted from SAP S/4HANA Bank Account Management. MANUAL: manually entered by treasury team. BANK_PORTAL: imported from bank\'s online portal.']
  statement_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which electronic bank statements are delivered from the bank to the ERP system. SWIFT: via SWIFT network. SFTP: secure file transfer. API: direct bank API integration. Email: email attachment. Manual: manual upload.']
  statement_format varchar [note: 'Format of the electronic bank statement (EBS) received from the bank for automated import into SAP S/4HANA. MT940: SWIFT legacy format. CAMT053: ISO 20022 XML format. BAI2: US cash management format. OFX: Open Financial Exchange. CSV: flat file.']
  swift_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and correspondent banking. Conforms to ISO 9362 standard. Required for cross-border payment routing in the Transportation Management System and ERP payment runs.']
  target_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Desired minimum or target cash balance for this account as set by treasury policy. Used in cash concentration/sweeping rules and liquidity forecasting to trigger inter-account transfers.']

  Note: 'Master data and complete transaction history for all corporate bank accounts used in treasury operations. Tracks bank name, account identifiers (masked account number, IBAN/SWIFT), currency, account type (operating, payroll, escrow, concentration), account status, and owning legal entity. Includes electronic bank statement records with statement dates, opening/closing balances, individual transaction lines (credits and debits), value dates, and reconciliation status. Supports automated bank reconciliation, daily cash position reporting, cash flow management, and liquidity forecasting in SAP S/4HANA treasury.'
}

Table finance.bank_statement {
  bank_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bank statement record in the enterprise data lakehouse. Primary key for the bank_statement data product.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger (GL) cash account associated with this bank statement. Links the bank statement to the corresponding GL account for reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the corporate bank account type. Determines cash management rules, interest treatment, and reconciliation procedures. Zero-balance accounts (ZBA) are common in e-commerce treasury structures.']
  auto_reconciled_count varchar [note: 'Number of transaction lines on this statement that were automatically matched and reconciled by the SAP S/4HANA automated bank reconciliation engine without manual intervention. Used to measure automation efficiency.']
  available_balance decimal(19,4) [note: 'The funds available for immediate use at the statement date, net of holds, pending transactions, and float. May differ from closing_balance due to uncleared items. Used for liquidity management and cash forecasting.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'The bank account number associated with this statement as held at the banking institution. Masked or tokenized in non-restricted environments per PCI DSS requirements. Used to identify the corporate cash account.']
  bank_bic_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT Bank Identifier Code (BIC) / Business Identifier Code uniquely identifying the banking institution and branch. Conforms to ISO 9362 standard. Used for international wire transfers and bank identification in SAP S/4HANA.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the banking institution where the account is held. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, cross-border reporting, and GDPR/CCPA applicability assessment.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the banking institution that issued the statement (e.g., JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, HSBC). Used for counterparty identification in cash management and treasury reporting.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the banking institution to this statement transmission or file. Used for bank-side traceability, dispute resolution, and re-import identification.']
  closing_balance decimal(19,4) [note: 'The account balance at the end of the statement period as reported by the banking institution. Represents the final cash position for the period. Used for cash flow reporting and GL reconciliation.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code identifying the legal entity that owns the bank account. Used for financial consolidation and intercompany reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bank statement record was first imported and created in the enterprise data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and provides data lineage for the Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bank account statement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary amounts on this statement are denominated in this currency.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transaction lines on this statement that could not be automatically matched to GL entries and require manual investigation. Used for reconciliation quality monitoring and workload management.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (1–12 for monthly, or 1–4 for quarterly) within the fiscal year to which this statement is assigned. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA posting periods for GL reconciliation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this bank statement belongs for financial reporting and GL period assignment in SAP S/4HANA. Used for annual financial close, SOX reporting, and MRR/ARR reconciliation.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the bank statement was posted to the General Ledger (GL) in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from statement_date due to processing lag. Used for period-end close and financial reporting accuracy.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number (IBAN) for the corporate bank account per ISO 13616 standard. Used for cross-border payment identification and reconciliation in international treasury operations.']
  import_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bank statement file was electronically received and imported into SAP S/4HANA or the data platform via EDI, SWIFT, or API. Used for SLA monitoring of bank data feeds and processing latency tracking.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank account is used for intercompany transactions between legal entities within the Ecommerce enterprise group. Intercompany statements require elimination entries during financial consolidation.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank statement record has been locked to prevent further modifications after period-end close or SOX audit sign-off. Locked statements cannot be altered without an authorized unlock workflow.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bank statement record, including reconciliation status changes, balance corrections, or posting updates. Required for SOX audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for finance team annotations regarding the bank statement, such as reconciliation exceptions, manual adjustments, or special handling instructions. Supports audit documentation requirements.']
  opening_balance decimal(19,4) [note: 'The account balance at the start of the statement period as reported by the banking institution. Used as the baseline for reconciliation; must equal the prior statement closing balance for continuity validation.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the bank statement coverage period. Defines the closing boundary of the statement window. Typically aligns with the statement_date for end-of-period statements.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the bank statement coverage period. Together with period_end_date, defines the statement window for which transactions are reported. Used for period-based cash flow analysis.']
  reconciled_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the finance team member who completed and approved the bank reconciliation. Required for SOX segregation of duties (SoD) controls and audit trail documentation.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the bank statement reconciliation was completed and approved. Null if reconciliation is still in progress. Used for period-end close tracking and SOX control evidence.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank statement reconciliation workflow. Tracks progression from import through automated matching to final posting in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unreconciled|in_progress|reconciled|exception|posted|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or channel through which the bank statement was received and imported. Used for data lineage tracking, ETL audit, and troubleshooting import failures.']
  statement_date date [note: 'The official date of the bank statement as issued by the banking institution. Represents the business event date for the statement period end. Used as the primary date for cash flow reporting and reconciliation.']
  statement_format varchar [note: 'Electronic file format in which the bank statement was received from the banking institution. Determines the parsing and import logic applied during ETL ingestion into SAP S/4HANA and the data lakehouse.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned statement number issued by the banking institution. Serves as the primary business identifier for the statement as received from the bank. Used for matching and audit trail.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank statement by reporting frequency or finality. Determines processing rules and reconciliation cadence in SAP S/4HANA. Intraday statements support real-time cash visibility.']
  total_credits decimal(19,4) [note: 'Aggregate sum of all credit (inflow) transactions on the statement for the period. Used for cash inflow analysis, revenue reconciliation, and GMV-to-cash settlement validation.']
  total_debits decimal(19,4) [note: 'Aggregate sum of all debit (outflow) transactions on the statement for the period. Used for cash outflow analysis, accounts payable settlement validation, and expense reconciliation.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual transaction lines reported on the bank statement for the period. Used for completeness validation during import and reconciliation to ensure all lines are processed.']
  unreconciled_amount decimal(19,4) [note: 'The monetary difference between the bank statement closing balance and the corresponding GL cash account balance that remains unmatched after automated reconciliation. A non-zero value triggers exception handling and investigation.']

  Note: 'Electronic bank statement records imported from banking institutions for cash reconciliation. Captures statement date, bank account, opening/closing balance, transaction lines (credits and debits), value dates, and reconciliation status. Supports automated bank reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and cash flow management.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  consolidation_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consolidation group this legal entity belongs to, used for consolidated financial reporting and intercompany eliminations.']
  parent_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent legal entity in the corporate ownership hierarchy. Null for the ultimate holding company.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'Financial reporting standard under which this legal entity prepares its statutory financial statements (IFRS, US GAAP, or local GAAP). Determines accounting policies, disclosure requirements, and consolidation adjustments.']
  business_activity_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary business activities conducted by this legal entity (e.g., marketplace operations, fulfillment services, technology platform, financial services). Used for regulatory filings and transfer pricing documentation.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this legal entity in SAP S/4HANA. Defines the set of general ledger accounts available for financial postings and ensures consistency in financial reporting across the group.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA company code assigned to this legal entity. Serves as the primary organizational unit for financial accounting in the ERP system and links to the general ledger, accounts payable, and accounts receivable modules.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method applied when consolidating this entity into the group financial statements. Drives intercompany elimination logic and ownership percentage calculations.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA controlling area code associated with this legal entity. Defines the organizational unit for management accounting, cost center hierarchies, and internal order management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity master data record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was formally dissolved, wound up, or deregistered with the relevant government authority. Null for active entities.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this legal entity record is considered active and valid for financial transactions, intercompany postings, and regulatory filings within the Ecommerce corporate group.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this legal entity record ceases to be valid for new transactions. Null for currently active entities. Populated upon dissolution, merger, or deregistration.']
  entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the entity as recorded with the relevant government or regulatory authority in the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  entity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the legal entity. Drives inclusion or exclusion in consolidation runs, intercompany transaction eligibility, and regulatory filing requirements.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity by its structural role within the corporate group. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holding_company|operating_company|subsidiary|joint_venture|branch|partnership|special_purpose_vehicle|associate — promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal year calendar for this entity (e.g., K4 for calendar year, V3 for April–March). Determines period mapping for financial reporting and budget cycles.']
  functional_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the primary currency of the economic environment in which the entity operates. Used for local financial statement preparation and SAP S/4HANA company code currency assignment.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity is subject to GDPR data privacy obligations, either as a data controller or data processor. True for entities operating in or processing data of EU residents.']
  gmv_to_revenue_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'The ratio of net revenue recognized to Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) processed by this legal entity. Used for GMV-to-revenue reconciliation in consolidated financial reporting and take-rate analysis. Value between 0.0000 and 1.0000.']
  incorporation_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country in which the legal entity was incorporated or registered.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was formally incorporated or registered with the relevant government authority. Used for entity age calculations and regulatory filing timelines.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (NAICS or SIC) assigned to this legal entity based on its primary business activity. Used for regulatory reporting, benchmarking, and transfer pricing comparability analysis.']
  intercompany_trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA trading partner code assigned to this legal entity for intercompany transaction identification and elimination during group consolidation. Enables automated intercompany reconciliation.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Specific legal jurisdiction (state, province, or territory) within the country of incorporation where the entity is registered. Relevant for sub-national regulatory compliance (e.g., Delaware, California, Ontario).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this legal entity master data record. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Silver Layer.']
  legal_form varchar [note: 'Legal structure or form of the entity as defined by the jurisdiction of incorporation (e.g., LLC, PLC, GmbH, S.A., Pvt Ltd, Corp). Determines governance, liability, and reporting obligations.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) code assigned by a Local Operating Unit (LOU) accredited by the Global LEI Foundation (GLEIF). Used for regulatory reporting and counterparty identification in financial transactions.']
  operating_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country where the entity conducts its business operations. May differ from the country of incorporation for tax-optimized structures.']
  ownership_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the legal entity owned by the immediate parent entity in the corporate hierarchy. Used to determine consolidation scope and minority interest calculations. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  pci_dss_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity is subject to PCI DSS compliance requirements due to processing, storing, or transmitting payment card data. Drives scope of PCI DSS audit and certification.']
  registered_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the official registered address of the legal entity. Used for regulatory filings, tax jurisdiction determination, and legal correspondence.']
  registered_address_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country of the entity\'s registered address. May differ from the country of incorporation for branch offices.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address of the legal entity as recorded with the government authority. Used for regulatory correspondence, tax filings, and legal notices.']
  registered_address_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the official registered address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, regulatory filings, and last-mile logistics planning for entity-level shipments.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official company registration number assigned by the government authority in the jurisdiction of incorporation (e.g., Companies House number in the UK, EIN in the US).']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for presenting the entity\'s financial statements in consolidated group reporting. Typically the group\'s presentation currency (e.g., USD for a US-listed parent).']
  revenue_recognition_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard applied for revenue recognition by this legal entity. Determines how and when revenue from e-commerce transactions, marketplace fees, and fulfillment services is recognized.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in internal reporting, system displays, and intercompany references.']
  sox_in_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity is within the scope of Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal controls assessment and financial reporting requirements. True for entities that are material to the consolidated group\'s financial statements.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) used for tax filings, regulatory reporting, and financial settlements. Classified as restricted due to sensitivity of tax data.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction applicable to this legal entity for indirect tax (VAT/GST) determination, withholding tax, and transfer pricing compliance. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA tax jurisdiction configuration.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax (VAT) or Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number issued by the tax authority in the entity\'s operating jurisdiction. Required for cross-border e-commerce tax compliance.']
  voting_rights_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of voting rights held by the parent entity in this legal entity. May differ from ownership percentage in dual-class share structures. Determines control assessment under IFRS 10.']

  Note: 'Master data for all legal entities within the Ecommerce corporate group. Tracks entity name, registration number, jurisdiction, country of incorporation, functional currency, tax identification number, and consolidation group membership. Serves as the organizational anchor for intercompany transactions, regulatory filings, and consolidated financial reporting.'
}

Table finance.accrual {
  accrual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the accrual record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the accrual.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the accrual.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'GL account to which the accrual amount is posted.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order that triggered the accrual, if applicable.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Legal entity (company) that owns the accrual.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center linked to the accrual.']
  related_contract_vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract associated with the accrual, if applicable.']
  related_invoice_supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice linked to the accrual, if applicable.']
  reversal_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accrual (reversal_accrual_id)']
  accrual_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual was recorded (business event time).']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the accrual was created.']
  accrual_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the accrual (e.g., ACCR-2023-0001).']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual.']
  accrual_type varchar [note: 'Category of the accrual indicating its nature (expense, revenue deferral, provision for returns, warranty reserve, bonus).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount of the accrual before adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the accrual.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the accrual amount.']
  data_privacy_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the accrual data for privacy and security handling.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the accrual becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date when the accrual ceases to be effective (nullable).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., month) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the accrual belongs.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates if GDPR privacy considerations apply to this accrual record.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrual amount is an estimate.']
  justification_narrative varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the accrual was created.']
  net_accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and other adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the accrual.']
  period_end date [note: 'End date of the accounting period the accrual covers.']
  period_start date [note: 'Start date of the accounting period the accrual covers.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual was reversed, if applicable.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the accrual has been reversed.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number to order accruals within the same period.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the accrual record.']
  sox_control_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the accrual is subject to SOX internal control requirements.']
  status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text detail about the accrual\'s current status.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the accrual (adjustment).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the accrual record.']

  Note: 'SSOT for financial accruals, provisions, and deferred items recorded during month-end and quarter-end close. Tracks accrual type (expense accrual, revenue deferral, provision for returns, warranty reserve, bonus accrual), accrual amount, reversal date, GL account, cost center, justification narrative, and approval status. Supports ASC 450 contingency provisions and IAS 37 compliance. Enables accurate period-end financial statements by recognizing obligations and income in the correct fiscal period regardless of cash timing.'
}

Table finance.payment_batch {
  payment_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_batch']
  consolidated_from_payment_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_batch (consolidated_from_payment_batch_id)']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., merchant or internal finance unit) associated with the batch.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval outcome for the batch.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the payment batch.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment batch purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the batch amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or notes about the payment batch.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing any processing error encountered for the batch.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment batch was initiated.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate fees or adjustments applied to the batch.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was generated automatically by the system.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees, representing the amount to be settled.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the batch payments were submitted.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the batch transactions.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch processing completed.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts performed for failed batch processing.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the batch processing.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was settled with the financial institution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the batch record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment batch.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary amount of all payments in the batch before adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_batch. Referenced by payment_batch_id.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fiscal_period']
  prior_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the period\'s start date in the Gregorian calendar.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the period follows the standard Gregorian calendar or a custom fiscal calendar.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year of the period\'s start date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fiscal period record was first created in the system.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days covered by the period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing context or notes about the period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'Week number within the fiscal year, useful for weekly reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year to which the period belongs, based on the company\'s fiscal calendar.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'True when all postings for the period are finalized and no further adjustments are allowed.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active fiscal period for ongoing transactions.']
  month_number varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the period\'s start date.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fiscal period, e.g., "FY2024 Q1" or "2024‑01".']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1 for Jan, 2 for Feb).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period for reporting and posting purposes.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Category of the period indicating its granularity (month, quarter, year, etc.).']
  quarter_number varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1‑4) derived from the start date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fiscal period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fiscal_period. Referenced by period_id.'
}

Table finance.consolidation_group {
  consolidation_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consolidation_group']
  parent_consolidation_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on consolidation_group (parent_consolidation_group_id)']

  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to consolidate financial results for the group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation group record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for group financial reporting.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the group.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the consolidation group becomes effective for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the consolidation group ceases to be effective (null for open‑ended).']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) when the group\'s fiscal year ends.']
  fiscal_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) when the group\'s fiscal year starts.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the consolidation group in financial systems.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the consolidation group.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group indicating its purpose or scope.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the consolidation group includes external entities.']
  legal_entity_count varchar [note: 'Number of legal entities that belong to the consolidation group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the group.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the group reports consolidated financial results.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consolidation group.']
  total_assets decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated asset value for the consolidation group.']
  total_liabilities decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated liabilities amount for the consolidation group.']
  total_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated revenue amount for the consolidation group in the reporting currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consolidation group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for consolidation_group. Referenced by consolidation_group_id.'
}

Table procurement.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for supplier']
  bank_account_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank account holder as registered for supplier payment remittance. Used in AP payment processing to validate beneficiary identity and prevent payment fraud. Subject to PCI DSS and SOX financial controls.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s bank account number for payment remittance. Stored in encrypted/tokenized form per PCI DSS requirements. Used exclusively by the AP payment processing module in SAP S/4HANA for wire transfers and ACH payments.']
  bank_routing_code varchar [note: 'Bank routing identifier for payment processing — either SWIFT/BIC code for international wire transfers or ABA routing number for US domestic ACH payments. Required for accurate payment routing in the AP settlement process.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s registered billing address. Used for invoice processing, payment remittance, and accounts payable (AP) in SAP S/4HANA. Required for PCI DSS-compliant payment processing and SOX financial controls.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s registered billing address. Used in conjunction with billing_address_line1 for complete address resolution in AP payment processing and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the supplier\'s billing address country. Drives tax jurisdiction rules, currency defaults, and cross-border trade compliance in the ERP procure-to-pay process.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the supplier\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, carrier rate determination, and address validation in the procure-to-pay workflow.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the current master vendor agreement or supplier contract. Triggers contract renewal workflows and procurement alerts when approaching expiry. Nullable for open-ended evergreen contracts.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current master vendor agreement or supplier contract. Defines the period during which negotiated pricing, payment terms, and SLAs are binding. Used in SAP S/4HANA outline agreements and procurement scheduling.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the supplier is legally registered and primarily operates. Used for import/export compliance, tariff classification, and WTO trade regulation adherence.']
  cpsc_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the supplier\'s CPSC product safety certification. Used to enforce product safety compliance gating in the Product Information Management System (PIM) catalog onboarding workflow.']
  cpsc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier\'s products meet Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards. Required for suppliers providing consumer goods sold on the e-commerce marketplace. Non-compliant suppliers are blocked from product catalog onboarding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the supplier lifecycle. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset per platform convention.']
  default_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard supplier lead time in calendar days from Purchase Order (PO) placement to goods receipt at the warehouse. Used as the default for inventory replenishment planning and MOQ calculations when no SKU-specific lead time is defined. Feeds into the WMS receiving schedule and ERP supply chain planning.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has signed a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) and is confirmed GDPR compliant for handling EU personal data. Mandatory for suppliers processing customer data on behalf of the e-commerce platform under GDPR Article 28.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between the buyer and supplier for goods delivery. Drives logistics cost allocation, insurance requirements, and customs documentation in the TMS and ERP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_diversity_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a recognized diversity certification (e.g., minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business). Tracked for supplier diversity program reporting, ESG compliance, and government contract requirements.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is designated as a preferred vendor in the approved vendor list (AVL). Preferred vendors receive priority in automated PO sourcing and are eligible for blanket purchase agreements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA vendor evaluation.']
  iso_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the supplier\'s ISO certification. Procurement compliance workflows use this date to trigger re-certification reminders and to flag suppliers with lapsed certifications for suspension review.']
  iso_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid ISO certification (e.g., ISO 9001 Quality Management, ISO 27001 Information Security, ISO 14001 Environmental Management). Drives compliance gating in the supplier onboarding workflow.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the supplier master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the ERP-to-lakehouse ETL pipeline and for audit trail compliance under SOX and GDPR accountability requirements.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the supplier entity as recorded in official business registration documents. Used for contract execution, tax filings, and regulatory compliance. Must match the name on vendor contracts and invoices.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) — the minimum number of units the supplier requires per purchase order line. Enforced during PO creation in the ERP to prevent under-minimum orders. Critical for inventory planning and procurement cost optimization.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier was formally approved and activated in the procurement system following completion of the onboarding workflow (KYC, compliance checks, contract execution). Marks the start of the active vendor relationship.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supplier in the procurement onboarding and vendor management workflow. Pending: application submitted. Under Review: compliance and due diligence in progress. Approved: cleared for procurement. Active: currently transacting. Suspended: temporarily blocked. Terminated: relationship ended.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the supplier invoices and is paid (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Drives FX conversion in SAP S/4HANA AP and financial settlement. Essential for multi-currency procurement operations.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code agreed with the supplier, defining the payment due date and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 meaning 2% discount if paid within 10 days, net due in 30). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms (ZTERM). Critical for cash flow management and AP settlement.']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is currently PCI DSS compliant, relevant for suppliers handling payment card data or integrated into the payment processing ecosystem. Required for suppliers in the Payment Gateway and Processing Platform integration.']
  pci_dss_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the supplier\'s PCI DSS compliance certification. Annual re-assessment is required per PCI SSC standards. Used to trigger compliance renewal workflows and to block non-compliant suppliers from payment data integrations.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the primary contact person at the supplier. Used for PO transmission, invoice correspondence, and procurement workflow notifications. Subject to GDPR legitimate interest basis for B2B processing.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the supplier organization. Used for procurement communications, PO acknowledgments, and relationship management. Not a personal consumer — represents a B2B contact.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the primary contact at the supplier organization. Used for urgent procurement escalations, delivery coordination, and supplier relationship management. Stored in E.164 international format.']
  primary_contact_role varchar [note: 'Business role or function of the primary contact person at the supplier. Determines routing of procurement communications (e.g., POs to account manager, invoices to finance contact, shipment queries to logistics coordinator).']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued business registration or incorporation number for the supplier entity. Used for legal verification, KYC (Know Your Customer) compliance, and regulatory filings. Format varies by country of registration.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification derived from the supplier risk assessment process. Low: minimal supply chain risk. Medium: moderate risk requiring monitoring. High: significant risk requiring mitigation plans. Critical: immediate action required. Used to trigger procurement escalation workflows.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk assessment score for the supplier on a 0.00–100.00 scale, where higher values indicate greater risk. Incorporates financial stability, geopolitical exposure, compliance history, and delivery performance. Used by procurement to prioritize supplier audits and diversification strategies.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to the supplier for cross-system identification. Used in Purchase Orders (POs), EDI transactions, and ERP integrations. Maps to the SAP S/4HANA vendor account number.']
  supplier_tier varchar [note: 'Procurement tier classification indicating the strategic importance and relationship depth with the supplier. Strategic: top-tier partners with deep integration and long-term contracts. Preferred: vetted suppliers with negotiated terms. Approved: qualified suppliers on the approved vendor list. Transactional: one-off or spot-buy suppliers.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier by their role in the supply chain. Manufacturer: produces goods directly. Distributor: resells from manufacturers. Wholesaler: bulk reseller. 3PL (Third-Party Logistics): logistics provider. Dropshipper: ships directly to end customer. Marketplace Seller: sells via the e-commerce marketplace platform.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier, such as EIN (Employer Identification Number) in the US, VAT number in the EU, or GST number in other jurisdictions. Required for tax reporting, invoice validation, and SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or trade name (Doing Business As) of the supplier, which may differ from the legal name. Used in day-to-day procurement communications, catalog displays, and seller portal references.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'VAT registration number for suppliers operating in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (primarily EU). Distinct from the general tax_id as it specifically governs VAT reclaim and cross-border EU trade compliance. Required for EU Electronic Commerce Directive compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers and vendors in the e-commerce procurement network. Captures supplier identity, classification, contact details (including multiple contact persons with roles and communication preferences), tax identifiers, payment terms, currency, country of origin, supplier tier (strategic/preferred/approved/transactional), onboarding status, compliance certifications (ISO/PCI/GDPR/CPSC with expiry tracking), lead time parameters per SKU-warehouse combination, and risk assessment scores. SSOT for supplier identity and supplier-level attributes across the procurement domain. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA vendor master.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal procurement buyer (employee) responsible for creating and managing this purchase order. Used for workload tracking, approval routing, and supplier relationship accountability.']
  blanket_po_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent blanket purchase order when this PO is a release against an open blanket agreement. Enables aggregation of spend and quantity consumed against the blanket PO ceiling.']
  buyer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Associates drop‑ship or direct‑to‑customer purchase orders with the buying customer.account for fulfillment and revenue reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑center allocation of PO spend is mandatory for internal budgeting and cost‑center performance reports.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Allows automatic posting of PO amounts to the correct GL account, supporting the standard purchase‑to‑pay financial flow.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for PO pricing reports: each purchase order is priced using a specific price list to compute expected cost and margin.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Business process: Seller‑initiated purchase order for inventory replenishment; reports require linking PO to the seller profile that placed it.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) master record to whom this purchase order is issued. Maps to the vendor master in SAP S/4HANA and the Seller Management Portal for marketplace suppliers.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying vendor contract or blanket purchase agreement (outline agreement) from which this purchase order was released. Enables contract compliance tracking, spend-against-contract analysis, and contract utilization reporting.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination warehouse or fulfillment center where goods are to be received. Drives WMS receiving workflow initiation, inventory allocation, and inbound logistics planning.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date goods were physically received at the ship-to warehouse as recorded by the WMS goods receipt process. Used to calculate supplier on-time delivery performance and lead time actuals.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal authorization state of the purchase order within the procurement approval workflow. Tracks whether the PO has cleared the required approval hierarchy (e.g., manager, finance controller) before transmission to the supplier. Distinct from lifecycle_status which tracks the broader PO state.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order received final internal approval and was authorized for transmission to the supplier. Used for approval cycle time analytics and SOX internal control evidence.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier upon order acknowledgement. Compared against requested_delivery_date to measure supplier on-time commitment accuracy and to trigger alerts for potential stockouts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in SAP S/4HANA and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the purchase order is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency for all monetary fields on this PO and drives foreign exchange conversion for consolidated spend reporting.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied to the purchase order, including negotiated volume discounts, promotional rebates, and early payment discounts. Reduces the gross amount to arrive at the net payable amount.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the PO currency to the company code local currency at the time of PO creation. Used for multi-currency spend consolidation, financial reporting, and variance analysis.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current status of goods receipt against this purchase order as recorded in the WMS and SAP S/4HANA MIGO. Tracks whether no goods have been received, some lines/quantities are received, or all ordered quantities have been fully received.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross value of the purchase order before any discounts, taxes, or adjustments. Calculated as the sum of all line item quantities multiplied by their unit prices. Used for spend analytics, budget commitment tracking, and GMV reporting.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between buyer and supplier. Determines who bears freight, insurance, and customs costs. Critical for landed cost calculation and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms condition (e.g., \'FOB Shanghai\', \'DAP Los Angeles\'). Specifies the exact point of risk transfer and cost responsibility as required by ICC Incoterms 2020.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Status of supplier invoice matching against this purchase order in the SAP S/4HANA three-way match process (PO, goods receipt, invoice). Tracks whether invoices have been received, partially matched, fully matched, or are under dispute.']
  is_drop_ship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order is a drop-ship arrangement where the supplier ships goods directly to the end customer rather than to a company warehouse. Triggers different fulfillment workflows in the OMS and WMS.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order has been flagged as urgent, requiring expedited processing, prioritized supplier communication, and accelerated receiving. Typically set when inventory levels are critically low (OOS risk) or to fulfill a high-priority customer order.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Agreed or estimated number of calendar days from PO issuance to expected goods receipt at the destination warehouse. Used for inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and supplier performance benchmarking.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the purchase order within the procure-to-pay lifecycle. Draft = created but not yet approved; Approved = internally authorized; Sent = transmitted to supplier; Acknowledged = supplier confirmed receipt; Partially_received = some line items received in WMS; Closed = fully received and matched; Cancelled = voided before completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|sent|acknowledged|partially_received|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) stipulated by the supplier for this purchase order. Represents the smallest quantity the supplier will accept per order. Used in replenishment planning to ensure order quantities meet supplier thresholds.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net payable amount of the purchase order after applying discounts and adding taxes. Represents the financial commitment to the supplier and is used for accounts payable matching, budget consumption, and financial reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and special instructions associated with the purchase order, including delivery instructions, quality requirements, packaging specifications, or buyer-supplier communication captured at PO creation or amendment.']
  order_date date [note: 'The business date on which the purchase order was formally issued and transmitted to the supplier. Represents the principal real-world event time for this procurement transaction. Used as the baseline for lead time calculations and payment term due date computation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms between the buyer and supplier, specifying the payment due period and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., Net30, Net60, 2/10Net30). Drives accounts payable due date calculation and cash flow planning.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable purchase order number assigned by SAP S/4HANA ME21N at PO creation. Used in all supplier communications, EDI transactions, and receiving workflows. Globally unique within the enterprise.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order by procurement strategy. Standard = one-time discrete order; Blanket = open quantity/value agreement with releases; Consignment = supplier-owned stock held at warehouse; Drop-ship = supplier ships directly to customer; Service = non-goods procurement.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA purchasing group code identifying the buyer team or commodity group responsible for this PO. Used for spend analytics, category management, and buyer performance reporting.']
  purchasing_org varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA purchasing organization code responsible for negotiating and issuing this purchase order. Determines the legal entity, currency, and terms applicable to the procurement transaction. Aligns with organizational hierarchy for spend reporting.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer requires the goods or services to be delivered to the ship-to location. Used for supplier SLA tracking, lead time analysis, and inventory replenishment planning. Distinct from the confirmed delivery date provided by the supplier.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'The purchase requisition number from SAP S/4HANA ME51N that originated this purchase order. Provides the audit trail from business need (requisition) to formal commitment (PO) for SOX compliance and spend authorization tracking.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order was electronically transmitted to the supplier via EDI, email, or supplier portal. Marks the start of the supplier lead time clock and is used for SLA and communication audit purposes.']
  ship_to_address varchar [note: 'Full delivery address for the purchase order, including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country. Used for carrier routing, customs documentation, and inbound logistics coordination. May differ from the warehouse address for drop-ship or direct-to-store orders.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this purchase order record was originated or last updated. Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and audit purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  supplier_reference_number varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own order confirmation or reference number provided upon acknowledgement of the purchase order. Used for cross-referencing in supplier communications, EDI transactions, and invoice matching.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the purchase order, including VAT, GST, import duties, and other applicable taxes. Computed based on the tax code and jurisdiction rules configured in SAP S/4HANA.']
  total_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of line items included in this purchase order. Used for PO complexity analysis, receiving workflow planning, and supplier performance metrics. Not a calculated aggregate — captured at PO creation from the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record, including status changes, quantity amendments, price revisions, or delivery date updates. Used for change tracking and incremental ETL processing.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a formal procurement commitment issued to a supplier. Captures PO number, PO type (standard/blanket/consignment/drop-ship), supplier reference, buyer, requested delivery date, ship-to warehouse, total PO value, currency, payment terms, incoterms, approval status, and PO lifecycle state (draft/approved/sent/acknowledged/partially_received/closed/cancelled). Integrates with SAP S/4HANA ME21N/ME22N and WMS receiving workflows.'
}

Table procurement.po_line_item {
  po_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each purchase order line item record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Needed for line‑item cost analysis: each PO line item references the exact price‑list item that supplied its unit price.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent purchase order header. Establishes the header-to-line relationship enabling multi-SKU PO tracking and partial fulfillment analysis. Mandatory for all line items. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: HEADER_REFERENCE.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product/material master record for the item being procured on this line. Links to the Product Information Management System for catalog enrichment and attribute management. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: RESOURCE_REFERENCE.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor fulfilling this line item. Enables supplier-level performance analysis, lead time tracking, and vendor compliance monitoring within the procure-to-pay process.']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'ERP account assignment category determining how the PO line item cost is posted in the general ledger (e.g., K=Cost Center, F=Order, P=Project, A=Asset, C=Sales Order, U=Unknown). Required for financial posting and SOX compliance.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date on which goods were received at the warehouse for this line item. Populated upon goods receipt posting in the WMS. Used for supplier on-time delivery performance measurement and lead time actuals.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier for this line item, as communicated via PO acknowledgement or supplier portal. Used for lead time analysis, on-time delivery KPI tracking, and logistics planning.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'ERP cost center to which the procurement cost for this line item is allocated. Used for budget tracking, departmental spend reporting, and financial controlling. Applicable when account_assignment_category = K.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PO line item record was first created in the source ERP system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and procurement process timing analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA change documents.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price and line total amount on this PO line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement and FX reporting.']
  delivery_lead_time_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in calendar days between the confirmed delivery date and the actual delivery date for this line item. Positive value indicates late delivery; negative indicates early delivery. Key supplier on-time delivery (OTD) performance metric.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount negotiated with the supplier for this line item, applied to the gross unit price to arrive at the net unit price. Supports procurement cost analysis and vendor contract compliance monitoring.']
  final_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has confirmed this as the final delivery for this PO line item, closing it against further goods receipts. True = final delivery confirmed; False = further deliveries expected. Used to close open PO lines.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the procurement expense or inventory value for this line item is posted. Supports financial reporting, accounts payable accruals, and SOX audit trails.']
  goods_receipt_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of goods received against this PO line (goods_receipt_quantity × unit_price). Used for inventory valuation, accrual accounting, and accounts payable liability recognition.']
  goods_receipt_blocked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods receipt posting is blocked for this PO line item (e.g., due to quality inspection hold, price discrepancy, or compliance issue). True = GR blocked; False = GR allowed. Used in WMS receiving workflows.']
  goods_receipt_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this line item received into the warehouse against this PO line. Populated from WMS goods receipt transactions. Used for partial fulfillment tracking, open quantity calculation, and 3-way invoice matching.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier for this line item. Critical for cross-border procurement and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoice verification (3-way match: PO vs. GR vs. invoice) has been completed for this line item. True = verified; False = pending verification. Used for accounts payable processing and payment release.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value invoiced by the supplier for this PO line. Used for 3-way match variance analysis, accounts payable processing, and financial accrual reconciliation.']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this line item for which a supplier invoice has been received and matched. Used for 3-way match (PO quantity vs. GR quantity vs. invoice quantity), accounts payable processing, and payment settlement.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the product or material being procured on this line, as captured at the time of PO creation. May differ from the current PIM catalog description if the product was updated after PO issuance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PO line item record in the source ERP system. Used for incremental data loading (ETL), change tracking, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between PO issuance and expected delivery for this line item, as agreed with the supplier. Used for inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and supplier performance benchmarking.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent purchase order, used to uniquely identify and order line items within a PO. Enables multi-SKU PO navigation and EDI interchange alignment. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_SEQUENCE.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this purchase order line item. Tracks the lifecycle from open order through goods receipt, invoicing, and closure. Enables partial fulfillment monitoring and open PO reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_received|fully_received|invoiced|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of this line item (ordered_quantity × unit_price). Used for PO value tracking, budget commitment, accounts payable accruals, and financial reporting. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The supplier\'s minimum order quantity requirement for this SKU as defined in the purchase info record or vendor contract. Used to validate moq_compliance_flag and support procurement planning decisions.']
  moq_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered quantity on this line meets or exceeds the supplier\'s Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement. True = MOQ compliant; False = below MOQ threshold. Critical for supplier contract compliance and procurement workflow validation.']
  open_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Remaining quantity yet to be received against this PO line (ordered_quantity minus goods_receipt_quantity). Supports open PO reporting, inventory replenishment monitoring, and supplier follow-up workflows.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU ordered on this line item as agreed with the supplier. Used for MOQ compliance validation, inventory replenishment planning, and partial fulfillment tracking. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_QUANTITY.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'ERP plant or fulfillment center code to which this PO line item is being delivered. Identifies the receiving warehouse or distribution center in the fulfillment network. Used for inventory planning and logistics routing.']
  price_variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary difference between the PO agreed unit price and the actual invoiced price for this line item. A key procurement KPI used for cost control, supplier negotiation analysis, and financial reporting under SOX.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason a PO line item was rejected, cancelled, or blocked (e.g., quality failure, price discrepancy, supplier non-compliance). Used for procurement analytics and supplier performance management.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer requests delivery of this line item from the supplier. Used for supply chain planning, inventory replenishment scheduling, and SLA monitoring. May differ from confirmed delivery date.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational source system from which this PO line item record was ingested (e.g., SAP_S4HANA_PROD, ERP_EU, ERP_US). Supports data lineage tracking, multi-system reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'WMS storage location or warehouse bin code designated for receiving and putaway of this PO line item. Used for inventory allocation, pick-pack-ship operations, and FIFO inventory management.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated tax amount applicable to this PO line item based on the tax code and line total. Used for accounts payable tax accruals, VAT/GST reporting, and financial compliance.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax determination code assigned to this PO line item, used to calculate applicable taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax) during invoice verification. Sourced from ERP tax configuration and required for financial compliance reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the ordered quantity on this line (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, PK=Pack, KG=Kilogram, PAL=Pallet). Sourced from the ERP material master and used for WMS receiving and inventory allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|KG|LB|L|M|M2|M3|PAL|BOX|ROLL|SET|DOZ — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Negotiated unit price for the SKU as agreed with the supplier on this PO line. Used for line total calculation, invoice matching (3-way match), and procurement cost analysis. Expressed in the PO currency.']

  Note: 'Individual line-level detail within a purchase order. Captures line number, SKU/material number, ordered quantity, unit of measure, agreed unit price, line total, MOQ compliance flag, requested delivery date per line, confirmed delivery date, goods receipt quantity, invoice quantity, and line status. Enables granular tracking of multi-SKU POs and partial fulfillment scenarios common in e-commerce replenishment.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_contract {
  vendor_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the vendor contract record in the procurement data product. Primary key for the vendor_contract entity.']
  approver_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who provided final authorization and approval for this contract or its most recent amendment. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for procurement authorization controls.']
  buyer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for B2B contract management reports linking each supplier contract to the buying customer.account.']
  primary_vendor_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal procurement employee or category manager who owns and is accountable for this vendor contract. Used for contract governance, renewal notifications, and performance review assignments.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contract management: contracts are negotiated between a specific seller and a supplier; linking enables contract compliance and SLA reporting per seller.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor party who is the counterparty to this contract. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement domain.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments (change orders) that have been executed against this contract since its original execution. Provides a quick indicator of contract stability and negotiation history. Incremented each time a change order is approved.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its expiry date without requiring a new negotiation cycle. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action. Critical for contract lifecycle management and budget planning.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this contract document indicating the sensitivity of its commercial terms and conditions. Restricted: highly sensitive strategic terms. Confidential: standard commercial terms not for external disclosure. Internal: accessible to all internal staff. Public: terms disclosed publicly (rare for vendor contracts).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the vendor contract as assigned by the procurement or ERP system (e.g., SAP scheduling agreement or outline agreement number). Used in all vendor communications, purchase orders, and audit trails.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract governing its operational validity. Draft: under internal review and not yet binding. Active: fully executed and in force. Amended: modified via change order but still active. Suspended: temporarily halted pending resolution. Expired: reached end date without renewal. Terminated: ended before expiry by either party.']
  contract_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable title or short name of the vendor contract (e.g., \'Annual Packaging Materials Supply Agreement FY2025\'). Used for display in procurement dashboards and contract registers.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract by commercial structure. Framework agreements establish terms for future orders; blanket purchase orders cover recurring purchases up to a ceiling value; spot contracts are one-time buys; consignment contracts allow inventory held at buyer premises billed on consumption; scheduled agreements define delivery schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|blanket|spot|consignment|scheduled — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor contract record was first created in the procurement system (SAP S/4HANA or data lakehouse). Represents the system-of-record creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
