  sourcing_method varchar [note: 'Recommended or applied sourcing approach for fulfilling this requisition. Determines whether the buyer should reference an existing contract, use a catalog price, issue an RFQ, or execute a spot buy.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition was formally submitted for approval, transitioning from draft to pending_approval status. Key lifecycle event timestamp for SLA measurement and approval cycle time analytics.']
  supplier_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt for the preferred supplier. Used to validate the required_by_date feasibility and to calculate the latest PO creation date.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, KG=Kilogram, L=Litre). Aligns with GS1 and SAP UOM standards for inventory and procurement processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|M|M2|M3|PK|BX|DZ|HR|DAY — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the purchase requisition record was most recently modified, including status changes, quantity revisions, or approval actions. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']

  Note: 'Internal purchase requisition raised by a business unit, buyer, or automated replenishment system (e.g., min/max reorder point trigger) requesting procurement of goods or services. Captures requisition number, requester, cost center, requested SKU, quantity, required-by date, estimated cost, sourcing recommendation (preferred supplier, contract reference), approval chain with delegation rules, and requisition status (draft/pending_approval/approved/converted_to_po/rejected/cancelled). Initiates the procure-to-pay workflow and provides the audit trail linking demand signal to purchase commitment.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_price_list {
  supplier_price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier price list record. Primary key for this entity in the procurement domain Silver layer.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price‑list maintenance needs cost‑center attribution for budgeting of negotiated pricing programs.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ensures price‑list changes are reflected in the GL for expense forecasting and audit compliance.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Enables segment‑specific pricing; the pricing engine selects a price list based on the customer.segment of the buyer.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Pricing: sellers receive negotiated price lists from suppliers; linking allows price‑list selection and margin calculations per seller.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price list entry applies. Each price list record covers exactly one SKU.']
  superseded_by_supplier_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer supplier_price_list record that replaced this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables version chain traversal for price history analysis and audit. Null if this is the current active version.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who owns this price list. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor contract or purchasing outline agreement under which this price list is governed. Null if the price list is a standalone purchasing info record not tied to a formal contract.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this price list was formally approved by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX compliance audit trails and procurement governance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the procurement officer or manager who approved this price list for use in purchase order processing. Supports SOX procurement controls and audit trail requirements.']
  auto_po_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list is enabled for automatic price determination during purchase order creation in the ERP system. When True, the system automatically applies this price list during PO generation without manual price entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price and all pricing tiers are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX conversion in cost-of-goods calculations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The agreed percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net unit price. Represents the base-level trade discount negotiated with the supplier, separate from volume-tier discounts. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this price list becomes valid and can be used for automated purchase order price determination and cost-of-goods calculations. Aligns with the validity start date in the ERP purchasing info record.']
  erp_info_record_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (PIR) number corresponding to this price list entry. Enables direct traceability between the Silver layer data product and the ERP system of record for procurement price determination.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this price list is no longer valid for purchase order pricing. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no defined expiration. Used to trigger price list renewal workflows.']
  freight_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight/shipping costs are included in the quoted unit price. When True, no separate freight charge is added during PO creation. When False, freight is billed separately or calculated via the TMS.']
  has_volume_tiers boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list includes a volume discount schedule (quantity break tiers) in addition to the base unit price. When True, the associated supplier_price_list_tier records define the tiered pricing structure.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the supplier and the e-commerce platform for shipments under this price list (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterm_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., \'Port of Shanghai\' for FOB, \'Buyer Warehouse\' for DDP). Required to fully specify the delivery terms per ICC Incoterms 2020 rules.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The agreed supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the warehouse. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and SLA monitoring.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum quantity of the SKU that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for the agreed unit price. Orders below the MOQ may be rejected by the supplier or subject to a surcharge. Key procurement constraint for replenishment planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for procurement team annotations, special conditions, or supplier-communicated caveats associated with this price list (e.g., \'Price valid only for orders placed before end of quarter\', \'Subject to raw material surcharge above $X/tonne\').']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the SKU under this price list. Used for import duty calculation, customs compliance, trade restriction checks, and landed cost estimation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms code applicable to purchases under this price list (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Aligns with the payment terms configured in the ERP vendor master and accounts payable module.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the unit price represents the gross list price (before discounts), the net agreed price (after discounts), or the landed cost (inclusive of freight, duties, and insurance). Critical for correct COGS calculation and PO price validation.']
  price_list_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the price list indicating its commercial purpose. \'standard\' = regular agreed pricing; \'promotional\' = time-limited promotional pricing; \'seasonal\' = seasonal pricing adjustment; \'clearance\' = end-of-life or excess inventory pricing; \'emergency\' = expedited sourcing price.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the price list, such as \'Q1 2025 Electronics Pricing\' or \'Annual Volume Agreement - Supplier XYZ\'. Used for display and search in procurement portals.']
  price_list_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this price list, used in supplier communications, purchase order references, and ERP purchasing info records. Follows the format PL-{alphanumeric code}.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by its commercial basis. \'contract_based\' = tied to a formal vendor contract; \'standalone_pir\' = standalone Purchasing Info Record (PIR) in ERP; \'spot_quote\' = one-time quoted price; \'catalog\' = supplier-published catalog price; \'blanket_order\' = price under a blanket purchase order.']
  price_list_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this price list, enabling tracking of successive revisions for the same supplier-SKU combination. Incremented each time the supplier submits a revised pricing schedule.']
  price_tolerance_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable percentage deviation between the price list unit price and the actual invoice price before a three-way match exception is raised. Used in automated invoice verification to flag price discrepancies requiring manual review.']
  price_unit_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity basis for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Used to correctly compute per-unit cost when the supplier quotes prices in bulk multiples. Corresponds to the SAP Purchasing Info Record \'Price Unit\' field.']
  renewal_reminder_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before the expiry date at which a renewal reminder notification should be triggered to the procurement team. Supports proactive price list renewal management and prevents procurement disruption due to expired pricing.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the source document from which this price list was derived, such as a supplier quotation number, RFQ response number, or EDI catalog transmission ID. Provides traceability back to the originating supplier communication.']
  supplier_price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price list. \'active\' = currently valid for PO pricing; \'expired\' = past expiry date; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version; \'draft\' = under preparation; \'pending_approval\' = awaiting procurement approval; \'suspended\' = temporarily deactivated.']
  supplier_sku_code varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own part number or item code for this SKU, as used in the supplier\'s catalog and order acknowledgements. Required for EDI purchase order transmission and supplier invoice matching. Distinct from the internal SKU identifier.']
  supplier_sku_description varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s description of the SKU as it appears in their catalog or price list document. Used for three-way matching during invoice verification and for resolving discrepancies between internal and supplier item descriptions.']
  tax_classification_code varchar [note: 'The tax classification code assigned to this SKU-supplier combination for VAT/GST determination during purchase order processing. Maps to the tax condition type in the ERP pricing procedure.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'The number of quantity break tiers defined in the volume discount schedule for this price list. Zero if has_volume_tiers is False. Provides a quick summary of pricing complexity without joining to tier detail records.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The agreed supplier unit price for one unit of the SKU at the base quantity tier. This is the SSOT for supplier-agreed cost used in automated PO price validation and cost-of-goods (COGS) calculations. Does not represent the selling price or invoice amount.']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the SKU is priced and ordered (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the UOM defined in the product master and used in purchase order line items.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price list record was last modified. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Agreed pricing catalog between the e-commerce platform and a supplier for specific SKUs, maintained under a contract or as a standalone purchasing info record. Captures supplier, SKU, price list version, unit price, currency, quantity break tiers (volume discount schedules), discount percentage, effective date, expiry date, and price list status (active/expired/superseded). SSOT for supplier-agreed pricing used in automated PO price validation, cost-of-goods calculations, and price comparison during requisition-to-PO conversion. Does not own dynamic selling prices (pricing domain) or actual invoice amounts (supplier_invoice).'
}

Table procurement.goods_return {
  goods_return_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Return-to-Vendor (RTV) goods return transaction record. Primary key for this entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Returned‑goods audits ensure compliance, detect fraud, and validate supplier performance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing must allocate reversal amounts to the originating cost center for accurate cost accounting.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Links return transactions to the GL account to post reversal entries, required for financial reconciliation.']
  primary_goods_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal procurement buyer or purchasing agent who initiated and owns this RTV return transaction. Used for workflow routing, approval tracking, and performance reporting.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original Goods Receipt (GR) document that recorded the inbound delivery from the supplier. Establishes the SSOT link between the return and the original receipt event.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original Purchase Order (PO) against which the goods were received and are now being returned.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Enables Returns Management dashboard to associate returned items with SKU master data for warranty, inventory, and financial reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor to whom goods are being returned. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or fulfillment center from which the goods are being dispatched back to the supplier.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RTV return was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if approval is not yet granted or not required. Used for SOX internal control audit trails.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the vendor chargeback raised against the supplier for costs incurred due to the return (e.g., handling, inspection, re-stocking, logistics). Null if no chargeback is raised.']
  chargeback_raised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a vendor chargeback has been formally raised against the supplier for this return (True = chargeback raised, False = no chargeback). Triggers the vendor chargeback workflow in accounts payable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this goods return record was first created in the ERP/procurement system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_issued_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the supplier issued the credit memo for the returned goods. Null until the return reaches \'credit_issued\' status. Triggers accounts payable offset in the finance domain.']
  credit_memo_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary value of the credit memo received from the supplier. May differ from the expected amount due to restocking fees, partial credit, or supplier disputes. Variance triggers chargeback resolution workflow.']
  credit_memo_expected_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected monetary value of the credit memo to be issued by the supplier for the returned goods, calculated based on the original purchase price and return quantity. Used for accounts payable offset and vendor chargeback workflows.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued credit memo document number received as financial settlement for the returned goods. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and vendor chargeback tracking in the finance domain.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this return record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the currency of the original Purchase Order.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs or export declaration document number required for cross-border return shipments. Null for domestic returns. Required for WTO and EU Electronic Commerce Directive compliance.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the RTV return was formally initiated by the procurement team. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction. Used for SLA tracking and supplier performance measurement.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the quality inspector detailing defects, damage observations, or non-conformance findings that support the return decision. Used for supplier quality management and dispute resolution.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection performed on goods prior to initiating the return. PASS indicates goods confirmed defective/returnable, FAIL indicates goods do not meet return criteria, PENDING indicates inspection in progress, WAIVED indicates inspection bypassed per supplier agreement.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this goods return record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier lot/batch number of the returned goods. Critical for traceability in quality defect scenarios, product recalls, and supplier quality audits.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product being returned, as recorded at the time of the return. Denormalized for operational reporting without requiring a product catalog join.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged by the supplier as a restocking fee for accepting the return. Deducted from the credit memo amount. Tracked for supplier contract compliance and procurement cost management.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued authorization number approving the return of goods. Required before physical shipment of returned goods to the supplier. Distinct from the internal RTV number.']
  return_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider used to ship the returned goods back to the supplier (e.g., FedEx, UPS, DHL, XPO Logistics).']
  return_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU being returned to the supplier in this RTV transaction. Must be a positive integer and cannot exceed the originally received quantity on the referenced Goods Receipt.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the reason for returning goods to the supplier. Drives supplier quality management, vendor chargeback workflows, and procurement analytics. Values: QUALITY_DEFECT (goods fail quality inspection), OVERSHIPMENT (supplier shipped excess quantity), WRONG_ITEM (incorrect SKU received), DAMAGED_IN_TRANSIT (goods damaged during inbound delivery), EXPIRED_GOODS (goods received past expiry), CONTRACT_VIOLATION (supplier breached contract terms).']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional detail about the reason for the goods return, supplementing the standardized return reason code. Used for supplier dispute resolution and quality audit trails.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RTV goods return transaction. Tracks progression from initiation through supplier receipt and credit issuance. Values: initiated (RTV created, pending shipment), shipped (goods dispatched to supplier), received_by_supplier (supplier confirmed receipt), credit_issued (credit memo received and applied), cancelled (return voided).']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return scope. FULL_RETURN indicates all received units are being returned; PARTIAL_RETURN indicates a subset of received units; REPLACEMENT_REQUEST indicates goods are returned in exchange for correct replacement items from the supplier.']
  return_warehouse_location varchar [note: 'Specific storage location or bin within the warehouse where returned goods are staged prior to dispatch to the supplier. Used by WMS for pick-pack-ship operations.']
  rtv_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the Return-to-Vendor (RTV) transaction. Used in supplier communications, logistics documentation, and credit memo reconciliation. Format: RTV-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Individual unit serial number for serialized products being returned. Applicable for high-value or regulated items requiring unit-level traceability. Null for non-serialized goods.']
  shipped_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the returned goods were physically dispatched from the warehouse to the supplier. Null until the return reaches \'shipped\' status.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the return shipment. FTL (Full Truck Load) for large returns, LTL (Less Than Truck Load) for partial loads, PARCEL for small packages, EXPRESS for urgent returns, FREIGHT for heavy/bulk goods.']
  supplier_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the supplier representative managing this return. Used for return authorization communications and credit memo follow-up. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR.']
  supplier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier representative responsible for managing this return authorization and credit memo issuance. Used for procurement communication and dispute escalation.']
  supplier_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the supplier\'s location to which goods are being returned. Used for customs documentation, international trade compliance, and logistics routing.']
  supplier_received_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the supplier confirmed receipt of the returned goods. Null until the return reaches \'received_by_supplier\' status. Used to calculate supplier return processing lead time.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued tracking number for the return shipment. Enables real-time shipment status monitoring via the Transportation Management System and provides proof of dispatch for supplier disputes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the return quantity (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Litre, M=Metre, BOX=Box, PALLET=Pallet). Aligns with the unit of measure on the original Purchase Order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|M|BOX|PALLET — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_return_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Per-unit price at which the goods are being returned to the supplier, typically matching the original Purchase Order unit price. Used to calculate the expected credit memo amount and for procurement cost analysis.']

  Note: 'Return-to-vendor (RTV) transaction record for goods returned to a supplier due to quality defects, overshipment, or wrong items received. Captures return authorization number, return reason code, returned SKU, quantity, original GR reference, return shipment carrier, tracking number, credit memo expected amount, and return status (initiated/shipped/received_by_supplier/credit_issued). SSOT boundary: procurement owns the RTV initiation and supplier credit tracking; service domain owns customer-facing RMA; inventory domain owns stock adjustment on return dispatch. Supports supplier quality management and vendor chargeback workflows.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_onboarding {
  supplier_onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the supplier onboarding workflow record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal procurement team member or user responsible for managing and progressing this supplier onboarding workflow to completion.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor master record being onboarded. Links the onboarding workflow to the supplier party entity.']
  tax_id varchar [note: 'Government-issued Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) for the supplier. Required for tax reporting, SOX compliance, and procure-to-pay settlement in SAP S/4HANA.']
  actual_activation_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the supplier was fully activated and set to operational status in the ERP and Seller Management Portal. Compared against target_activation_date for SLA performance measurement.']
  application_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted their initial onboarding application via the Seller Management Portal. Marks the official start of the onboarding workflow and SLA clock.']
  bank_account_name varchar [note: 'Name of the account holder as registered with the supplier\'s bank. Must match the supplier legal name for payment validation and fraud prevention.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s bank account number used for payment settlement and disbursements in the procure-to-pay process. Stored in tokenized/masked form per PCI DSS requirements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'American Bankers Association (ABA) routing transit number or SWIFT/BIC code identifying the supplier\'s bank for ACH and wire transfer settlement. Required for procure-to-pay processing.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason(s) why the onboarding workflow is currently blocked or on hold (e.g., missing tax certificate, KYB verification failure, incomplete bank details). Populated when onboarding_status is \'blocked\' or \'on_hold\'.']
  business_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued business registration or incorporation number for the supplier entity. Required for Know Your Business (KYB) verification and regulatory due diligence under FTC and GDPR vendor management requirements.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory and policy compliance review for the supplier, covering FTC advertising standards, GDPR data processing agreements, CPSC product safety, and internal vendor code of conduct.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the supplier is legally incorporated or registered (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives applicable regulatory framework and trade compliance rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier onboarding record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and compliance reporting.']
  document_checklist_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required onboarding documents (business registration, tax certificates, insurance certificates, bank letters, signed agreements) have been collected and validated.']
  edi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) for automated exchange of purchase orders, invoices, and advance ship notices. Drives integration setup decisions in the ERP procure-to-pay workflow.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The EDI messaging standard supported by the supplier (e.g., ANSI X12, UN/EDIFACT, cXML, RosettaNet). Populated only when edi_capable is true. Determines the integration protocol configured in the ERP.']
  erp_vendor_code varchar [note: 'The vendor account code assigned to the supplier in SAP S/4HANA upon system setup completion. Used to link the onboarding record to the ERP vendor master (LFA1) for procure-to-pay processing.']
  fulfillment_model varchar [note: 'The fulfillment model agreed with the supplier: FBA (Fulfillment By Amazon-style — platform warehouses and ships), FBM (Fulfilled By Merchant — supplier ships directly), dropship, cross_dock, or consignment. Determines WMS and TMS integration requirements.']
  gdpr_dpa_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has signed a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) as required when the supplier processes personal data on behalf of the business. Mandatory for EU-facing suppliers.']
  insurance_certificate_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier\'s required insurance certificates (e.g., general liability, product liability) have been collected and verified as valid. Required for CPSC product safety compliance.']
  kyb_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Know Your Business (KYB) identity and legitimacy verification check performed on the supplier entity. Required for FTC compliance and fraud prevention in vendor onboarding.']
  kyb_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the KYB verification check was completed and a pass/fail determination was recorded. Provides audit trail for regulatory due diligence.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier onboarding record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt. Captured during onboarding for inventory replenishment planning and WMS configuration.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) agreed with the supplier — the smallest quantity that can be ordered per purchase order line. Captured during onboarding for procurement planning and ERP configuration.']
  onboarding_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number for the supplier onboarding case (e.g., ONB-2024-000123). Used in communications with the supplier and internal stakeholders.']
  onboarding_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the supplier initiated the onboarding process: portal_self_service (Seller Management Portal), procurement_team (internal sourcing), referral, api_integration (B2B API), or trade_show. Used for onboarding funnel analytics.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the supplier onboarding workflow lifecycle: application (initial submission), kyb_verification (Know Your Business identity check), document_collection (gathering required legal/tax/banking docs), compliance_review (FTC/GDPR due diligence), system_setup (ERP/EDI provisioning), activated (fully onboarded and operational).']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the onboarding workflow record, distinct from the stage. Indicates whether the workflow is actively progressing, paused, blocked by an issue, successfully completed, or cancelled.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which the supplier will be paid (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Configured during onboarding for ERP payment run setup.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms for the supplier relationship, defining the number of days after invoice receipt before payment is due (e.g., net_30, net_60). Configured in SAP S/4HANA during system setup stage.']
  portal_access_provisioned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has been granted access credentials to the Seller Management Portal for order management, invoice submission, and performance monitoring.']
  portal_access_provisioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier\'s Seller Management Portal access credentials were provisioned and activated. Used for access audit trails per ISO 27001 requirements.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact used for onboarding communications, document requests, and portal access provisioning. Subject to GDPR data minimization principles.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization responsible for the onboarding process and ongoing relationship management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary supplier contact in E.164 international format. Used for onboarding verification calls and urgent communications.']
  product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product category codes or names that the supplier is authorized to supply on the platform (e.g., \'Electronics, Home & Garden\'). Drives catalog management and PIM system configuration.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for rejecting the supplier onboarding application (e.g., failed KYB, sanctions list match, non-compliance with GDPR DPA requirements). Populated when onboarding_status is \'cancelled\' or compliance_review_status is \'rejected\'.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the OFAC/sanctions list screening performed on the supplier entity and its beneficial owners. A \'flagged\' status blocks onboarding progression until legal review is completed.']
  target_activation_date date [note: 'Target date by which the supplier should be fully activated and operational in the system. Used for SLA tracking, procurement planning, and escalation management.']

  Note: 'Supplier onboarding workflow record tracking the end-to-end process of qualifying and activating a new vendor. Captures onboarding stage (application/KYB_verification/document_collection/compliance_review/system_setup/activated), business registration number, tax ID, bank account details, EDI capability flag, portal access status, onboarding owner, target activation date, and blocking reasons. Ensures regulatory compliance with FTC and GDPR vendor due diligence requirements.'
}

Table procurement.approved_vendor_list {
  approved_vendor_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each record in the approved vendor list.']
  approved_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.supplier']
  primary_approved_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor (supplier) referenced in the approved list.']
  superseded_approved_vendor_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on approved_vendor_list (superseded_approved_vendor_list_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor\'s approval within the list.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor was officially approved.']
  compliance_iso_certified boolean [note: 'True if the vendor holds a current ISO certification relevant to the business.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s contract terminates or expires.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned to the vendor contract.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s contract with the enterprise begins.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract.']
  cpsc_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration of the vendor\'s CPSC product safety certification.']
  cpsc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with Consumer Product Safety Commission regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approved vendor record was first created.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Logical source identifier used for data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s approved status becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s approved status expires, requiring re‑evaluation.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the vendor adheres to GDPR data‑protection obligations.']
  is_diversity_supplier boolean [note: 'True if the vendor qualifies under the company\'s diversity supplier program.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is marked as a preferred supplier for strategic sourcing.']
  iso_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s ISO certification.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent periodic review of the vendor\'s approval status.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days the vendor takes to deliver after order receipt.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the vendor.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for payments to the vendor.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the agreed payment terms (e.g., NET30, EOM).']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor complies with PCI DSS requirements.']
  pci_dss_expiry date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s PCI DSS compliance certification expires.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  qualification_criteria_met varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific criteria the vendor satisfied for approval.']
  qualification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score reflecting how well the vendor meets qualification criteria.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating assigned to the vendor based on financial, compliance, and performance factors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the vendor data (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Broad product or service category the vendor supplies (e.g., electronics, apparel).']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on its business role.']

  Note: 'Master list of pre-qualified and approved vendors eligible for purchase order issuance, capturing approval status, qualification criteria met, and expiration dates.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_center_contract {
  supplier_center_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplierCenterContract association']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fulfillment center']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier‑center contract']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes active']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the contract ends']
  sla_target_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Target fulfillment time in minutes agreed in the contract']

  Note: 'This association captures the contractual agreement between a supplier and a fulfillment center. It records the contract number, effective and expiry dates, and the SLA target time that apply to each supplier‑center pairing.. Existence Justification: Suppliers enter formal contracts with fulfillment centers. A single supplier can have contracts with many centers, and a single center can hold contracts with many suppliers. Each contract records its own number, effective/expiry dates, and SLA target time, which are attributes of the relationship itself.'
}

Table procurement.evaluation_period {
  evaluation_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for evaluation_period']
  prior_evaluation_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on evaluation_period (prior_evaluation_period_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the evaluation period record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the evaluation period.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date on which the evaluation period is active; nullable for open‑ended periods.']
  evaluation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the evaluation period based on its recurrence pattern.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the evaluation period repeats or is scheduled.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the default selection for new supplier evaluations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the evaluation period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the evaluation period in procurement processes.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the evaluation period.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date on which the evaluation period is active.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the evaluation period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the evaluation period record.']
  weighting decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight (percentage) applied to this period when aggregating supplier scores.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for evaluation_period. Referenced by evaluation_period_id.'
}

Table service.service_support_case {
  service_support_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer support case or service ticket. Primary key for the service support case entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Account‑level case reporting KPI for B2B customers requires linking each support case directly to the billing account.']
  assigned_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent currently assigned to work on this case. May be null if case is unassigned or in queue.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign Effectiveness Support Impact Report linking each support case to the marketing campaign that caused the issue.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Support case investigations often need the carrier responsible for the shipment to resolve delivery issues; agents reference carrier_id for accurate root‑cause analysis.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: When a case concerns service level or transit time, the specific carrier service used must be known for SLA compliance reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Support cases often arise from issues at a specific fulfillment center (e.g., inventory or pickup problems); linking enables Center‑Level case reporting and SLA tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each support case to be charged to a cost center for internal accounting.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Customer service cases about delivery delays, tracking errors, or lost parcels need a direct reference to the shipment for root‑cause analysis and compensation decisions.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this support case, if the case relates to a specific order (e.g., shipping issue, order cancellation, item defect).']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Support Case Investigation process records the specific content item that triggered the case, enabling root‑cause analysis and content quality reports.']
  knowledge_article_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the knowledge base article used to resolve this case. Links case resolution to documented solutions for knowledge management and self-service improvement.']
  lead_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.lead. Business justification: Needed for Lead‑to‑Support Conversion tracking, showing which marketing leads become support cases.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Required for support case reporting: each case must reference the specific marketplace transaction that triggered the issue, enabling accurate SLA tracking and root‑cause analysis.']
  parent_case_service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent case if this case is a child or follow-up case. Used for linking related cases and tracking case hierarchies (e.g., original case and subsequent escalation or related inquiry).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of support services links cases to profit centers to measure contribution.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Required for Promotion Impact on Support Cases report, linking each case to the promotional campaign that triggered the purchase, enabling analysis of support volume per campaign.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Privacy incident case tracking requires linking each support case to the specific regulation (e.g., GDPR) for reporting and audit compliance.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA record if this support case resulted in or is associated with a product return authorization.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketplace seller if this case involves a third-party seller transaction or marketplace dispute. Null for first-party sales.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to this support case, if the case involves a specific product (e.g., defect, return, compatibility issue).']
  assigned_queue varchar [note: 'The service queue or team to which this case is currently assigned (e.g., Tier1_Support, Billing_Team, Returns_Processing, Escalations, VIP_Support).']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique case identifier displayed to customers and agents. Typically follows a format like CS-12345678 or CASE-0001234567.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the support case by the nature of the customer issue: billing inquiry, shipping problem, product defect, account access issue, returns processing, refund request, marketplace dispute, or general inquiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|product_defect|account_access|returns|refund|marketplace_dispute|general_inquiry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was formally closed and removed from active case inventory. May occur after resolution once customer confirms satisfaction or after a waiting period.']
  compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation or goodwill credit provided to the customer as part of case resolution (e.g., account credit, discount voucher, refund). Null if no compensation was provided.']
  contact_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of times the support team attempted to contact the customer regarding this case. Used for tracking customer responsiveness and case aging.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the customer initiated the support case (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the support case was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the case lifecycle and SLA clock.']
  csat_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Measures customer satisfaction with the support experience and resolution quality.']
  customer_entitlement_tier varchar [note: 'The service entitlement level of the customer at the time the case was created: standard (basic support), premium (priority support), VIP (dedicated support), enterprise (white-glove service). Influences SLA targets and routing.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and to what level the case has been escalated: not_escalated (standard handling), tier2 (specialist support), tier3 (expert/engineering), management (supervisor review), executive (C-level attention).']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback comments provided by the customer in post-case surveys. Captures qualitative insights about the support experience, agent performance, and areas for improvement.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the first substantive response was provided to the customer by an agent. Key SLA metric for measuring initial response time.']
  interaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer-agent interactions (calls, emails, chats) associated with this case. Indicates case complexity and effort required for resolution.']
  is_vip_case boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this case involves a VIP or high-value customer requiring special handling, expedited service, or executive attention. True for VIP cases, False otherwise.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the case was handled (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to any field in the case record. Used for tracking case activity and audit purposes.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected from the customer after case resolution, on a scale of 0 to 10. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the company based on the support experience.']
  originating_channel varchar [note: 'The customer service channel through which the case was initially created: phone call, email, live chat, social media (Twitter, Facebook), self-service portal, or mobile app.']
  outcome_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the case: resolved (issue successfully addressed), unresolved (could not be resolved), duplicate (merged with another case), transferred (moved to another system or team), withdrawn (customer cancelled request).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the case based on urgency and impact. P1 (critical/urgent), P2 (high), P3 (medium), P4 (low). Determines SLA targets and routing rules.']
  reopened_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. Indicates resolution quality issues or recurring customer problems.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the resolution provided to the customer, actions taken, root cause identified, and any follow-up required. Populated when case is resolved or closed.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was marked as resolved, indicating that a solution was provided to the customer. Key SLA metric for measuring time-to-resolution.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause identified during case resolution (e.g., system_error, process_gap, product_defect, user_error, policy_exception, carrier_issue). Used for trend analysis and continuous improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_error|process_gap|product_defect|user_error|policy_exception|carrier_issue|payment_failure|inventory_issue|data_quality — promote to reference product]']
  sentiment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score derived from customer communications (emails, chat transcripts) during the case lifecycle. Scale typically -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive).']
  service_support_case_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the customer issue, inquiry, or request as captured during case creation. May include customer-provided details, agent notes, and initial diagnostic information.']
  service_support_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the support case: new (just created), open (assigned), pending_customer (awaiting customer response), pending_internal (awaiting internal action), in_progress (actively being worked), escalated (moved to higher tier), resolved (solution provided), closed (finalized), cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|pending_customer|pending_internal|in_progress|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this case breached its SLA targets for first response time or resolution time. True indicates SLA was breached, False indicates SLA was met.']
  sla_policy_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the SLA policy governing response and resolution time targets for this case, based on customer tier, case priority, and case type.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary or subject line describing the customer issue or inquiry. Typically 100-200 characters.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a customer support case or service ticket. Tracks the full lifecycle from creation through resolution including case type (billing, shipping, product defect, account access, returns, marketplace dispute referral), priority level (P1-P4), originating channel (phone, email, chat, social, self-service), assigned agent, current queue, escalation status, SLA policy reference, customer entitlement tier, first response timestamp, resolution timestamp, CSAT score, NPS score, and outcome disposition (resolved, unresolved, duplicate, transferred). Serves as the central operational record linking all service interactions, escalations, RMAs, refunds, and feedback for a single customer issue.'
}

Table service.case_interaction {
  case_interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the case interaction record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Interaction logs are aggregated by account to compute enterprise support metrics and SLA compliance per account.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer involved in this interaction. Links to customer master entity.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Interaction logs need the transaction context to surface correct communication history in support dashboards.']
  primary_case_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent who handled this interaction. Links to employee or agent entity.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent support case that this interaction belongs to. Links to the service case entity.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of file attachments associated with this interaction (screenshots, documents, logs).']
  attachment_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of attachment identifiers or URIs pointing to files stored in external document management system.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the interaction occurred: phone, email, live chat, mobile app, web portal, or social media platform.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'The specific contact method used for this interaction (phone number, email address, chat handle, social media handle).']
  content_summary varchar [note: 'Summarized content or transcript of the interaction, capturing key points discussed without full verbatim text.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country from which the interaction originated (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the system in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score provided by the customer for this specific interaction, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Direction of the interaction: inbound (customer-initiated) or outbound (agent-initiated).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the interaction in seconds, applicable for calls and chat sessions.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction resulted in an escalation to a higher support tier or management (True/False).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for escalating the interaction, if escalation occurred.']
  external_interaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this interaction in the external source system, used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  feedback_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback or comment provided by the customer about this interaction.']
  full_transcript_reference varchar [note: 'Reference pointer or URI to the full transcript or recording stored in external system (e.g., CDN (Content Delivery Network), document management system).']
  interaction_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the interaction, typically formatted as INT- followed by numeric sequence.']
  interaction_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this interaction within the parent case lifecycle, starting from 1.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the interaction: completed, pending follow-up, escalated to higher tier, abandoned by customer, or transferred to another agent.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the interaction event occurred in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of interaction event: call, email, chat, SMS, social media, or web form submission.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments added by the agent for internal reference, not visible to the customer.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code representing the language used in the interaction (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was last modified or updated in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the customer for this interaction, typically on a scale of 0-10.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome or result of the interaction: resolved, unresolved, escalated, referred to another department, information provided, or callback scheduled.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this interaction: low, medium, high, or critical.']
  resolution_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a resolution or answer was provided during this interaction (True/False).']
  response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds taken by the agent to provide the first substantive response after interaction initiation.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score for the interaction, typically ranging from -1.00 (negative) to +1.00 (positive), derived from NLP (Natural Language Processing) analysis of interaction content.']
  sla_breach_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or explanation for SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, if the interaction did not meet SLA targets.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction met the defined SLA (Service Level Agreement) targets for response and resolution time (True/False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that captured this interaction (e.g., CRM (Customer Relationship Management), call center platform, chat system).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose or topic of the interaction.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or labels applied to categorize or classify the interaction (e.g., billing, technical, complaint, refund).']
  transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the interaction was transferred to another agent or department (True/False).']
  wait_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds the customer waited before the interaction was answered or acknowledged by an agent.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every individual interaction or communication event within a support case lifecycle. Tracks inbound and outbound messages, call logs, chat transcripts, email threads, internal notes, and agent responses. Stores interaction channel, direction (inbound/outbound), agent ID, timestamp, content summary, attachment references, and interaction outcome. Enables full audit trail of case communications and supports agent performance measurement.'
}

Table service.rma {
  rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return merchandise authorization record. Primary key for the RMA entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: RMA processing often needs the customer\'s account to validate credit limits and apply account‑level return policies.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Return processing reports require linking each RMA to the carrier handling the return; replaces the free‑text return_carrier field.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Return RMA tracking includes the specific carrier service tier used, enabling cost and SLA analysis for returned shipments.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing costs are allocated to cost centers for internal budgeting and expense tracking.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer initiating the return. Links RMA to the customer master record for service history and analytics.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: RMA processing requires the original fulfillment order to reconcile inventory, validate return eligibility, and generate accurate financial adjustments.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Return management must verify compliance with consumer protection obligations; linking each RMA to its compliance_obligation enables audit of policy adherence.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability of return handling is measured by linking each RMA to a profit center.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse facility designated to receive and process the returned item. Used for routing and capacity planning.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this RMA requires manual approval by customer service or management. Triggered by policy exceptions or high-value returns.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service representative or manager who approved the RMA request. Used for audit trail and performance tracking.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA request was approved by the system or customer service. Marks the start of the return fulfillment process.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA case was fully closed and all associated workflows completed. Marks the end of the return lifecycle for reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this RMA record. Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.']
  customer_declared_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the returned item as declared by the customer at the time of RMA request. Used for initial eligibility assessment and compared against inspection findings.']
  customer_service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by customer service representatives regarding the RMA case. Captures context, customer interactions, and special handling instructions.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the returned item will be handled post-inspection. Drives inventory allocation, financial write-offs, and reverse logistics.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Estimated date by which the returned item is expected to arrive at the warehouse. Used for receiving planning and exception alerts.']
  inspected_condition varchar [note: 'Actual condition of the returned item as determined by warehouse inspection team. Used to validate customer declaration and determine disposition.']
  inspection_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse inspection of the returned item was completed. Triggers disposition decision and refund processing.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by warehouse inspection team regarding the physical condition and findings of the returned item.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the physical inspection process for the returned item. Tracks warehouse receiving and quality control workflow.']
  is_within_policy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the RMA request complies with the applicable return policy rules. Used for auto-approval workflows and exception handling.']
  label_issued_date date [note: 'Date when the return shipping label was generated and provided to the customer. Used for tracking customer action and carrier pickup scheduling.']
  label_paid_by varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying the return shipping cost. Determines financial settlement and customer experience.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  received_date date [note: 'Actual date when the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Triggers inspection workflow and service level measurement.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount to be refunded to the customer in the transaction currency. May differ from original order amount due to restocking fees or partial returns.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund will be issued to the customer. Drives payment processing workflow and customer communication.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'Date when the refund transaction was successfully processed and funds were released to the customer. Used for financial reconciliation and customer satisfaction tracking.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the refund transaction. Tracks financial settlement workflow and customer notification triggers.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Date when the customer initiated the RMA request. Used for return window calculation and service level tracking.']
  restocking_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, deducted from the refund amount. Applied per return policy and product category.']
  return_label_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost of the return shipping label in the transaction currency. Used for return cost analysis and customer charge-back decisions.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the return. Used for root cause analysis, product quality tracking, and return rate analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|not_as_described|damaged_in_transit|no_longer_needed|size_fit_issue|buyer_remorse|duplicate_order|late_delivery|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the return shipment. Enables real-time visibility into return transit status.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return resolution method requested by the customer or determined by policy. Drives downstream financial and fulfillment workflows.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from original order delivery date within which the return must be initiated per policy. Used for eligibility validation.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique RMA number displayed to customers and customer service representatives. Externally-known identifier for tracking return authorization.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks the return authorization workflow from initial request through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|rejected|label_issued|in_transit|received|inspecting|completed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Return Merchandise Authorization master record managing the end-to-end RMA lifecycle for e-commerce returns. Captures RMA number, originating order reference, return reason code, return type (refund, exchange, store credit), customer-declared condition, carrier return label details, expected return window, actual receipt date, inspection status, disposition decision (restock, liquidate, destroy), and financial settlement status. SSOT for all return authorization records across the platform.'
}

Table service.rma_line {
  rma_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RMA line item. Primary key for the RMA line detail record.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the item being returned. Links to the product catalog.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new order created for the exchange shipment, if applicable. Links the return to the replacement order.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original order line item being returned. Links the return to the original purchase transaction.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Operational need: returned items must be tied to the exact lot they originated from; adding lot_id replaces lot_number for precise inventory reconciliation.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent RMA authorization under which this line item is processed. Links this line to the RMA header.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Needed for accurate inventory and refund calculations; RMA line must reference the SKU entity to determine restocking fees, unit price, and stock adjustments.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific warehouse location where the returned item was received and inspected. Supports multi-warehouse return processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RMA line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Ensures proper currency handling for international returns.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision for the returned item. Determines how the item will be handled after inspection and processing.']
  exchange_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has requested an exchange rather than a refund for this item. True if exchange requested, false if refund only.']
  exchange_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the replacement product requested by the customer if this is an exchange. Null if this is a refund-only return.']
  inspected_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse staff member who performed the inspection of this returned item. Supports quality control and accountability.']
  inspected_date date [note: 'The date the returned item completed quality inspection. Marks the completion of the inspection process and readiness for disposition.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes recorded by warehouse inspection staff regarding the condition, completeness, and quality of the returned item. Supports disposition decisions.']
  item_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized assessment of the physical condition of the returned item upon receipt at the warehouse. Determines restocking eligibility and disposition.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this RMA line item. Tracks the line through the return workflow from initial request to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING_RECEIPT|RECEIVED|INSPECTING|APPROVED|REJECTED|REFUNDED|EXCHANGED|CLOSED — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_subtotal decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount for this line before taxes and fees, calculated as unit price multiplied by quantity approved. Represents the base refund amount.']
  line_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The tax amount to be refunded for this line item. Calculated based on the approved quantity and applicable tax rates.']
  quantity_approved varchar [note: 'The number of units approved for refund or exchange after inspection. May be less than quantity received if some units fail inspection criteria.']
  quantity_received varchar [note: 'The actual number of units physically received back from the customer for this SKU. May differ from quantity requested if customer sends partial return.']
  quantity_requested varchar [note: 'The number of units the customer has requested to return for this SKU. Represents the customer\'s initial return request quantity.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and refund processing timelines.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total refund amount for this line item including subtotal, tax, minus any restocking fees. Represents the actual amount to be credited to the customer.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'The date the refund for this line item was processed and initiated to the customer\'s payment method. Marks completion of the financial return transaction.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this line item was rejected for refund or exchange, if applicable. Provides transparency to customer and supports dispute resolution.']
  restocking_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item is in suitable condition to be restocked and resold as new inventory. True if eligible for resale, false if must be liquidated or disposed.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The restocking fee charged to the customer for this line item, if applicable. Typically applied for non-defective returns or opened items per return policy.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the customer is returning this specific item. Used for return analytics and quality improvement.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer or service agent describing the specific reason for returning this item.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the specific unit being returned, if applicable for serialized products. Enables unit-level tracking and warranty validation.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The specific product SKU being returned. Identifies the exact product variant at the most granular inventory level.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original unit price paid by the customer for this SKU at the time of purchase. Used to calculate refund amounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RMA line record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail record for each SKU included in an RMA. Captures the specific product SKU, quantity requested for return, quantity actually received, unit return reason, item condition on receipt, restocking eligibility flag, refund amount per line, exchange SKU if applicable, and inspection notes. Supports multi-item returns under a single RMA authorization and enables SKU-level return analytics and inventory disposition.'
}

Table service.service_refund {
  service_refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service refund record. Primary key for the service refund entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Refunds must be associated with the billing account for financial reconciliation and audit reporting.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the refund or compensation. Links to the customer master record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Refund transactions must be posted to the general ledger for financial reconciliation and reporting.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original order associated with this refund. Links the refund to the transaction being remediated.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Refunds are processed against the original marketplace transaction; linking provides auditability and financial reconciliation across systems.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Refund processing is subject to financial compliance (e.g., AML); associating refunds with the relevant compliance_obligation supports regulatory reporting.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Enables audit of refunds by payment method, supporting compliance reports and method‑level performance metrics.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment domain transaction record that executed the financial refund. Links service authorization to payment execution.']
