  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Needed for Refund Pricing Source Audit, tracking which price list was used for the original sale to ensure correct refund calculations and compliance.']
  primary_service_issuing_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who initiated the refund request. Links to the employee or agent record.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA that initiated this refund or compensation. Links the refund to the originating return case.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case that authorized this refund or compensation. Links to the support ticket or inquiry.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the refund or compensation issued to the customer. Represents the gross amount before any adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Authorization state of the refund request. Indicates whether the refund has been approved and by what authority level.']
  approval_tier varchar [note: 'Authorization level required for this refund based on amount thresholds. Defines the approval hierarchy tier.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was authorized by the agent or manager. Null if still pending approval or if rejected.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was successfully settled and funds were returned to the customer. Marks the end of the refund lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount. Defines the monetary unit of the refund value.']
  customer_satisfaction_impact varchar [note: 'Assessed impact of the refund on customer satisfaction based on follow-up surveys or agent assessment. Used for service quality tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the compensation instrument (store credit, gift card, coupon) can no longer be redeemed. Null for direct refunds with no expiration.']
  is_goodwill_gesture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund was issued as a proactive goodwill gesture rather than in response to a specific complaint or return. True for retention offers.']
  is_retention_offer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compensation was issued as part of a customer retention strategy to prevent churn. True for proactive retention gestures.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was most recently updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  loyalty_points_granted varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points awarded as compensation. Zero if compensation was not in the form of loyalty points.']
  manager_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund required manager override of standard policy or amount thresholds. True if exception was granted.']
  net_refund_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost to the business including refund amount and processing fees. Used for financial impact analysis and reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments from service agents regarding the refund. Used for context and audit trail documentation.']
  nps_survey_response varchar [note: 'Customer NPS score (0-10) collected after refund resolution. Null if no survey response was received. Used for service quality measurement.']
  original_payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment method used in the original order. Used to determine refund routing and processing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|gift_card|store_credit|cash_on_delivery|buy_now_pay_later — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approving manager for policy override or exception. Required when manager_override_flag is true.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment system for execution. Marks handoff from service to payment domain.']
  processing_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by payment processor or financial institution for processing the refund transaction. Represents cost to business.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of why the refund or compensation was issued. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective_product|wrong_item_shipped|damaged_in_transit|late_delivery|service_failure|customer_dissatisfaction|pricing_error|goodwill_gesture|retention_offer|policy_exception — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the specific reason for issuing the refund or compensation. Free-text field for agent notes.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'Unique code issued for store credit, gift card, or coupon compensation. Used by customer to claim the value. Confidential business data.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether non-monetary compensation (store credit, gift card, coupon) has been used by the customer. Not applicable for direct refunds.']
  redemption_value_remaining decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unredeemed value for partial-use compensation instruments like store credit or gift cards. Null for one-time refunds.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund or compensation is delivered to the customer. Defines the channel for value return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original_payment_method|store_credit|gift_card|bank_transfer|check|loyalty_account|promotional_code — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the refund. Used in customer communications and tracking.']
  remediation_type varchar [note: 'Category of customer remediation issued. Defines the form of compensation provided to the customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full_refund|partial_refund|store_credit|gift_card|coupon_code|free_shipping_voucher|loyalty_points|goodwill_compensation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was initially requested by the customer or initiated by the service agent. Marks the start of the refund lifecycle.']
  service_refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the refund. Tracks the refund through authorization, processing, and completion stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processing|completed|failed|redeemed|expired|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  settlement_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment gateway or financial system confirming the refund transaction was settled. Links to payment domain records.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or channel where the refund request was initiated. Used for data lineage and integration tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|oms|rma_system|service_portal|call_center|chat_system|email_system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing all customer remediation, refunds, and compensation issued through the service domain. Covers full refunds, partial refunds, store credits, gift cards, coupon codes, free shipping vouchers, loyalty point grants, and goodwill compensation for service failures or proactive retention gestures. Tracks remediation type (refund, exchange credit, compensation, goodwill gesture, retention offer), amount/value, currency, method (original payment method, store credit, gift card), status lifecycle (pending, approved, processing, completed, failed, redeemed, expired), initiating RMA or case reference, approval workflow with authorization thresholds by amount tier, issuing agent, manager override flag, redemption status, expiry date, compensation reason category, and settlement confirmation reference. Owns the service-side authorization decision while payment domain owns financial execution. SSOT for all service-originated customer remediation and compensation records.'
}

Table service.agent {
  agent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service agent. Primary key for the agent entity.']
  supervisor_agent_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.agent']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current employment and operational status of the agent. Determines whether the agent is eligible for case assignment.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current real-time availability status of the agent. Used by the case routing engine to determine if the agent can accept new cases.']
  average_handle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rolling average time in minutes the agent takes to resolve a case from assignment to closure. Key performance indicator for agent efficiency.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the agents current service certification expires and requires renewal. Null if certification does not expire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agent record was first created in the service domain system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  customer_satisfaction_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average customer satisfaction score for cases handled by this agent, typically on a 1-5 scale. Derived from post-interaction surveys.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Corporate email address for the agent. Used for internal communication and system notifications.']
  employee_code varchar [note: 'External employee identifier from the HR system. Links the agent to their employee record in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.']
  escalation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases handled by the agent that required escalation to a supervisor or specialist. Lower rates indicate higher agent competency.']
  first_contact_resolution_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases resolved by the agent on first contact without escalation or follow-up. Key quality metric for agent performance.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the customer service agent as recorded in the HR system.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the agent was hired into the customer service organization. Used for tenure analysis and performance benchmarking.']
  language_proficiencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages the agent can support with proficiency level (e.g., EN-native, ES-fluent, FR-conversational). Enables multilingual customer support routing.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the agents most recent login to the customer service platform. Used for activity monitoring and availability tracking.']
  max_concurrent_cases varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases the agent can handle simultaneously. Used by the workload balancing engine to prevent agent overload.']
  net_promoter_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average Net Promoter Score (NPS) for interactions handled by this agent. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on agent service.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Agent performance classification tier based on experience, skill level, and performance metrics. Used for case complexity routing and escalation paths.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Internal phone extension number for the agent within the contact center phone system.']
  quality_assurance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average quality assurance audit score for the agent based on case review and compliance checks. Typically scored 0-100 by QA team.']
  shift_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the agents current shift schedule or roster. Links to workforce management system for capacity planning.']
  skill_set_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of agent competencies and specializations (e.g., returns, billing, technical, escalations, fraud, international). Used for intelligent case routing and workload balancing.']
  sla_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases where the agent met or exceeded the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) response and resolution time targets.']
  supported_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer service channels the agent is trained and authorized to handle (e.g., phone, email, chat, social media, SMS).']
  team_assignment varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the service team to which the agent is assigned (e.g., Returns Team, Billing Support, Technical Support, Escalations).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the agent left the organization or was terminated. Null for active agents. Used for historical case ownership tracking.']
  total_cases_handled varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all cases assigned to and handled by the agent since hire date. Used for productivity and experience analysis.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the agent completed initial onboarding and training program and was certified to handle customer cases independently.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agent record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record for customer service agents and support staff operating within the service domain. Captures agent name, employee ID reference, team assignment, skill set tags (returns, billing, technical, escalations), supported channels, language proficiencies, current availability status, shift schedule reference, performance tier, and active/inactive status. Serves as the SSOT for agent identity within the service domain and supports case routing, workload balancing, and performance measurement.'
}

Table service.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service team. Primary key.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who leads this team. Used for escalation paths and team performance accountability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finance cost center to which this team\'s expenses are allocated. Enables financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  parent_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent team in a hierarchical team structure. Null for top-level teams. Enables organizational hierarchy and escalation path modeling.']
  primary_escalation_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team to which cases are escalated when this team cannot resolve them. Null if this is the highest escalation tier. Enables escalation path configuration.']
  capacity_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total team capacity expressed in Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount. Used for workforce planning, capacity forecasting, and case load balancing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the team record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  csat_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Key performance indicator (KPI) for customer experience quality.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the team became operational and started accepting cases. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical reporting.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the team, used for case assignment notifications and internal communication. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this team serves, using 3-letter ISO country codes or region identifiers (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, EUR, APAC, GLOBAL). Supports geo-based routing and regional SLA (Service Level Agreement) management.']
  is_remote boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates remotely (distributed workforce) or from a centralized physical location. True if remote, False if on-site.']
  language_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages the team can support using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh, ja). Enables language-based case routing.']
  max_concurrent_cases varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases the team can handle concurrently based on capacity and workload models. Used for case routing throttling and queue management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the team record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the team (e.g., special operating procedures, temporary capacity changes, pilot program details).']
  nps_target varchar [note: 'Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the team, ranging from -100 to +100. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on team interactions.']
  operates_holidays boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates on public holidays. True if holiday coverage is provided, False if holidays are non-operational.']
  operates_weekends boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). True if weekend coverage is provided, False if weekends are non-operational.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time of the team\'s operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team\'s local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time of the team\'s operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team\'s local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the team, used for internal escalations and coordination. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Physical office or site location where the team is based (e.g., New York HQ, Manila Contact Center, Remote). Used for workforce planning and facility management.']
  primary_function varchar [note: 'Brief description of the team\'s primary business function and scope of responsibility (e.g., Order inquiries and tracking, Returns and refunds processing, Account and billing support, Technical troubleshooting).']
  quality_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target quality assurance score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Represents the expected standard for case handling quality, monitored through QA audits.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA tier this team is assigned to deliver: standard (baseline service levels), priority (faster response for high-value segments), premium (enhanced service commitments), vip (white-glove service for top customers). Drives case routing and performance targets.']
  specialization_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specialization keywords or skill tags that describe the team\'s expertise (e.g., rma, fraud_detection, vip_service, technical_support, billing_disputes). Used for intelligent case routing based on issue type.']
  target_first_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for the team to provide the first response to a customer inquiry, as defined by the team\'s SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for responsiveness.']
  target_resolution_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for the team to resolve a case, as defined by the team\'s SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for case closure efficiency.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the team for operational systems and reporting (e.g., T1-FRONT, T2-ESC, RMA-SPEC).']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer service team (e.g., Tier-1 Frontline Support, Returns Specialist Team, VIP Customer Care).']
  team_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the team: active (currently operational and accepting cases), inactive (temporarily not operational), suspended (on hold pending review), dissolved (permanently closed).']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team based on its role in the service organization: tier_1_frontline (first contact resolution), tier_2_escalation (complex issue handling), specialist (domain-specific expertise such as RMA or fraud), back_office (non-customer-facing support), quality_assurance (monitoring and coaching), training (onboarding and skill development).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the team was dissolved or permanently closed. Null for active teams. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the team\'s operating location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo). Used to calculate local operating hours and SLA deadlines.']

  Note: 'Master record defining customer service teams and organizational groupings within the support operation. Captures team name, team type (tier-1 frontline, tier-2 escalation, specialist, back-office), supported contact channels, operating hours, geographic coverage, team lead reference, SLA tier assignment, and capacity targets. Enables hierarchical case routing, team-level SLA tracking, and workforce planning for the service organization.'
}

Table service.sla_policy {
  sla_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA policy record. Primary key.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: SLA policies must meet regulatory consumer protection standards; linking SLA policy to the governing regulation allows compliance monitoring.']
  superseded_by_policy_sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer SLA policy that replaces this one. Nullable for current active policies. Creates version chain for policy evolution tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the SLA policy. Policies must be approved before becoming active. Supports governance and change control processes.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this SLA policy. Typically a senior operations leader or executive. Nullable for unapproved policies.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy was approved. Nullable for unapproved policies. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  auto_escalation_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic escalation is enabled when escalation_threshold_hours is reached. True enables automated escalation workflow, False requires manual escalation.']
  breach_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of penalty or credit applied when SLA is breached. Applicable when breach_penalty_type is financial or credit. Stored in base currency.']
  breach_penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the breach penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  breach_penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty or action triggered when SLA is breached. Options include no penalty, financial penalty, customer credit, automatic escalation, or notification to management.']
  business_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business days when SLA is active (e.g., \'Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday\'). Used when time_calculation_method is business_hours to exclude weekends or specific days.']
  business_hours_end varchar [note: 'End time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.']
  business_hours_start varchar [note: 'Start time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of cases this SLA policy applies to. Used in conjunction with case_type to form the SLA matrix (e.g., High priority order inquiries have different SLA targets than Low priority inquiries).']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer service case this SLA policy applies to (e.g., \'Order Inquiry\', \'Product Return\', \'Technical Issue\', \'Billing Dispute\', \'Account Management\'). Defines the scope of cases governed by this policy.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Customer service channel this SLA policy applies to. Different channels may have different response time commitments (e.g., chat has faster response than email).']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SLA policy is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR data subject request response times, financial services complaint handling). True indicates regulatory mandate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment this SLA policy applies to (e.g., \'VIP\', \'Premium\', \'Standard\', \'Enterprise\', \'SMB\'). Enables differentiated service levels by customer tier.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SLA policy expires or is superseded by a new version. Nullable for currently active policies with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this SLA policy becomes active and begins governing case handling. Part of the effective date range for policy versioning.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric escalation tier this SLA policy triggers (e.g., 1 = supervisor, 2 = manager, 3 = director). Defines the organizational level to which cases are escalated when thresholds are breached.']
  escalation_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time threshold in hours that triggers automatic escalation if the case is not resolved. Typically set before the resolution target to allow time for escalated handling.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Description of cases or conditions excluded from this SLA policy (e.g., \'Excludes cases opened during system maintenance windows\', \'Does not apply to third-party vendor issues\'). Defines policy boundaries.']
  first_response_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for the first response to a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed before initial acknowledgment or response to the customer.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this SLA policy applies to (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports region-specific service level commitments.']
  is_default_policy boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default SLA policy applied when no specific policy matches a case. Only one policy per case_type should be marked as default.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was last updated. Part of standard audit trail for change tracking.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automated notifications are sent when SLA thresholds are approaching or breached. True enables notification workflow.']
  notification_threshold_percent varchar [note: 'Percentage of SLA target time elapsed that triggers a warning notification (e.g., 80 means notify when 80% of resolution_target_hours has passed). Used for proactive SLA management.']
  owner_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user or manager responsible for defining and maintaining this SLA policy. Typically a customer service director or operations manager.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the SLA policy used in operational systems and reporting.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA policy (e.g., \'Premium Support SLA\', \'Standard Response Policy\').']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA policy indicating the service area it governs (customer support, technical support, returns processing, escalation handling, VIP service, standard service).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory framework or law that mandates this SLA policy (e.g., \'GDPR\', \'CCPA\', \'FTC Consumer Protection\', \'PCI DSS\'). Populated when compliance_required is True.']
  resolution_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for full resolution of a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed to completely resolve and close the case.']
  sla_policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the SLA policy including its purpose, scope, and any special conditions or exceptions. Provides context for policy application and interpretation.']
  sla_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA policy indicating whether it is actively enforced, inactive, in draft state, archived, or temporarily suspended.']
  time_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate SLA time targets. Business hours counts only operational hours (e.g., 9am-5pm weekdays), while calendar hours counts all hours including nights and weekends.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this SLA policy. Incremented when policy terms are updated. Supports policy change tracking and historical analysis.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining SLA (Service Level Agreement) policies governing response and resolution time targets for customer support cases. Captures SLA policy name, applicable case type and priority matrix, first response time target (hours), resolution time target (hours), escalation trigger thresholds, business hours vs. calendar hours flag, breach penalty rules, and effective date range. Provides the authoritative SLA configuration used to evaluate case compliance and drive automated escalations.'
}

Table service.escalation {
  escalation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the escalation event. Primary key.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with the escalated case, enabling customer-centric escalation pattern analysis.']
  escalation_escalating_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support agent who initiated or triggered the escalation.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Escalations triggered by compliance breaches must reference the underlying compliance_obligation to track remediation and reporting.']
  receiving_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support agent who received and accepted the escalated case.']
  receiving_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support team or queue to which the case was escalated.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating support case that was escalated.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the escalation was initiated or communicated.']
  compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of compensation issued to the customer as part of escalation resolution.']
  compensation_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether customer compensation (refund, credit, discount, gift) was issued as part of escalation resolution (True) or not (False).']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'The type of compensation provided to the customer (e.g., refund, account credit, discount code, gift card, free shipping, product replacement).']
  cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost associated with handling the escalation, including labor, compensation, and operational overhead.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction score for the escalated case, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment or tier at the time of escalation, used for prioritization and SLA (Service Level Agreement) determination.']
  escalation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the escalation event, formatted as ESC-NNNNNNNN.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the escalation event within the support workflow.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation event occurred, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  escalation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the escalation mechanism: functional (to specialist team), hierarchical (to supervisor/manager), automatic (system-triggered), or manual (agent-initiated).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments provided by the escalating agent explaining the context, urgency, or special handling requirements for the escalation.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected after escalation resolution, measuring customer loyalty on a scale of 0-10.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to the escalation, indicating urgency and required response time.']
  reason varchar [note: 'The trigger or reason that caused the case to be escalated. Common reasons include SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, customer demand, case complexity, VIP customer status, regulatory concern, or repeat issue.']
  resolution_at_escalated_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the case was successfully resolved at the escalated tier (True) or required further escalation (False).']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating how the escalated case was resolved (e.g., refund issued, replacement shipped, technical fix applied).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalated case was resolved, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'The identified root cause category for the issue that led to escalation, used for continuous improvement and pattern analysis.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the escalation was triggered by an SLA breach (True) or not (False).']
  sla_target_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The target resolution time in minutes as defined by the applicable SLA for the escalated case.']
  source_tier varchar [note: 'The support tier or level from which the case was escalated.']
  target_tier varchar [note: 'The support tier or level to which the case was escalated.']
  time_to_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in minutes from escalation timestamp to resolution timestamp, used for performance analysis and SLA compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation record was last modified, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking case escalation events within the support workflow. Captures the originating case reference, escalation trigger reason (SLA breach, customer demand, complexity, VIP customer), escalation tier (tier-1 to tier-2, tier-2 to specialist, agent to supervisor), escalating agent, receiving agent or team, escalation timestamp, resolution at escalated tier flag, and time-to-resolution post-escalation. Supports escalation pattern analysis and service quality improvement.'
}

Table service.feedback_response {
  feedback_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for feedback_response']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who provided the feedback response. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this feedback, if the survey was triggered by an order-related service interaction or post-delivery survey.']
  primary_feedback_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who handled the case being evaluated. Used for agent performance tracking and coaching.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service case or support ticket that triggered this feedback survey. Links the feedback to the specific service interaction being evaluated.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'The category of the service case that triggered this feedback (e.g., order_inquiry, return_request, product_defect, delivery_issue, account_management, technical_support). Used to analyze satisfaction by case type and identify problem areas.']
  case_resolution_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total time in hours from case creation to case closure for the service case being evaluated. Captured at the time of survey to enable correlation analysis between resolution speed and customer satisfaction.']
  channel_of_service varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer received service for the case being evaluated. Used to analyze satisfaction by service channel and optimize channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|social_media|in-app|self-service|store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  classification_category varchar [note: 'The categorical classification derived from the numeric score. For NPS: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6). For CSAT: satisfied (4-5), neutral (3), dissatisfied (1-2). For CES: low_effort (6-7), high_effort (1-3). Used for segmentation and aggregate metrics calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promoter|passive|detractor|satisfied|neutral|dissatisfied|low_effort|high_effort — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contact_reason varchar [note: 'The specific reason the customer contacted support, as classified by the service team. More granular than case_category, capturing the detailed nature of the inquiry or issue (e.g., damaged_item, late_delivery, wrong_item_shipped, refund_status, promo_code_issue).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this feedback response record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and tracking data freshness.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment classification at the time of feedback submission. Used to analyze satisfaction and loyalty metrics by customer cohort (e.g., VIP, frequent_buyer, new_customer, at_risk, dormant). Enables segment-specific experience improvement strategies.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the survey was delivered to the customer. Email and SMS are common for post-interaction surveys, in-app and web for real-time feedback, IVR for phone-based surveys, chat for conversational feedback collection.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was escalated to a supervisor or specialist team. Escalated cases typically have different satisfaction patterns and require special analysis.']
  external_survey_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this survey response in the external survey platform or CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and to link back to the source system for detailed survey metadata.']
  first_contact_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer\'s issue was resolved on the first contact without requiring follow-up interactions. Key performance indicator (KPI) for service efficiency and a strong predictor of customer satisfaction.']
  follow_up_action_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feedback response requires follow-up action from the service team. Typically set to true for detractor responses, low CSAT scores, or when verbatim feedback indicates unresolved issues. Triggers service recovery workflows.']
  follow_up_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the required follow-up action has been completed. Used to track closure of service recovery loops and ensure no customer feedback is left unaddressed.']
  follow_up_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the follow-up action was completed. Used to measure time-to-resolution for service recovery and closed-loop feedback processes.']
  incentive_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an incentive (discount, reward points, gift card) was offered to encourage survey completion. Used to analyze response bias and response rate impact of incentives.']
  interaction_count varchar [note: 'The total number of customer-agent interactions required to resolve the case being evaluated. Used to measure case complexity and correlate with effort scores and satisfaction ratings.']
  numeric_score varchar [note: 'The numeric rating provided by the customer. For CSAT surveys this is typically 1-5 (1=very dissatisfied, 5=very satisfied). For NPS surveys this is 0-10 (0=not at all likely to recommend, 10=extremely likely). For CES surveys this is typically 1-7 (1=very difficult, 7=very easy).']
  primary_feedback_theme varchar [note: 'The main topic or theme identified in the verbatim feedback through text analytics or manual coding. Examples include product_quality, delivery_speed, agent_professionalism, resolution_time, website_usability, pricing, return_process. Used for root cause analysis and improvement prioritization.']
  response_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer to submit the feedback response. Used to analyze response patterns by device and optimize survey design for different form factors.']
  response_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the customer provided their feedback. Used for multilingual sentiment analysis and to route feedback to appropriate language-capable service teams.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Status of the survey response. Completed indicates all required questions were answered, partial indicates the survey was started but not finished, abandoned indicates the customer left mid-survey, expired indicates the survey link expired before response, bounced indicates the survey invitation was undeliverable.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer submitted their feedback response. Used to calculate response time from survey delivery and to track feedback recency.']
  secondary_feedback_theme varchar [note: 'Additional topic or theme identified in the verbatim feedback when multiple themes are present. Captures the secondary concern or compliment expressed by the customer.']
  sentiment_classification varchar [note: 'Categorical sentiment classification derived from sentiment score and verbatim text analysis. Positive indicates favorable feedback, negative indicates unfavorable feedback, neutral indicates balanced or no strong sentiment, mixed indicates conflicting sentiments within the same response.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Machine-generated sentiment score derived from verbatim feedback text analysis. Typically ranges from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive), with 0 being neutral. Used to augment numeric ratings with natural language understanding.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was resolved within the committed SLA timeframe. Used to correlate SLA compliance with customer satisfaction and identify the impact of SLA breaches on customer experience.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that captured this feedback response (e.g., Qualtrics, Medallia, SurveyMonkey, Salesforce Service Cloud, custom feedback platform). Used for data lineage and multi-system integration tracking.']
  survey_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the survey invitation was sent to the customer. Used to calculate response lag and survey completion rates.']
  survey_trigger_event varchar [note: 'The specific business event that triggered the delivery of this feedback survey. Identifies the touchpoint in the customer journey being measured.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer feedback survey administered. CSAT measures satisfaction with specific interactions (1-5 scale), NPS measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend (0-10 scale), CES measures ease of interaction (effort score). Post-Delivery surveys capture feedback after order fulfillment, Relationship surveys are periodic assessments, Post-Case surveys follow service case closure.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used. Enables tracking of survey design changes over time and ensures consistent analysis when survey questions or scales are modified.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this feedback response record was last modified. Tracks changes to follow-up status, theme classification, or other mutable fields.']
  verbatim_feedback_text varchar [note: 'Free-form text comments provided by the customer explaining their rating. This qualitative feedback provides context for the numeric score and is used for sentiment analysis, theme extraction, and identifying specific improvement opportunities. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of customer-specific details.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all customer feedback survey responses tied to service interactions. Covers CSAT (1-5 satisfaction rating for specific interactions), NPS (0-10 loyalty score with promoter/passive/detractor classification), and CES (Customer Effort Score) surveys. Stores survey type, numeric score, classification category, verbatim feedback text, survey trigger event (post-case closure, post-delivery, periodic relationship survey), delivery channel (email, SMS, in-app), response timestamp, associated case and agent references, customer segment, and follow-up action flag. SSOT for all service-domain customer feedback and satisfaction measurement data.'
}

Table service.knowledge_article {
  knowledge_article_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the knowledge base article. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary product category this article applies to. Links article to relevant product catalog segments.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Associates each knowledge article with the search index it resides in, required for index management, re‑indexing, and search performance reporting.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Knowledge Base creation reuses existing content items; linking provides traceability and version sync between KB articles and source content.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Knowledge‑base articles often address issues specific to a marketplace listing; the link enables article‑to‑listing relevance reporting.']
  primary_knowledge_author_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent or knowledge manager who authored the article.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Knowledge articles that address regulatory guidance need to be tied to the applicable regulation to ensure content validity and auditability.']
  reviewer_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the agent or manager who reviewed and approved the article for publication.']
  agent_usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times agents have referenced or attached this article during case resolution. Indicates agent knowledge utilization.']
  article_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique article number used for reference and citation in support interactions. Format: KA-XXXXXXXX.']
  article_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the article content type. Determines presentation format and usage context.']
  attachment_urls varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of URLs pointing to supplementary attachments such as PDFs, videos, screenshots, or downloadable resources stored in Content Delivery Network (CDN).']
  case_deflection_count varchar [note: 'Number of support cases attributed as deflected by this article through self-service resolution. Measured via customer journey tracking.']
  case_type_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of case types this article is designed to resolve. Links article to specific support case categories.']
  content_body varchar [note: 'Rich text content body of the article containing the full resolution steps, policy details, or instructional content. May include HTML formatting, images, and embedded media.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the article record was first created in the knowledge management system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the article is scheduled to expire and be automatically archived or removed from active knowledge base. Nullable for evergreen content.']
  helpful_votes varchar [note: 'Count of positive helpfulness ratings (thumbs up) received from users who viewed the article.']
  helpfulness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated helpfulness percentage based on ratio of helpful to total votes. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Used to identify high-quality and low-quality content.']
  issue_taxonomy_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of issue taxonomy tags for categorization and search optimization. Examples: shipping_delay, payment_failure, account_access, product_defect.']
  knowledge_article_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the article in the approval and publication workflow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the article record was last modified, including content updates, metadata changes, or status transitions.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when the article content was last modified and republished.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Language and region locale code for the article content. Format: ISO 639-1 language code underscore ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., en_US, es_MX, fr_FR).']
  not_helpful_votes varchar [note: 'Count of negative helpfulness ratings (thumbs down) received from users who viewed the article.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level of the article content. Critical articles address high-impact issues and are promoted in search results.']
  publish_date date [note: 'Date when the article was first published and made available to agents or customers.']
  related_article_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of knowledge article IDs that are contextually related or frequently viewed together. Used for recommendation engine.']
  review_by_date date [note: 'Target date by which the article should be reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Used to trigger periodic content audits.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases optimized for internal search engine indexing and article discovery.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the knowledge article displayed in search results and article headers.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the article. Incremented with each published revision to track content evolution.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the article has been viewed by agents or customers. Used to measure article popularity and relevance.']
  visibility_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the audience scope for article visibility. Determines which user groups can access the article.']

  Note: 'Master record for knowledge base articles supporting agent-assisted resolution and customer self-service deflection. Captures article title, rich content body, article type (FAQ, troubleshooting guide, policy document, how-to, known issue), applicable product categories and issue taxonomy tags, authoring agent, review/approval workflow status, publish date, expiry/review-by date, version history, locale/language, view count, helpfulness rating (thumbs up/down), linked case types, and case deflection attribution metrics. Enables measurement of self-service effectiveness, agent knowledge utilization, and content gap identification.'
}

Table service.case_status_history {
  case_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each status transition record in the support case lifecycle. Primary key for the case status history table.']
  assigned_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support team that owned the case at the time of this status transition. Tracks case routing and ownership changes.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns the support case. Denormalized from support_case for efficient customer-level reporting and segmentation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this support case if the case relates to an order issue. Null for non-order-related cases.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Case status changes often stem from compliance obligations; linking status history to the obligation provides traceability for audits.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA record if this status transition relates to a return or refund workflow. Null for non-RMA cases.']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent support case that experienced this status transition. Links this history record to the originating case entity.']
  triggered_by_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who initiated this status transition. Null if the transition was system-triggered or automated.']
  automation_rule_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the workflow automation rule that triggered this status transition if it was system-initiated. Null for manual transitions.']
  business_hours_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition occurred during defined business hours. True if during business hours, False if after hours or weekend. Used for SLA calculation adjustments.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the support case type at the time of this transition. Denormalized for workflow analysis and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: order_issue|product_defect|shipping_delay|payment_issue|account_inquiry|technical_support|return_refund|complaint|feedback|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the support case. Denormalized from support_case for reporting convenience and audit trail clarity.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The customer service channel through which the case was originally created. Denormalized for channel performance analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|web_form|mobile_app|social_media|in_store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  comment_added_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an agent or system comment was added to the case at the time of this status transition. True if comment was added, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status history record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The escalation tier at the time of this transition. 0 for no escalation, 1 for first-level escalation, 2 for second-level, etc. Tracks case severity progression.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The status the case transitioned to at this event. Captures the to-state in the case lifecycle state machine. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|in_progress|pending_customer|pending_internal|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The channel used to notify the customer of this status change. None if no notification was sent.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification was sent as a result of this status transition. True if notification was sent, False otherwise.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status the case was in immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the from-state in the case lifecycle state machine. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|in_progress|pending_customer|pending_internal|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_at_transition varchar [note: 'The case priority level that was in effect at the time of this status transition. Captures priority changes over the case lifecycle.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the time spent in the previous status exceeded the SLA target, resulting in an SLA breach. True if breached, False if within SLA.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'The SLA target duration in seconds that was applicable for the previous status. Used to determine if the case met or breached SLA during this status phase.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that recorded this status transition. Examples: Salesforce Service Cloud, Zendesk, Custom CRM. Used for data lineage and multi-system integration tracking.']
  time_in_previous_status_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration in seconds that the case spent in the previous status before this transition. Calculated as the difference between this transition timestamp and the prior transition timestamp. Critical for SLA compliance measurement and bottleneck identification.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status transition occurred. Enables categorization and root cause analysis of case workflow patterns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_response|agent_action|auto_escalation|sla_breach|resolution_confirmed|duplicate_case|customer_request|system_trigger|manager_override|workflow_rule — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transition_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the status transition. Captures agent notes or system-generated rationale.']
  transition_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this transition within the cases complete lifecycle. First transition is 1, second is 2, etc. Enables chronological ordering and lifecycle stage analysis.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the status transition occurred. Primary business event timestamp for this history record. Used for SLA calculation and workflow bottleneck analysis.']
  triggered_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or workflow engine that triggered this transition if not manually initiated by an agent. Examples: SLA Monitor, Auto-Escalation Engine, Workflow Automation.']

  Note: 'Transactional history table tracking every status transition in a support case lifecycle. Records the case reference, previous status, new status, transition timestamp, triggering agent or system, transition reason, and time spent in previous status. Enables full case lifecycle audit trail, bottleneck identification in case workflows, and SLA compliance verification. Distinct from the current status field on support_case — this table preserves the complete state machine history.'
}

Table service.entitlement {
  entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service entitlement record. Primary key.']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual subscription fee charged for this entitlement tier in base currency (USD). Null indicates no annual fee option.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer subscriptions to this entitlement automatically renew at the end of the billing period.']
  business_hours_coverage varchar [note: 'Support availability hours under this entitlement (business hours 9-5, extended hours 7am-11pm, or 24x7).']
  cancellation_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for customer to cancel this entitlement without penalty.']
  concierge_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement includes white-glove concierge service for complex requests, special orders, or personalized assistance.']
  contact_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of support contact channels included in this entitlement (e.g., email, phone, chat, social_media, video_call, in_person).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this entitlement record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment eligible for this entitlement tier (standard consumer, prime member, VIP, B2B enterprise account, marketplace seller, or partner).']
  dedicated_agent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement includes assignment of a dedicated support agent or account manager.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this entitlement tier expires or is discontinued. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this entitlement tier becomes active and available for assignment to customer accounts.']
  entitlement_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique code for the entitlement tier (e.g., STD_BASIC, PRIME_PRIORITY, VIP_DEDICATED, B2B_ENTERPRISE).']
  entitlement_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service entitlement benefits, coverage scope, and included features.']
  entitlement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service entitlement tier (e.g., Standard Support, Prime Priority Support, VIP Dedicated Support).']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entitlement tier (active and assignable, inactive, deprecated and being phased out, or pending approval).']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Escalation path tier for unresolved cases (standard queue, expedited to management, executive escalation, or no escalation).']
  free_return_shipping_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether return shipping costs are waived for customers under this entitlement.']
  language_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages supported for customer service under this entitlement (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh, ja).']
  max_cases_per_month varchar [note: 'Maximum number of support cases the customer can open per calendar month under this entitlement. Null indicates unlimited.']
  monthly_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monthly subscription fee charged for this entitlement tier in base currency (USD). Null indicates no recurring fee.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier associated with this entitlement (free, basic paid, standard paid, premium paid, enterprise custom pricing).']
  priority_routing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cases under this entitlement receive priority routing to senior agents or specialized teams.']
  proactive_outreach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement includes proactive outreach for service issues, order updates, or account reviews.']
  refund_processing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for refund processing under this entitlement (standard 7-10 days, expedited 3-5 days, immediate 24 hours).']
  resolution_time_sla_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum resolution time in hours guaranteed by SLA for case closure or escalation.']
  response_time_sla_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum response time in hours guaranteed by SLA for initial agent response to customer inquiry.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after delivery during which the customer can initiate a return under this entitlement (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  rma_auto_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RMA requests under this entitlement are automatically approved without manual review.']
  self_service_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access to self-service tools, knowledge base, and automated resolution features (basic, advanced, premium, full).']
  sla_document_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the detailed SLA document specifying response times, resolution commitments, and service guarantees.']
  support_tier varchar [note: 'Service support tier level defining the quality and speed of support (basic, priority, premium, dedicated, enterprise).']
  technical_support_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether technical product support (setup, troubleshooting, configuration) is included in this entitlement.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the full terms and conditions document governing this entitlement tier.']
  trial_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of free trial period offered for this entitlement tier. Null indicates no trial available.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this entitlement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was last modified.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this entitlement configuration, incremented with each modification to track change history.']
  warranty_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of additional warranty months provided beyond standard manufacturer warranty under this entitlement. Null indicates no extension.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the service entitlements and support tier benefits associated with customer accounts or order types. Captures entitlement name, customer segment eligibility (standard, prime, VIP, B2B enterprise), support tier (basic, priority, dedicated), included contact channels, maximum cases per period, response time SLA tier, dedicated agent assignment flag, and entitlement validity period. Drives case routing logic and SLA policy assignment based on customer service tier.'
}

Table service.case_queue {
  case_queue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the case queue configuration.']
  assigned_team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the support team primarily responsible for this queue.']
  escalation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the escalation policy applied when SLA thresholds are breached.']
  overflow_case_queue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on case_queue (overflow_case_queue_id)']
  average_wait_time_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Desired average wait time for cases in this queue, expressed in minutes.']
  capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases that can be simultaneously assigned to the queue.']
  case_queue_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the queue purpose and behavior.']
  case_queue_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the queue.']
  case_queue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the queue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the queue record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the queue configuration ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the queue configuration becomes effective.']
  is_priority_queue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the queue is designated for high‑priority cases.']
  last_modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  max_wait_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Hard ceiling for how long a case may wait in the queue before escalation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the queue.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time for queue operation (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time for queue operation (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  overflow_routing_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied when the queue exceeds its capacity (e.g., redirect to overflow queue).']
  queue_depth_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases that can be queued awaiting assignment.']
  queue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the queue routing strategy.']
  routing_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to distribute cases to agents.']
  sla_target_abandon_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable percentage of cases abandoned before service.']
  sla_target_average_wait_minutes varchar [note: 'Service‑level agreement target for average wait time, in minutes.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the queue\'s operating schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the queue record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining service case queues and routing configurations for the contact center operation. Captures queue name, queue type (skill-based, round-robin, priority, overflow), supported case types and priorities, assigned team reference, operating hours, capacity limits, overflow routing rules, average wait time targets, and active/inactive status. Drives real-time case distribution to agents, enables workload balancing across teams, and supports queue-level performance monitoring (average handle time, abandonment rate, queue depth). Essential for contact center workforce management and case routing automation.'
}

Table marketplace.marketplace_listing {
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the marketplace listing.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Promotion ROI Report linking each listing to the marketing campaign that promoted it; marketers expect this direct association.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Sellers often designate a default carrier for their listings; needed for contract compliance and rate negotiations.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Required for listing‑catalog synchronization report that maps each marketplace listing to the internal catalog item for inventory and pricing alignment.']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: REQUIRED: Listing page needs primary product image; the primary_asset_id FK links to digital_asset for image rendering on marketplace listings.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Shipping SLA and cost calculations are based on the delivery zone assigned to each listing.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: REQUIRED: Marketplace listing displays rich product description and SEO fields stored in content_item; FK enables content management system to supply listing copy.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for Listing Price Determination Report linking each listing to the price list used for pricing calculations, essential for audit and dynamic pricing.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory Compliance Report requires each listing to reference the regulation it must satisfy (e.g., safety, labeling). This is standard in e‑commerce product compliance.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller who owns this marketplace listing.']
  seo_metadata_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seo_metadata. Business justification: REQUIRED: Per‑listing SEO overrides (title, meta tags) are managed in seo_metadata; FK allows marketplace to apply SEO settings specific to each listing.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Needed for inventory and fulfillment tracking per SKU, enabling real‑time stock reconciliation between marketplace listings and internal SKU records.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Assign fulfillment warehouse for merchant‑fulfilled (FBM) listings; used in fulfillment planning and inventory allocation reports.']
  advertised boolean [note: 'True when the listing includes FTC‑compliant advertising disclosures.']
  buy_box_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing qualifies for the marketplace buy‑box algorithm.']
  category_path varchar [note: 'Hierarchical category taxonomy path for the product (e.g., Electronics > Mobile Phones > Smartphones).']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the sale price retained by the marketplace as commission.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Physical state of the item being sold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the listing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the listing price currency.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the listing expires or is deactivated.']
  featured boolean [note: 'True if the seller has paid for promotional placement of the listing.']
  ftc_ad_disclosure boolean [note: 'True when the listing includes required FTC advertising disclosure statements.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the product is delivered to the buyer.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the product for dimensional shipping.']
  inventory_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units the seller has allocated to this marketplace listing.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'True if the listing is included in search and recommendation results.']
  is_visible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing is visible to shoppers on the marketplace.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the product for dimensional shipping.']
  listing_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the listing record (e.g., seller uploaded, system generated, bulk import).']
  listing_type varchar [note: 'Business model of the listing (B2C, C2C, auction, or fixed‑price).']
  marketplace_listing_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the product, including features and specifications.']
  marketplace_listing_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the listing.']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount the buyer pays before taxes, fees, or discounts.']
  rating_average decimal(3,2) [note: 'Mean rating score from buyer reviews (scale 1‑5).']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer reviews submitted for the listing.']
  shipping_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Weight of the product used for shipping calculations.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the listing becomes active for purchase.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current availability state of the inventory for this listing.']
  title varchar [note: 'The headline or title of the product offering displayed to buyers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the listing record.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the product for dimensional shipping.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a seller\'s product offering on the marketplace platform. Captures the full lifecycle of a marketplace listing including listing status (active, inactive, suspended, pending review), listing type (B2C, C2C, auction, fixed-price), buy-box eligibility status, featured listing flag, listing creation and expiry dates, listing title, description, condition (new, used, refurbished), category path, brand, ASIN/UPC/EAN identifiers, seller SKU, marketplace-assigned listing ID, fulfillment type (FBA-style, seller-fulfilled, dropship), listing visibility flags, and FTC-compliant advertising disclosure indicators. SSOT for marketplace catalog listings distinct from the product domain\'s master catalog.'
}

Table marketplace.listing_offer {
  listing_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the seller\'s offer on a marketplace listing.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (SKU) that the offer is for.']
  listing_category_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketplace.listing_category']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Offers often belong to marketing campaigns (discounts, bundles); linking enables Offer‑Campaign performance dashboards.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace listing to which this offer belongs.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Needed for Offer Price Audit to trace each offer back to the specific price list item that generated its price, supporting compliance and price change traceability.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller who submitted the offer.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Enables offer pricing and stock allocation per SKU, essential for dynamic pricing engine.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Offer availability must reflect allocated stock; linking offer to stock position enables quantity validation and replenishment triggers.']
  buy_box_winner_flag boolean [note: 'True if the offer is the current buy‑box winner for the listing.']
  buyer_feedback_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated rating given by buyers who purchased this specific offer.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the offer price retained by the marketplace as commission.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the offer meets required FTC advertising disclosures.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional information regarding compliance status or special handling.']
  condition varchar [note: 'State of the product as described by the seller.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the offer_price and related monetary fields are expressed.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Logistics approach used to deliver the item to the buyer.']
  gift_wrap_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional charge for gift‑wrap when selected by the buyer.']
  handling_time_sla_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the seller commits to process the order before shipment.']
  is_gift_wrap_available boolean [note: 'True if the seller offers gift‑wrap service for this offer.']
  is_prime_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the offer qualifies for accelerated shipping benefits.']
  is_returnable boolean [note: 'True if the seller accepts returns for this offer.']
  listing_offer_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the offer within its lifecycle.']
  marketplace_region varchar [note: 'Regional market segment where the offer is displayed.']
  net_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount the seller receives after deductions.']
  offer_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offer is no longer eligible for purchase.']
  offer_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base price of the item as offered by the seller, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  offer_rank varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the offer in the ranking algorithm (1 = highest).']
  offer_source varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the offer was entered into the marketplace.']
  offer_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offer starts being visible to buyers.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which the offer_price is valid.']
  price_expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date after which the offer_price is no longer valid.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price is a regular list price, sale price, or discounted price.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'True if the offer is tied to a marketplace promotion or discount campaign.']
  quantity_available varchar [note: 'Number of units the seller can fulfill under this offer.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Timeframe within which a buyer may request a return.']
  seller_fulfillment_sla_met boolean [note: 'True if the seller consistently meets the handling time SLA for this offer.']
  seller_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated rating score for the seller, on a 0‑5 scale.']
