  shipping_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Shipping fee associated with the offer, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  shipping_estimate_days varchar [note: 'Projected transit time from shipment to delivery.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Business or policy reason that caused the offer to be hidden from buyers.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Applicable tax calculated on the offer price.']
  total_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined price of the item and shipping, representing the amount the buyer would pay.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the offer record.']

  Note: 'Represents a specific seller\'s price offer on a marketplace listing at a point in time. A single listing may have multiple competing offers from different sellers. Captures offer price, shipping cost, condition, seller fulfillment method, offer rank, buy-box winner flag, offer start and end dates, quantity available, handling time SLA, and offer suppression reason. Drives buy-box eligibility determination and competitive pricing intelligence on the marketplace.'
}

Table marketplace.buy_box_rule {
  buy_box_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the buy box rule definition.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Buy box rule determines eligibility for a specific marketplace listing; linking buy_box_rule to marketplace_listing captures this parent‑child relationship.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the seller currently holding the buy box under this rule.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Buy‑box eligibility rules are evaluated per SKU to determine winning offer based on price and seller metrics.']
  buy_box_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  category_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product categories to which the rule applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Logical expression or JSON defining seller and offer eligibility for the buy box.']
  eligibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score indicating how well the offer meets the rule\'s eligibility criteria.']
  max_wins_per_day varchar [note: 'Upper limit on how many times a single seller can win the buy box for a given listing each day.']
  price_threshold_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum price difference allowed for an offer to be considered for the buy box.']
  price_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the price threshold amount.']
  rotation_fairness_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether rotation fairness logic is active for this rule.']
  rotation_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum time in minutes before the buy box can rotate to another eligible seller.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule purpose and logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the buy box rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining its primary function.']
  scoring_weights varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited string representing weightings for each scoring factor (e.g., price, rating, fulfillment speed).']
  seller_rating_at_win decimal(3,2) [note: 'Seller rating (0‑5) captured at the moment the buy box was awarded.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation why the rule suppressed a buy box assignment.']
  suppression_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that suppresses the rule from assigning a buy box.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  winning_fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Fulfillment method associated with the winning offer.']
  winning_offer_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price of the offer that currently holds the buy box.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional entity managing the complete buy-box lifecycle on the marketplace. Owns business rule definitions (eligibility criteria, scoring weights, pricing thresholds, category applicability, suppression triggers, rotation fairness parameters) and the full transactional event history of buy-box ownership changes (winner/loser tracking per listing per timestamp, ownership duration, rotation timestamps, winning offer price and fulfillment method, seller rating at time of win, eligibility score, suppression reason). Provides the authoritative single source of truth for buy-box assignment logic, ownership event audit trail, rotation fairness auditing, seller competitive intelligence, and GMV optimization reporting.'
}

Table marketplace.marketplace_transaction {
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketplace transaction. Primary key for the marketplace transaction entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier performance & cost allocation report requires linking each transaction to the carrier that shipped it.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Exact carrier service used per transaction is needed for service‑level compliance and buyer ETA guarantees.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Supports sales analytics that aggregate revenue by catalog item independent of SKU variations.']
  commission_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.commission_schedule. Business justification: Marketplace transactions need to reference the commission schedule that defines their commission rates; adds commission_schedule_id FK to marketplace_transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for internal cost allocation reports that assign transaction processing costs to specific cost centers.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Required for order fulfillment shipping address, used in delivery performance reports and compliance with shipping regulations.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who purchased the item through the marketplace platform.']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment center responsible for shipping the item, if applicable for marketplace-fulfilled transactions.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Required for the daily accounting posting process that creates a journal entry for each marketplace transaction.']
  listing_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific seller offer accepted by the buyer for this transaction.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Regulated/lot‑tracked products require linking transaction to lot for traceability, recall, and compliance reporting.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketplace catalog listing that was purchased in this transaction.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Transaction audit requires linking each transaction to the compliance obligation it satisfies (anti‑fraud, consumer rights), supporting the Transaction Compliance Dashboard.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: REQUIRED: Capturing the stored payment method used for each transaction supports PCI compliance, analytics, and dispute handling.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'External transaction identifier from the payment gateway or payment processor for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for Transaction Pricing Source Report to identify which price list was applied to calculate transaction totals, used in finance reconciliation.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Enables Detailed Pricing Attribution linking each transaction to the exact price list item (SKU, tier) used, needed for margin analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit‑center profitability reporting by linking each transaction to the responsible profit center.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Route optimization and cost analysis reports tie each transaction to the logistical route it traversed.']
  seller_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.seller_settlement. Business justification: Each transaction is settled in a seller settlement period; adding seller_settlement_id to marketplace_transaction records which settlement the transaction belongs to.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Allows transaction‑level reconciliation to internal SKU for accurate inventory deduction and financial reporting.']
  sponsored_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sponsored advertising campaign if this transaction resulted from a sponsored listing.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link sales transaction to stock position for real‑time inventory deduction and on‑hand stock reporting.']
  buy_box_winner_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seller won the buy box for this listing at the time of transaction, affecting marketplace visibility and conversion.']
  buyer_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the buyer at the time of transaction, used for fraud detection and geographic analysis.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate the marketplace commission. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this marketplace transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dispute_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a dispute was opened for this transaction, if applicable.']
  dispute_resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was resolved, if applicable.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any buyer-seller dispute associated with this transaction.']
  fraud_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of fraud review process for this transaction.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection system indicating the likelihood of fraudulent activity (0-100 scale).']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the purchased item will be fulfilled and delivered to the buyer.']
  gmv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction amount representing the gross merchandise value before any marketplace fees or commissions. This is the total amount the buyer paid for the item(s).']
  is_sponsored_listing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this transaction resulted from a sponsored (paid advertising) listing, requiring FTC-compliant advertising disclosure.']
  item_subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Subtotal amount for the item(s) purchased, excluding shipping, taxes, and other fees.']
  marketplace_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission amount earned by the marketplace platform on this transaction. This is the marketplace revenue from facilitating the transaction.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel or interface through which the buyer completed the payment.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category classification for the item sold in this transaction, used for GMV reporting by category.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the item purchased in this transaction.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the buyer if the transaction was fully or partially refunded.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the refund, used for return analytics and seller performance monitoring.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed, if applicable.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Return Merchandise Authorization number issued for product returns associated with this transaction.']
  seller_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount to be paid to the seller after deducting marketplace commission and any other fees from the transaction amount.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping and handling charges paid by the buyer for this transaction.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount collected on this transaction, including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique transaction number for customer and seller reference. Format: MKT-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the marketplace transaction indicating its processing state and outcome.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the marketplace transaction was completed and recorded. Primary business event timestamp for GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) reporting.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketplace transaction model: B2C (Business to Consumer), C2C (Consumer to Consumer), or B2B (Business to Business).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this marketplace transaction record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing each completed C2C or B2C transaction facilitated through the marketplace platform. Records transaction date and time, buyer and seller identifiers, listing and offer references, transaction amount (GMV contribution), marketplace commission amount, commission rate, payment method, transaction currency, transaction status (pending, completed, cancelled, disputed, refunded), fulfillment type, and applicable tax amounts. SSOT for GMV reporting and marketplace revenue recognition distinct from the order domain\'s OMS records.'
}

Table marketplace.commission_schedule {
  commission_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the commission schedule record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Commission schedules are applied per product category; a FK ensures accurate fee calculation and compliance reporting.']
  base_commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage applied before any variable adjustments.']
  cap_type varchar [note: 'Method used to cap commission (none, fixed amount, or percentage).']
  cap_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the commission cap, interpreted according to cap_type.']
  commission_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code in which commission amounts are expressed.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the commission schedule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the commission schedule becomes effective.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which transactions qualify for this schedule.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the order is fulfilled, influencing commission calculations.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the schedule is exclusive to the specified seller tier/category combination.']
  max_commission_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ceiling amount for commission; calculated commission will not exceed this value.']
  min_commission_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor amount for commission; if calculated commission falls below this, the minimum is applied.']
  promotional_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is tied to a promotional period.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter country code indicating the geographic region the schedule applies to.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the commission schedule used for reporting and UI display.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  seller_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the seller tier (e.g., bronze, silver, gold) to which the schedule applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  variable_fee_structure varchar [note: 'Textual definition of any variable fee components (e.g., tier‑based bonuses, volume discounts).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management and audit.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that created the schedule.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the commission rate structures applied to marketplace transactions by seller tier, product category, fulfillment type, and promotional period. Captures commission schedule name, applicable seller tier, product category path, base commission rate percentage, variable fee structure, minimum and maximum fee caps, effective date range, currency, and schedule status. Drives automated commission calculation on marketplace transactions and seller settlement processing.'
}

Table marketplace.seller_settlement {
  seller_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the seller settlement record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑center allocation is required for internal reporting of settlement processing expenses.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Settlement payouts are recorded in the general ledger via a journal entry for each seller settlement.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller receiving the settlement.']
  bank_transfer_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier provided by the banking institution for the settlement transfer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amounts.']
  disbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the settlement payment.']
  fee_adjustments_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional fee adjustments (e.g., chargebacks, penalties) applied to the settlement.']
  gross_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales revenue generated by the seller during the settlement period before any deductions.']
  net_disbursement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount transferred to the seller after all deductions and credits.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the settlement payment was initiated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer the settlement funds to the seller.']
  promotional_credits_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credits applied to the seller for promotional funding or marketing incentives.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first captured in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  refund_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount subtracted for buyer refunds processed during the period.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of the settlement period such as weekly, monthly, or quarterly.']
  settlement_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the settlement period.']
  settlement_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the settlement record was created by the system.']
  settlement_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information or remarks about the settlement.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the settlement, often used in finance and reporting.']
  settlement_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the settlement period.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement is periodic (e.g., weekly) or ad‑hoc.']
  tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of tax withheld from the seller\'s payout as required by jurisdictional regulations.']
  total_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of marketplace commissions deducted from the seller\'s gross sales.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the periodic financial settlement disbursed to marketplace sellers. Tracks settlement period start and end dates, seller identifier, gross sales amount, total commissions deducted, refund deductions, promotional funding credits, net disbursement amount, settlement currency, disbursement status (pending, processing, disbursed, on-hold), bank transfer reference, and settlement generation timestamp. Supports seller financial reconciliation and SOX-compliant revenue reporting.'
}

Table marketplace.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the dispute record.']
  assigned_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace agent responsible for handling the dispute.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the buyer who initiated or is involved in the dispute.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order that is the subject of the dispute.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: A dispute is always about a specific marketplace transaction; linking dispute to marketplace_transaction provides the necessary reference.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Dispute handling follows defined compliance obligations (e.g., GDPR, consumer rights); linking enables the Dispute Compliance Traceability report.']
  related_product_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product involved in the dispute.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Dispute resolution creates a support case; the link ties dispute records to the corresponding case for end‑to‑end tracking.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was closed in the system.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel used for communications related to the dispute.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  dispute_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the dispute creation.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the dispute.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category of the dispute indicating the underlying issue.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Any punitive action taken against the offending party.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute was escalated to a senior team or external authority.']
  evidence_count varchar [note: 'Number of evidence items (photos, documents, messages) attached to the dispute.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any fees, penalties, or processing charges applied to the dispute.']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the dispute was initially filed by the initiating party.']
  ftc_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FTC consumer‑protection disclosure is required for this dispute.']
  gdpr_data_subject_consent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating that the data subject (buyer/seller) has consented to processing of personal data for this dispute.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value claimed in the dispute before any adjustments.']
  is_duplicate_dispute boolean [note: 'True if this dispute is identified as a duplicate of an existing case.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute is suspected to involve fraudulent activity.']
  last_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status change or activity on the dispute.']
  marketplace_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee retained by the marketplace platform for handling the transaction.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after fees, representing the amount to be refunded or credited.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text capturing additional details, comments, or observations about the dispute.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the dispute for handling urgency.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the specific reason for the dispute.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication with parties.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the dispute after investigation.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was formally resolved.']
  sla_achieved_hours varchar [note: 'Actual time taken (in hours) to resolve the dispute.']
  sla_compliance boolean [note: 'True if the dispute was resolved within the SLA target.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Service level agreement target duration (in hours) for dispute resolution.']
  total_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount refunded to the buyer as a result of the dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Master entity managing the full lifecycle of seller-buyer dispute cases on the marketplace platform, including granular event audit trail. Captures dispute details (reference number, type, initiating party, status, resolution outcome, enforcement actions) and all lifecycle events (status changes with timestamps, evidence submissions, escalations, agent assignments, decisions, appeals, communications). Supports dispute types including item-not-received, item-not-as-described, unauthorized transactions, return disputes, and listing policy violations. Each event records actor type (buyer, seller, marketplace agent, automated system), event notes, attached evidence references, and resulting status. Provides complete audit trail for FTC consumer protection compliance, RMA workflows, SLA tracking, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table marketplace.sponsored_listing {
  sponsored_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the sponsored listing record.']
  ad_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ad group within the campaign that contains this sponsored listing.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of the advertising campaign that owns this sponsored listing.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying product listing that is being sponsored.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller who purchased the sponsored placement.']
  ad_performance_clicks varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of clicks received by the sponsored ad.']
  ad_performance_impressions varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the sponsored ad was shown.']
  ad_performance_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount spent on the sponsored listing to date.']
  ad_placement_type varchar [note: 'Type of sponsored ad placement (product, brand, or display).']
  bid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the seller is willing to pay per click or impression, expressed in the campaign currency.']
  bid_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to set bids: manual (seller‑defined) or automatic (platform‑optimized).']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'Date when the sponsored listing stops serving (inclusive).']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'Date when the sponsored listing becomes eligible to serve.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sponsored listing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for bid and budget amounts.']
  daily_budget_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total spend allowed for the sponsored listing each day.']
  disclosure_label_text varchar [note: 'Text displayed to satisfy FTC advertising disclosure requirements.']
  ftc_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required FTC advertising disclosure is attached to the listing.']
  impression_share decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of eligible impressions captured by this sponsored listing.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last transitioned.']
  sponsored_listing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sponsored listing.']
  target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific keyword, category identifier, or ASIN value used for targeting.']
  targeting_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to target the ad (keyword, product category, or specific ASIN).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sponsored listing record.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a paid sponsored listing placement on the marketplace. Captures sponsorship campaign reference, listing identifier, seller identifier, ad placement type (sponsored product, sponsored brand, sponsored display), bid amount, daily budget cap, campaign start and end dates, targeting type (keyword, category, ASIN), ad status (active, paused, ended, rejected), FTC advertising disclosure flag, disclosure label text, and impression share. Supports SEM/PPC campaign management and FTC-compliant advertising disclosure enforcement.'
}

Table marketplace.ad_event {
  ad_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ad_event']
  ad_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advertising campaign to which the event belongs.']
  ad_placement_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific ad placement instance.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order that resulted from the ad conversion, if any.']
  sponsored_listing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsored product listing that generated the ad event.']
  ad_bid_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The bid price submitted for the ad placement.']
  ad_bid_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the bid amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|... — promote to reference product]']
  ad_click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the click event (populated when event_type = click).']
  ad_conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the conversion event (populated when event_type = conversion).']
  ad_cost_model varchar [note: 'Billing model for the ad: cost per mille (CPM) or cost per click (CPC).']
  ad_impression_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the impression event (if event_type is impression).']
  ad_revenue_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Revenue credited to the seller for the ad interaction (e.g., commission).']
  ad_revenue_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the ad revenue amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|... — promote to reference product]']
  ad_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad event record.']
  attribution_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Configured time window used to attribute conversions to clicks.']
  buyer_session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the buyer\'s browsing session associated with the ad event.']
  click_to_conversion_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed seconds between the click event and the resulting conversion, if applicable.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for the ad event based on the billing model (CPC or CPM).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the ad cost amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|... — promote to reference product]']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Device category used by the buyer during the ad interaction.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the ad event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of ad interaction: impression, click, or conversion.']
  ftc_disclosure_rendered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required FTC advertising disclosure was displayed to the buyer.']
  page_type varchar [note: 'Page context of the ad event: product detail page, product listing page, search results, or homepage.']
  placement_position varchar [note: 'Logical placement identifier (e.g., top, sidebar, below_fold) where the ad was shown.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ad event record.']
  viewability_flag boolean [note: 'True if the ad met viewability standards (e.g., 50% of pixels in view for 1 second).']
  viewability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured percentage of the ad that was in view during the impression.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all advertising interaction events for sponsored listings on the marketplace, serving as the single source of truth for the complete ad interaction funnel. Records event type (impression, click, conversion), timestamp, sponsored listing reference, placement position, page type (PDP, PLP, search results, homepage), device type, buyer session reference, cost amount (CPM/CPC basis), FTC disclosure rendering status, viewability metrics, click-to-conversion attribution, and conversion order reference. Enables comprehensive ROAS calculation, CTR/CVR analysis, CPC/CPM billing to sellers, impression-level cost tracking, and advertising performance measurement across the full interaction funnel.'
}

Table marketplace.listing_category {
  listing_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketplace listing category.']
  parent_category_listing_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent category in the hierarchy.']
  buy_box_eligible boolean [note: 'True if listings in this category are eligible for buy‑box placement.']
  category_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the representative image for the category.']
  category_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path (e.g., "Electronics > Computers > Laptops").']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Default commission percentage applied to sales in this category (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%).']
  cpsc_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the category must meet Consumer Product Safety Commission requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the category record was first created in the system.']
  depth_level varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the level of the category in the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Integer used to order categories in UI displays.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the category becomes active for listings.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the category is retired or no longer usable (nullable).']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external catalog or ERP systems.']
  is_age_restricted boolean [note: 'True if the category contains age‑sensitive products (e.g., alcohol, tobacco).']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'True if the category includes hazardous or regulated items.']
  is_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the category is restricted by policy or regulation.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'True if the category is included in search index.']
  is_sponsored_allowed boolean [note: 'True if sellers may purchase sponsored placements within this category.']
  is_visible boolean [note: 'True if the category is visible to buyers in the marketplace.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the category was last reviewed for compliance or relevance.']
  listing_category_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the category (e.g., internal SKU‑style code).']
  listing_category_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the category purpose and scope.']
  listing_category_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the category used in UI and reports.']
  listing_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the category.']
  promotional_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the category can participate in platform promotions or discounts.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent compliance or content review.']
  seo_friendly_name varchar [note: 'Optimized name for search‑engine visibility.']
  tax_exempt_category boolean [note: 'True if sales in this category are exempt from sales tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the category record.']

  Note: 'Reference hierarchy defining the marketplace-specific product category taxonomy used for listing classification, commission schedule mapping, buy-box rule application, and search relevance. Captures category node identifier, category name, parent category reference (supporting multi-level hierarchy), category path string, category depth level, applicable commission rate reference, restricted category flag, age-restricted flag, hazmat flag, category status, and CPSC compliance requirement indicator. Distinct from the product domain\'s catalog taxonomy as it governs marketplace-specific rules.'
}

Table marketplace.listing_review {
  listing_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the review record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Anonymized identifier of the customer who submitted the review.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Listing reviews should be directly linked to the listing they review for easier joins; adds listing_id FK to listing_review.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Links customer reviews to the specific SKU, allowing quality monitoring and product‑specific sentiment analysis.']
  compliance_ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the buyer exercised a CCPA opt‑out for data processing.']
  compliance_gdpr_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR consent was recorded for this review.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the buyer\'s country at review time.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to submit the review.']
  edit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent edit to the review content.']
  helpful_vote_count varchar [note: 'Number of times other buyers marked the review as helpful.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at review submission (used for fraud detection).']
  is_spam_flag boolean [note: 'True if the review was automatically flagged as potential spam.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 two‑letter code of the language used in the review.']
  listing_review_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the review record.']
  media_attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if the review includes photos, videos, or other media.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Current moderation state of the review.']
  moderation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the moderation decision was recorded.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the buyer (e.g., 1‑5 stars).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']
  removal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a review was removed or hidden.']
  review_text varchar [note: 'Full textual feedback submitted by the buyer.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the buyer submitted the review.']
  review_title varchar [note: 'Optional short title summarizing the review.']
  review_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for edited reviews.']
  seller_response_text varchar [note: 'Optional textual response from the seller to the review.']
  seller_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seller posted a response.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric sentiment value (e.g., -1.00 to 1.00) derived from review text.']
  solicitation_source varchar [note: 'Channel that prompted the buyer to leave a review.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Technical channel through which the review was submitted.']
  target_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reviewed entity (listing ID or seller ID).']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the review is for a product listing or a seller.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or app user‑agent string captured at submission.']
  verified_purchase boolean [note: 'True if the review is linked to a verified purchase.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all buyer-submitted reviews, ratings, and feedback on the marketplace platform. Supports product-level reviews (star rating, review text, verified purchase flag, media attachments) and seller-level feedback (delivery speed, item accuracy, communication ratings) via a target_type discriminator. Records submission timestamp, target type (listing or seller), target identifier, buyer identifier (anonymized per GDPR/CCPA), rating scores, review/feedback text, moderation status, helpful vote count, seller response, solicitation source, and removal reason if applicable. Drives BSR calculation, listing quality scoring, seller performance scorecards, buy-box eligibility inputs, and buyer trust signals on the marketplace.'
}

Table marketplace.gmv_snapshot {
  gmv_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying each GMV snapshot record.']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace platform instance to which the snapshot belongs.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: GMV snapshots aggregate transaction data; linking each snapshot to a transaction provides traceability.']
  average_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per transaction (gross_gmv_amount / transaction_count).']
  category_path varchar [note: 'Hierarchical category string (e.g., Electronics > Mobile Phones > Smartphones) used for GMV breakdown.']
  commission_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total commission earned by the marketplace from the GMV.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which monetary amounts are expressed.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Type of fulfillment associated with the GMV (standard shipping, express, store pickup, digital delivery).']
  gross_gmv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross merchandise value before any deductions, expressed in the snapshot currency.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net revenue after commissions, refunds, and promotional adjustments.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the snapshot record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the snapshot record (e.g., status change).']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control of the snapshot record.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment of the GMV (Business‑to‑Consumer, Business‑to‑Business, Consumer‑to‑Consumer).']
  snapshot_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential identifier for the snapshot (e.g., SN‑20231130‑001).']
  snapshot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the snapshot record.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the GMV snapshot was generated (point‑in‑time for reporting).']
  snapshot_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the snapshot (daily, weekly, or monthly).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the underlying data (e.g., OMS, WMS).']
  take_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of GMV retained by the marketplace as commission (commission_revenue_amount / gross_gmv_amount * 100).']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual marketplace transactions included in the snapshot.']

  Note: 'SOX-required operational point-in-time record capturing GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) positions for financial close and regulatory compliance. Stores snapshot date, marketplace segment (B2C, C2C, B2B), category breakdown, fulfillment type split, total GMV amount, transaction count, average order value, take rate percentage, net revenue amount, commission revenue, and currency. Immutable once generated — serves as the authoritative basis for period-end financial close processes, revenue recognition journal entries, SEC/regulatory disclosures, and audit trail integrity. Generated on a defined schedule (daily/weekly/monthly) aligned with accounting periods.'
}

Table marketplace.marketplace_promotion {
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Promotions are funded from specific budgets; linking to the finance budget enables budget‑vs‑actual tracking.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Promotions are defined at the listing level; adding marketplace_listing_id FK ties each promotion to its target listing.']
  budget_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total monetary value the promotion can consume across all enrollments.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the promotion budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount subtracted when the discount mechanism is fixed amount based.']
  discount_mechanism varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the promotional discount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value applied when the discount mechanism is percentage based.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which listings or sellers may enroll in the promotion.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires or is no longer applicable.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment window closes for the promotion.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment request for a listing.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment window opens for the promotion.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of a listing\'s enrollment request.']
  ftc_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion must include FTC-mandated advertising disclosures.']
  ftc_disclosure_text varchar [note: 'Standard disclosure language to be shown with the promotion.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for financing the promotion.']
  geographic_scope_type varchar [note: 'Level of geographic granularity for the promotion\'s applicability.']
  geographic_scope_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Specific geographic identifiers (e.g., country codes, region names) matching the scope type.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other promotions on the same listing.']
  marketplace_promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion.']
  max_enrollment_quantity varchar [note: 'Upper limit on the number of listings that can be enrolled in the promotion.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority used for scheduling and conflict resolution among concurrent promotions.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the promotion in business processes and reporting.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the promotion shown to sellers and buyers.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of the promotion defining its business rules and display format.']
  promotional_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed price applied to the listing when promotional_price_override is true.']
  promotional_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the promotional price.']
  promotional_price_override boolean [note: 'If true, the promotion sets a specific price for the listing regardless of original price.']
  seller_acceptance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a seller must explicitly accept the promotion terms before enrollment.']
  stackable_with varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other promotion codes that may be stacked with this promotion.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes active for eligible listings.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Segment of buyers or sellers the promotion is intended for.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion record.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing marketplace-level promotional campaigns, deal events, and their enrolled listing associations. Captures promotion lifecycle (name, type, status, date range, discount mechanics, funding source, budget cap, FTC disclosure requirements) and listing enrollment details as child records (enrolled listing references, promotional pricing, quantity limits, seller acceptance status, enrollment source, enrollment date, eligibility score at enrollment, enrollment status). Supports lightning deals, deal-of-the-day, coupons, bundle deals, and seasonal events. Manages the many-to-many relationship between promotions and marketplace listings including enrollment workflows and seller opt-in tracking.'
}

Table marketplace.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Policies apply to specific listings; adding marketplace_listing_id FK connects policies to listings.']
  applicable_marketplace_segment varchar [note: 'Marketplace segment(s) to which the policy applies.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific compliance obligations outlined in the policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL to the full policy document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy becomes effective.']
  enforcement_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level for enforcement actions when the policy is violated.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the policy expires or is no longer in effect.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent policy review.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next policy review.']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the policy purpose. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: listing_standards|seller_conduct|buyer_protection|advertising_standards|prohibited_items|ftc_compliance|gdpr_data_handling — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the policy, used in reporting and compliance.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the policy.']
  regulatory_authority_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory body governing the policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the policy record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the policy, e.g., \'v1.2\'.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Numeric version of the policy for ordering and comparison.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the operational policies governing marketplace participant behavior, listing standards, and platform rules. Captures policy identifier, policy name, policy category (listing standards, seller conduct, buyer protection, advertising standards, prohibited items, FTC compliance, GDPR data handling), policy version, effective date, expiry date, policy document URL, applicable marketplace segment (B2C, C2C, B2B), enforcement severity, and regulatory authority reference (FTC, CPSC, GDPR, CCPA). Provides the authoritative policy registry for marketplace compliance enforcement.'
}

Table marketplace.buyer_protection_claim {
  buyer_protection_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the buyer protection claim record.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace transaction that triggered the claim.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Buyer protection claims are evaluated against specific consumer‑protection regulations; the claim record must reference the governing regulation.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the seller associated with the disputed transaction.']
  service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Buyer protection claims are handled as support cases; linking enables unified case management and reporting.']
  appeal_deadline timestamp [note: 'Cut‑off date‑time by which an appeal must be submitted.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount approved for reimbursement after investigation.']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount the buyer is seeking to recover.']
  claim_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the claim reason.']
  claim_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the claim amount.']
  claim_description varchar [note: 'Narrative provided by the buyer describing the issue.']
  claim_filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the buyer submitted the protection claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable claim reference number assigned at filing.']
  claim_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned for processing the claim.']
  claim_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the claim initiation.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the claim.']
  claim_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the claim category.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the buyer\'s grievance.']
  fraud_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any fraud investigation linked to the claim.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score generated by the fraud detection engine.']
  is_appealed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buyer has filed an appeal against the decision.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the buyer (after any deductions).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  reimbursement_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for funding the claim payout.']
  requested_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount initially requested by the buyer before any adjustments.']
  resolution_date timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the claim was resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from the investigator or support agent about the resolution.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Outcome selected by the marketplace after claim investigation.']
  supporting_evidence_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of file or document identifiers that substantiate the claim.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that performed the most recent update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that created the claim record.']

  Note: 'Master entity tracking buyer protection guarantee claims filed on the marketplace platform. Captures claim reference number, transaction reference, buyer identifier, claim type (item not received, significantly not as described, unauthorized purchase), claim filed date, claim amount, claim currency, supporting evidence references, claim status (filed, under investigation, approved, denied, appealed, closed), resolution type (full refund, partial refund, replacement, no action), resolution date, and reimbursement source (seller-funded, marketplace-funded). Supports FTC consumer protection compliance and buyer trust programs.'
}

Table marketplace.listing_compliance {
  listing_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the listing_compliance association']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace_listing']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance_obligation']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the listing for this obligation (e.g., active, waived, pending)']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'URL to the evidence document supporting compliance for this listing‑obligation pair']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contract between a marketplace listing and a compliance obligation. It captures the compliance status and supporting evidence for each listing‑obligation pair.. Existence Justification: A marketplace listing must satisfy multiple compliance obligations, and each compliance obligation applies to many listings. The compliance team actively creates, updates, and deletes these links, tracking status and evidence per listing‑obligation pair.'
}

Table marketplace.ad_placement {

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the placement definition expires or is retired; null if indefinite.']

  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this placement is the default option for new campaigns.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ad placement (e.g., "Homepage Banner").']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the placement describing its visual/functional format.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple placements are eligible; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the placement definition.']
  targeting_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules that define audience or contextual targeting for the placement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the placement record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ad_placement. Referenced by ad_placement_id.'
}

Table marketplace.marketplace {

  buyer_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct buyer accounts that have placed at least one order.']
  code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the marketplace in contracts and reporting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance state of the marketplace.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the marketplace record was first created in the data lake.']

  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the marketplace\'s purpose, target audience, and unique features.']
  gmv_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of goods and services transacted on the marketplace in the most recent fiscal year.']
  is_ftc_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the marketplace meets FTC advertising disclosure requirements.']

  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketplace (e.g., US Marketplace, EU Marketplace).']
  privacy_policy_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the marketplace\'s privacy policy document.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region the marketplace serves, expressed as a 3‑letter ISO country code.']
  seller_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct seller accounts actively listing on the marketplace.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the marketplace.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the marketplace was permanently closed or retired (null if still active).']
  terms_of_service_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the marketplace\'s terms of service document.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of marketplace based on scope and product focus.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the marketplace record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for marketplace. Referenced by marketplace_id.'
}

Table marketplace.ad_group {
  ad_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ad_group']

  ad_group_version varchar [note: 'Version number for change tracking of the ad group definition.']

  daily_cap_currency varchar [note: 'Currency for the daily spend cap.']
  device_target varchar [note: 'Device types targeted by the ad group.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group stops serving ads; null if open‑ended.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Name of the experiment the ad group belongs to, if applicable.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of impressions per user within the defined window.']
  frequency_cap_window varchar [note: 'Time window for the frequency cap.']

  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad group uses automated bidding.']

  is_smart boolean [note: 'True if the ad group leverages machine‑learning optimization.']
  language_target varchar [note: 'Language codes for audience targeting.']
  last_served_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent ad impression served by this group.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the advertising group.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple groups compete for the same inventory.']

  start_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group becomes active for serving ads.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad group.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the audience segment targeted by the ad group.']
  targeting_criteria varchar [note: 'Serialized criteria (e.g., JSON) defining demographic, interest, and behavior filters.']
  targeting_location varchar [note: 'Country codes representing geographic targeting for the ad group.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of advertising placement for the ad group.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ad group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ad_group. Referenced by ad_group_id.'
}

Table search.index {
  index_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each search index configuration.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for price‑aware search ranking; each index uses a specific price list to display correct regional prices and generate the Price‑List Performance report.']
  relevance_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.relevance_config. Business justification: An index uses a single relevance configuration; moving relevance settings to relevance_config eliminates duplication and creates a clear parent‑child relationship.']
  superseded_index_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on index (superseded_index_id)']
  analyzer varchar [note: 'Name of the text analyzer used (standard or custom).']
  build_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent index build operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the index record was created.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents currently indexed.']
  index_code varchar [note: 'System‑generated unique code for the index, often used in API calls.']
  index_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the index purpose and scope.']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the index used in UI and reporting.']
  index_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the index.']
  index_type varchar [note: 'Category of assets the index serves (e.g., product catalog, content pages, seller profiles, or category taxonomy).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this index is the default for its type.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the index is exposed to public search APIs.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 two‑letter language code for the indexed content.']
  last_build_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the index was last successfully built.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier combining language and region (e.g., en_US).']
  max_result_window varchar [note: 'Maximum number of search results that can be returned in a single query.']
  refresh_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval in minutes for incremental index refreshes.']
  retention_policy_days varchar [note: 'Number of days indexed documents are retained before expiration.']
  schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the index schema definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']
  size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical storage size of the index in megabytes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the data for this index.']
  stemming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stemming is applied during indexing.']
  stop_word_list varchar [note: 'Reference name of the stop‑word list applied to the index.']
  tokenization_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to split text into searchable tokens.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the index record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that created the index record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the search index configuration for a product catalog or content corpus. Captures index name, index type (product, content, seller), schema version, language locale, tokenization strategy, stemming configuration, stop-word lists, and index build status. Serves as the authoritative registry of all active and archived search indexes powering the e-commerce platform.'
}

Table search.indexed_document {
  indexed_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each searchable document in the index.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Enables search UI carrier filter, allowing customers to select products shipped by preferred carriers; required for shipping option display and delivery time estimation.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating entity (product, seller listing, or content page).']
  content_digital_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: MEDIA SEARCH: Linking assets to indexed_document enables asset‑level rights tracking and performance metrics for image/video search results.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Supports zone‑based search filtering to show products deliverable to a customer’s region and calculate zone‑specific shipping costs and SLA.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Each indexed document belongs to a specific search index; adding index_id creates the required parent‑child link.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion campaign applied to the document.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory tagging: each indexed product must be linked to its compliance obligation for audit and display compliance status.']
  previous_version_indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on indexed_document (previous_version_indexed_document_id)']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Ensures each indexed document references its price list item for accurate price display and supports the Search‑Price Synchronization audit.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller who owns the listing.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: SKU‑LEVEL SEARCH: SKU-specific documents need a direct FK to sku.sku_id to support locale/variant indexing and inventory visibility in search results.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Needed for real‑time availability in search index; indexing pulls stock_position to populate inventory_quantity for each SKU.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Required for Supplier Catalog Search and compliance reports that filter indexed products by supplying vendor, a core e‑commerce operation.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Search index may be scoped per warehouse for location‑specific availability; linking provides node metadata for indexing.']
  asin varchar [note: '10‑character identifier used on Amazon‑style marketplaces.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current stock availability of the item.']
  boost_score decimal(5,3) [note: 'Manual boost factor applied to influence ranking.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Brand associated with the product or listing.']
  category_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path of categories (e.g., Electronics>Computers>Laptops).']
  color varchar [note: 'Color variant of the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the document record was first created.']
  dimension_height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the product in centimeters.']
  dimension_length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the product in centimeters.']
  dimension_width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the product in centimeters.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the list price.']
  discount_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Promotional price after discount, if applicable.']
  ean varchar [note: '13‑digit international product identifier.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the primary product image used in search results.']
  index_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the document was indexed.']
  indexed_document_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description used for relevance scoring.']
  indexed_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the indexed document.']
  is_promoted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is part of a paid promotion.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of keywords extracted for indexing.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO language code of the document content.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the indexed document.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO country code representing the locale.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the product.']
  personalization_score decimal(5,3) [note: 'Score reflecting personalization signals for the user segment.']
  price_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard list price of the item in the catalog.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the price.']
  rating_average decimal(3,2) [note: 'Mean star rating from customer reviews.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,3) [note: 'Algorithmic relevance score computed at indexing time.']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total count of customer reviews for the item.']
  seller_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the seller.']
  shipping_weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Weight of the item used for shipping calculations.']
  size varchar [note: 'Size or dimension variant (e.g., Small, Medium, Large).']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit used for inventory management.']
  source_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of source entity that the indexed document represents (e.g., SKU, seller listing, content page).']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL to a small thumbnail version of the product image.']
  title varchar [note: 'Primary title displayed in search results.']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit global product identifier.']

  Note: 'Represents a single indexed document (SKU, catalog item, seller listing, or content page) within a search index. Captures document ID, source entity type and reference, indexed field payload (title, description, attributes, price, availability), boost score, index timestamp, and document status (active, suppressed, pending). This is the operational record of what is actually searchable on the platform at any point in time.'
}

Table search.query_log {
  query_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for each query log record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant applied to the search experience for this query.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who issued the query.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Each query_log record is generated against a specific search index; adding index_id enables direct join to index without indirect lookup.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Search audit: queries that may retrieve regulated items need to be associated with the relevant compliance obligation for reporting.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Captures the active price list at query time for pricing‑impact analytics, required for the Pricing Effectiveness Dashboard.']
  refined_from_query_log_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on query_log (refined_from_query_log_id)']
  applied_filters varchar [note: 'JSON-encoded string of filter criteria applied to the search (e.g., price range, brand).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used to submit the query.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search service instance or node that processed the query.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the search query was issued by the user or agent.']
  event_type_or_channel varchar [note: 'Category of the search interaction, e.g., standard search, autocomplete, or suggestion request.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City component of the client location.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the client IP.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region component of the client location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client device that issued the query.']
  normalized_query varchar [note: 'Standardized version of the query after tokenization, stemming, and case folding.']
  platform_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the search request was made.']
  query_language varchar [note: 'ISO language code of the query text (e.g., en, es, fr).']
  query_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from query submission to result delivery.']
  raw_query varchar [note: 'Exact text entered by the user before any normalization or preprocessing.']
  result_count varchar [note: 'Number of search results returned for the query.']
  search_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the search algorithm or configuration used for this query.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the query to a user session for behavioral analysis.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Sorting option selected for the query results.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user-agent header identifying client software.']
  zero_result_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query returned zero results (true) or at least one result (false).']

  Note: 'Immutable transactional record capturing every search query submitted by a user or automated agent on the platform. Stores raw query string, normalized query, session ID, customer reference, device type, platform channel, timestamp, result count returned, zero-result flag, applied filters, sort order, and query latency in milliseconds. Primary behavioral signal source for relevance tuning and personalization.'
}

Table search.result {
  result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the search result record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who performed the search.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or content document displayed as the result.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Result filtering: each search result must reference the compliance obligation of the document to enforce compliance rules at display time.']
  previous_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on result (previous_result_id)']
  query_log_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the search query that produced this result set.']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the user clicked the result (null if not clicked).']
  clicked_flag boolean [note: 'True if the user clicked on the result; otherwise false.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for the search (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet).']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search engine instance or service that produced the result.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the search result was generated.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event, e.g., search_result or search_suggestion.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the user\'s location at query time.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the result was personalized for the user (true) or generic (false).']
  position varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the result within the returned result set (1‑based).']
  query_text varchar [note: 'The literal text entered by the user for the search.']
  ranking_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ranking algorithm used to produce the score.']
  relevance_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Score assigned by the ranking algorithm indicating relevance of the document to the query.']
  search_query_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category or intent classification of the query (e.g., electronics, apparel).']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user session during which the query was executed.']
  sponsored_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result is a paid/sponsored placement; otherwise false.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or app user‑agent string identifying the client software.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the ranked result set returned for a specific search query execution. Stores query log reference, result position, document reference, relevance score, ranking algorithm version, personalization flag, sponsored flag, and whether the result was clicked. Enables offline relevance evaluation, A/B test measurement, and ranking model training.'
}

Table search.click_event {
  click_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the click event record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who performed the click.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'A/B test or experiment identifier associated with the click.']
