  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or content document that was clicked.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Enables Click‑to‑Purchase Conversion Tracking, a core metric in marketing dashboards linking a click event to the resulting transaction.']
  previous_click_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on click_event (previous_click_event_id)']
  query_log_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the originating search query record.']
  result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.result. Business justification: A click event should be linked to the specific result set it originated from for end‑to‑end traceability. Adding result_id (FK) to click_event enables direct navigation from click to result record wit']
  click_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in milliseconds between result display and user click.']
  click_rank_score double [note: 'Score assigned by the ranking algorithm to the clicked item at click time.']
  click_source varchar [note: 'Originating UI element or channel that generated the click.']
  click_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the click source (e.g., organic search, sponsored ad, recommendation widget).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the associated conversion was recorded (null if no conversion).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the user\'s location at click time.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used for the click.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the click event occurred.']
  experiment_variant varchar [note: 'Variant of the experiment shown to the user.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at click time.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'True if the click is identified as generated by an automated bot.']
  is_conversion boolean [note: 'True if the click later resulted in a purchase or other conversion event.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code of the user\'s interface.']
  page_section varchar [note: 'Logical section of the page where the result was displayed.']
  page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page where the click occurred.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result was personalized for the user.']
  recommendation_widget_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recommendation widget that displayed the clicked item.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the user to the current result.']
  result_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based position of the clicked item in the result list.']
  search_query varchar [note: 'The raw text entered by the user for the search.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the user session during which the click occurred.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string reported by the client.']

  Note: 'Transactional behavioral signal record capturing each click interaction a user makes on a search result or recommendation widget. Captures query log reference, clicked document reference, result position at time of click, click timestamp, session ID, customer reference, device type, and downstream conversion flag. Core input to click-through rate (CTR) computation and learning-to-rank (LTR) model training.'
}

Table search.relevance_config {
  relevance_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each relevance configuration record.']
  content_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test linked to this relevance configuration.']
  superseded_relevance_config_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on relevance_config (superseded_relevance_config_id)']
  active_version_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the currently active version for its type.']
  bm25_b decimal(5,2) [note: 'BM25 length normalization factor.']
  bm25_k1 decimal(5,2) [note: 'BM25 term frequency scaling factor.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the configuration in downstream systems.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the relevance configuration (e.g., "Base Search Boost").']
  config_type varchar [note: 'Category of the configuration indicating its primary purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the configuration record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the configuration becomes effective for live traffic.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the configuration is no longer applied (null if open‑ended).']
  enable_spellcheck boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether automatic spell‑checking is performed on user queries.']
  enable_synonyms boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether synonym expansion is applied during query processing.']
  field_boost_brand decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative boost applied to the brand attribute in relevance calculations.']
  field_boost_category decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative boost applied to the product category field.']
  field_boost_description decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative boost applied to the product description field.']
  field_boost_title decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative boost applied to the product title field during relevance scoring.']
  freshness_decay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after publication when freshness decay begins.']
  freshness_decay_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Daily percentage reduction applied to relevance after the decay start.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this configuration is the default fallback for its type.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (language and region) for which the configuration applies, e.g., "en_US".']
  max_results varchar [note: 'Upper limit on the number of results returned for a query.']
  min_query_length varchar [note: 'Minimum number of characters required for a query to be processed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, change‑log entries, or operational comments.']
  personalization_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight given to personalized signals (e.g., user behavior) in the final relevance score.']
  popularity_boost_coefficient decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied based on product popularity metrics (e.g., sales velocity).']
  ranking_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to compute relevance scores.']
  relevance_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the configuration.']
  relevance_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the configuration.']
  rollout_percentage varchar [note: 'Percentage of traffic exposed to this configuration during an experiment.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the configuration.']
  use_tfidf boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether TF‑IDF scoring is enabled instead of BM25.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the configuration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining the relevance tuning parameters applied to a search index. Captures field boost weights (title, brand, category, description), BM25 or TF-IDF parameter settings, freshness decay function, popularity boost coefficients, personalization weight, and active version flag. Owned by the search team and versioned to support rollback and A/B comparison of relevance configurations.'
}

Table search.ranking_model {
  ranking_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the ranking model record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dataset used to train the model.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: A ranking model is scoped to a particular index; linking via index_id avoids orphan models.']
  model_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the model to an experiment run.']
  primary_ranking_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data science owner or team responsible for the model.']
  superseded_ranking_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ranking_model (superseded_ranking_model_id)']
  artifact_location varchar [note: 'URI or path to the stored model artifact (e.g., S3 bucket).']
  champion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the model is the current champion in production.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the model processes personal data subject to GDPR.']
  compliance_pci boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the model handles payment card data subject to PCI DSS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ranking model record was created in the data lake.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment role of the model in production.']
  evaluation_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to evaluate model performance.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric score of the selected evaluation metric.']
  feature_set_version varchar [note: 'Version of the feature set used during training.']
  hyperparameters varchar [note: 'Serialized hyperparameter settings (JSON string).']
  last_deployed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent deployment of the model.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the model, used in governance and reporting.']
  model_deployment_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or cloud zone where the model is deployed.']
  model_deprecation_date date [note: 'Planned date when the model will be deprecated.']
  model_framework varchar [note: 'Machine learning framework used to build the model.']
  model_input_schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the input feature schema expected by the model.']
  model_last_evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent model evaluation.']
  model_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Average inference latency in milliseconds.']
  model_output_schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the output schema produced by the model.']
  model_precision decimal(5,4) [note: 'Precision metric of the model on validation data.']
  model_recall decimal(5,4) [note: 'Recall metric of the model on validation data.']
  model_retirement_date date [note: 'Planned date when the model will be retired from service.']
  model_throughput_qps varchar [note: 'Maximum queries per second the model can handle.']
  model_training_compute varchar [note: 'Specification of compute resources used for training (e.g., instance type).']
  model_training_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for training the model.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the model algorithmic approach.']
  ranking_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the model purpose and characteristics.']
  ranking_model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ranking model.']
  ranking_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model.']
  training_data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or description of the training data origin.']
  training_data_volume varchar [note: 'Number of records in the training dataset.']
  training_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when model training completed.']
  training_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when model training started.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ranking model record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier (e.g., 1.2.3).']

  Note: 'Master record representing a trained or rule-based ranking model used to order search results. Captures model name, model type (LTR, BM25, neural, hybrid), training dataset reference, feature set version, evaluation metric (NDCG, MRR, MAP), deployment status, champion/challenger flag, and model artifact location. Enables governance of which ranking model is live in production versus in experimentation.'
}

Table search.experiment {
  experiment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the experiment record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for the experiment.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Experiments are run against a specific index; the FK ties the experiment to its target index.']
  iterated_from_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on experiment (iterated_from_experiment_id)']
  price_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_experiment. Business justification: Links search experiments to the corresponding price experiments, enabling the Price‑Experiment Impact report that attributes search metrics to price changes.']
  control_definition varchar [note: 'Configuration details of the control group (baseline) for the experiment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the experiment record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment is scheduled to end or was concluded.']
  experiment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the experiment for tracking across systems.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the experiment used in reporting and UI.']
  experiment_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the experiment, such as global, specific product category, brand, or segment.']
  experiment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the experiment.']
  experiment_type varchar [note: 'Category of experiment defining the change being tested.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'Business hypothesis being tested, expressed in plain language.']
  is_randomized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether traffic allocation to arms is performed randomly.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the experiment.']
  platform varchar [note: 'System or product area where the experiment is applied.']
  primary_success_metric varchar [note: 'Key metric used to evaluate experiment performance (e.g., conversion_rate).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment is scheduled to begin.']
  success_metric_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target value for the primary success metric that defines experiment success.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer segment targeted by the experiment.']
  traffic_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total traffic directed to the treatment arm(s).']
  treatment_definition varchar [note: 'Configuration details of the treatment group(s) for the experiment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the experiment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the experiment definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Master record for A/B and multivariate search experiments testing ranking algorithms, relevance configurations, query understanding changes, or UI treatments. Captures experiment name, hypothesis, control and treatment arm definitions, traffic allocation percentage, target audience segment, start and end dates, primary success metric, and experiment status (draft, running, concluded). Owned exclusively by the search domain.'
}

Table search.experiment_assignment {
  experiment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the experiment assignment record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the session.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search experiment to which the assignment belongs.']
  reassigned_from_experiment_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on experiment_assignment (reassigned_from_experiment_assignment_id)']
  arm varchar [note: 'Arm of the experiment assigned to the session (e.g., control or treatment).']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the user to an experiment arm.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the assignment process.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the experiment assignment was made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used in the session.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event recorded (fixed value for experiment assignment).']
  experiment_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for the experiment.']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the session origin. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|... — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the assignment.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user session (e.g., cookie hash).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the session originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or app user‑agent string for the session.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the assignment of a user session or customer to a specific search experiment arm. Stores experiment reference, arm identifier (control/treatment), session ID, customer reference, assignment timestamp, and assignment method (cookie hash, user ID hash, segment-based). Enables per-user experiment attribution and prevents assignment churn across sessions.'
}

Table search.query_rewrite_rule {
  query_rewrite_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the query rewrite rule.']
  chained_query_rewrite_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on query_rewrite_rule (chained_query_rewrite_rule_id)']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Query rewrite rules are defined per index; adding index_id associates the rule with its index.']
  case_sensitive boolean [note: 'Specifies if the pattern matching should respect case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null indicates no planned expiration.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the rule has been applied.']
  is_regex boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trigger_pattern is a regular expression.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (e.g., en_US) indicating the language context for the rule.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent time the rule was applied to a query.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining order of rule application; lower numbers run first.']
  query_rewrite_rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code uniquely representing the rule within the search configuration.']
  query_rewrite_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule\'s purpose and behavior.']
  query_rewrite_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rewrite rule used for display and management.']
  query_rewrite_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  replacement varchar [note: 'Text or set of terms that replace or augment the trigger pattern.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of rewrite logic applied (e.g., synonym expansion, spell correction).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system or module that supplied the rule (e.g., Search Engine Config).']
  trigger_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern or term that, when detected in a user query, activates the rewrite.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule for change‑management tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining query understanding and rewrite rules applied during query processing. Captures rule type (synonym expansion, spell correction, stemming override, stopword removal, query expansion), trigger pattern, replacement or expansion terms, language locale, priority order, active flag, and effective date range. Enables the search team to manage query normalization logic without code deployments.'
}

Table search.synonym_set {
  synonym_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each synonym set.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Synonym sets are scoped to an index; the FK links the set to its index.']
  parent_synonym_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on synonym_set (parent_synonym_set_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the synonym set record was first created.']
  directionality varchar [note: 'Indicates whether synonyms are applied in one direction only or both directions.']
  domain_context varchar [note: 'Business domain or vertical to which the synonym set is scoped.']
  is_case_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether synonym matching respects character case.']
  is_exact_match boolean [note: 'If true, synonyms are applied only when the term matches exactly; otherwise partial matches are allowed.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'ISO language and optional region code for which the synonym set applies (e.g., en-US).']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the synonym set was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes for internal stakeholders.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or team that performed the last review.']
  set_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the synonym set, often used in integration payloads.']
  set_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the synonym set used for display and search configuration.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the synonym set (e.g., Search Engine, PIM).']
  synonym_set_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the purpose and usage of the synonym set.']
  synonym_set_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the synonym set.']
  synonyms varchar [note: 'Pipe‑delimited list of synonym terms belonging to the set.']
  term_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual terms in the synonym set.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the synonym set.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a named synonym group used in query expansion and index-time tokenization. Captures synonym set name, synonym terms list, directionality (unidirectional vs bidirectional), language locale, domain context (electronics, fashion, grocery), active flag, and last reviewed date. Managed by the search merchandising team to improve recall for equivalent search terms.'
}

Table search.facet_config {
  facet_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the facet configuration record.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Facet configurations belong to a specific index; the FK creates the parent relationship.']
  parent_facet_config_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on facet_config (parent_facet_config_id)']
  applicable_category varchar [note: 'Product category (e.g., electronics, apparel) where the facet is active.']
  applicable_index varchar [note: 'Name of the search index or collection to which the facet applies.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier recorded for audit trail of creation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the facet configuration record was first created.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the facet in the filter panel.']
  display_style varchar [note: 'UI presentation style for the facet.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the facet configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the facet configuration expires (null if indefinite).']
  facet_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the facet purpose and usage.']
  facet_config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name shown to users for the facet.']
  facet_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the facet configuration.']
  facet_key varchar [note: 'Technical key used in the search index to reference this facet.']
  facet_type varchar [note: 'Category of UI control used for the facet.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facet is part of the default filter set.']
  is_multi_select boolean [note: 'True if users can select multiple values for this facet.']
  is_personalized boolean [note: 'True if the facet values are personalized per user behavior.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'True if the facet must be applied before search results are returned.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'True if the facet participates in search relevance calculations.']
  max_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound for numeric range facets.']
  max_values_shown varchar [note: 'Maximum number of distinct facet values displayed to the user.']
  min_doc_frequency varchar [note: 'Minimum number of documents required for a facet value to be displayed.']
  min_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound for numeric range facets.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the facet configuration.']
  value_step decimal(10,2) [note: 'Increment step for range sliders.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the facet configuration.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the facet configuration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining the faceted navigation filters available on search result pages for a given category or index context. Captures facet name, attribute field reference, facet type (checkbox, range slider, color swatch, rating), display order, max values shown, minimum document frequency threshold, and active flag. Drives the dynamic filter panel rendered on PLPs and SERPs.'
}

Table search.merchandising_rule {
  merchandising_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchandising rule.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product document (e.g., SKU, ASIN) that the rule targets.']
  overrides_merchandising_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on merchandising_rule (overrides_merchandising_rule_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the rule\'s governance workflow.']
  boost_delta decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric boost score applied to the target document when rule_type is boost.']
  bury_delta decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric penalty applied to the target document when rule_type is bury.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is deactivated (null if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active.']
  merchandising_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Integer priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules apply; higher values win.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'System‑generated code that uniquely identifies the rule within the search platform.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the business intent behind the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule used by merchandisers.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of intervention applied to search results (e.g., pin, boost, bury, block, redirect).']
  trigger_category varchar [note: 'Product category or taxonomy node that activates the rule when matched.']
  trigger_query varchar [note: 'Search query string that activates the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the merchandiser who created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master record for manual search merchandising interventions applied to specific queries or category pages. Captures rule type (pin, boost, bury, block, redirect), trigger query or category, target document reference, boost/bury score delta, effective date range, created by merchandiser, and approval status. Enables the merchandising team to override algorithmic ranking for commercial and promotional purposes.'
}

Table search.recommendation_model {
  recommendation_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each recommendation model record.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Recommendation models are trained for a particular index; linking via index_id provides clear ownership.']
  superseded_recommendation_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recommendation_model (superseded_recommendation_model_id)']
  training_data_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data snapshot used for training the model.']
  challenger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is a challenger being evaluated against the champion.']
  champion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is the current production champion.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the model (e.g., compliant, non‑compliant, pending).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_privacy_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the data privacy level associated with the model\'s training data.']
  deployment_context varchar [note: 'Target UI or channel where the model is deployed (e.g., product detail page widget, cart page, homepage, email).']
  feature_set_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the feature set used during model training.']
  hyperparameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded string of hyperparameter key‑value pairs used during training.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the model is in experimental mode and not yet production‑ready.']
  last_deployed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent deployment of this model to a production environment.']
  metric_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance metric was recorded.']
  metric_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the performance metric at the time of evaluation.']
  model_artifact_uri varchar [note: 'Location (e.g., cloud storage path) of the serialized model artifact.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the model in downstream systems.']
  model_cpu_cores varchar [note: 'Number of CPU cores allocated for model inference.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the model\'s purpose, scope, and key characteristics.']
  model_framework varchar [note: 'Machine‑learning framework used to build the model (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, Spark ML, XGBoost).']
  model_gpu_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GPU resources are required for model inference.']
  model_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Observed average inference latency in milliseconds.']
  model_memory_mb varchar [note: 'Memory footprint of the model when loaded for inference.']
  model_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the team or individual responsible for the model.']
  model_retirement_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the model will be retired from production.']
  model_retirement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for retiring the model (e.g., performance degradation, business rule change).']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the model (active, inactive, deprecated, testing).']
  model_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of business tags or labels applied to the model (e.g., seasonal, high‑value).']
  model_training_environment varchar [note: 'Computing environment where training was executed (e.g., Databricks Spark cluster).']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recommendation algorithm (e.g., collaborative filtering, content‑based, hybrid, trending, frequently bought together, similar items).']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string of the model (e.g., 1.2.0).']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Name of the primary performance metric used to evaluate the model (e.g., precision_at_k, ndcg).']
  recommendation_model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the recommendation model.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the model received required regulatory approval, if applicable.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory approval record linked to the model.']
  training_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithmic technique used to train the model (e.g., ALS, Gradient Boosting, Neural Network).']
  training_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the completion of the model training run.']
  training_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the start of the model training run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a trained recommendation engine model deployed on the platform. Captures model name, recommendation type (collaborative filtering, content-based, hybrid, trending, frequently bought together, similar items), training algorithm, feature set version, deployment context (PDP widget, cart page, homepage, email), champion/challenger flag, and model artifact location.'
}

Table search.recommendation_event {
  recommendation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the recommendation event record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific variant shown to the customer.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.catalog_item.catalog_item_id — Recommendation-to-product tracing. Without this FK, recommendation effectiveness cannot be measured against actual product catalog — breaking personalization analytics and A/B test evaluation.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who received the recommendation.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test or experiment governing the recommendation.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recommended item (product, category, brand, or content).']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Recommendation compliance: recommended items must be checked against obligations (e.g., age‑restricted) before being shown to users.']
  previous_recommendation_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recommendation_event (previous_recommendation_event_id)']
  recommendation_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine‑learning model that produced the recommendation.']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the recommendation algorithm used.']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the click event, if applicable.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the conversion event, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recommendation event record was first persisted.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used by the customer (desktop, mobile, tablet).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the recommended document.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the recommendation event occurred (impression, click, or conversion).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event captured: impression, click, or conversion.']
  is_clicked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recommendation was clicked by the customer.']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the click led to a conversion (e.g., purchase).']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to generate the recommendation.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the customer\'s location at event time.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'True if the recommendation was personalized based on the customer\'s profile.']
  placement varchar [note: 'Surface where the recommendation was displayed (home page, product detail page, cart, email, or search results).']
  recommendation_event_status varchar [note: 'Processing status of the recommendation event.']
  recommendation_list_json varchar [note: 'JSON array containing the ordered list of recommended document IDs and scores.']
  recommendation_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based rank position of the document within the recommendation list.']
  recommendation_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Confidence score assigned by the model to the recommendation.']
  request_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the recommendation request for tracing.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user session associated with the recommendation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system that generated the recommendation event (e.g., recommendation_engine).']
  surface varchar [note: 'Channel or device family through which the recommendation was served.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recommendation event record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string of the client device.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each recommendation impression served to a user on any platform surface. Stores recommendation model reference, placement context (PDP, cart, homepage, email), session ID, customer reference, recommended document list with positions, impression timestamp, and downstream click and conversion flags. Primary signal for measuring recommendation effectiveness and training feedback loops.'
}

Table search.personalization_profile {
  personalization_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each personalization profile record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active A/B test variant influencing ranking logic.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the registered customer to which this profile belongs.']
  merged_from_personalization_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on personalization_profile (merged_from_personalization_profile_id)']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Privacy governance: user profiles are linked to the governing regulation (e.g., GDPR) to track consent and data‑protection requirements.']
  behavioral_vector varchar [note: 'Serialized representation (e.g., JSON) of the user’s latent interest vector used for ranking.']
  click_count_last_30d varchar [note: 'Number of product result clicks recorded in the last 30 days.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when privacy consent was recorded.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering personalized communications.']
  conversion_rate_estimate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Projected probability (0‑1) that a presented result will convert for this profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the profile record was first created.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used during the session (mobile, desktop, tablet).']
  gdpr_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the user exercised a GDPR right to opt out of personalization.']
  interest_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated numeric score (0‑100) representing the strength of the user’s interest in the catalog.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'True if the profile belongs to an unauthenticated (guest) session.']
  last_model_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the personalization model was last refreshed for this profile.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the user’s location.']
  location_region varchar [note: 'Sub‑national region or state code of the user’s location.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the recommendation/personalization model that generated the profile state.']
  personalization_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the personalization profile.']
  preferred_brand_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of brand identifiers the user prefers.']
  preferred_category_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of category identifiers the user shows affinity for.']
  price_range_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the price range the user typically targets.']
  price_range_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the price range the user typically targets.']
  privacy_consent_given boolean [note: 'True if the user has consented to personalized data processing.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the personalization profile (e.g., "John Doe Profile" or "Guest Session 123").']
  recently_viewed_skus varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of SKU identifiers viewed in the recent session.']
  recommendation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned by the recommendation engine to indicate relevance of top‑ranked items.']
  search_query_count_last_30d varchar [note: 'Number of distinct search queries issued in the last 30 days.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer/session for segmentation (e.g., new, returning, high_value, low_value).']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for an anonymous browsing session when no customer is logged in.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the browsing session ended or timed out.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the browsing session began.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the profile.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing the search and recommendation personalization state for an individual customer or anonymous session. Stores customer or session reference, preferred categories, brand affinities, price range preferences, recently viewed SKU signals, behavioral interest vector, personalization model version, and last updated timestamp. Distinct from the customer domain profile — this is the search-domain-owned behavioral state used exclusively for ranking and recommendation personalization.'
}

Table search.behavioral_signal {
  behavioral_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the aggregated behavioral signal record.']
  aggregated_from_behavioral_signal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on behavioral_signal (aggregated_from_behavioral_signal_id)']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who generated the signal.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or content document the signal relates to.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the browsing session associated with the signal.']
  behavioral_signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the signal record.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the aggregated behavioral signal record was created.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the behavioral signal record was first persisted.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used for the interaction.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction was generated by an automated bot.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the user\'s location.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'True if the signal was generated in a personalized search context.']
  recency_weighted_value double [note: 'Signal value adjusted for recency, giving higher weight to more recent interactions.']
  signal_count varchar [note: 'Number of raw events of the given signal type observed within the aggregation window.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Type of user interaction captured (e.g., view, add‑to‑cart, purchase, wishlist, dwell time, scroll depth).']
  signal_weight double [note: 'Numeric weight applied to the signal for ranking algorithms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the raw interaction events.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the behavioral signal record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Raw user‑agent header identifying browser, OS, and device details.']
  window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the rolling time window over which signals were aggregated.']
  window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the rolling time window over which signals were aggregated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing aggregated behavioral signals used as ranking and personalization inputs. Captures signal type (view, add-to-cart, purchase, wishlist, dwell time, scroll depth), document reference, signal count within a rolling window, recency-weighted signal value, customer or session reference, and signal collection timestamp. Aggregated from raw click and interaction events to serve as feature inputs for ranking and recommendation models.'
}

Table search.zero_result_query {
  zero_result_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the zero‑result query event.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant active at query time, if any.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who issued the query.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Zero‑result queries occur on a particular index; linking them directly to index improves analysis of index performance.']
  retry_of_zero_result_query_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on zero_result_query (retry_of_zero_result_query_id)']
  applied_filters varchar [note: 'JSON string representing any filters (price range, brand, category, etc.) applied at query time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zero‑result record was first persisted.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for the search (desktop, mobile, tablet).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the search query was executed.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of search event; for this product always \'zero_result\'.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client issuing the query.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was identified as originating from an automated bot.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale of the query in language_country format (e.g., en_US).']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the IP address.']
  normalized_query varchar [note: 'Standardized version of the query after tokenization, lower‑casing and stop‑word removal.']
  platform_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the search request was made (e.g., web, mobile app, API).']
  query_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the query intent (e.g., product, brand, category).']
  query_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from query submission to response generation.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'Original query string entered by the user.']
  request_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the HTTP request handling the query.']
  result_count varchar [note: 'Number of results returned for the query (always zero for this product).']
  search_engine_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search engine instance that processed the query.']
  search_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the search algorithm or index used.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user session during which the query was issued.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record (e.g., after enrichment).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or client user‑agent header value.']
  zero_result_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates that the query returned no results (always true).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing search queries that returned zero results. Stores raw query string, normalized query, session ID, customer reference, timestamp, applied filters at time of zero result, language locale, and platform channel. Used by the search team to identify gaps in catalog coverage, missing synonyms, and query understanding failures requiring remediation.'
}

Table search.autocomplete_suggestion {
  autocomplete_suggestion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the autocomplete suggestion entry.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant influencing this suggestion.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Autocomplete suggestions are generated per index; the FK ties suggestions to their index.']
  parent_autocomplete_suggestion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on autocomplete_suggestion (parent_autocomplete_suggestion_id)']
  autocomplete_suggestion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the suggestion is currently active and served to users.']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of clicks to impressions for the suggestion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the suggestion record was created.']
  display_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank determining the order of suggestion display; lower numbers appear higher.']
  editorial_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the editorial source or team responsible for the suggestion.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the suggestion should no longer be served.']
  impression_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the suggestion has been shown to users.']
  is_editorial boolean [note: 'Indicates if the suggestion was manually curated by editors.']
  is_personalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the suggestion is personalized for the user.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (e.g., en_US) indicating the language and region for the suggestion.']
  last_refreshed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the suggestion data was last refreshed from source.']
  personalization_source varchar [note: 'Source of personalization data (e.g., user profile, behavior model).']
  popularity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score representing the popularity of the suggestion based on query frequency.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating relevance of the suggestion to the user\'s intent.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the suggestion (e.g., query logs, editorial curation).']
  suggestion_text varchar [note: 'The text displayed as an autocomplete suggestion.']
  suggestion_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the suggestion as a query completion, popular search, trending term, category, or brand.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Customer segment for which the suggestion is targeted.']
  trigger_prefix varchar [note: 'The prefix string that triggers this suggestion when a user types it.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the suggestion record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the autocomplete and typeahead suggestion entries served in the search bar. Captures suggestion text, suggestion type (query completion, popular search, trending, category, brand), display rank, trigger prefix, language locale, active flag, impression count, and click-through rate. Managed by the search team and refreshed periodically based on query frequency and editorial curation.'
}

Table search.redirect_rule {
  redirect_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the redirect rule.']
  fallback_redirect_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on redirect_rule (fallback_redirect_rule_id)']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed redirect_rule to index']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redirect rule record was first created.']
  destination_url varchar [note: 'Full URL to which the user is redirected when the rule matches.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the redirect rule is no longer active; null means indefinite.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the redirect rule becomes active.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Current operational flag; true if the rule is active based on lifecycle and dates.']
  is_case_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query matching is case‑sensitive.']
  is_regex boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query_pattern is interpreted as a regular expression.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated test of the rule\'s validity.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Method used to compare the incoming query with the query_pattern.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer priority determining rule precedence when multiple rules match; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  query_pattern varchar [note: 'Exact query string or regular‑expression pattern that triggers the redirect.']
  redirect_rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the rule, used for lookup and integration.']
  redirect_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context, business purpose, or notes for the rule.']
  redirect_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the redirect rule.']
  redirect_type varchar [note: 'HTTP status code indicating permanent (301) or temporary (302) redirect.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the rule\'s business purpose.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that authored or manages the redirect rule.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the last rule test: pass, fail, or warning.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining query-to-URL redirect rules that bypass standard search results and send users directly to a landing page, category page, or PDP. Captures trigger query (exact or pattern match), destination URL, redirect type (301 permanent, 302 temporary), priority, effective date range, created by, and active flag. Used for branded queries, campaign landing pages, and high-confidence navigational queries.'
}

Table search.index_build_job {
  index_build_job_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the index build job record.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search index that this job builds or refreshes.']
  triggered_by_index_build_job_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on index_build_job (triggered_by_index_build_job_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job record was first created in the system.']
  documents_failed varchar [note: 'Number of source documents that failed to process.']
  documents_processed varchar [note: 'Number of source documents successfully processed during the job.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the index build job completed or terminated.']
  error_summary varchar [note: 'Concise description of errors or exceptions encountered during the job.']
  index_build_job_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system component that initiated the job.']
  is_real_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the job processes data in real‑time (true) or batch mode (false).']
  job_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the job is a full rebuild, incremental (delta) update, or real‑time update.']
  job_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the job definition or configuration used.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the job execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Relative priority of the job for scheduling purposes.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the index build job started execution.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the job trigger: scheduled timer, event‑driven, or manual initiation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the job record.']

  Note: 'Transactional operational record tracking each full or incremental search index build or refresh job. Captures job type (full rebuild, delta update, real-time update), index reference, trigger source (scheduled, event-driven, manual), start timestamp, end timestamp, documents processed count, documents failed count, job status, and error summary. Enables the search ops team to monitor index freshness and diagnose build failures.'
}

Table search.quality_eval {
  quality_eval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the search quality evaluation event.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific variant within the experiment.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the evaluator (human judge or automated system) that performed the evaluation.']
  baseline_quality_eval_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_eval (baseline_quality_eval_id)']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test or experiment associated with this evaluation.']
  query_log_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the query log entry associated with this evaluation.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level associated with the overall quality score (0.00–100.00).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the evaluation record was first created in the data lake.']
  evaluation_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to assess search relevance for this evaluation.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the evaluator about the evaluation.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the evaluation was performed.']
  evaluator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the evaluation was performed by a human judge or an automated system.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the evaluation completed successfully.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ranking model evaluated.']
  overall_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated quality score for the query (e.g., 0.00–100.00).']
  quality_eval_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the evaluation record.']
  query_string varchar [note: 'The raw search query text that was evaluated.']
  ranking_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version of the underlying ranking algorithm used during evaluation.']
  rated_document_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document IDs that were evaluated.']
  rated_document_positions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of result positions corresponding to the rated documents.']
  relevance_grades varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated relevance grades assigned to each document in the result set (e.g., 3|2|0|1).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that generated the evaluation record (e.g., search_engine).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the evaluation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a human or automated search quality evaluation event for a specific query. Stores query string, evaluator type (human judge, automated), evaluation methodology (NDCG, MRR, precision@k), rated document list with relevance grades, evaluation timestamp, ranking model version evaluated, and overall query quality score. Feeds the offline relevance evaluation pipeline used to gate ranking model promotions.'
}

Table search.trending_query {
  trending_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the trending query record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant influencing ranking for this period.']
  index_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Trending queries are tracked per index; the FK associates the trend record with its index.']
  prior_period_trending_query_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on trending_query (prior_period_trending_query_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'SKU of the most frequently clicked product for this query.']
  confidence_score decimal(4,3) [note: 'Algorithmic confidence (0‑1) that the trend classification is accurate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trending query record was first created.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Device category used to issue the query.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name of the query origin.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the query origin.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'Region or state name of the query origin.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client that issued the query.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'True if the query originated from an automated bot.']
  is_promoted boolean [note: 'True if the query is being actively promoted in UI widgets.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query is identified as seasonal.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the query (e.g., en, fr).']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent occurrence of the query within the period.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (e.g., en_US) representing language and region.']
  normalized_query varchar [note: 'Standardized version of the query after lower‑casing, stemming, and stop‑word removal.']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End time of the trend aggregation window.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start time of the trend aggregation window.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the aggregation window for the trend (hourly, daily, weekly, monthly).']
  query_hash varchar [note: 'Deterministic hash of the normalized query used for fast deduplication.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'Exact text entered by the user in the search box.']
  query_volume varchar [note: 'Number of times the query was issued during the period.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the record.']
  related_product_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct products associated with the query during the period.']
  search_engine_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the search algorithm used during the period.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the query (web, mobile, app).']
  trend_classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the query trend (rising, peak, declining, stable).']
  trending_query_category varchar [note: 'Top‑level product or content category linked to the query.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  user_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment classification applied at query time.']
  volume_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in query volume compared to the previous period.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking trending and seasonally popular search queries on the platform. Captures query string, normalized query, trend period (hourly, daily, weekly), query volume, volume change percentage versus prior period, trend classification (rising, peak, declining), category association, and language locale. Used to power trending search widgets, autocomplete prioritization, and inventory demand signals.'
}

Table search.filter_usage {
  filter_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the filter usage event.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant influencing the search experience.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who applied the filter.']
  facet_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facet configuration that defines this filter.']
  previous_filter_usage_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on filter_usage (previous_filter_usage_id)']
  query_log_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original search query associated with this filter usage.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment classification at the time of filter usage.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for the search session (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filter usage event occurred.']
  filter_action varchar [note: 'Action taken on the filter: apply to narrow results or remove to broaden.']
  filter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facet filter applied (e.g., brand, price_range, color).']
  filter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value selected for the filter (e.g., \'Nike\', \'100-200\', \'Red\').']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was identified as automated bot traffic.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the user\'s location.']
  result_count_after varchar [note: 'Number of search results returned after the filter was applied.']
  result_count_before varchar [note: 'Number of search results returned before the filter was applied.']
  result_delta varchar [note: 'Difference in result count after applying the filter (after minus before).']
  search_version varchar [note: 'Version of the search engine or algorithm used for this session.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the search session in which the filter was used.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the search session in seconds.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the search session ended.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the search session started.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User agent string of the client making the search request.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each facet filter application event during a search session. Stores query log reference, facet config reference, filter value selected, filter action (apply, remove), session ID, customer reference, result count before and after filter application, and timestamp. Enables analysis of filter adoption rates, filter effectiveness, and facet configuration optimization.'
}

Table search.search_session {
  search_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for search_session']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who performed the search session.']
  previous_search_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on search_session (previous_search_session_id)']
  click_count varchar [note: 'Number of result clicks recorded during the session.']
  clicked_result_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of result identifiers that were clicked.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the user location.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for the search session.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the search session started (event occurrence).']
  event_type_or_channel varchar [note: 'Category of the search event (e.g., standard search, recommendation, autocomplete).']
  experiment_variant varchar [note: 'A/B test variant assigned to the session for search experiments.']
  filters_applied varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of any facet filters applied during the search.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client initiating the search session.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the session was identified as automated (bot) traffic.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO language code of the user\'s preferred language.']
  page_number varchar [note: 'Page number of the result set viewed in the session.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'True if personalization algorithms were applied to the search results.']
  ranking_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ranking algorithm used for this session.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the session record.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sub‑national region or state code of the user location.']
  result_count varchar [note: 'Number of results returned for the query.']
  results_clicked varchar [note: 'Number of displayed results that were clicked.']
  results_shown varchar [note: 'Total number of results displayed to the user.']
  search_query varchar [note: 'The raw text query entered by the user.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the session in seconds.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the search session ended.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the search session.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Sorting order applied to the result set (e.g., relevance, price_asc).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user-agent string for the session.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for search_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table analytics.kpi_definition {
  kpi_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each KPI definition. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role categories and is a master registry of KPI metadata.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business party (e.g., department, team, or individual) responsible for the KPI.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: KPI definitions are often scoped to product categories for category performance reporting and targets.']
  parent_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kpi_definition (parent_kpi_definition_id)']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: KPIs like "Price Elasticity per List" are defined per price list; the FK ties the KPI definition to its relevant price list.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting KPIs must be tied to a specific regulation; linking KPI to regulation enables compliance reporting and audit traceability.']
  aggregation_level varchar [note: 'Granularity at which the KPI is aggregated for reporting.']
  business_line varchar [note: 'High‑level business line or division (e.g., Marketplace, Fulfillment) that the KPI supports.']
  calculation_formula varchar [note: 'Logical expression or algorithm used to compute the KPI from source data elements.']
  change_log varchar [note: 'Free‑text log of modifications made to the KPI definition, including rationale.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance constraints that the KPI must satisfy (e.g., GDPR, SOX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KPI definition record was first created in the registry.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the KPI data according to enterprise data policy.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the assessed quality of the source data feeding the KPI.']
  data_source_domain varchar [note: 'Primary data domain (e.g., order, customer, finance) that supplies the raw inputs for the KPI.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition becomes valid for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition is retired or superseded (null if indefinite).']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the KPI contains confidential information that requires restricted access.']
  kpi_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI definition.']
  kpi_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of what the KPI measures and why it matters.']
  kpi_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the KPI as used in reports and dashboards.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last formal review of the KPI definition for relevance and accuracy.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the metric such as Performance, Financial, Operational, or Customer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the KPI.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Business role or title of the KPI owner (e.g., Finance Analyst, Marketing Manager).']
  refresh_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the KPI value is recomputed and refreshed in reporting.']
  regulatory_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory reporting mandate (e.g., SEC, GDPR) that the KPI fulfills.']
  related_kpi_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other KPI definition IDs that are logically related or derived.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the KPI is expected to be reported to stakeholders.']
  target_threshold_lower decimal(20,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable KPI range (used when threshold_type = \'range\').']
  target_threshold_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the KPI has an upper bound, lower bound, or a range of acceptable values.']
  target_threshold_upper decimal(20,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable KPI range (used when threshold_type = \'range\').']
  target_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Numeric target that the KPI is expected to achieve (used for single‑threshold KPIs).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the KPI value is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the KPI definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the KPI definition to track changes over time.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all enterprise KPI definitions used across business intelligence and reporting. Captures KPI name, business description, calculation formula, unit of measure, target thresholds, owner, data source domain, refresh cadence, and governance metadata. Serves as the semantic layer contract between data producers and BI consumers. Owned by the analytics domain as the authoritative catalog of what each metric means and how it is computed.'
}

Table analytics.metric_value {
  metric_value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the metric value record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for KPI dashboards that show metric values per marketing campaign, enabling campaign‑level performance analysis.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Needed for Center Performance KPI reports that track metrics per fulfillment center (e.g., daily throughput).']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Supports order‑processing time and volume metrics that must reference the exact fulfillment order.']
  indexed_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.indexed_document. Business justification: KPI \'Document Click‑Through Rate\' report needs to tie metric values to specific indexed documents for performance tracking.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Required for the Content Performance KPI Report which tracks KPI values per individual content item, enabling per‑page performance dashboards.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the KPI definition that this metric value belongs to.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Needed for shipment‑level KPI reporting (e.g., on‑time delivery) used in operational dashboards.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Required for transaction‑level KPI drill‑down reports that let analysts trace metric values back to the underlying payment transaction.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Metrics such as average discount or price elasticity are calculated per price list; linking metric values to price_list provides traceability.']
  prior_period_metric_value_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on metric_value (prior_period_metric_value_id)']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Receiving efficiency KPI tracks goods receipt timeliness per receipt, requiring metric values linked to procurement_goods_receipt.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Spend analysis and PO cycle‑time KPI need metric values tied to the originating purchase order.']
  result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to search.result. Business justification: Result‑position performance KPI aggregates metrics per search result; linking enables per‑result analysis in dashboards.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Required for Seller Performance KPI dashboards; stores metric values per seller for reporting and payout calculations.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for SKU‑level KPI dashboards that track conversion, sales, and inventory metrics per SKU.']
  snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.snapshot. Business justification: Metric values are derived from a particular inventory snapshot; the FK enables reproducibility of metric calculations and traceability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier performance KPI dashboard requires linking metric values to each supplier to calculate on‑time delivery and cost metrics.']
  computation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute the metric (e.g., aggregation, statistical model, manual entry).']
  compute_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the metric value was calculated or materialized.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑100) associated with the metric value, typically for predictive or ML‑derived metrics.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code when the metric is monetary.']
  data_freshness_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of how current the metric data is.']
  dimension_category varchar [note: 'Product category dimension associated with the metric.']
  dimension_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel dimension (e.g., online, mobile app, physical store).']
  dimension_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region.']
  dimension_seller_tier varchar [note: 'Seller tier classification (e.g., gold, silver, bronze).']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the metric value is an estimate rather than a definitive measurement.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the metric or KPI.']
  metric_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the point in time the metric value applies to (e.g., end of the reporting period).']
  metric_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Calculated numeric result of the metric.']
  metric_value_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the metric record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional context, comments, or exceptions related to the metric value.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the metric.']
  period_grain varchar [note: 'Granularity of the reporting period for the metric.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the metric.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the metric record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the metric record.']
