  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the underlying data for this metric.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the metric value (e.g., USD, count, percent).']

  Note: 'Transactional store of computed metric values for each KPI definition across time periods, business dimensions, and organizational units. Captures the calculated numeric result, period grain (daily/weekly/monthly), dimensional context (channel, region, category, seller tier), computation timestamp, and data freshness indicators. Serves as the pre-aggregated semantic layer powering dashboards and self-service BI tools without requiring raw transactional joins.'
}

Table analytics.reporting_dimension {
  reporting_dimension_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the reporting dimension.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Reporting dimensions need a direct link to product_category to support hierarchical drill‑downs in analytics.']
  parent_dimension_reporting_dimension_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent dimension in the hierarchy.']
  parent_reporting_dimension_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reporting_dimension (parent_reporting_dimension_id)']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Needed for Warehouse Location dimension in dashboards, linking dimension rows to actual warehouse nodes for accurate location‑based KPI reporting.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the dimension metadata.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dimension record was created in the system.']
  default_format varchar [note: 'Default display format for dimension values (e.g., yyyy-MM-dd).']
  default_sort_order varchar [note: 'Default ordering direction for the dimension values.']
  dimension_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of dimensions for reporting purposes.']
  domain varchar [note: 'Business domain to which the dimension belongs.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the dimension definition is effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the dimension definition is effective; null if indefinite.']
  grain_level varchar [note: 'Temporal granularity of the dimension when applicable.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Number of levels in the dimension hierarchy.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the full hierarchy (e.g., Country>State>City).']
  is_custom boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dimension is custom‑defined rather than standard.']
  is_hierarchical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the dimension has a hierarchical structure.']
  is_time_dimension boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the dimension represents time.']
  is_time_zone_aware boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the dimension accounts for time zones.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the dimension when it represents a quantitative attribute.']
  reporting_dimension_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code identifying the dimension across systems.']
  reporting_dimension_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the dimension purpose and usage.']
  reporting_dimension_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the reporting dimension used in analytics and dashboards.']
  reporting_dimension_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dimension.']
  reporting_dimension_type varchar [note: 'Category of the dimension indicating its analytical purpose.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that defines the dimension.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Standard time zone identifier associated with the dimension.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the dimension record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the dimension definition.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all reporting dimensions available in the enterprise semantic layer. Defines dimension name, dimension type (time, geography, product hierarchy, customer segment, channel, seller tier), grain level, associated domain, and hierarchy depth. Provides the authoritative list of slicing and dicing axes available to BI consumers and ensures consistent dimensional labeling across all dashboards and reports.'
}

Table analytics.dimension_member {
  dimension_member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each dimension member record.']
  parent_dimension_member_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dimension_member (parent_dimension_member_id)']
  parent_member_dimension_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent member in the hierarchy, enabling tree traversal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dimension member record was first created in the system.']
  dimension_member_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the member.']
  dimension_member_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the member within the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the member becomes valid for reporting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the member ceases to be valid; null indicates open‑ended.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is currently active in the dimension.']
  member_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the dimension member (e.g., SKU, GEO code, org unit code).']
  member_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label of the dimension member.']
  member_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dimension member indicating the domain it belongs to (e.g., product category, geographic region, organizational unit).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the member among its siblings for display ordering.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that supplied the member (e.g., OMS, WMS, PIM, CDP).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dimension member record.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual members within a reporting dimension hierarchy. Captures member code, member label, parent member reference for hierarchy traversal, sort order, level within hierarchy, effective date range, and is_active flag. Enables consistent roll-up and drill-down navigation across product categories, geographies, organizational units, and customer segments in all BI tools.'
}

Table analytics.dashboard_definition {
  dashboard_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each dashboard definition record.']
  cloned_from_dashboard_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dashboard_definition (cloned_from_dashboard_definition_id)']
  owner_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: Dashboards are owned by specific support teams; linking owner_team_id to team.team_id supports governance and access control.']
  access_tier varchar [note: 'Security classification governing who may view the dashboard.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Governance approval state of the dashboard.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role that approved the dashboard.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dashboard received approval.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dashboard meets regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dashboard definition record was first created.']
  dashboard_definition_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code used to reference the dashboard in governance systems.']
  dashboard_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the dashboard\'s purpose and content.']

  dashboard_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dashboard.']
  dashboard_definition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dashboard by primary audience or purpose.']

  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0‑100) reflecting the quality of source data feeding the dashboard.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Planned date for retiring the dashboard from active use.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dashboard serves as a reusable template for other dashboards.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or service that performed the most recent update to the dashboard definition.']
  last_refreshed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dashboard data was last refreshed.']
  layout_configuration varchar [note: 'JSON string describing the visual layout, tile arrangement, and sizing of the dashboard.']
  metric_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct metrics displayed on the dashboard.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or annotations about the dashboard.']
  privacy_level varchar [note: 'Privacy classification aligned with corporate data protection policies.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dashboard is publicly available, hidden, or scheduled for release.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the dashboard data is refreshed (e.g., real‑time, hourly, daily).']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how long the dashboard definition is retained after deprecation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Underlying analytics platform or tool that authored the dashboard (e.g., Databricks, Tableau).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary consumer group for the dashboard.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the dashboard definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for change management.']
  visualization_count varchar [note: 'Total number of visual components (charts, tables, maps) in the dashboard.']

  Note: 'Master record for each operational and executive dashboard deployed on the analytics platform. Captures dashboard name, business owner, target audience (executive, operational, seller, finance), refresh frequency, data domains consumed, layout configuration metadata, publication status, and access tier. Serves as the governance registry for all dashboards ensuring discoverability and ownership accountability.'
}

Table analytics.dashboard_widget {
  dashboard_widget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dashboard widget.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Widgets often display campaign‑specific charts; linking ties widget data to the campaign it visualises.']
  dashboard_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.dashboard_definition. Business justification: Dashboard widgets belong to a dashboard; adding dashboard_definition_id creates parent link and removes no redundant columns.']
  drilldown_dashboard_widget_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dashboard_widget (drilldown_dashboard_widget_id)']
  owner_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.agent. Business justification: Widgets may be customized by individual agents; linking owner_agent_id to agent.agent_id tracks widget ownership and change history.']
  bound_kpi varchar [note: 'Identifier of the KPI or metric bound to the widget.']
  chart_color_scheme varchar [note: 'Selected color scheme for chart visualizations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the widget record was initially created.']
  dashboard_widget_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the widget purpose and content.']
  dashboard_widget_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the dashboard widget.']
  dashboard_widget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the widget.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the underlying data source or query used by the widget.']
  dimension_filters varchar [note: 'Serialized list of dimensional filters applied to the widget data.']
  display_format varchar [note: 'Formatting options for the widget display, such as number format, color scheme, or unit.']
  drilldown_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether drill‑down navigation is enabled.']
  error_state varchar [note: 'Current error state of the widget after the last refresh.']
  exportable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the widget data can be exported by end users.']
  height varchar [note: 'Height of the widget in grid units.']
  is_shared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the widget is shared across multiple dashboards.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful data refresh for the widget.']
  position_x varchar [note: 'Horizontal grid column position of the widget within the dashboard layout.']
  position_y varchar [note: 'Vertical grid row position of the widget within the dashboard layout.']
  refresh_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Data refresh interval for the widget in minutes.']
  tooltip_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether tooltips are enabled on the widget.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the widget record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the widget definition for change tracking.']
  widget_type varchar [note: 'Type of visualization displayed by the widget.']
  width varchar [note: 'Width of the widget in grid units.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual visualization widgets embedded within a dashboard. Captures widget name, widget type (bar chart, line chart, scorecard, table, funnel, heatmap), bound KPI or metric, dimensional filters applied, display format, position coordinates within the dashboard layout, and data refresh configuration. Enables fine-grained governance and reuse of visualization components across multiple dashboards.'
}

Table analytics.forecast_model {
  forecast_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the forecast model record.']
  superseded_forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_model (superseded_forecast_model_id)']
  training_data_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immutable data snapshot used for training.']
  algorithm_family varchar [note: 'Machine‑learning algorithm family used to build the model.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created.']
  data_privacy_level varchar [note: 'Classified privacy level of the model\'s input data.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the model in production.']
  evaluation_metric varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to assess model performance.']
  feature_set_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the features engineered for model training.']
  forecast_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of future days the model forecasts.']
  hyperparameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded hyper‑parameter settings for the model.']
  last_trained_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent model training run.']
  model_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall accuracy of the model expressed as a percentage.']
  model_deployment_environment varchar [note: 'Target environment where the model is deployed.']
  model_deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model was deployed to the target environment.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of model purpose, scope, and methodology.']
  model_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'HTTP endpoint exposing the model for inference.']
  model_feature_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct input features used in the model.']
  model_last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent performance evaluation run.']
  model_mae decimal(10,4) [note: 'Mean absolute error of the model on validation data.']
  model_mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean absolute percentage error of the model on validation data.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the forecasting model.']
  model_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by data scientists or reviewers.']
  model_owner_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary owner responsible for the model.']
  model_owner_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the model owner for urgent communications.']
  model_retirement_date date [note: 'Planned date for model decommissioning.']
  model_risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating (0‑100) based on governance, bias, and compliance assessments.']
  model_rmse decimal(10,4) [note: 'Root mean squared error of the model on validation data.']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the model within the analytics platform.']
  model_training_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated compute cost of the training run in US dollars.']
  model_training_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total wall‑clock time taken to train the model, in seconds.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Category of business problem the model addresses.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier following semantic versioning.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Team responsible for model development and maintenance.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the model with GDPR, PCI DSS, etc.']
  target_variable varchar [note: 'Business metric the model predicts (e.g., daily sales, stock‑out risk).']
  training_data_domain varchar [note: 'Primary business domain of the data used to train the model (e.g., order, inventory, customer).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each predictive and forecasting model deployed in the enterprise analytics platform. Captures model name, model type (demand forecast, revenue forecast, inventory forecast, churn forecast), algorithm family (ARIMA, Prophet, XGBoost, LSTM), training data domain, feature set description, target variable, evaluation metrics (MAPE, RMSE, MAE), model version, deployment status, and owner team. Serves as the model registry for all production forecasting assets.'
}

Table analytics.forecast_run {
  forecast_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each forecast model execution.']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.forecast_model. Business justification: Each forecast run is generated by a specific forecast model; adding forecast_model_id consolidates model metadata.']
  input_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the input data snapshot used for this run.']
  rerun_of_forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_run (rerun_of_forecast_run_id)']
  compliance_gdpr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run complies with GDPR data handling requirements.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the forecast confidence interval.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the forecast confidence interval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast run record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary forecasts.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of data quality assessment for the run.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the input data snapshot.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Error details if the run failed.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the forecast run.']
  forecast_horizon varchar [note: 'Temporal granularity of the forecast (e.g., days, weeks).']
  forecast_units varchar [note: 'Measurement unit of the forecasted values.']
  horizon_value varchar [note: 'Numeric length of the forecast horizon.']
  is_comparison_base boolean [note: 'Marks the run as a baseline for model comparisons.']
  is_reproducible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run can be exactly reproduced.']
  model_owner varchar [note: 'Team or business unit responsible for the model.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the run.']
  output_period_end date [note: 'End date of the forecasted period.']
  output_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the forecasted period.']
  parameter_set_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parameter set applied to the model.']
  resource_usage_cpu_seconds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total CPU time consumed by the run.']
  resource_usage_memory_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Peak memory usage in megabytes.']
  run_environment varchar [note: 'Deployment environment where the run executed.']
  run_id_external varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external orchestration systems.']
  run_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the run request.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the forecast run was initiated.']
  run_user varchar [note: 'User or service that triggered the forecast run.']
  runtime_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time in seconds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each execution of a forecast model. Stores run timestamp, model version used, forecast horizon (days/weeks/months), input data snapshot reference, output forecast period, execution status, runtime duration, and data quality flags. Provides the audit trail for all forecast computations enabling reproducibility, model comparison, and governance of forecast outputs consumed by demand planning, inventory, and finance.'
}

Table analytics.forecast_output {
  forecast_output_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each forecast record.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target entity for which the forecast is generated.']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the forecast execution batch.']
  revised_forecast_output_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_output (revised_forecast_output_id)']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Observed actual value after the forecast period, used for accuracy tracking.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence level expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95).']
  confidence_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the confidence interval for the forecast value.']
  confidence_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the confidence interval for the forecast value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary forecasts.']
  forecast_period_end date [note: 'End date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast was generated.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the forecast record.']
  forecast_target_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity the forecast applies to (e.g., SKU, category, region, seller).']
  forecast_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Predicted metric value for the forecast period.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the forecasting model used.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the forecast record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the input data for the forecast. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OMS|WMS|PIM|CDP|ERP|TMS|Marketing|Search — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the forecasted value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the forecast record.']
  variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between forecasted value and actual value.']

  Note: 'Transactional store of point-in-time forecast values produced by a forecast run. Captures the forecast target entity (SKU, category, region, seller), forecast period date, predicted value, confidence interval lower and upper bounds, actuals value (populated post-period for accuracy tracking), and variance from actuals. Enables demand planners, inventory managers, and finance teams to consume structured forecast data for operational decision-making.'
}

Table analytics.report_definition {
  report_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each report definition.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who owns or is accountable for the report.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Scheduled reports that monitor campaign performance need a direct FK to the campaign they report on.']
  owner_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: Report definitions are owned by support teams; the FK ties reports to the responsible team for lifecycle management.']
  parent_report_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on report_definition (parent_report_definition_id)']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory reports are produced to satisfy a particular regulation; the FK allows the reporting engine to validate required regulatory coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the report definition record was first created.']
  data_domains varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of data domains (e.g., customer, order, finance) referenced by the report.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of email addresses or user identifiers that receive the report.']
  estimated_output_size_mb varchar [note: 'Typical size of the generated report file.']
  estimated_run_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Typical execution duration for the report, used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  format varchar [note: 'File or delivery format in which the report is produced.']
  governance_classification varchar [note: 'Data governance classification indicating sensitivity level.']
  is_executive boolean [note: 'True if the report is intended for executive leadership.']
  is_financial boolean [note: 'True if the report contains financial metrics or statements.']
  is_operational boolean [note: 'True if the report supports day‑to‑day operational monitoring.']
  is_regulatory boolean [note: 'True if the report is required for regulatory compliance.']
  is_scheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the report runs on a schedule (true) or on demand (false).']
  is_seller boolean [note: 'True if the report is primarily consumed by marketplace sellers.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the report.']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next scheduled execution.']
  owner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the report owner or steward.']
  parameters_json varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded parameter set used at runtime to filter or customize the report.']
  report_definition_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code used to reference the report.']
  report_definition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the report purpose, content, and audience.']
  report_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the report.']
  report_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the report definition.']
  report_definition_type varchar [note: 'Category of the report indicating its primary audience or purpose.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the report definition, incremented on each change.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the report definition and its metadata are retained before archival.']
  schedule_cron varchar [note: 'Cron expression that defines the recurring schedule for the report.']
  schedule_frequency varchar [note: 'Human‑readable frequency of the report schedule.']
  schedule_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier (e.g., America/Los_Angeles) for schedule calculations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User name or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the report definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User name or system identifier that created the report definition.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all scheduled and on-demand reports distributed across the enterprise. Captures report name, report type (operational, executive, regulatory, seller, financial), delivery format (PDF, Excel, CSV, embedded), distribution list, schedule cadence, data domains consumed, owning team, and governance classification. Provides the authoritative registry of all enterprise reports enabling deduplication and lifecycle management.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the execution record was first created in the system.']

  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code associated with a failure or partial execution.']
  error_details varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or stack trace when execution_status is failure or partial.']
  execution_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to generate the report, measured in seconds.']
  execution_environment varchar [note: 'Logical environment where the report was generated (e.g., production, development).']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the report run: success, failure, or partial.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the report run started.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the generated report file in bytes.']

  report_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the report definition used for this execution.']
  row_count varchar [note: 'Number of data rows returned by the report.']
  triggered_by varchar [note: 'Mechanism that initiated the report run: scheduled, manual, or API.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the execution record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each scheduled or on-demand report generation event. Stores execution timestamp, report definition reference, triggered by (scheduled/manual/API), execution status (success/failure/partial), row count returned, file size, delivery channel (email/S3/BI tool), recipient list, and error details if failed. Provides the operational audit trail for all report runs supporting SLA monitoring and troubleshooting.'
}

Table analytics.kpi_target {
  kpi_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each KPI target record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for achieving the target.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: KPI targets are set per campaign; linking stores the campaign that each target applies to.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Allows carrier‑specific KPI targets (e.g., delivery time SLA) for performance contracts.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: KPI targets are frequently set per product category to align business goals with category performance.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: KPI targets are set to meet specific compliance obligations; linking provides direct traceability from performance targets to the obligation they satisfy.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific business unit, channel, region, or seller tier.']
  owner_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: KPI targets are managed by support teams; linking owner_team_id to team.team_id enables team‑level KPI accountability.']
  primary_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the KPI definition that this target applies to.']
  superseded_kpi_target_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kpi_target (superseded_kpi_target_id)']
  tertiary_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'FK to analytics.kpi_definition']
  ceiling_threshold decimal(20,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable performance level; values above may indicate data issues.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance frameworks governing the KPI (e.g., GDPR, PCI, SOX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KPI target record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code when the target is monetary.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplies the KPI definition (e.g., OMS, PIM, CDP).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the KPI target expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  floor_threshold decimal(20,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable performance level; values below trigger alerts.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the target is evaluated.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target is considered critical for executive scorecards.']
  kpi_target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI target.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the target was last reviewed or adjusted.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., percent, USD, count).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the target.']
  organizational_scope varchar [note: 'Level of the organization to which the target applies.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the KPI target becomes effective.']
  stretch_target_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Aggressive optional target used for high‑performance incentives.']
  target_owner_name varchar [note: 'Readable name of the target owner.']
  target_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the target is measured (e.g., monthly, quarterly).']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the target is an absolute value, a relative change, or a percentage.']
  target_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Primary numeric target for the KPI (e.g., revenue amount, conversion rate).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the KPI target record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining business performance targets for each KPI across organizational units and time periods. Captures KPI definition reference, target period (monthly/quarterly/annual), organizational scope (company-wide, business unit, channel, region, seller tier), target value, stretch target value, floor threshold, and owner. Enables variance analysis between actuals and targets across all enterprise dashboards and executive scorecards.'
}

  domain varchar [note: 'Business domain to which the dataset belongs (e.g., core, marketing, finance).']
  grain varchar [note: 'Level of granularity of the dataset (e.g., per order, per customer, per product). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: order|customer|product|inventory|payment|session|daily|hourly — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dataset is currently active and available for consumption.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dataset was last refreshed from its source systems.']

  storage_path varchar [note: 'File system or object store location where the dataset resides.']
  update_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the dataset is refreshed or recomputed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the dataset metadata.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of curated analytical datasets and feature stores published in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer for consumption by BI tools, data scientists, and ML pipelines. Captures dataset name, business description, owning domain, grain level, update frequency, schema version, data classification, row count, storage path, and consumer SLA. Serves as the data product catalog enabling governed self-service analytics across the enterprise.'
}

Table analytics.cohort_definition {
  cohort_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the cohort definition record.']
  parent_cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cohort_definition (parent_cohort_definition_id)']
  cohort_definition_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or short code for the cohort, unique within the analytics domain.']
  cohort_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the cohort purpose and business rules.']
  cohort_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cohort used in reports and dashboards.']
  cohort_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cohort definition.']
  cohort_definition_type varchar [note: 'Category of cohort – acquisition (first‑time customers), behavioral (based on actions), value (revenue‑based), or custom.']
  cohort_size_estimate varchar [note: 'Projected number of members in the cohort at formation time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cohort definition record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall quality rating (0‑100) of the source data used for the cohort.']
  formation_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON or serialized expression defining the logical rules used to select members of the cohort.']
  formation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the cohort formation period ends; null for open‑ended cohorts.']
  formation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the cohort formation period begins.']
  grain varchar [note: 'Granularity of the cohort – whether members are customers, sellers, or individual SKUs.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cohort updates automatically as new data arrives (true) or is static (false).']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cohort membership was last recomputed.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the analytics team responsible for maintaining the cohort.']
  privacy_level varchar [note: 'Data privacy classification governing access to the cohort data.']
  refresh_frequency_days varchar [note: 'How often (in days) the dynamic cohort is refreshed.']
  retention_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after cohort formation that members are tracked for retention analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Primary operational system that provides the raw data used to build the cohort. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDP|OMS|WMS|PIM|Payment|ERP|TMS|Marketing|Search — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cohort definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for changes to the cohort definition.']

  Note: 'Master record defining named customer or seller cohorts used for longitudinal analytics and retention analysis. Captures cohort name, cohort type (acquisition cohort, behavioral cohort, value cohort), cohort formation criteria, formation period, cohort grain (customer, seller, SKU), and owning analytics team. Enables repeat purchase analysis, LTV cohort curves, seller growth tracking, and retention funnel reporting across the e-commerce platform.'
}

Table analytics.cohort_membership {
  cohort_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cohort membership record.']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cohort definition to which the member belongs.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Supports Seller Cohort Analysis; tracks each seller\'s entry/exit dates and status within retention cohorts.']
  superseded_cohort_membership_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cohort_membership (superseded_cohort_membership_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cohort membership record was created.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the member was first added to the cohort.']
  exit_date date [note: 'Date when the member left the cohort, if applicable.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cohort membership is currently active.']
  member_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that is a member of the cohort (customer, seller, or SKU).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free-text notes about the cohort membership.']
  period_index varchar [note: 'Zero-based index representing the period (e.g., month) relative to cohort entry for retention analysis.']
  retention_status varchar [note: 'Current retention status of the member within the cohort.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the cohort membership record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cohort membership record.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual customers, sellers, or SKUs to their assigned cohorts. Captures cohort definition reference, member entity type, member entity identifier, cohort entry date, cohort period index (period 0, 1, 2...), and retention status. Enables cohort-based retention curves, LTV progression analysis, and behavioral segmentation reporting without requiring ad-hoc cohort recomputation at query time.'
}

Table analytics.funnel_definition {
  funnel_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each funnel definition record.']
  parent_funnel_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on funnel_definition (parent_funnel_definition_id)']
  conversion_window_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days allowed between entry and exit events for a conversion to be counted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the funnel definition record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the funnel definition becomes active for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the funnel definition is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  entry_event varchar [note: 'Name of the event that marks a user entering the funnel.']
  exit_event varchar [note: 'Name of the event that marks a user exiting the funnel (conversion or drop‑off).']
  funnel_definition_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the funnel across systems.']
  funnel_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the funnel purpose and scope.']
  funnel_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the conversion funnel.']
  funnel_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the funnel definition.']
  funnel_definition_type varchar [note: 'Category of funnel (e.g., purchase, seller onboarding, checkout, search‑to‑purchase).']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Internal team responsible for maintaining the funnel definition.']
  stages varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded ordered list of stage identifiers that compose the funnel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the funnel definition.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for changes to the funnel definition.']

  Note: 'Master record defining conversion funnels tracked across the e-commerce platform. Captures funnel name, funnel type (purchase funnel, seller onboarding funnel, checkout funnel, search-to-purchase funnel), ordered list of funnel stages, entry event, exit event, conversion window, and owning team. Provides the semantic definition of all conversion funnels enabling consistent funnel analysis across marketing, product, and growth analytics.'
}

Table analytics.funnel_event {
  funnel_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the funnel event record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant presented to the user.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the event.']
  click_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the click that led to the funnel entry.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test experiment.']
  funnel_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the funnel definition to which this event belongs.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order generated as a result of conversion, if any.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Conversion funnel analysis includes the payment step; linking to the transaction enables measurement of success/failure rates per payment.']
  previous_funnel_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on funnel_event (previous_funnel_event_id)']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Captures Seller Onboarding Funnel events; allows analysis of conversion rates through onboarding stages.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the user session.']
  abandonment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for funnel abandonment, if known.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Model used to attribute credit for the conversion (e.g., last_click).']
  browser varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser used (e.g., Chrome, Safari).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel through which the entity interacted (e.g., web, mobile app).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity progressed to the next funnel stage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the funnel event record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the order value.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (e.g., advertising ID).']
  device_os_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the operating system on the device.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used (desktop, mobile, tablet).']
  entered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the entity entered the funnel stage.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity progressing through the funnel (customer, seller, or session).']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Raw payload or JSON string containing additional event details.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System or component that generated the funnel event (e.g., web app, mobile SDK).']
  exited_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the entity left the funnel stage (if applicable).']
  funnel_stage_entry_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the entity entered the stage (click, view, or automatic).']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the client location.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the client location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at event time.']
  is_abandoned boolean [note: 'True if the entity abandoned the funnel after this stage.']
  is_new_user boolean [note: 'True if this is the first recorded interaction for the entity.']
  is_returning_user boolean [note: 'True if the entity has prior interactions before this event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by analysts.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., Windows, iOS).']
  order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the order associated with the conversion.']
  page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page where the funnel event occurred.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the user to the current page.']
  screen_resolution varchar [note: 'Screen resolution of the device at event time (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  source_medium varchar [note: 'Medium through which the traffic arrived (organic, paid, referral, email).']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the funnel stage entered.']
  stage_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the stage within the funnel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the funnel event record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Full user‑agent string sent by the browser.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each customer or seller progression event through a defined conversion funnel stage. Stores funnel definition reference, entity type (customer/session/seller), entity identifier, funnel stage entered, event timestamp, session context, channel, device type, and whether the entity converted to the next stage. Powers funnel drop-off analysis, A/B test conversion measurement, and checkout optimization reporting.'
}

Table analytics.ab_test_result {
  ab_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the A/B test result record.']
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific variant (treatment) within the experiment.']
  attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.attribution_model. Business justification: AB test results can be analyzed using an attribution model; linking provides direct reference.']
  baseline_ab_test_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ab_test_result (baseline_ab_test_result_id)']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the experiment that generated this result.']
  ab_test_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the result record.']
  confidence_interval_lower double [note: 'Lower bound of the confidence interval for the lift.']
  confidence_interval_upper double [note: 'Upper bound of the confidence interval for the lift.']
  confidence_level double [note: 'Confidence level (e.g., 95.0 for 95% confidence) used for the interval.']
  control_value double [note: 'Measured value of the metric for the control group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the result record was first created in the system.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Serialized JSON payload containing raw result details; retained for audit.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event; for this entity always \'result\'.']
  experiment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment concluded or was stopped.']
  experiment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the experiment began.']
  is_statistically_significant boolean [note: 'True if the result meets the predefined significance threshold.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business metric evaluated in the experiment (e.g., conversion_rate, revenue_per_user).']
  observed_value double [note: 'Measured value of the metric for the variant.']
  p_value double [note: 'Statistical p‑value indicating significance of the observed lift.']
  relative_lift double [note: 'Percentage lift of the variant over the control (observed_value - control_value) / control_value.']
  result_recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this result record was captured (event timestamp).']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of users or sessions included in the experiment for this variant.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of experiment: A/B test or multivariate test.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the statistical results and business outcomes of A/B and multivariate experiments run across the platform. Captures experiment reference (linked to content, pricing, or search experiment), variant identifier, metric evaluated, observed value, control value, relative lift, p-value, confidence interval, sample size, and statistical significance flag. Serves as the authoritative results store for all platform experimentation enabling data-driven decision-making.'
}

Table analytics.attribution_model {
  attribution_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the attribution model.']
  baseline_attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on attribution_model (baseline_attribution_model_id)']
  attribution_model_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the model in external systems.']
  attribution_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the model purpose and logic.']
  attribution_model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the attribution model.']
  attribution_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the attribution model.']
  attribution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to assign conversion credit across touchpoints.']
  channel_weighting_json varchar [note: 'JSON representation of channel‑specific weightings used by the model.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the model under GDPR, CCPA, etc.']
  conversion_event varchar [note: 'Business event that qualifies a conversion for attribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source(s) used to train or calibrate the model (e.g., CRM, web analytics).']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment state of the model in production.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is the default attribution model for reporting.']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent performance evaluation of the model.']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to a conversion event that touchpoints are considered.']
  model_owner varchar [note: 'Business team or department responsible for the model.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the model implementation.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) representing model accuracy or fit.']
  privacy_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of the privacy impact assessment for the model.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the model definition is retained after deprecation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the model record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining marketing attribution models used to assign conversion credit across customer touchpoints. Captures model name, attribution type (last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, data-driven), lookback window, channel weighting configuration, conversion event definition, and deployment status. Provides the semantic definition of how marketing spend is credited to revenue outcomes across all attribution reporting.'
}

Table analytics.attribution_result {
  attribution_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each attribution result record.']
  attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.attribution_model. Business justification: Attribution results are produced by a specific attribution model; replace string column with FK.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Attribution results must be tied to the originating marketing campaign for revenue attribution reporting.']
  conversion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the conversion event (e.g., order, signup) to which the attribution credit is assigned.']
  reattributed_attribution_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on attribution_result (reattributed_attribution_result_id)']
  attributed_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of orders assigned to the touchpoint based on the fractional credit.']
  attributed_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of revenue credited to the touchpoint after attribution calculation.']
  attribution_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the attribution record.']
  computation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the attribution credit was computed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the attribution result record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  credit_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fractional weight (0‑1) representing the proportion of credit allocated to this touchpoint.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the attributed revenue.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attributed revenue is an estimate rather than a definitive amount.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the touchpoint within the conversion path.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the attribution calculation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the raw touchpoint data (e.g., SFMC, Google Ads).']
  touchpoint_campaign varchar [note: 'Identifier or code of the marketing campaign associated with the touchpoint.']
  touchpoint_channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel through which the touchpoint was delivered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the attribution result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the computed attribution credit assigned to each marketing touchpoint for a conversion event under a specific attribution model. Stores attribution model reference, conversion event identifier, touchpoint channel, touchpoint campaign, attributed revenue value, attributed order count, fractional credit weight, and computation timestamp. Powers marketing ROI reporting, channel budget optimization, and ROAS analysis.'
}

Table analytics.gmv_daily_snapshot {
  gmv_daily_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate key uniquely identifying each GMV daily snapshot record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: GMV daily snapshots are derived from analytical datasets; linking provides lineage.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Daily GMV snapshots are broken down by marketing channel; FK enables channel‑level financial analysis.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Required for audit of daily GMV snapshot to trace back to source transactions for compliance and dispute resolution.']
  prior_day_gmv_daily_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gmv_daily_snapshot (prior_day_gmv_daily_snapshot_id)']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Enables Daily GMV Snapshot Report per seller, used in financial reconciliation and seller payout processes.']
  active_buyer_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct customers who placed at least one order on the snapshot date.']
  average_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per order (GMV gross divided by order count).']
  cancellation_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of orders cancelled relative to total orders (decimal).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the orders were placed.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  data_freshness_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of how current the snapshot data is relative to source system ingestion.']
  gmv_daily_snapshot_category varchar [note: 'Top‑level product category aggregated for the snapshot.']
  gmv_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross value of merchandise sold before discounts, returns, and fees.']
  gmv_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net GMV after deducting returns, cancellations, and platform fees.']
  order_count varchar [note: 'Number of orders placed on the snapshot date for the given dimensions.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The sales platform (e.g., website, mobile app) where the transactions occurred.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the snapshot record was initially created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the snapshot record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic region of the snapshot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|AUS — promote to reference product]']
  return_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of orders returned relative to total orders (decimal).']
  seller_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of seller based on performance or agreement level.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which the GMV metrics are captured.']
  snapshot_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the snapshot record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the underlying transactional data.']
  take_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Portion of GMV retained by the platform as revenue (percentage expressed as decimal).']

  Note: 'Daily point-in-time snapshot of Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) and key revenue metrics aggregated at the platform, channel, category, and seller tier level. Captures snapshot date, GMV gross, GMV net, order count, average order value, take rate, cancellation rate, return rate, and active buyer count. Serves as the pre-computed operational fact table for executive GMV dashboards, financial reporting, and trend analysis without requiring real-time transactional joins.'
}

Table analytics.category_performance {
  category_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the category performance record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a periodic analytics fact table, does not fit other roles.']

  average_items_per_order decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of items contained in each order.']

  confidence_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑100) for estimated metrics.']

  gmv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross merchandise value for the period.']
  gmv_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for GMV.']
  gross_margin_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Gross margin expressed as a percentage of GMV.']

  inventory_turnover_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of cost of goods sold to average inventory value.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the metrics are estimated (true) or based on actual data (false).']
  net_margin_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Net margin after expenses expressed as a percentage of GMV.']
  new_seller_count varchar [note: 'Count of newly onboarded sellers in the category during the period.']

  promotional_discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount amount divided by GMV, expressed as a decimal.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'Periodicity of the performance record.']
  return_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of returned units to units sold, expressed as a decimal.']
  sell_through_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of inventory sold during the period.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that provided the raw data (e.g., OMS, WMS, PIM).']
  top_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of top‑selling SKUs contributing to the majority of sales.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of units sold across all orders.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance record capturing key commercial and operational metrics for each product category. Stores category identifier, reporting period, GMV, order count, units sold, average selling price, conversion rate, return rate, top SKU count, new seller count, and inventory coverage days. Enables category management teams to monitor health, identify growth opportunities, and benchmark performance across the product taxonomy.'
}

Table analytics.seller_performance_snapshot {
  seller_performance_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each seller performance snapshot record.']
  analytical_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_dataset. Business justification: Seller performance snapshots are derived from analytical datasets; linking provides data provenance.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the snapshot record was initially created in the lakehouse.']
