  period_grain varchar [note: 'Granularity of the reporting period (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the reporting period covered by the snapshot.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code representing the primary market of the seller for the snapshot.']
  return_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of orders that resulted in a return or refund.']
  seller_performance_snapshot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the snapshot record (active = latest, inactive = historical).']
  seller_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the seller based on performance metrics (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum).']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'The calendar date representing the reporting period for the snapshot (e.g., end of day).']
  total_units_sold varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of individual product units sold by the seller during the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the snapshot record.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance snapshot capturing operational and commercial KPIs for each marketplace seller at a defined reporting grain. Captures seller identifier, snapshot period, GMV, order count, cancellation rate, late shipment rate, defect rate, return rate, customer rating average, and tier classification. Complements seller.scorecard by providing the analytics-layer pre-computed time series enabling trend analysis, tier eligibility assessment, and seller health monitoring at scale.'
}

Table analytics.customer_retention_fact {
  customer_retention_fact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each row in the customer retention fact table.']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the acquisition cohort to which the customers belong (e.g., month‑year of first purchase).']
  prior_period_customer_retention_fact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on customer_retention_fact (prior_period_customer_retention_fact_id)']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Retention fact tables aggregate by customer segment; FK enables drill‑down from fact to segment definition for reporting.']
  active_buyer_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct customers who made at least one purchase in the reporting period.']
  average_order_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean monetary value of orders placed by active buyers, expressed in US dollars.']
  churn_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers from the cohort who did not make any purchase during the reporting period and are considered churned.']
  churn_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of the cohort that churned during the reporting period (value between 0 and 1).']
  customer_retention_fact_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the fact row (active = current, inactive = superseded, archived = retained for history).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business-defined segment or cohort classification of the customer (e.g., high‑value, new, churn‑risk).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the underlying raw data (e.g., OMS, CDP, Payment Gateway).']
  new_customers_count varchar [note: 'Number of first‑time buyers acquired during the reporting period.']
  purchase_frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of purchases per active buyer within the reporting period.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fact row was first inserted into the analytics layer.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fact row.']
  repeat_purchase_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of active buyers who placed more than one order during the period (value between 0 and 1).']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period covered by the fact row.']
  reporting_period_grain varchar [note: 'Temporal granularity of the reporting period (e.g., day, week, month, quarter, year).']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period covered by the fact row.']
  retention_30_day_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of customers from the cohort who made a purchase within 30 days after their first purchase.']
  retention_60_day_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of cohort customers who made a purchase within 60 days after acquisition.']
  retention_90_day_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of cohort customers who made a purchase within 90 days after acquisition.']
  total_orders varchar [note: 'Total number of orders placed by the segment/cohort in the reporting period.']
  total_revenue_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross revenue generated by the segment/cohort in US dollars for the reporting period.']

  Note: 'Periodic analytical fact record capturing customer retention and engagement metrics at the customer segment and acquisition cohort level. Stores reporting period, customer segment, cohort reference, active buyer count, repeat purchase rate, purchase frequency, average order value, 30/60/90-day retention rates, and churn count. Powers retention dashboards, loyalty program effectiveness reporting, and customer lifecycle analytics for growth and CRM teams.'
}

Table analytics.operational_alert {
  operational_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each operational alert record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Alerts are raised when campaign KPIs breach thresholds; linking identifies the affected campaign.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Enables alerts tied to carrier SLA breaches, supporting carrier performance monitoring.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: KPI breach alerts often target a specific fulfillment center; linking alerts to center enables root‑cause analysis.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the KPI or metric that triggered the alert.']
  owner_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.agent. Business justification: Alerts are assigned to support agents for resolution; linking owner_agent_id to agent.agent_id enables alert ownership tracking in SLA monitoring.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Real‑time fraud and settlement alerts must reference the specific payment transaction that triggered the alert for investigation.']
  related_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related incident or ticket, if applicable.']
  related_operational_alert_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on operational_alert (related_operational_alert_id)']
  alert_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the alert purpose.']
  alert_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the cause and context of the alert.']
  alert_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the alert, e.g., "High Cart Abandonment Rate".']
  alert_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of user‑defined tags for filtering and reporting.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alert indicating why it was generated.']
  anomaly_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence score for anomaly‑based alerts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was first inserted.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the underlying data that triggered the alert.']
  kpi_target_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the KPI target is an upper bound, lower bound, or a range.']
  kpi_target_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Target value defined for the KPI at the time of alert generation.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the metric associated with the alert.']
  observed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value observed at the time of the alert (numeric or categorical).']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role or function of the alert owner (e.g., Analyst, Manager).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority used for escalation routing.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing actions taken to resolve the alert.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the alert\'s handling workflow.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the alert was marked as resolved or closed.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact level of the alert.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the analytics or monitoring system that emitted the alert.']
  threshold_operator varchar [note: 'Logical operator used to compare observed value with the threshold.']
  threshold_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Configured threshold that the observed value was compared against.']
  triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the alert was generated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the alert record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing threshold-breach alerts generated by the analytics monitoring layer when KPI values deviate from defined targets or anomaly detection rules are triggered. Captures alert name, alert type (threshold breach, anomaly, data quality), KPI or metric reference, observed value, threshold value, severity level (critical/warning/info), triggered timestamp, assigned owner, resolution status, and resolution notes. Enables proactive operational monitoring and escalation workflows.'
}

Table analytics.conversion_event {
  conversion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for conversion_event']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user who performed the conversion.']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of billing address used.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign identifier that drove the conversion.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product involved in the conversion.']
  originating_conversion_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on conversion_event (originating_conversion_event_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Associated order identifier if conversion resulted in purchase.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user session associated with the conversion.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of shipping address used.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model used (e.g., last_click, first_click).']
  attribution_touchpoint varchar [note: 'Touchpoint identifier used for attribution.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Web browser used for the conversion.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Result status of the conversion process.']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Category of conversion action.']
  conversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the conversion.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for conversion value.']
  custom_attributes_json varchar [note: 'JSON string for any additional custom attributes captured at conversion time.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for the conversion.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Promotional discount code applied, if any.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the conversion event occurred.']
  geo_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of user\'s location.']
  geo_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of user\'s location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the user at time of conversion.']
  is_new_user boolean [note: 'Indicates if the user is new (first conversion).']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'Landing page URL where conversion occurred.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of user\'s location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: list of country codes — promote to reference product]']
  location_region varchar [note: 'Region or state within the country.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment method used for purchase conversion.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment transaction.']
  processing_time_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to process the conversion.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the product.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units involved in the conversion.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the conversion event record was first ingested.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the conversion event record.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the user to the conversion.']
  shipping_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping cost associated with the conversion.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the user arrived (e.g., web, mobile app).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the conversion.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount (conversion value + tax + shipping).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User agent string of the browser/device.']
  user_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the user.']
  user_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the user.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for conversion_event. Referenced by conversion_event_id.'
}

Table analytics.session {
  session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for session']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who generated the session.']
  ad_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ad group linked to the session\'s traffic source.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the session.']
  previous_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on session (previous_session_id)']
  app_version varchar [note: 'Version of the mobile or web application used.']
  average_page_load_time_ms varchar [note: 'Mean time in milliseconds for pages to load during the session.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Browser used during the session.']
  city varchar [note: 'City associated with the session\'s IP location.']
  client_time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier of the client device.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'True if the session resulted in a conversion (e.g., purchase).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the session\'s origin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the purchase amount.']
  device_brand varchar [note: 'Manufacturer brand of the device.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (e.g., mobile advertising ID).']
  device_model varchar [note: 'Model name/number of the device.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used for the session.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total length of the session in seconds.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the type of error encountered.']
  error_flag boolean [note: 'True if an error was recorded during the session.']
  geo_latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate derived from IP geolocation.']
  geo_longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate derived from IP geolocation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the session originated.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'True if the session is identified as automated traffic.']
  is_new_visitor boolean [note: 'True if this is the visitor\'s first session.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language preference detected for the session.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last recorded activity within the session.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Type of network connection used during the session.']
  os varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device.']
  page_view_count varchar [note: 'Number of pages viewed during the session.']
  purchase_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of purchases made in the session.']
  purchase_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct purchase events in the session.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the user to the site.']
  screen_resolution varchar [note: 'Screen resolution reported by the device (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  session_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session ended or was terminated.']
  session_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session began.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the session.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'High‑level purpose of the session.']
  traffic_source varchar [note: 'Marketing channel that drove the user to the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the session record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Full user‑agent string reported by the browser.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table analytics.incident {
  incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for incident']

  related_incident_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on incident (related_incident_id)']
  reporter_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (customer, internal user, or external partner) who reported the incident.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the incident type.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was closed and archived.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with handling the incident (e.g., labor, refunds).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the incident cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the problem reported.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the incident.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was escalated to the next level.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the incident\'s impact on business operations.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the incident by the incident management system.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was initially reported.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this incident is a duplicate of an existing one.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the incident for triage and resolution scheduling.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of how the incident was resolved.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was marked as resolved.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Type of resolution applied to the incident.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the incident after investigation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating indicating the impact and urgency of the incident.']
  sla_actual_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual time (in hours) taken to resolve the incident.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target time (in hours) defined by SLA for resolving the incident.']
  source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the incident was reported.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the main category.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for incident. Referenced by related_incident_id.'
}

Table analytics.report_schedule {
  report_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for report_schedule']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user responsible for the schedule.']
  superseded_report_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on report_schedule (superseded_report_schedule_id)']
  alert_enabled boolean [note: 'Whether alerts are sent on schedule failures or successes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days to retain run logs and output before archival.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the schedule purpose and scope.']
  email_recipients varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of email addresses to receive schedule notifications.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule expires; null for indefinite.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual reason for the most recent execution failure.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval for the schedule (e.g., 2 for every 2 days).']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit associated with the frequency interval.']
  is_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is currently enabled for execution.']
  last_failed_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent failed execution of the schedule.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the report.']
  last_successful_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful execution of the schedule.']
  max_retries varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts after a failed run.']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next execution of the report.']
  notify_on_failure boolean [note: 'Whether to send notifications when a run fails.']
  notify_on_success boolean [note: 'Whether to send notifications when a run succeeds.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the schedule owner for notifications.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to order execution when multiple schedules run concurrently.']
  retry_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Delay in minutes between retry attempts.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Execution duration of the most recent run, measured in seconds.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'System‑generated code that uniquely identifies the schedule across environments.']
  schedule_expression varchar [note: 'Cron‑style expression defining the schedule timing.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the schedule used in reporting dashboards.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Category of schedule defining its recurrence pattern.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule becomes effective.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the schedule.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier used to interpret schedule times.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for report_schedule. Referenced by schedule_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulation {
  regulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each regulation record.']
  parent_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulation (parent_regulation_id)']
  audit_evidence_required varchar [note: 'Types of evidence needed to demonstrate compliance (e.g., logs, certificates).']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which compliance actions must be completed.']
  compliance_obligation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates if compliance with the regulation is legally mandatory or voluntary.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture against the regulation.']
  consumer_protection_requirements varchar [note: 'Obligations to protect consumer rights (e.g., refund policies, advertising standards).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulation record was first created in the system.']
  data_protection_level varchar [note: 'Level of data protection required (e.g., "high", "standard").']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulation becomes legally binding.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Whether the regulation is currently being enforced by the authority.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulation ceases to be in effect (nullable for open‑ended regulations).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the regulation is mandatory for the business, false otherwise.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction to which the regulation applies (e.g., "EU", "California").']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for this regulation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations.']
  payment_security_requirements varchar [note: 'Requirements related to payment data security (e.g., PCI DSS compliance).']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty stipulated for non‑compliance.']
  privacy_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific privacy obligations (e.g., consent, data minimization).']
  regulation_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the regulation purpose.']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code or identifier for the regulation (e.g., "GDPR", "PCI_DSS").']
  regulation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the regulation\'s purpose and requirements.']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the regulation (e.g., "General Data Protection Regulation").']
  regulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulation record.']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'More specific type within the category (e.g., "Consent Management" under Data Privacy).']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority that issues or enforces the regulation (e.g., "European Commission", "PCI SSC").']
  scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of applicability.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulation document or web page.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the regulation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the regulation record after amendments.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all applicable regulations, laws, and standards governing the e-commerce platform. Captures regulation name, jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA, PCI DSS, COPPA, CAN-SPAM, consumer protection acts), regulatory body, effective date, expiry date, applicability scope (global, regional, country-specific), regulation category (data privacy, payment security, consumer protection, tax, trade), compliance obligation type (mandatory, voluntary), and current enforcement status. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all regulatory obligations the platform must satisfy.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance obligation record.']
  parent_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on obligation (parent_obligation_id)']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent regulation or standard that creates this obligation.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the audit log entry for this obligation.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the obligation.']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to stored evidence supporting compliance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the obligation record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Calendar date by which the obligation must be fulfilled.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the obligation becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the obligation expires or is no longer applicable (null if indefinite).']
  enforcement_penalty varchar [note: 'Description of penalties or sanctions for non‑compliance.']
  evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting evidence must be provided for this obligation.']
  evidence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required compliance evidence.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the obligation is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review.']
  next_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next compliance review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code that uniquely identifies the obligation within the organization.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of what the obligation requires.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the compliance obligation.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the compliance requirement.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the obligation originated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: order_management|warehouse_management|product_information|customer_data_platform|payment_gateway|erp|transportation_management|marketing_automation|search_recommendation|seller_portal — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'How often the obligation repeats.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Internal business unit accountable for satisfying the obligation.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to retain records related to the obligation.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the obligation based on potential impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the obligation record.']

  Note: 'Defines specific compliance obligations derived from regulations and standards applicable to the e-commerce platform. Captures obligation name, parent regulation reference, obligation type (data subject rights, breach notification, consent management, record retention, security control, audit requirement), responsible business unit, due date, recurrence pattern (one-time, annual, quarterly, continuous), obligation status (active, waived, deferred), and risk tier. Bridges the regulation registry to operational compliance activities.'
}

Table compliance.program {
  program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance program.']
  parent_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on program (parent_program_id)']
  applicable_regulations varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulations (e.g., GDPR, PCI DSS, CCPA) that the program addresses.']
  audit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of audits performed to date.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned interval between compliance audits.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary budget allocated to the program.']
  ccpa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program meets CCPA requirements.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the program achieved certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification outcome for the program.']
  compliance_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of open compliance issues identified.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0‑100) reflecting compliance health.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget.']
  data_protection_level varchar [note: 'Classification of data handled by the program.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the central repository of program documentation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the program becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the program ends or is retired (nullable for open‑ended programs).']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program meets GDPR obligations.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction(s) the program applies to.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit execution.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the compliance program.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Planned date for the upcoming audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the program.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person responsible for the program.']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  privacy_impact_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'True when a Privacy Impact Assessment has been performed and approved.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'Secondary categorization (e.g., data protection, financial, consumer rights).']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the program objectives and scope.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compliance program.']
  program_scope varchar [note: 'Brief description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the program.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the program purpose.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority overseeing the program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTC|PCI_SSC|ISO|GDPR|CCPA|CPSC|WTO|NIST|SOX — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Target date for resolving all open compliance issues.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk tier based on the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) derived from risk assessments.']
  sponsoring_executive varchar [note: 'Name of the senior executive sponsoring the program.']
  stakeholder_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct internal or external stakeholders involved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master record defining named compliance programs managed by the compliance function, such as GDPR Compliance Program, PCI DSS Certification Program, CCPA Readiness Program, or Consumer Protection Program. Captures program name, program owner, sponsoring executive, program scope, applicable regulations, program lifecycle status (planning, active, remediation, certified, retired), program start and end dates, budget allocation, and overall risk posture. Provides the organizational container for grouping related obligations, audits, and certifications.'
}

Table compliance.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the audit record.']
  follow_up_audit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit (follow_up_audit_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Audit belongs to a compliance program; replace string with proper FK']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Audit references the regulation it audits; replace free‑text reference with a foreign key to regulation.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Audit records are scoped to a specific seller; compliance audits require linking each audit to the seller being examined.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Governance decision on audit acceptance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the audit was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the audit.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the audit focus area.']
  audit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit (e.g., "Q2 2024 PCI DSS Audit").']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the audit by the organization.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Category of audit indicating its origin and purpose.']
  auditing_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the internal team or external firm conducting the audit.']
  ccpa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with California Consumer Privacy Act.']
  certification_expiry date [note: 'Date when the certification granted by the audit expires.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification state resulting from the audit.']
  cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost incurred for conducting the audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for audit_cost.']
  data_protection_impact varchar [note: 'Assessed impact on data protection controls.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit report or supporting documentation.']
  evidence_count varchar [note: 'Number of evidence items collected during the audit.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of audit findings and observations.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Planned recurrence interval for this type of audit.']
  gdpr_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with GDPR obligations.']
  issues_count varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct audit issues identified.']
  lead_auditor varchar [note: 'Primary individual responsible for leading the audit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location where the audit was performed.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Methodological approach used for the audit.']
  next_audit_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subsequent audit cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by auditors.']
  overall_opinion varchar [note: 'Final judgment of the audit outcome.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Individual accountable for the audit execution.']
  pci_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PCI DSS requirements were satisfied.']
  period_end date [note: 'Date when the audit period ends.']
  period_start date [note: 'Date when the audit period begins.']
  privacy_impact varchar [note: 'Assessed impact on personal data privacy.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to address identified findings.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the audit results were reviewed by governance.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk level assigned to the audit based on findings.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defined boundaries and focus areas of the audit (e.g., payment processing, data privacy).']
  sox_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with Sarbanes‑Oxley Act requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the audit record (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master record for each compliance audit conducted against the e-commerce platform, including internal audits, external third-party audits, and regulatory examinations. Captures audit name, audit type (internal, external, regulatory, SOC 2, PCI QSA, ISO 27001), audit scope, auditing entity or firm, lead auditor, audit period start and end dates, audit status (scheduled, in-progress, findings-issued, remediation, closed), overall audit opinion (pass, qualified, fail), and associated compliance program. Serves as the SSOT for all audit lifecycle management.'
}

Table compliance.audit_schedule {
  audit_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each audit schedule record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Schedule must reference the audit it belongs to; creates parent-child relationship']
  rescheduled_from_audit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_schedule (rescheduled_from_audit_schedule_id)']
  assigned_auditor varchar [note: 'Name of the internal auditor or external audit firm responsible for the audit.']
  assigned_auditor_contact varchar [note: 'Email address for contacting the assigned auditor or audit firm.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit is performed by internal staff or an external firm.']
  audit_fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the audit is attributed.']
  audit_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location where the audit activities will be conducted.']
  audit_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the audit (high, medium, low).']
  audit_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit schedule.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the functional, geographic, or process scope of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (e.g., compliance, security, financial, operational).']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or department that is subject to the audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit schedule record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the audit plan, checklist, or related documentation.']
  estimated_effort_hours varchar [note: 'Projected number of person‑hours required to complete the audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the audit schedule.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Date the audit is planned to conclude.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Date the audit is planned to commence.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the audit repeats.']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulation, standard, or policy driving the audit (e.g., "PCI_DSS", "GDPR").']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the audit based on impact and likelihood.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for cancellation or delay).']
  target_period_end date [note: 'End date of the audit coverage period.']
  target_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the audit coverage period (e.g., fiscal quarter being audited).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit schedule record.']

  Note: 'Operational scheduling record defining the planned audit calendar for the compliance domain. Captures scheduled audit name, audit type, target audit period, planned start date, planned end date, assigned auditor or audit firm, business unit in scope, associated regulation or standard, scheduling status (planned, confirmed, rescheduled, cancelled), and recurrence pattern (annual, semi-annual, quarterly). Enables proactive audit planning and resource allocation across the compliance calendar.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each audit finding.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overall audit session or campaign this finding belongs to.']
  related_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_finding (related_audit_finding_id)']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Date the finding was officially closed or accepted as risk.']
  affected_control varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal control or process impacted by the finding.']
  audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the observation, deficiency, or recommendation.']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework to which the finding relates.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation or artifacts stored in the evidence repository.']
  finding_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the finding, used in reports and communications.']
  finding_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the finding was recorded in the audit system.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of the finding indicating its nature and impact.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the legal jurisdiction affected.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level for remediation effort.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit finding record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit finding record.']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual accountable for executing the remediation.']
  remediation_recommendation varchar [note: 'Suggested actions to correct or mitigate the finding.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score derived from severity and impact factors.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Explanation of the underlying cause that led to the finding.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity assigned to the finding based on potential impact.']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Planned date by which remediation should be completed.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise title summarizing the audit finding.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each finding, observation, or deficiency identified during a compliance audit. Captures finding title, finding description, finding type (critical deficiency, major non-conformance, minor observation, best-practice recommendation), severity rating (critical, high, medium, low), affected control or process, root cause analysis, remediation recommendation, finding status (open, in-remediation, remediated, accepted-risk, closed), target remediation date, and actual closure date. Core operational record for audit finding lifecycle management.'
}

Table compliance.remediation_plan {
  remediation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the remediation plan record.']

  linked_risk_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk assessment record associated with the remediation plan.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the remediation plan was actually completed.']
  approach_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the remediation approach, steps, and methodology.']
  audit_evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit evidence record linked to this remediation plan.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain the plan belongs to (e.g., PCI_DSS, GDPR, CCPA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the remediation plan record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the remediation plan becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the remediation plan expires or is retired (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the remediation plan has been escalated to higher management.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to stored evidence (documents, screenshots, logs) proving remediation completion.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the remediation plan was last reviewed for relevance or updates.']
  milestone_checkpoints varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing milestone names, target dates, and completion flags.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra comments or observations.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the remediation plan, used in external reporting and tracking.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation plan objectives and scope.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the remediation plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the remediation plan by its nature (e.g., policy change, technical fix, process improvement, training).']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all remediation activities.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the remediation plan.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulation or standard clause addressed (e.g., GDPR Art.5, PCI DSS 12.3).']
  remediation_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the remediation plan.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent plan review.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the compliance gap addressed by the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the remediation plan record.']

  Note: 'Operational record tracking the remediation plan and action items associated with audit findings, compliance gaps, or risk assessments. Captures plan name, linked finding or risk, remediation owner, remediation approach description, planned completion date, actual completion date, remediation status (draft, approved, in-progress, completed, overdue, cancelled), milestone checkpoints, escalation flag, and evidence of completion. Enables structured tracking of compliance gap closure across the organization.'
}

Table compliance.certification {
  certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each certification record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance program that this certification supports.']
  renewed_from_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on certification (renewed_from_certification_id)']

  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned frequency of audits required to maintain the certification.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit (e.g., full, partial, specific control set).']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or certificate number assigned by the certifying body.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., PCI DSS Level 1).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification (e.g., PCI SSC, ISO).']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred to obtain or maintain the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the certification becomes effective for the organization.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the certification ceases to be effective (may differ from expiry).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires if not renewed.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was officially issued.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent audit associated with this certification.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for certification renewal activities.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the renewal process.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the certification\'s scope.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the business processes, systems, or locations covered by the certification.']
  standard varchar [note: 'Standard or framework under which the certification is granted.']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the certification standard (e.g., ISO 27001:2022).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for suspension).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Master record for compliance certifications and attestations obtained by the e-commerce platform, including PCI DSS Level 1 certification, ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, GDPR Article 42 certification, and similar. Captures certification name, certifying body, certification standard version, certification scope, issue date, expiry date, certification status (active, expired, suspended, revoked, in-renewal), certificate number, and associated compliance program. SSOT for the certification lifecycle and renewal management.'
}

Table compliance.evidence {
  evidence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance evidence record.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Evidence is tied to a specific compliance obligation; replace string with FK']

  audit_comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes recorded during audit reviews of the evidence.']
  chain_of_custody varchar [note: 'Notes documenting handling, transfers, and verification steps for the evidence.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the evidence file for integrity verification.']
  collected_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or system that performed the evidence collection.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the evidence was originally collected.']
  collector_role varchar [note: 'Role or function of the collector (e.g., auditor, system, compliance officer).']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework to which the evidence relates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this evidence record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code or number assigned to the evidence artifact for tracking.']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or details about the evidence.']
  evidence_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the evidence.']
  evidence_type varchar [note: 'Category of the evidence artifact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_record — promote to reference product]']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the evidence file in bytes.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored evidence artifact.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the evidence contains confidential or restricted information.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the jurisdiction governing the evidence.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the evidence must be archived or destroyed per policy.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Path, URL, or repository identifier where the evidence artifact is stored.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the evidence artifact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the evidence record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing compliance evidence artifacts collected to demonstrate adherence to regulations, audit requirements, or certification standards. Captures evidence title, evidence type (policy document, screenshot, log extract, test result, attestation, contract clause, training record), collection date, collected by, associated obligation or audit finding, evidence status (collected, reviewed, accepted, rejected), storage location reference, retention expiry date, and chain-of-custody notes. Core operational record for evidence management in audit and certification workflows.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_notice {
  privacy_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the privacy notice record.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Privacy notice is issued to satisfy a regulation; link to regulation']
  superseded_privacy_notice_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on privacy_notice (superseded_privacy_notice_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the notice within the legal review workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notice was approved.']
  character_count varchar [note: 'Number of characters in the notice text.']
  compliance_ccpa boolean [note: 'True if the notice satisfies California Consumer Privacy Act requirements.']
  compliance_eprivacy boolean [note: 'True if the notice satisfies the EU ePrivacy Directive.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'True if the notice satisfies GDPR requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notice record was first created in the system.']

  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored notice.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the notice becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the notice expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended notices).']
  is_internal_use_only boolean [note: 'True if the notice is intended for internal staff only and not published publicly.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notice is required by law for the given jurisdiction.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction the notice applies to.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale identifier (e.g., "en_US", "fr_FR") indicating language and regional formatting.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last compliance review of the notice.']
  legal_owner varchar [note: 'Internal department or team responsible for the notice.']
  notice_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the notice in internal systems.']
  notice_text varchar [note: 'Complete textual content of the privacy notice.']
  notice_type varchar [note: 'Category of the notice indicating its legal purpose.']
  privacy_notice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notice.']
  publication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel where the notice is presented to users.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between mandatory reviews of the notice.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that authored or uploaded the notice (e.g., CMS, Legal Portal).']
  summary_text varchar [note: 'Short summary or excerpt of the notice for quick reference.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the privacy notice (e.g., "Privacy Policy").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the notice record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version of the notice (e.g., "1.0" or "2.1.3").']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Number of words in the notice text.']

  Note: 'Master record for all privacy notices, cookie policies, and data processing disclosures published by the e-commerce platform. Captures notice name, notice type (privacy policy, cookie notice, terms of service, data processing agreement summary), applicable jurisdiction, version number, effective date, expiry or superseded date, publication channel (website, mobile app, checkout flow), language/locale, notice status (draft, published, archived), and the full notice text body. Enables version-controlled management of all customer-facing privacy disclosures required under GDPR, CCPA, and ePrivacy regulations.'
}

Table compliance.legal_hold {
  legal_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the legal hold record.']
  superseded_legal_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on legal_hold (superseded_legal_hold_id)']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'Date when the hold was officially released; null if still active.']
  anticipated_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for hold termination, if known.']
  audit_evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting audit evidence must be collected for this hold.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which compliance actions related to the hold must be completed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the hold.']
  consumer_protection_requirements varchar [note: 'Requirements from consumer‑protection regulations (e.g., FTC) relevant to the hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the legal hold record was first created in the system.']
  custodians_notified varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of individuals or teams notified about the hold.']
  data_categories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of data categories (e.g., PII, financial, transaction logs) that are subject to the hold.']
  data_protection_level varchar [note: 'Classifies the sensitivity of the data under hold.']
  hold_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification (e.g., litigation, regulatory, internal).']
  hold_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the legal hold for easy identification.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Business or legal rationale for initiating the hold.']
  hold_scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the scope of data, documents, and systems covered by the hold.']
  hold_start_date date [note: 'Date when the legal hold becomes effective.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold: litigation, regulatory investigation, or internal investigation.']
  hold_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the hold record to track amendments.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Legal team, department, or external authority that issued the hold.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction governing the hold (e.g., US, EU).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit performed for this hold.']
  legal_matter_reference varchar [note: 'External reference or case number linking the hold to a specific legal matter.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by legal or compliance teams.']
  payment_security_requirements varchar [note: 'PCI DSS or other payment‑security obligations tied to the hold.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Potential monetary penalty associated with non‑compliance of the hold.']
  privacy_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific privacy obligations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) applicable to the hold.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority whose requirements drive the hold (e.g., FTC, GDPR, PCI SSC).']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days data must be retained under the hold.']
  systems_in_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of operational systems (e.g., OMS, WMS, PIM) whose data are covered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the legal hold record.']

  Note: 'Master record managing legal hold orders placed on data and documents to preserve evidence for litigation, regulatory investigation, or e-discovery. Captures hold name, hold type (litigation hold, regulatory investigation, internal investigation), issuing authority or legal team, hold scope description, data categories and systems in scope, hold start date, anticipated hold end date, actual release date, hold status (active, released, extended, disputed), custodians notified, and associated legal matter reference. SSOT for legal hold lifecycle management ensuring data preservation obligations are met.'
}

  custodian_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the custodian: internal employee, external vendor, or system owner.']
  data_sources_in_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of data source systems (e.g., OMS, WMS, CDP) covered by this custodian\'s legal hold.']

  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the legal hold custodian record.']

  Note: 'Association record linking individual data custodians (employees, system owners, third-party processors) to active legal holds. Captures custodian name, custodian role, custodian type (internal employee, external vendor, system owner), notification date, acknowledgment status and date, data sources in custodian scope, custodian hold status (active, released), and any custodian-specific hold instructions. Enables granular tracking of legal hold notification and acknowledgment obligations across all relevant data custodians.'
}

Table compliance.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each risk assessment record.']
  previous_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_assessment (previous_risk_assessment_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier risk assessment informs procurement sourcing decisions and supplier selection.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment was performed.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the risk assessment.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether risk is acceptable, accepted, or requires remediation.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the assessment, describing the systems, processes, or business units covered.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the assessment, such as Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) or PCI DSS risk assessment.']
  assessment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the risk assessment record, supporting change tracking.']
  audit_evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether audit evidence is mandatory for this assessment.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework under which the assessment was conducted.']
  control_gaps varchar [note: 'Description of gaps in controls identified during the assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk assessment record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to stored evidence supporting the assessment.']
  evidence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of evidence collection.']
  identified_risks varchar [note: 'Free‑text list or JSON of risks identified during the assessment.']
  impact_rating varchar [note: 'Impact rating assigned to the identified risks.']
  inherent_risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the risk before controls are applied.']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood rating assigned to the identified risks.']
  mitigation_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which mitigation actions should be completed.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to address identified control gaps.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next risk reassessment.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body or authority associated with the regulation (e.g., FTC, PCI SSC).']
  residual_risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) after existing controls are considered.']
  reviewed_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the reviewer.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the risk (e.g., Data Protection, Payment Security).']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified risk.']
  risk_owner_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk owner.']
  risk_score_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology used to calculate inherent and residual risk scores.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk assessment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing formal compliance and privacy risk assessments conducted across the e-commerce platform, including Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) required under GDPR Article 35, PCI DSS risk assessments, and general compliance risk reviews. Captures assessment name, assessment type (DPIA, PCI risk assessment, vendor risk assessment, general compliance review), assessment scope, assessment date, risk owner, identified risks with likelihood and impact ratings, inherent risk score, residual risk score after controls, control gaps identified, assessment outcome (acceptable risk, risk accepted, remediation required), and next review date.'
}

Table compliance.control {
  control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance control.']
  parent_control_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control (parent_control_id)']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Control applies to a specific regulation; replace free‑text attribute with a foreign key to regulation.']
  automation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is automated (true) or manual (false).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the control against its applicable regulation.']
  continuous_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is continuously monitored by automated systems.']
  control_category varchar [note: 'Broad category indicating whether the control is technical, administrative, or physical.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Business‑unique alphanumeric code used to reference the control.']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and mechanics of the control.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control as defined in the compliance framework.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control by its purpose: preventive, detective, or corrective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created in the system.']
  dependent_controls varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other control codes that this control depends on.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier (e.g., policy number, SOP ID) to the formal documentation governing the control.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control is scheduled to be retired or become obsolete (nullable).']
  evidence_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days evidence for this control must be retained per regulatory requirement.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored evidence supporting the control\'s effectiveness.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed or evaluated.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent audit activity related to this control.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date when the control was most recently tested.']
  last_test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent control test.']
  next_test_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled test of the control.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks, observations, or exceptions.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for the control.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned corrective action if the control fails its test.']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific reporting obligation linked to the control (e.g., quarterly PCI DSS audit).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) reflecting the control\'s impact and likelihood.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the control\'s failure.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the business processes, systems, or data covered by the control.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the control definition (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master record defining the compliance controls framework implemented across the e-commerce platform to satisfy regulatory and certification requirements. Captures control ID, control name, control description, control type (preventive, detective, corrective), control category (technical, administrative, physical), applicable regulation or standard (GDPR, PCI DSS, CCPA, SOC 2), control owner, control frequency (continuous, daily, monthly, annual), automation flag, control status (active, inactive, compensating), and last test date. Serves as the SSOT for the internal controls inventory.'
}

Table compliance.control_test {
  control_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the control test record.']
  control_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control that is being tested.']
  retest_of_control_test_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control_test (retest_of_control_test_id)']
  tester_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who performed the test.']
  audit_evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting audit evidence is required for this test.']
  audit_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the audit evidence file or repository.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which compliance remediation must be completed.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework governing the control.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the control after testing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control test record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the test record for data governance.']
  deficiencies varchar [note: 'Text description of any deficiencies or gaps found during testing.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting evidence or documentation for the test.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was executed.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the test was performed by automated monitoring.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country code of the jurisdiction governing the control.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the test result.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Response or remediation plan provided by management for identified deficiencies.']
  next_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next scheduled review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the tester.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body responsible for the regulation (e.g., FTC, PCI SSC).']
  result_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the degree of effectiveness.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the test record must be retained for compliance.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Planned date for a follow‑up test to verify remediation.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the control test outcome.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of items, transactions, or records sampled for the test.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the control being tested.']
  test_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the test, used for reference in audit logs and reports.']
  test_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to execute the test, measured in seconds.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Technical environment where the test was performed.']
  test_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology or approach used to conduct the test.']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the control test.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the test indicating control effectiveness.']
  test_scope varchar [note: 'Scope or boundary of the test (e.g., process, system, transaction).']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test execution.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of test performed to evaluate control effectiveness.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control test record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the results of compliance control testing and effectiveness evaluations. Captures test name, tested control reference, test type (design effectiveness, operating effectiveness, walkthrough, automated monitoring), test execution date, tester identity, test methodology, sample size, test result (effective, ineffective, partially effective), deficiencies identified, management response, and retest date. Provides the audit trail for control testing activities required by SOC 2, PCI DSS, and internal audit programs.'
}

Table compliance.vendor_compliance_assessment {

  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party vendor being assessed.']
  assessment_conducted_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that performed the assessment.']
