  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance assessment of the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the assessor.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority associated with the compliance framework (e.g., GDPR, PCI SSC).']
  remediation_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific actions the vendor must take to remediate findings.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score derived from the assessment findings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']
  vendor_compliance_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing third-party vendor and processor compliance assessments conducted as part of GDPR Article 28 processor due diligence, PCI DSS third-party service provider (TPSP) requirements, and general vendor risk management. Captures vendor name, vendor type (data processor, sub-processor, payment processor, logistics partner), assessment date, assessment type (questionnaire, on-site audit, certification review), compliance frameworks assessed, assessment outcome (compliant, conditionally compliant, non-compliant), critical findings, remediation requirements, next assessment due date, and data processing agreement status. Distinct from procurement supplier assessments — this record is owned by compliance and focuses on regulatory obligations.'
}

Table compliance.consent_policy {
  consent_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consent policy record.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Consent policy is defined for a specific regulation; replace string with FK']
  superseded_consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on consent_policy (superseded_consent_policy_id)']
  audit_evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether audit evidence must be collected for this policy.']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to documentation or evidence supporting compliance status.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the consent policy.']
  consent_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent policy.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent being governed (e.g., marketing email, SMS, push notification, cookie analytics, cookie advertising, profiling).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent policy record was first created.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data may be retained under this consent policy.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the consent policy becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consent policy expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  granularity_level varchar [note: 'Level of detail at which consent is applied (global, category, item, or purpose).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this policy is the default for its consent type.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent is mandatory under applicable regulation.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review of the consent policy.']
  mechanism varchar [note: 'Method by which consent is obtained (opt‑in, opt‑out, double opt‑in).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the consent policy.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'System-generated code uniquely identifying the consent policy.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the consent policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the consent policy.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent policy (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the consent policy must be reviewed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or maintains the consent policy record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent policy record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the consent policy.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the consent collection policies and configurations governing how customer consent is captured, stored, and managed across the e-commerce platform. Captures policy name, consent type (marketing email, SMS, push notification, cookie analytics, cookie advertising, data sharing with third parties, profiling), applicable regulation (GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy), consent mechanism (opt-in, opt-out, double opt-in), consent granularity level, policy version, effective date, expiry date, and policy status. Distinct from customer.consent_event (which records individual customer consent actions) — this entity defines the policy framework governing those events.'
}

Table compliance.retention_policy {
  retention_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the retention policy record.']
  regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Retention policy is governed by a regulation; replace code with FK']
  superseded_retention_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on retention_policy (superseded_retention_policy_id)']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the policy against its governing regulation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created.']
  data_category varchar [note: 'Category of data to which the retention policy applies.']
  data_sensitivity_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating the sensitivity of the data subject to the policy.']
  deletion_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to remove or render data unusable after the retention period.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the retention policy becomes effective.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was last reviewed.']
  maximum_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the data may be retained before deletion.']
  minimum_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the data must be retained.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next policy review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the policy.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business code or short identifier used to reference the policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the retention policy.']
  policy_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or responsible team for the retention policy.']
  retention_action varchar [note: 'Action to be taken when the retention expiry date is reached.']
  retention_audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to audit evidence supporting the policy\'s compliance.']
  retention_basis varchar [note: 'Legal or business basis for retaining the data.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the data must be deleted or otherwise disposed.']
  retention_extension_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retention period may be extended beyond the maximum.']
  retention_extension_justification varchar [note: 'Reason and approval details for any extension of the retention period.']
  retention_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the retention policy.']
  retention_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation why the data must be retained.']
  retention_review_owner varchar [note: 'Person or team responsible for conducting the periodic policy review.']
  retention_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent retention policy review.']
  retention_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event that initiates the retention period (e.g., order completion, account closure).']
  retention_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retention rule (e.g., legal, contractual, internal policy).']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory policy reviews.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system or application where the policy was originally defined.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the policy record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the policy.']

  Note: 'Master record defining data retention and deletion policies for each data category managed on the e-commerce platform. Captures policy name, data category (customer PII, order records, payment data, behavioral data, audit logs, marketing data), applicable regulation driving retention (GDPR Article 5(1)(e), PCI DSS Requirement 3, tax law, consumer protection), minimum retention period, maximum retention period, retention basis (legal obligation, legitimate interest, contractual), deletion method (anonymization, pseudonymization, hard delete), policy owner, effective date, and review cycle. SSOT for data lifecycle and retention governance.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for compliance training programs and courses required for employees and contractors on the e-commerce platform. Enables management of mandatory compliance training obligations.'
}

}

Table compliance.compliance_exception {
  compliance_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance exception record.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Compliance exception must reference the obligation it exempts']
  renewed_from_compliance_exception_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compliance_exception (renewed_from_compliance_exception_id)']
  affected_policy varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the policy, control, or regulation that the exception impacts.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was formally approved.']
  approving_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the approving manager or compliance officer.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the primary business event associated with the exception (e.g., date of request submission).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the exception is required from a business perspective.']
  compensating_controls varchar [note: 'Description of alternative controls put in place to mitigate risk while the exception is active.']
  compliance_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was first created in the system.']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code assigned to the exception for tracking and reference.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the exception: policy exception, control exception, or regulatory deviation.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception expires or is scheduled to be revoked.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information captured by compliance analysts.']
  remediation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the remediation plan that addresses the root cause of the exception.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or organization submitting the exception request.']
  risk_acceptance_rationale varchar [note: 'Reasoning for accepting the residual risk associated with the exception.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the residual risk associated with the exception.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception becomes effective.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title summarizing the nature of the compliance exception.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing formally approved exceptions to compliance policies, controls, or regulatory requirements where full compliance cannot be immediately achieved. Captures exception title, exception type (policy exception, control exception, regulatory deviation), affected policy or control, business justification, risk acceptance rationale, exception requestor, approving authority, approval date, exception start date, exception expiry date, compensating controls in place, exception status (pending-approval, approved, expired, revoked), and remediation plan reference. Provides the governance audit trail for risk-accepted compliance deviations.'
}

Table compliance.supervisory_correspondence {
  supervisory_correspondence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each supervisory correspondence record.']
  in_reply_to_supervisory_correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on supervisory_correspondence (in_reply_to_supervisory_correspondence_id)']
  linked_compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance program governing this correspondence.']
  associated_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Regulation code (e.g., GDPR, PCI_DSS) that this correspondence relates to.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the correspondence.']
  audit_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the location of audit evidence files stored in the document repository.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit log entry for this correspondence.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the supervisory authority (e.g., Federal Trade Commission).']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Category of the authority handling the correspondence.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel used for the correspondence exchange.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which compliance actions related to the correspondence must be completed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the correspondence with respect to the associated regulation.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the correspondence data per corporate policy.']
  correspondence_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the communication with the supervisory authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the correspondence record was first created in the system.']
  data_protection_level varchar [note: 'Level of data protection applied to the correspondence content.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the correspondence has been escalated for higher-level review.']
  evidence_attached_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting evidence documents are attached to the correspondence.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Date by which any required follow‑up must be completed.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional action is required after the initial response.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether responding to this correspondence is mandatory under law.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the authority\'s jurisdiction.']
  last_response_date date [note: 'Date the most recent response was sent to the authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by compliance staff.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level for handling the correspondence.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the correspondence record.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulatory authority or internal tracking system.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'Date by which a response must be provided to the authority.']
  response_method varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the response to the authority.']
  response_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the authority\'s review of the submitted response.']
  response_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the response was submitted.']
  response_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of the response provided to the authority.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the correspondence record must be retained per policy.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the correspondence based on potential impact.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the correspondence was initially submitted to the authority.']
  supervisory_correspondence_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the correspondence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the correspondence record.']

  Note: 'Master record managing all formal correspondence and communications with regulatory supervisory authorities, including data protection authorities (DPAs), payment card industry bodies, consumer protection agencies, and tax authorities. Captures correspondence reference number, authority name, authority type (DPA, PCI SSC, FTC, CFPB, national consumer authority), correspondence type (inquiry, investigation notice, enforcement action, voluntary disclosure, response submission, approval request), submission date, response deadline, correspondence status (received, acknowledged, response-submitted, closed, escalated), associated regulation, and linked compliance program or breach incident. SSOT for regulatory authority engagement tracking.'
}

Table compliance.tester {
  tester_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tester']
  supervised_by_tester_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tester (supervised_by_tester_id)']
  category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the tester type for reporting and governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tester record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tester, including its objectives, scope, and any special considerations.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed documentation or specification for the tester.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tester becomes valid for use in compliance activities.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tester is retired or no longer applicable. Null if indefinite.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether execution of this tester is mandatory for compliance certification.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this tester in the compliance program.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the tester, describing its purpose or scope.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for maintaining the tester definition.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tester record.']
  test_level varchar [note: 'Granularity level of the tester within the testing hierarchy.']
  tester_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the tester in compliance documentation and system configurations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tester record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tester. Referenced by tester_id.'
}

Table compliance.data_processing_agreement {
  data_processing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for data_processing_agreement']
  superseded_data_processing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on data_processing_agreement (superseded_data_processing_agreement_id)']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the data processing agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement indicating the regulatory framework it addresses.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the agreement.']
  audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Interval in months at which compliance audits are performed.']
  breach_notification_requirements varchar [note: 'Obligations and timelines for notifying breaches under the agreement.']
  ccpa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the agreement meets CCPA requirements.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the agreement.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for agreement-related communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for agreement-related communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  data_access_controls varchar [note: 'Controls governing who can access the data under the agreement.']
  data_categories varchar [note: 'Types of personal data covered (e.g., identifiers, contact information, transaction data). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: identifiers|contact|transaction|behavioral|location|financial|health — promote to reference product]']
  data_controller varchar [note: 'Organization that determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.']
  data_encryption_at_rest boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data is encrypted while stored.']
  data_encryption_in_transit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data is encrypted during transmission.']
  data_processor varchar [note: 'Organization that processes personal data on behalf of the controller.']
  data_retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy describing how long data is retained and deletion procedures.']
  data_subject_rights varchar [note: 'Procedures for handling data subject access, correction, deletion, etc.']
  data_transfer_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location(s) where transferred data will be stored or processed.']
  data_transfer_mechanism varchar [note: 'Legal mechanism used for cross‑border data transfers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the agreement expires or ends; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  gdpr_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the agreement meets GDPR requirements.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary legal jurisdiction governing the agreement, expressed as ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']
  lawful_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis for processing personal data under GDPR.']
  next_audit_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit.']
  pci_dss_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of PCI DSS applicability within the agreement.']
  purpose_of_processing varchar [note: 'Business purpose(s) for which personal data is processed under the agreement.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Whether the agreement renews automatically, requires manual renewal, or does not renew.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data is retained before deletion.']
  security_measures varchar [note: 'Technical and organizational safeguards applied to protect data.']
  signed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who signed the agreement on behalf of the organization.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was signed by the parties.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing conditions under which the agreement may be terminated.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  third_party_sharing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data is shared with third parties beyond the processor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the agreement document.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for data_processing_agreement. Referenced by data_processing_agreement_id.'
}
